(c) require the HRM Officer to comply with the filling up of the vacant position in the LDRRMO in accordance with NDRRMC, DILG, DBM and CSC JMC No. 2014- 1 to effectively carry out the DRRM activities and programs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
(a) Instruct the BPLO to:
    i. strictly enforce the Revenue Code of Quezon, Nueva Ecija;
    ii. efficiently utilize reports from the Accounting Office for assessing business tax on entities with transactions in the Municipality; and
    iii. mandate all business taxpayers to submit a certified copy of their Income Tax Return (ITR).
(b) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to examine and verify the books of accounts and pertinent records for business establishments with unreliable declared sales amounts to ensure accuracy.
(c) Task both the BPLO and the Municipal Treasurer to:
    i. Verify, assess, and validate the identified under-collection and subsequently demand the collection thereof, including the disclosed under-collection of business taxes from six business establishments totaling ₱32,681.83; and
    ii. Require the registration of the identified businesses that were not included in the list of registered business within the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor instruct:
• The City Agriculturist, together with the Inventory Committee, to conduct physical inventory of the breeding stocks, if still feasible, and provide the OIC-CGSO and the City Accountant with the inventory reports, for updating of the property cards and ledger cards.
However, in case of death or disposal, facilitate the request for relief of accountability in accordance with Sections 151 and 152 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992; and
• The OIC-CGSO and the Acting City Accountant to update their property cards and ledger cards based on the inventory reports to be provided by the City Agriculturist.
observations.managementAction:
The City Agriculturist, Office conducted the inspection and physical inventory of the breeding stocks. The City Agriculturist together with the Inventory Committee, to conduct physical inventory of the breeding stocks, if still feasible, and provide the OIC-CGSO and the City Accountant with the inventory reports, for updating of the property cards and ledger cards.
However, in case of death or disposal, facilitate the request for relief of accountability in accordance with Sections 151 and 152 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992; and
The OIC-CGSO and the Acting City Accountant to update their property cards and ledger cards based on the inventory reports to be provided by the City Agriculturist.
observations.reason: No action was undertaken by the Management. Copies of updated inventory reports were not provided to the City Accounting Office and not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to:
• Submit to the Audit Team the supporting documents for the transactions to prove the validity of the claims; and/or
• Revert to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund those transactions that cannot be provided with supporting documentation to support its validity.
• Make the adjustments on the transactions that were already paid but still included in the Accounts Payable account in the amount of P6,432.40;
• Reconcile the schedule of Accounts Payable with the amount reflected in the financial statements and make the necessary adjustment on the deficiency noted amounting to P73,556.75.
Henceforth, we recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to ensure that the recording of Accounts Payable is in accordance with Section 4(s) of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Section 47 of PD No. 1177.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting Office made a submission of the transactions supporting P17,196,891.34 recorded as Accounts Payable as of year-end. For preparation of RTS for recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated with modifications. The City Accountant made a submission of the transactions recorded as Accounts Payable to prove the validity of the claims; Audit Team noted submission of the supporting documents and recordings in JEVs. The City Accountant made the necessary adjustments to the transactions that were already paid as recorded in JEV No. 114 dated February 29, 2024. Revision of the schedule of Accounts Payable was made in CY 2024. Hence, the schedule of Accounts Payable was already reconciled with the reflected amount in the financial statements as of December 31, 2024.
observations.reason: There are still Accounts Payable recorded amounting to P128,858.24 which were not supported with complete documentation. Majority of the recorded accounts payable were supported with documents. There are still Accounts Payable amounting P128,858.24 which were not supported with complete documentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor revisit the possibility of the full implementation of the payment of salaries and wages of JOs through Automated Teller Machine (ATM) as internal control to prevent delayed liquidation or unliquidated cash advances. We also recommended the City Mayor to revisit the possibility of full implementation of payment of salaries and wages of JOs through ATM accounts and to require and enroll the JOs through ATM accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Human Resource Management Officer is monitoring the enrolment of JOs with ATM accounts. The City has reduced the number of days delayed in liquidation of salaries and wages of JOs, but there are still a significant number of JOs paid through ATMs and some not enrolled.
observations.reason: There are still a significant number of JOs that are not paid through ATMs; enrollment and implementation remain incomplete.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City GSO or its representative/staff to:
• Expedite the application of the insurable properties with the GSIS; and
• Prepare and submit the Property Insurance Form (PIF) to the Supervising Auditor/Audit Team Leader, and the General Insurance Fund (GIF), GSIS, not later than April 30 of each year.
We also recommended that the City Mayor instructs the City Engineering Office to issue the Certificate of Occupancy Permit for the 51 buildings as required and insure the same with the GSIS under the GIF for the recovery of the property in case of loss due to fire or other force majeure in compliance with the provisions of RA No. 656.
observations.managementAction: No proof of application of the insurable properties with the GSIS was submitted to the Audit Team. No proof of submission of the PIF to the Supervising Auditor/Audit Team Leader and the GIF, GSIS was submitted to the Audit Team. No proof of application action was submitted.
observations.reason: No document or proof of application of the insurable properties to the GSIS was submitted to the Audit Team. No document or proof of application of the insurable properties to the GSIS was submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all: a. Collections are deposited not later than the next banking day and intact; b. Report of Collections and Deposits are properly certified by its collector, acknowledged by her, and supported with its corresponding Accountable Forms and Validated Deposit Slips; and c. Collectors remit their collections to her not later than the close of each business day.
Also, require the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Audit Team those RCDs/AFs/VDSs which were not presented during the audit subject for verification.
Reiterated in Observation No. 2, Part II of this Report.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer issued an office memorandum requiring the collectors and Barangay Treasurers yearly to enforce their accountability.
The Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Audit Team the supporting documents to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: The RCDs were not supported with VDSs which hindered proper verification of their compliance. Improper documentation and acknowledgement were still observed in CY 2024. Despite such communication remittance after the close of each municipal business day was still observed. The submission of these documents was prioritized. For CY 2024, some RCDs were still not supported with subject VDS.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Make the necessary steps to strengthen the Municipality's internal control to avoid the occurrence of over- or under-remittance of the mandatory deductions and contributions; and b. Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to immediately remit the aforementioned balances to the BIR and GSIS.
Also, ensure that the prescribed managerial policies and relevant laws, rules, regulations regarding proper remittance of these monthly deductions/contributions are fully adhered to.
observations.managementAction: The balances were already remitted to the BIR and GSIS. Efforts to reconcile and remit are being observed. Balances will be checked at yearend.
observations.reason: Over- and under-remittances of these deductions and remittances still occur due to timing of remittance and deduction due to timing differences and due to newly-elected officials and employees. Lapses pertaining to the deductions and remittances were still observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to henceforth require all MCs to remit their collections daily.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer issued an office memorandum to all collectors and Barangay Treasurers yearly to enforce their accountability.
observations.reason: Despite actions taken, some MCs were not able to compliance with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Direct the Municipal Human Resource Management Office to publish the vacancy and expedite the hiring of the mandatory positions; and
b. Fill out the vacant positions, as prescribed in Section 443 (a) (b) of RA No. 7160 and in accordance with the existing guidelines and rules and regulations of the Civil Service Commission (CSC), for more effective administration of the said offices which in turn will benefit the Municipality, as a whole.
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented
As of December 31, 2024, only five Plantilla positions remained vacant, with the recruitment process still ongoing.
Additionally, three mandatory positions—Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, Municipal Treasurer (MT), and Municipal Agriculturist—had not been filled by year-end.
During CY 2025, the acting MT was promoted for the MT position.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2024, only five Plantilla positions remained vacant, with the recruitment process still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Officer in Charge - Municipal Assessor to expedite the titling of the 14 parcels of land in the name of the Municipality to avoid third party claims in the future and possible land disputes.
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented
Two of the 13 lots already have a registered land title. However, the titling of the remaining 11 land has been delayed due to difficulties in expediting the process, primarily caused by the indefinite timelines and lack of prompt action from other concerned agencies in providing the necessary documentary requirements to support the application for transfer of title.
observations.reason: Titling of the remaining 11 land has been delayed due to difficulties in expediting the process, primarily caused by the indefinite timelines and lack of prompt action from other concerned agencies in providing the necessary documentary requirements to support the application for transfer of title.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert more efforts in requiring the liquidation of the funds transfer to the Barangay. Likewise, instruct the Municipal Treasurer to demand for the 30 percent of income from the concerned Barangays, as stipulated in the MOA. In case the said funds were not utilized, demand for the return of the unused funds and institute proper charges in accordance with the MOA.
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented
During CY 2024, Barangay Bacong and Buenavista liquidated fund transfers amounting to P13,743.00 and P19,000.00, respectively. However, the remaining Nine Barangays have yet to liquidate their allocated funds as of year-end.
observations.reason: The remaining Nine Barangays have yet to liquidate their allocated funds as of year-end.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Council to:
a. Update the LDIP for CYs 2018 to 2023 to specify PPAs to be implemented by the Municipality, and include the vital details as to the office/department, schedule of implementation, expected outputs, funding source and amount; and
b. Henceforth, comply with guidelines set in the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020 and DILG- NEDA-DBM and Local DBM-DOF JMC No. 1 dated November 18, 2016, thus the linkage of the local development plan to the budget and the attainment of the proposed 20 percent Development Fund (DF) of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Revisions of the LDIP are still in process. The Comprehensive Land Use Plan (CLUP) has just been recently approved by the Provincial Land Use Committee (PLUC) and duly endorsed to Sangguniang Bayan for local adoption and enactment.
observations.reason: The revision of the development plans is still in process.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Development Council and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to:
a. Immediately submit to the Audit Team the approved CDP and LDIP showing the linkage of the listed PPAs in the AIP/budget, which include the proposed 20 percent DF PPAs for CY 2023; and
b. Henceforth, comply with guidelines set in the DILG-NEDA-DBM-DOF JMC Circular No. 1 dated November 18, 2016.
observations.reason: CDP and LDIP is still not submitted for approval of the Sangguniang Bayan (SB).

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Development Council and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to:
a. Ensure that all development projects to be funded out of the 20 percent DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready; and
b. Henceforth, comply with the guidelines set in the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 in the appropriation and utilization of the 20 percent DF of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Among the six (6) projects, four (4) are completed and the two (2) projects are on going as of June 30, 2023. This will be tasked to the concerned Office of the Municipal Engineer to implement the two (2) projects.
observations.reason: Three development projects for CY 2024 with a total appropriation of P8,000,000.00 were not implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to remind the contractors to submit the documents pertaining to their claims. If they are unable to support their claims with the necessary documents, revert the account to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General fund (Section 98 of PD No. 1445), subject to an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan through a Resolution with a condition that appropriation shall be made in the event that claim arises in the future pursuant to the provisions of Section 98 of PD No. 1445.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Designated General Services Officer (GSO) to: • Coordinate with the concerned department/offices for the data needed for the preparation of RPCPPE for Land Asset accounts for the eventual submission to the Audit Team; • Strictly submit the RPCPPE on or before January 31 of each year; and • Refrain from using the forms prescribed by Government Accounting Manual (GAM) for LGUs until such time that the deferral has been lifted.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Designated General Services Officer to prepare, maintain and submit to the Audit Team the complete and updated Property Plant and Equipment (PPE) Ledger Cards and property cards for Land Asset accounts, the reconciliation of property and accounting records as prescribed by Section 114 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction:
Not Due to the lack of available records/data.

Implemented
The RPCPPE for the year-ended December 31, 2024 was submitted to the Audit Team on January 31, 2025.

Implemented
The submitted RPCPPE were in accordance with the MNGAS for LGUs Volume II.

Not Implemented
The same was not submitted to the Audit Team due to the reasons stated above.
observations.reason: To the lack of available records/data.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor: • Strictly comply with all other prescribed procedures and guidelines under RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented
The municipality submitted a new application for the issuance of ECC. However, certain requirements, specifically, the Land Plan, must be retraced back to the original owner of the land designated for the sanitary landfill. The Land Plan is an essential prerequisite for obtaining CENRO Certification.
observations.reason: Certain requirements, specifically, the Land Plan, must be retraced back to the original owner of the land designated for the sanitary landfill.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to: a. Transfer their depository accounts in the nearest accounts in government-owned bank to their respective area of jurisdiction; and b. Henceforth, comply with the provisions of Section 311 of RA No. 7160 when opening new bank accounts.
observations.managementAction: Awaiting closure of three remaining accounts in Gumaca.
observations.reason: The Municipality still maintains bank accounts at the Gumaca Branch.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Development Council (MDC) and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to: a. Ensure that all development projects to be funded out of the 20 percent DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready; and b. Henceforth, comply with guidelines set in the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 in the appropriation and utilization of the 20 percent DF of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in Observation No. 2, Part II of this Report.
observations.reason: The Municipality is still non-compliant, but measures will be taken to ensure compliance by CY 2025.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the General Services Officer to immediately insure the remaining 94 insurable properties with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS.
observations.reason: Insufficient funds to pay insurance premiums and unavailability of documentary requirements to apply for insurance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile her records with the Municipal Treasurer and to make necessary adjustments in the appropriate record. Henceforth, ensure compliance with Section 111 of PD No. 1445 and Section 10 of the MNGAS for LGU.
observations.managementAction: To maintain or monitor the list of each real property owners from Municipal Treasurer’s collection of RPT, Municipal Receivable in their respective books at the end of year. Continuous reconciliation is being conducted to ensure that all entries are accurate and aligned with the updated records.
observations.reason: Reconciliation between the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant is currently ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to: a. Facilitate the titling of the cemetery in Barangay Sta. Maria and the parcel of land in Barangay Pinagkamaligan; and b. Make the necessary follow-ups regarding the titling of the various lots within the market site.
observations.managementAction: Application for the issuance of 3rd owner's duplicate copy of title of the original owner (vendor) is pending; continuous follow-up has been made. Comments and verification sent/made by the DENR Regional Office to LRA Office is still in process prior to issuance of corresponding certification.
observations.reason: Pending petition for issuance of 3rd owner’s duplicate copy; continuous follow-ups are required and issuance depends on DENR/LRA processes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor consider the One-Time cleansing of PPE account balances as prescribed by Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006 to come up with reliable PPE balances.
observations.managementAction: One-time cleansing of PPE account balances is on-going as at year-end.
observations.reason: PPE and PPELCs maintained by the Accounting Office were still not reconciled, resulting in a variance of P6,271,453.91 as of December 31, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, after considering the undertaking of the One-Time cleansing of PPE account balances, instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the accurate depreciation expense of all depreciable Property, Plant and Equipment owned by the Municipality in accordance with the existing rules and regulations.
observations.reason: The necessary accounting entry to correct the depreciation accounts had not yet been made as of December 31, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the General Services Officer (GSO) to: Expedite the application of the insurable properties with GSIS; Prepare and submit the Property Insurance Form (PIF) to the Supervising Auditor/Audit Team Leader, and the General Insurance Fund (GIF), GSIS, not later than April 30 of each year; Verify the motor vehicles and equipment that were already covered with GSIS insurance and submit its list to the Audit Team for review and verification; Facilitate the proper procedures in transferring the ownership of properties to its end-users and furnish the Municipal Accountant all the necessary documents supporting such transfers. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary accounting entries relating to transfers and recognition of properties upon receipt of pertinent documents, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: Comply with the recommendation. The GSO acknowledges the audit observations and commits to comply with the listed action plan: Application of insurable properties with GSIS; Submission of PIF to Audit Team not later than April 30 of each year; Verification of motor vehicles and equipment that were covered with GSIS and submission of verified list to the Audit Team; Facilitation of proper procedures in transferring the ownership of properties to its end-users; Preparation of necessary accounting entries relating to transfers and recognition of properties upon receipt of pertinent documents.
observations.reason: Some properties are not yet covered by GSIS insurance as of December 31, 2024. The GSO submitted PIF for Multi-purpose building at Barangay Buenavista West, Candelaria only on March 27, 2025. A verified list of motor vehicles and equipment with corresponding GSIS insurance payment as recorded in books of accounts was not submitted to the Audit Team for review. Not all properties that are necessary to be transferred to be end-users were processed/donated during CY 2024. The necessary accounting entries for the transfers and recognition of properties had not yet been recorded in the books of accounts upon receipt of the pertinent documents from the GSO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Chairperson, GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to adopt necessary measures to ensure that: Municipality’s GPB is submitted to the Audit Team within five working days from the receipt of the DILG-approved GPB; Duly signed GAD AR is submitted to the Audit Team within five working days after the end of January of the succeeding year.
observations.managementAction: Promptly comply with the audit recommendation. The Chairperson of the GAD Focal Point System considered the submission of GPB and GAD AR to the Audit Team. The GAD AR for CY 2024 was submitted to the Audit Team last April 7, 2025. The Municipal Mayor instructed the Chairperson of the GAD Focal Point System to comply and submit to the Audit Team the DILG-approved GPB as required.
observations.reason: The GPB for CY 2025 was not yet submitted to the Audit Team as of this date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update Subsidiary Ledgers (SL).
observations.managementAction: Prepare and/or update the necessary Subsidiary Ledgers. The Office of the Municipal Accountant will appreciate the guidance of the Audit Team on the preparation of the necessary Subsidiary Ledgers.
observations.reason: Maintenance and updating of Subsidiary Ledgers are still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor: require the Municipal Accountant to check the validity of non-moving prior years’ balances of Inter-Agency Payables with the amount withheld/deducted against the amounts remitted then remit the valid payables and adjust those without supporting documents for the fair presentation of the account in the financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1; and instruct the Municipal Treasurer for the prompt remittance of the provincial shares from property, special education, professional and transfer tax and barangay shares from RPT and Community Tax Certificate (CTC).
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office will: (a) reconcile the unremitted balance of Due to BIR amounting to P12,366.00; (b) reconcile the unremitted balance of Due to PhilHealth amounting to P996.40; and (c) reconcile the recorded balance of Due to BIR to identify the unremitted amount totaling P223,447.55 which is unsupported. In addition, the Municipal Treasurer was instructed to remit the valid payables and adjust those without supporting documents.
observations.reason: The non-moving agency payable to prior BIR amounting to P2,008,479.95 was remitted on September 7, 2023. The Municipal Treasurer is still working on the reconciliation of inter-agency payable accounts by checking current transactions and making sure that the billing of inter-agency is reconciled with the amount withheld and remitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal General Services Officer to ensure that all the insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with appropriate insurance with the GSIS in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 and RA No. 656 by requiring the Municipal Engineer to expedite the completion of necessary documents required by the GSIS to facilitate the application for property insurance of all the properties of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The General Services Office commits to acquiring appropriate insurance by GSIS for all the insurable property of the Municipality. The GSO will expedite the completion of necessary documents and submission of PIF to Audit Team not later than April 30 of each year.
observations.reason: Some insurable properties of the Municipality are still not yet covered by GSIS insurance as of December 31, 2024.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Appraisal Committee/Team to appraise the donated land as to their fair values at the time of acquisition and apply the same to the purchased land if the acquisition costs cannot be determined reliably; and
b. Municipal Accountant to record the land once the fair values and ownership of the parcels of land are established, for fair presentation of the FS.
observations.reason: The head of the Appraisal Committee tried to secure the Deeds of Conveyance to be used as a basis for valuation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Identify the breakdown and establish the validity of the unreconciled balances of the Due to PhilHealth and Due to Pag-IBIG accounts;
b. Afterwards, make necessary adjustments or remit to the PhilHealth and Home Development Mutual Fund (HDMF) the established balances after reconciliation; and
c. Henceforth, ensure the proper monitoring and recording of contributions withheld and remitted in the said accounts.
observations.reason: Not prioritized by the management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to:

a. Update/revise the LDIP for CYs 2021 to 2026 to specify PPAs to be implemented by the Municipality; and
b. Henceforth, comply with guidelines set in the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020 and DILG-NEDA-DBM-DOF JMC No. 1 dated November 18, 2016, in the appropriation and utilization of the 20 percent DF of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Updated LDIP was not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to expedite the immediate cleansing of property tax records to have a reliable RPT Assessment Roll and RPT and SET Receivables accounts.
observations.reason: Cleansing of property records is still on-going and will take time to complete.

observations.recommendation: We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MGSO to request from the Registry of Deeds (RD) regarding the owner’s copy of title for the six parcels of land and expedite the titling of eight parcels of land owned by the Municipality.
observations.reason: The remaining three parcels were still not titled.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor: • In case of non-settlement of the amount, file the appropriate charges against the concerned persons, otherwise require the Municipal officials who authorized the use of Municipal funds for payment of personal loans of employees to settle/refund the same.
observations.reason: The Municipality did not prioritize the implementation of the recommended action.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
 Inventory Committee to immediately act on the disposal of unserviceable properties to avoid further deterioration and to generate additional income from their disposal; and
 Municipal Accountant to exert effort to identify the unaccounted properties from prior year's records and provide depreciation based on the recorded cost or revalued amount for the fair presentation of the PPE accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Inventory taking Not The Inventory Committee has not acted on the disposal of unserviceable properties. The Municipality is conducting one-time cleansing of PPE account balances and said properties identified for disposal are currently being prepared for bidding. The Municipal Property Inventory, Appraisal and Disposal Committee is on the stage of appraising and evaluating the identified assets that are due for disposal.
observations.reason: The Inventory Committee has yet to act on the disposal of unserviceable properties. The appropriate strategies to strengthen the internal control on PPEs are to be prepared after the one-time cleansing of PPE balances. The PPE records of the Accounting Office and Property Unit have not reconciled their balances.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior recommendation, and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned office/s to complete the supporting documents and submit to the Audit Team the disbursement voucher and supporting documents covering the cash advance for confidential/intelligence fund in the amount of P135,000.00 of the former Municipal Mayor. Henceforth, ensure the submission by the Municipal Accountant of all disbursement vouchers for Confidential/Intelligence Fund cash advances, with complete supporting documents to the concerned COA Audit Team Leader within seven working days from release of the checks. We also reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor exert more effort to locate the liquidation reports and complete the supporting documents pertaining to prior years’ cash advances totaling P265,750.00 and submit to the COA ICFAU, in order that expenses and/or refunds relative thereto are taken up for the fair presentation of the receivables and government equity accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: No action has been undertaken by the Municipality. The Municipality has prioritized other initiatives, and implementation is scheduled for a later time. Some relevant disbursement documents for the Confidential/Intelligence Fund cash advances were already submitted to COA for post-audit. The Municipal Accountant is exerting effort to locate the liquidation reports and supporting documents but to no avail. The Municipality stated that documents were processed and submitted to COA for post audit.
observations.reason: The Municipality has prioritized other initiatives, implementation scheduled for a later time. Documents were not submitted to COA ICFAU within the reglementary period; liquidation reports and supporting documents could not be located.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor demands the MT to: a. Deposit immediately the cash balance of P3,677,635.73 as of December 31, 2023; and b. Strictly comply with Section 69(4) of PD No. 1445 and Section 32, Chapter III, Volume I of MNGAS.
observations.managementAction: Verification of these were part of the cash Examination conducted, which were still not yet fully restituted as of December 31, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MT to ensure that issuances of RAAF represent the actual issuances of the individual Accountable Officers for the month are reported.
observations.reason: Validation was not done due to non-submission of Consolidated RAAF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor requires the GAD Focal Person and all concerned officials to: a. Prepare the GAD AR including the supporting documents required under the DILG DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 and furnish the Audit Team for further evaluation; and b. Submit the GAD Plan and Budget and the GAD AR within the deadline set by COA Circular No. 2014-001.
observations.reason: The CY 2024 GAD AR was submitted but the required documents was not included. GAD AR for CY 2024 was still not submitted within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the General Services Officer (GSO) to dispose the unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445 to avoid further deterioration and earn at least the salvage value from the sale of these assets. Likewise, create an Appraisal and Disposal Committee who will be responsible in appraising the scrap/salvage value of those unserviceable properties.
observations.reason: The newly designated GSO will undergo training prior to carrying out the implementation of the recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor require the:
 Municipal Treasurer to continue to exert extra effort to collect from the identified debtors and/or gather all the required documents required under COA Circular No. 2016-005 and re-file the request for write-off to COA; and
 Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to identify the particular account/s for which the collections of partial payments for Loans Receivable-Others were taken up and prepare the necessary adjusting entries.
observations.managementAction: The Acting Municipal Treasurer will continue to exert extra effort to collect from the identified debtors and/or gather all the required documents required under COA Circular No. 2016-005 and re-file the request for write-off to COA; and the Municipal Treasurer will coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to retrieve and submit the necessary supporting documents and prepare the necessary adjusting entries as evidenced by JEV No. 2022-11-6705 dated November 15, 2022.
observations.reason: The Municipality has not yet gathered the documents in the submission of the request for write-off to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the ICO-GSO to continue exerting efforts to ensure that all the insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with appropriate insurance with the GSIS in compliance with the existing regulations by expediting the gathering of all the supporting documents required by the GSIS for the immediate processing of their application for property insurance of all the insurable properties of the Municipality. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the ICO-GSO to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer to assist the ICO-GSO by providing the necessary supporting documents required by the GSIS to immediately process their application for this insurance of all the insurable properties of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: MGSO has requested and received a quotation from the GSIS for the insurance coverage of the government buildings; MGSO has requested a Supplemental Budget to cover the funding gap; MGSO will immediately proceed with the submission of all required documents to GSIS and will closely coordinate with the Budget Office and Municipal Accountant to ensure the prompt allocation and release of funds for the insurance payment.
observations.reason: Processing of the insurance application for all insurable properties is still pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Require the Municipal Accountant to properly reclassify the Due to BIR totaling P44,073.86 to the Accounts Payable account; and b. Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to refund the two percent excess tax totaling P44,073.86 to the concerned creditors/suppliers pursuant to RR No. 2 s. 2021.
observations.managementAction: The MA reclassified the Due to BIR amounting to P44,073.86 to the Accounts Payable account; and the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to refund the two percent excess tax totaling P44,073.86 to the concerned creditors/suppliers pursuant to RR No. 2 s. 2021. To date, no proof of refund of the said amount to the suppliers/creditors were submitted to the AT.
observations.reason: To date, no proof of refund of the said amount to the suppliers/creditors were submitted to the AT.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor include in the proposed budget the amount required for the purchase of land and utilize the funds intended for the construction of the Gumaca Municipal Nursery.
observations.managementAction: The Management is currently awaiting the approved survey plan in order to finalize the project details. The Management is still in the process of creating the site development plan.
observations.reason: Awaiting the approved survey plan to finalize project details / still in the process of creating the site development plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a) Property Unit and/or Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to:  Update the Property Cards (PCs) by posting all unrecorded acquisitions/receipt, issue/transfer and disposal of PPE, if any;  Work together with the Accounting Unit to reconcile the PCs with the PPELCs (PPE Ledger and Cards (PPELCs) maintained by the Accounting Unit;  Update the PPELCs as necessary in the course of reconciliation;  Ensure that the total balance of PPELCs/SLs tally with the balances of PPE accounts in the FS;  Provide the Audit Team with the complete set of PPE Schedules supporting the PPE account balances presented in the financial statements. Also, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Inventory Committee, Property Unit and Accounting Unit to ensure that the remaining procedural guidelines for the reconciliation, disposition procedures, derecognition and accounting procedures are strictly observed and followed pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006.
observations.managementAction: The office of MGSO is currently updating all unrecorded acquisitions/receipt, issue/transfer and disposal of PPE, if any; Both offices are currently reconciling their respective records. The Municipal Accountant provided the Audit Team of the schedules of PPE accounts. Also, he agreed to reconcile their records with the MGSO, make the necessary adjusting entries and to submit copy of Journal Entry Vouchers for the adjustments made. In addition, he assured to record the transactions under the 20% Development Fund pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009. Moreover, the MGSO and the Municipal Accountant both committed to proceed with the completion of the procedures under the One-Time Cleansing of PPE accounts within the year.
observations.reason: The MGSO did not submit proof that PCs are already posted in its records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:  Trace all available records to provide supporting details to the four (4) recorded CIP transactions totaling P14,225,507.05 and for its proper disposition in the books of accounts;  Justify or provide the Audit Team with the status of the five (5) transactions totaling P23,200,545.52 which are included in the ledgers but excluded in the CIP account balance, for further verification in audit. Moreover, ensure that all recorded transactions are supported with complete details pursuant to Section 111 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant reviewed the existing and subject for verification of the transactions. Also, he prepared JEV No. 2024-09-008466 dated September 27, 2024 adjusting the CIP – Land Improvements account totaling P3,980,278.22 to Prior Period Adjustment account since its supporting documents cannot be traced. The Municipal Accountant reviewed the entries made and identified erroneous entries that caused the non-reconciliation of both accounts. The JEV was submitted by the Municipal Accountant on May 31, 2024, which was verified and found in order by the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Records of both offices remain unreconciled. The CIP account has a remaining balance of P10,245,228.78 which are still subject for verification of the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team proof that goods and services are accepted or rendered, and supplier/creditor bills are received for claims totaling P1,830,648.69, together with the Reiterated and contract/Purchase Order, if any, for further verification; otherwise, prepare an adjusting entry Debiting the Accounts Payable and Crediting the corresponding account/s. Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the recording of Accounts Payable is in accordance with Section 4(s) of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted on May 18, 2024 the remaining subsidiary ledgers supporting the Due to NGAs account with total amount of P102,516.25 as of December 31, 2023 for further evaluation of the Audit Team. The Management committed to comply with the recommendation. The Municipal Accountant made partial compliance with the recommendation on May 29, 2025 and submitted to the Audit Team proof that goods and services are accepted or rendered. Also, he asserted that a corresponding adjusting entry will be made for items without valid supporting documents.
observations.reason: All current payables to GSIS, BIR, Pag-IBIG, and PhilHealth were remitted before January 10, 2025. There are payables totaling P7,829,274.70 included in the schedule, recorded in lump-sum and tagged as “Others” without breakdown nor disclosures provided in the Notes to Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:  Require the execution and submission to the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) of all gross receipts of JOs/COS income payees to the Municipal General Services Officer in accordance with the format per Annex B-2 of Section 3 of RR No. 11-2018 on or before January 15 of each year or before the initial income payment, whichever is applicable;  Instruct the MGSO to comply with the execution of Income Payor/Withholding Agent’s Sworn Declaration (Annex “C”) stating the number of payees who shall not be subjected to withholding taxes and have duly submitted their income payees’ sworn declarations and copies of Certificate of Registration (COR), and submit the same to the concerned BIR office on or before the last day of January of each year or on the fifteenth (15th) day of the following month when a new income recipient submitted the payee’s sworn declaration; and  Require the Municipal Accountant to withhold the prescribed withholding tax from all JOs/COS earning P250,000.00 and below in any 12-month period who was unable to comply with the requirements for tax exemption.
observations.managementAction: The MGSO coordinated with the BIR concerned office to clarify the compliance and execution of the reports. The MGSO complied with the execution and submission of the required forms to the BIR. The MGSO observed the submission of Income Payor/Withholding Agent’s Sworn Declaration and submit the same to the BIR. The Municipal Accountant is to withhold the prescribed withholding tax from all JOs/COS below P250,000.00 who failed to submit the payee’s sworn declaration.
observations.reason: Withholding of prescribed withholding tax from all JOs/COS earning P250,000.00 and below was not made by the Municipal Accountant during CY 2024. The form was not accomplished by the MGSO.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the MGSO to coordinate for the reconciliation of their records and make the necessary adjustments in their respective records, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated and presented anew in Audit Observation No. 3, Page Nos. 44 to 48, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert effort in tracing the records to provide adequate information on the balances of receivables reflected in the schedule and to make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balance of the account for the fair presentation in the financial statements in accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is still tracing the records to verify and to correct the balance. Reiterated and presented anew in Audit Observation No. 2, Page Nos. 41 to 44, Part II of this Report.
observations.reason: All documents to prove the validity of Accounts Payable account amounting to P1,830,648.69 as of December 31, 2023 were not provided to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: Henceforth, ensure that there is proper monitoring of the Payables account so that claims nearing the maturity of two years, are communicated to the respective creditors/suppliers/contractors for the processing of their claims; Effect the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances of these accounts for their fair presentation in the financial statements in accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1; and Exert effort in tracing from the records the information on the age and validity of the claims for Due to Officers and Employees account in the amount of P7,087,649.03. Thereafter, make the necessary adjusting entries in the books of accounts to fairly present the balances of the accounts in the financial statements in accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is still tracing the records to verify and to correct the balance. Reiterated and presented anew in Audit Observation No. 2, Page Nos. 41 to 44, Part II of this Report.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant was still in the process of tracing and checking the records. The Due to Officers and Employees account totaling P1,514,058.14 for CY 2024 still includes lump-sum amounts; adjustments were not yet made and not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Agriculturist to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and source agencies for the implementation of the projects.
observations.managementAction: Out of the P10,885,441.22 initially noted in CY 2020, funds for the Construction of the Food Terminals and Bottom-up Budgeting for Small Water Impounding Project amounting to P875,441.22 and P3,000,000.00, respectively, provided the respective agencies respectively, remain unutilized by the Municipality and outstanding in the book of accounts as of year-end CY 2024. For the Small Water Impounding Project, its fund has been requested for the purchase of vehicle and fund assistance to farmer fund association for their marketing activities. On the other hand, inquiry/verifications were done from the National Irrigation Administration (NIA) and Department of Agriculture (DA) – Regional Field Office No. 4 CALABARZON to obtain copies of the submitted proposal, Memorandum of Agreement (MO), and design, and to request for technical assistance for the possible design revision since the current design is not suitable anymore.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the person in-charge for the titling of the parcels of land to: • request/ask help from other government agencies on how to secure the titles for Items 1 to 3 of Annex J wherein no available documents can be found in the Municipality; • find document/ basis that the Municipality does not own the land recorded in the books of accounts; • support the claim that the Municipality does not own land recorded in the books of accounts; • continue exerting effort in processing the titles; • expedite the processing of the land title for Items 21 to 27 of Annex J.
observations.managementAction: • request help from other government agencies on how to secure the titles of land with no available documents in the Municipality; • find document/ basis that the Municipality does not own the land recorded in the books of accounts; • continue exerting effort in processing the titles.
observations.reason: Not enough documentation recovered to support the claim that the Municipality does not own the land recorded. Continuous effort in 2025. To date, the status of implementation is the same as provided in Audit Observation No. 6, Part III, pages 64 to 66 of CY 2024 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to expedite the gathering of pertinent documents required under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 16, 2016 so that authority from the Sangguniang Bayan through a Resolution authorizing him to file the request for write-off of uncollectible receivables totaling P250,000.00 is secured/obtained and eventually, the write-off of these accounts be undertaken upon approval by the Commission on Audit.
observations.managementAction: Request shall be initiated by the Sangguniang Bayan once the Municipal Resolution authorizing the Mayor to so request for the write-off of the uncollectible receivables is available.
observations.reason: Waiting for the Sangguniang Bayan resolution authorizing the request for the write-off. To date, the status of implementation is the same as provided in Audit Observation No. 7, Part III, page 66 of CY 2024 AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor address the weak internal control over the updating of employee records, and in the recording and remittance of employees and government contributions to the GSIS by providing control mechanisms wherein:
a. Changes in the status of employment such as separated employees or other changes in employee information are immediately reported by the Department Heads to the Human Resource Department (HRD);
b. The Electronic Remittance Advice (ARA) for the said changes in status and salary rate is immediately prepared by the HRD, through the designated agency-authorized officer, to update the records of the GSIS; and
c. Monthly reconciliation of amounts withheld and remitted to GSIS by the Accounting Department to include schedule or breakdown per employee of any unremitted contributions is sent to the HRD for ARA, if necessary.
We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balance of P236,783.15.
observations.reason: The balances for past years cannot be reconciled due to the unavailability of records/documents. Furthermore, the Accounting Department and the HRMO are planning to conduct another reconciliation meeting with GSIS personnel to settle the matter.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Identify the breakdown and establish the validity of the unreconciled balance of the Due to PhilHealth account amounting to P25,333.54; and
b. Make adjustments or remit to PhilHealth the established balances after reconciliation, if necessary.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Department and the HRMO have already coordinated with PhilHealth to verify whether there were unpaid premiums established based on Philippine Health Insurance Corporation records and to request any related documents. However, PhilHealth declined to provide the requested documents.
observations.reason: The balances from previous years cannot be determined due to the unavailability of source documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and HRMO to:
a. Trace and analyze the beginning balance of the Due to Pag-IBIG account, reconcile records with the Pag-IBIG, and identify the reconciling items such as over/under remittances or erroneous entries; and
b. Prepare adjusting journal entries or remit to the respective agencies any contributions due them, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: Tracing the balances using the limited available documents requires significant time and human resources. Therefore, the Municipality decided to inquire directly with Pag-IBIG. Based on the initial inquiry, there were no records according to Pag-IBIG records. However, the Municipality has scheduled another meeting with Pag-IBIG personnel in May 2025 to revalidate the existing records and make final adjustments.
observations.reason: Tracing the balances using the limited available documents requires significant time and human resources. Therefore, the Municipality decided to inquire directly with Pag-IBIG. A meeting with Pag-IBIG personnel is scheduled to revalidate records and make final adjustments.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to:
a. Update/revise the LDIP for CYs 2023 to 2025 to specify PPAs to be implemented by the Municipality; and
b. Henceforth, comply with guidelines set in the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020, and DILG-NEDA-DBM-DOF Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 dated November 18, 2016, in the appropriation and utilization of the 20 percent DF of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Updated LDIP was not submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Updated LDIP was not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Disaster Risk and Management Officer (MDRRMO) to carefully study the feasibility of the projects/activities before its inclusion in the Municipal Disaster Risk and Management Plan (MDRRMP) to ensure its implementation in order for the Municipality to be adequately prepared for any upcoming disaster/calamity to reduce its risk. Henceforth, ensure the implementation of all planned disaster PPAs included in MDRRMP, particularly those included in the 70 percent LDRRMF-MF in accordance with RA No. 10121.
observations.managementAction: Development PPAs were not implemented due to either being provided by other Local Government Unit’s, lack of coordination, or because the PPA output was already delivered through another municipal project.
observations.reason: Various PPAs were not implemented due to either being provided by other Local Government Unit’s, lack of coordination, or because the PPA output was already delivered through another municipal project.

observations.recommendation: We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the preparation of tax declaration and the immediate titling of the lots once ownership by the Municipality is determined to avoid possible third part claims and incurrence of litigation costs in the future.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality is contemplating to sell the parcel located in Barangay Pajarillo as land is no longer needed. An annotation is required on the land title of the ecopark. While a Deed of Donation has not yet been executed for the school site.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO)-Designate and all other members of Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (MSWMB) expedite the:
a. Preparation of the revised 10-Year SWMP and submit the same to the National Solid Waste Management Council (NSWMC) and to the Environment Management Bureau (EMB) to ensure that programs/projects for SWM are consistent with the National Solid Waste Management Framework (NSWMF); and
b. Process the documentary requirements regarding the construction of sanitary landfill.
observations.managementAction: Reformulation of the 10-year SWMP is not yet ongoing. The title of the land, which is a prerequisite in the submission of the application for the construction of a sanitary landfill was not secured.
observations.reason: Some documents for the reformulation were not submitted. The title of the land, which is a prerequisite for submitting the application for the construction of a sanitary landfill, was not secured.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor send a final written notice to the contractor, otherwise, take legal actions to compel the contractor to refund the un-recouped advance payment of P7,384,897.05.
observations.managementAction: The contractor cannot be located, hence refund of the amount is still pending.
observations.reason: The contractor cannot be located anymore despite efforts made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to: ➢ Draw the necessary adjusting journal entries and update accounting records to reflect the results of the one-time cleansing of Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) items in the RPCPPE that are not recorded in the books; and ➢ Prioritize conducting a reconciliation activity with the GSO to determine the cause/s of the variances noted and update their respective records to provide accurate PPE records of the Municipality. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GSO to ensure that the RPCPPE is submitted to the Audit Team on or before January 31 of each year. Further, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the Municipal Accountant and the GSO to adapt collaborative internal strategies that shall facilitate periodic reconciliation of their respective PPE records, such that any variances are detected in a timely manner and accountabilities for PPE are regularly reviewed and updated.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared the adjusting entries as evidenced by Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 102-2024-08-04366 which pertains to Land account. However, Road Networks and Flood Control Systems accounts were not yet adjusted in the books of accounts. The Municipal Accountant prepared the adjusting entries as evidenced by JEV Nos. 100-2024-06-02548 and 200-2024-06-1013 both dated June 30, 2024. All issued semi-Expendable properties were expensed and already closed to the Government Equity account as of December 31, 2024. Conducted the reconciliation of accounting and records. The RPCPPE as of December 31, 2024 was submitted to the Audit Team on March 5, 2025.
observations.reason: There are still properties which were recorded in the books but were not reported in RPCPPE and vice versa. Road Networks and Flood Control Systems accounts were not yet adjusted in the books of accounts. From CY 2023 unreconciled balance of P168,851,477.49, records have still an unreconciled balance of P76,671,289.20 in CY 2024. The late submission of the report was due to delayed reconciliation between the Accounting and GSO records. Quarterly reconciliation of accounts will be prioritized. There are still variances for reconciliation between the Accounting and GSO records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal GSO/ Inventory/ Disposal Committee and Acting Municipal Accountant to conduct the necessary procedures for the disposition and derecognition of the Non-existing/Missing PPEs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality already submitted a request for authority to derecognize non-existing/missing PPEs under GF amounting to P28,998,145.19 and SEF amounting to P22,596,036.40 to the Audit Team as per advice of COA Satellite Office. Copy of request is attached. The request for derecognition on the remaining non-existing/missing PPE under SEF totaling P22,596,036.40 is not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MGSO to continue exerting efforts to cause the immediate titling of the 16 parcels of land in the name of the Municipality to avoid possible third-party claims that may arise in the future.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was able to process the titling of one lot. Request for authority and processing of titling of remaining untitled lots are ongoing. There are still difficulty in collating supporting documents for lots acquired more than 10 years ago.
observations.reason: Eight of the nine lots remained untitled. Difficulty in collating supporting documents for lots acquired more than 10 years ago.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: • Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office, the Municipal Agriculturist, the Municipal Tourism Officer, the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Office to conduct visitorial and monitoring of the projects and continue exerting effort to collect the receivables from the concerned beneficiaries; • Municipal Treasurer to prepare schedule of beneficiaries of livelihood projects and payment thereof per program/project basis and account the nature of livelihood collections totaling P341,361.91; and • adopt efficient collection strategies to enforce collection from the concerned beneficiaries to ensure recovery of the amount released to the beneficiaries. • Municipal Accountant to continue to reconcile records with Municipal Treasurer’s Office and conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of receivables and determine concerned debtors to ensure collection when due and demandable in compliance with COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: The concerned officers conducted visitorial and monitoring of the projects and exerted effort to collect the receivables from the beneficiaries. The Municipal Treasurer is still collecting the balances due. The Municipal Treasurer provided payments are being made through Official Receipts. The Municipal Accountant continues reconciliation and verification of records. The Municipal Treasurer is still checking to whom payments will be accounted for and verification is ongoing.
observations.reason: Difficulty in collecting receivables because the beneficiaries refuse to pay. The remaining unaccounted balance of P175,137.91 is still unaccounted. Payments are still being verified; no proof of periodic verification was furnished to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and the Municipal Accountant to exert effort to fast track and exhaust all posisble means to submit the information/data of these projects and for the immediate recovery of the long outstanding advances to contractors.
We also recommended to ensure the recoupment/payment/s to contractors and strict compliance with the above-cited regulations.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant has exhausted related efforts to obtain the details of the said advances to contractors so that the balance reported in the financial statements may be presented with complete details.
Otherwise, make the necessary adjusting entry.
observations.reason: The concerned offices have already taken the initial steps to retrieve related documents. However, they are still unable to identify the breakdown of such amount.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to:
a.Designate a Gender and Development (GAD) Focal Person and ensure functionality of the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS);
b.Ensure the submission of GAD Plan and Budget to the DILG and secure a DILG reviewed and endorsed GPB and submit the same to the Audit Team within five working days from receipt thereof; and
c.Furnish a copy of the breakdown of the expenditures to further verify the GAD Accomplishment Report.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has already designated a Gender and Development (GAD) Focal Person, Ms. Mylene Casidsid. The Municipality was able to submit the GPB for 2024 to the DILG for review. However, since the submission was late, no certification was issued, and the DILG provided several observations for possible revision.
observations.reason: For the 2024 budget, the Municipality was able to submit the GPB to the DILG for review. However, since the submission was late, no certification was issued, and the DILG provided several observations indicating the need for possible revisions to the GAD Plan and Budget.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MPDO and Municipal Engineer to:
a.Ensure that all on-going projects and those that are to be implemented during the year are included in the list submitted at the beginning of each year.
b.Submit the monthly monitoring report and ensure that it is as current and updated as possible.
c.Inform the Auditor within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices were already posted for validation; and
d.Henceforth, strictly comply with items 2.1, 3.1 and 3.2 of COA Circular No. 2013-004.
observations.managementAction: The MPDO and Municipal Engineer will ensure that all on-going and to-be-implemented projects are included in the list submitted at the beginning of each year. They will prepare monthly monitoring reports that are updated. The Municipality will try to ensure the Auditor’s recommendations will be implemented in the succeeding years.
observations.reason: The MPDO and Municipal Engineer were not able to submit the list of all projects to be implemented for the current year. Monthly monitoring reports were not submitted to the Auditor. The LGU was not able to inform the Auditor regarding posting of signboards and/or notices.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality comply with the documentary requirements in filing for the write-off of receivables with the Commission on Audit in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016. Exhaust all possible means to obtain the information/documents and to submit the necessary supporting documents to the COA to support the claim that these accounts are already unrecoverable.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant will aim to submit the necessary supporting documents to support the request for write off of these loans to NGOs. However, with the issuance of COA Circular 2023-008, there are additional documents which needs to be submitted to the COA to support the write off of the accounts. The Municipal Accountant will exhaust all possible means to obtain such requirements.
In addition, out of these amount, 70,000 were already approved for write-off by the COA.
observations.reason: Out of the total amount, only P70,000 has been approved for write-off by COA. Additional documents required under COA Circular 2023-008 have not yet been submitted to the Audit Team/COA.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer to continue their effort in facilitating the immediate tittling of subject lots in the name of the Municipality to avoid contingent claims that may arise in the future in compliance with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer continue their efforts in facilitating the immediate titling of the remaining lands in the 2024 and 2025 AIP. The titling of one parcel of land was conducted during this year and the payment of the necessary duties and taxes has been made. The Municipal Assessor has yet to secure other documents related to the titling of lands.
observations.reason: As of end 2023, the Municipal Assessor secured the title for the land located in the New Public Market and Integrated Terminal. However, he has not yet secured the other three remaining land titles.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the GSO to ensure that all insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with appropriate insurance with the GSIS in compliance with the provision of COA Circular No. 92-30 and RA No. 656.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-GSO has already secured the insurance for some of the properties of the LGU. However, there are still some properties which were not yet insured due to lack of documents. The OIC-GSO will continue the process of completing the required documents for the application of insurance with the GSIS.
observations.reason: Due to lack of documents, the OIC-GSO was not able to secure the insurance for all of the LGU properties. Certain properties have yet to be insured.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to require the: a. Municipal Accountant to make an adjusting entry and recognize an obligation for the stale checks by debiting Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current Account and crediting Accounts Payable in the GF books; and b. Municipal Treasurer to send a written notice to the payee/s of the existence of the stale check/s for them to present the checks for replacement.
observations.managementAction: a.Municipal Accountant to make an adjusting entry and was made thru JEV No. 100-2024-04-00529 dated April 15, 2024. b.Municipal Treasurer to send a written notice to the payee/s of the existence of the stale check/s for them to present the checks for replacement.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer was unable to send written notices to the payees of the stale checks since they have no record/s of the addressees of the payees.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer-Designate to institute measures to properly and completely dispose of all fully-depreciated and unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445 and Sections 379 to 381 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) was prepared and submitted, however actual disposal was not prioritized by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the immediate titling of subject lots in the name of the Municipality and continuously coordinate with other government agencies to avoid contingent claims that may arise in the future in compliance with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was not able to facilitate the transfer of title for the remaining untitled lots due to issues with identifying the rightful owner of the land. Various individuals are claiming to be heirs, but no documents have been presented to support their claims.
observations.reason: Various individuals are claiming to be heirs, but no documents have been presented to support their claims.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Acting Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor to coordinate with the Bids and Awards Committee to expedite the procurement of surveying activity and completion of the documentary requirements for the titling of the four lots in the name of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: On-going process of titling of the lot purchased in CY 2024.
observations.reason: There is a difference in the lot numbers stated on the Certificate of Registration from the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) and Sangguniang Bayan (SB) Resolution, thus the titling for Proposed Solid Waste Site did not materialize. Relatively, the Municipality is considering other alternative ways to secure the title in favor of the Municipality for the Right of Way Eco. Park with an area of 2819 sqm.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor:
• instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to continue pursuing the collection of the receivables from the beneficiaries of the livelihood project who are able to pay by sending demand letters; and
• to re-file the request for write-off on the uncollectible balances in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: Continuous collection of receivables. For CY 2024, the Municipality was able to collect from seven beneficiaries totaling P20,820.00 covered by Official Receipt Nos. 5815547 to 5815550 and 5822657 to 5822659.
observations.reason: A balance of P154,393.10 as of December 31, 2024 remains uncollected by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer to assist the Acting Municipal Treasurer in providing the necessary supporting documents required by the GSIS to immediately process their application for property insurance of all the insurable properties of the Municipality. Henceforth, ensure that all insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with appropriate insurance with the GSIS in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 and RA No. 656.
observations.managementAction: Various Insurable Properties were insured with the General Insurance Fund (GIF), as administered by the GSIS, to insure against any insurable risk of the significant/most used properties, assets, and interests of the Municipality.
observations.reason: School buildings that were intended for donation but have not yet been donated remain uninsured under the Government Insurance Fund (GIF) administered by the GSIS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor consider undergoing the One-Time cleansing of PPE account balances as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2020-006 to come up with reliable PPE balances.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented Management is still on the process of the preliminary activities related to the one-time cleansing of PPEs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to:
• Coordinate with the source agencies to establish the availability of the cash pertaining to these unutilized trust funds and after which, require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit to the accounts of the unexpended/unutilized balances of the trust funds or to the National Treasury for unidentified source agencies.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented The Municipal Accountant is retrieving available records for the trust funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to achieve the Municipality’s sanitary landfill full operation.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented However, the MENRO has already coordinated with the Sangguniang Bayan for the creation of the said ordinances. As regards the other comments and recommendation s of the Environmental Management Bureau (EMB), the MENRO is still in the process of complying.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor consider undergoing the One-Time Cleansing of PPE account balances as prescribed by Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006 to come up with reliable PPE balances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has yet to commence the conduct of One-time cleansing of PPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: • Direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to comply with the procedures in Manual on New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for LGUs pertaining to reporting of disbursements/checks issued; and • Develop and implement internal control measures to enable all undelivered checks in the custody of Municipal Treasurer’s Office (MTO) to be communicated to the Office of the Municipal Accountant.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Henceforth, ensure that undelivered checks as of year-end are reported as part of the Cash in bank account in compliance with the existing rules and regulations.
