observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the following adjusting entry in the GF books of accounts: Accounts Payable 524,188.45 Due to Officers and Employees 43,549.63 Leave Benefits Payable 29,998.23 Other Payables 357,859.31 Due to Other Funds 92,781.28 Prior Period Adjustment 118,579.50 Accounts Payable 118,579.50
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor file a request for write-off of the uncollectible receivables with the Audit Team following the guidelines and procedures under COA Circular 2023-008.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Request for write-off of the uncollectible receivables was not yet filed to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: • Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the return of any unused balances for completed projects to the respective source agencies or the National Treasury in compliance with the above laws and regulations; and • Municipal Accountant to validate the projects which have negative balances and prepare adjusting entries, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Unused balances from completed projects amounting to P175,182.15 at year-end were not yet returned nor transferred to the source agencies and reconciliation for their negative balances was still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately to the Audit Team the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) recording the various claims to the Accounts Payable account, including its supporting documents such as Delivery Receipt, Inspection and Acceptance Report, Purchase Orders, etc.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant has not yet submitted the JEVs recording the various claims to the Accounts Payable account, including its supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: • Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) - Designate and the Municipal Accountant to pursue the reconciliation of the accounting with property records in order to establish the correct PPE balances and adjust any discrepancy that will be noted.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has yet to commence the conduct of One Time Cleansing of PPE.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the relieved Municipal Treasurer (Mr. Leovino V. Ayuma) to deposit intact with authorized government banks (AGDBs) all undeposited collections of P3,094,631.96 as prescribed by Section 69(4) of PD No. 1445 and Section 32 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer was relieved. The Municipal Mayor filed a case to the Office of the Ombudsman dated August 30, 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant: • to identify the details of unaccounted Advances to Contractors totaling P208,902.20; and • Demand for the immediate refund of the unrecouped advances from the contractor of the completed projects.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant was not able to identify the details of the unaccounted Advances to Contractors due to passage of time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: • instruct the Municipal Engineer (ME) and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to submit the list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities ("PPA") and those that are to be implemented during the year and its monthly reporting within the prescribed period; and • inform the Audit Team of posting of appropriate project signboards and/or public notices 10 days upon award of infrastructure projects and start of the program/activity.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Monthly monitoring reports were not submitted to the Audit Team, instead submission was made quarterly. Posting notification was only made quarterly. The required list was not submitted within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to send written notices or demands to the Accountable Officers to immediately settle the unliquidated cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: In CY 2024, out of P300,260.11, P228,838.45 remain unliquidated and P190,107.45 was outstanding. Former Municipal Treasurer failed to liquidate long outstanding advances.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer to verify the details of the unremitted amounts or the accumulated/ unreconciled discrepancies in the Due to BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth accounts, and reconciliation for their fair presentation in the financial statements in accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1, and remit whatever balances due to the agencies concerned to avoid incurrence of penalties and surcharges.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Verification and reconciliation of the details of the unremitted balances for prior years was not yet completed as of report date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor request the: a. Sangguniang Bayan (SB) to authorize him to file the request for write-off of dormant receivables; and b. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer (MT) to support the request for authority for write-off of dormant receivables following the guidelines and procedures as set forth in COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer is still waiting for the submission of proof of insolvency, death certificate and other documents to validate the existence of conditions affecting the collectability of the receivables from the borrowers.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II Observation No. 1 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MT to strictly adhere with the regulations and deposit all municipal collections promptly and intact in order to protect municipal funds against risk of loss and possible misuse.
observations.reason: The daily collections cannot be deposited daily due to the geographical area of the Municipality and the Municipal Treasurer deposits the collection intact on a weekly basis.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor should require the Municipal Assessor to continue with the exerting efforts in securing all of the adjacent requirements/documents needed for the titling of the said lots in the name of the Municipality to avoid third party claims in the future and possible land disputes.
observations.managementAction: Senior Citizen Building – Coordination has already been made with the heirs of the lot owner and the owner of the adjacent property to facilitate the processing of the land title, as the property is still covered under a mother title. Cemetery – The correction of the land’s technical description has already been filed with the Department of Environment and National Resources (DENR); however, the LGU has yet to receive a response. Public Market – The DENR was unable to evaluate the property as they do not have a copy of the survey plan per submitted certification from LGU.
observations.reason: Titling process is still on-going, as the property is still covered under a mother title.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipality, through the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (MSWMB), work for the completion of their 10-year SWMP and secure the approval of the plan from the NSWMC.
observations.managementAction: The 10-year SWMP was reviewed by the NSWMC and revisions were recommended. The work for the revised 10-year SWMP will be submitted to NSWMC for review on April 2025.
observations.reason: The NSWMC requires the municipality to have a sanitary landfill before approving the 10-year SWMP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Head of the Inventory Committee to ensure that the inventory-taking activities for the one-time cleansing of PPE is accordance with the approved Physical Inventory Plan (PIP), and the Accounting Unit to conduct the preliminary activities prior to the inventory taking activities in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006. We further recommended the completion of the one-time cleansing of PPE to establish a reliable balance as to existence, condition and accountability.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Complete conduct of PPE inventory count was not made due to non-prioritization of inventory of infrastructure projects and work/zoo animals. Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 1, Page Nos. 41 to 42, Part II of this Report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: a. Identify the breakdown and establish the validity of the unreconciled balances of the Due to PhilHealth and Due to Pag-IBIG accounts; b. Afterwards, make the necessary adjustments or remit to the PhilHealth and Home Development Mutual Fund (HDMF) the established balances after reconciliation; and c. Henceforth, ensure the proper monitoring and recording of contributions withheld and remitted in the said accounts.
observations.reason: Not prioritized by the management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned personnel to: a. Complete the information in the preparation of APP to help ensure timely execution of projects; and b. Henceforth, comply with the guidelines set in GPPB Circular No. 07-2015 dated July 16, 2015 in the preparation of the APP.
observations.reason: Not prioritized by the management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor conduct the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts by requiring the Inventory Committee to complete the physical inventory in three months or less and require them to prepare and submit the approved Physical Inventory Plan to the AT at least 10 days before the scheduled start of inventory taking activities in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Conduct of PPE inventory count was not made due to non-prioritization of inventory of infrastructure projects and work/zoo animals. Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 1, Page Nos. 41 to 42, Part II of this Report

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to continue in exerting extra efforts to complete the titling process of the aforementioned parcels of land.
observations.reason: Three out of the four parcels of land owned by the Municipality have already been titled, while the titling process for the remaining parcel is still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Development Council (MDC) and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to: a. Prepare the LDIP of the Municipality as to the PPAs to be implemented by the Municipality, and include the vital details as to the implementing office/department, schedule of implementation, expected outputs, funding source and amount; and b. Henceforth, comply with guidelines set in the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020 and DILG-NEDA-DBM-DOF JMC No. 1 dated November 18, 2016 in the appropriation and utilization of the 20 percent DF of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Provincial Land Use Committee (PLUC) reviewed the Comprehensive Land Use Program (CLUP) and suggested minor revisions. The LGU targeted to accomplish the revised CLUP at the end of 2025.
observations.reason: The Comprehensive Development Plan (CDP) and LDIP for 2025 to 2030 are currently being finalized and are expected to be completed by May 2025.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Officer in Charge (OIC)-Municipal Assessor to: a. Accelerate the titling of the two remaining lots whose titling process already commenced in CY 2018; and b. Immediately start the process of the titling for the six lots where legal documents were found, continue exerting more efforts in locating the documents of the five lots and make all the necessary actions/representations with the DENR and RD in the titling of the lots.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor is in the process of retrieving the required documents to transfer the land title in favor of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) Designate to work on securing all the requirements needed for the issuance of the ECC and establishment of the proposed Sanitary Landfill pursuant to RA No. 9003. We also recommended that the Municipality, through the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, follow-up the status of the review of the revised plan and secure the approval of its 10-year SWMP from the NSWMC for compliance with the provisions under the above cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality is in the process of securing the ECC; hence, the sanitary landfill has not been established yet. The TYSWMP has been reviewed by the EMB and compliance with the findings is on-going.
observations.reason: The 10-year SWMP of the Municipality is on-going and is yet to be submitted to the NSWMC for approval.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous years’ recommendations that the Municipal Mayor:
• Require the MGSO and the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with each other and complete the reconciliation of the affected PPE accounts to establish the correct PPE balances, and to investigate and make necessary adjustments in their respective records, as necessary; and
• Instruct the MGSO to prepare an updated Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and submit a copy to the Audit Team; and
• Enjoin the Asset Disposal Committee to cause the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties, in accordance with existing rules and regulations, to avoid further deterioration of the assets and generate additional income for the Municipality.
Additionally, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• Review and revise, if necessary, the composition of the Inventory Committees, and facilitate the conduct of One-time cleansing of PPE accounts, in accordance with Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006; and
• Adopt internal strategies that shall cause the periodic reconciliation between accounting and MGSO records to ensure the accuracy of the PPE balances, in accordance with Section 114 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has not provided any reconciliation documents between the MGSO and the Municipal Accountant. The MGSO and the Municipal Accountant were still not able to reconcile their respective Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) records. The MGSO committed to comply with the audit recommendations. The MGSO and the Municipal Accountant reconciled their records and submitted the RPCI as of December 31, 2024, on January 31, 2025. The MGSO committed to comply with the audit recommendations. The MGSO has prepared an updated Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and submission to the Audit Team once finalized and submit a copy to the Audit Team. The Municipality has not provided the necessary documents used for the disposal of the unserviceable properties, in accordance with existing rules and regulations, to avoid further deterioration of the assets and generate additional income for the Municipality. The Municipality has not provided the schedule used for the preparation of the IIRUP as to Audit Team.
observations.reason: For continuation of one-time cleansing preparation of updated IIRUP and submission to Audit Team. For continuation of preparation of updated IIRUP as to Audit date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MGSO to complete the filing of the application for insurance coverage of the public market and slaughterhouse and ensure that all insurable properties owned by the Municipality are covered with appropriate property insurance with the GSIS in strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 and RA No. 656.
observations.managementAction: The insurance for the Public Market, Slaughterhouse and Municipal Government Center was paid under Official Receipt (OR) Nos. 5650016685, 569003221 and 5650018066 respectively. In addition, 11 motor vehicles were insured in 2024. In remaining insurable properties, identification is ongoing and due to budget limitations, filing for application of insurance for the remaining insurable properties of the Municipality was not performed.
observations.reason: The Municipality is in the process of identifying the properties for proper turnover to the respective end-users/beneficiaries. Also, due to budget limitations, filing for application of insurance for the remaining insurable properties of the Municipality was not performed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• Require the Municipal Accountant to allocate time for the preparation of the necessary documentation to support the request for authority to write-off dormant receivables totaling P1,280,833.21 for its filing with the COA through the Audit Team following the guidelines and procedures provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and
• Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to intensify efforts to collect receivables totaling P218,760.00 from the beneficiaries of the Livelihood Programs by constant follow-up and by sending collection notice to make the accounts current.
observations.managementAction: The Sangguniang Bayan had already issued Resolution to request for write-off dormant receivables totaling P1,280,833.21 for filing with the COA. Demand letters were sent by the Municipal Treasurer to request for collection for the P218,760.00; livelihood programs were turned over to the Municipal Treasury but not properly collected. The Municipal Treasurer sent demand letters to the Punong Barangay of Barangays Bagong Silang and Tanauan. The Treasurer disclosed that most beneficiaries are no longer capable to settle their accountabilities due to financial difficulty.
observations.reason: For preparation of supporting documents for request for write-off. Livelihood programs turned over to Municipal Treasury not properly collected; beneficiaries not capable to settle; request for write-off has not yet been filed with COA.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor to continue exerting extra effort in retrieving the documents required by the Register of Deeds to facilitate the immediate titling and registration of the lots in the name of the Municipality to avoid contingent claims that may arise in the future and in compliance with Section 39(2) of PD No.1445 and Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386, and for the fair presentation of the Land account in the financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: Certified True copy of Deed of Sale/Deed of conveyances was obtained from the Clerk of Court of Lucena City and Regional Trial Court of Mauban. The titles were transferred immediately to the Municipality. The Municipality is still in the process of transferring the TCTs in the name of the Municipality. Various documents required for the titling are being obtained from the Assessor’s Office to the Mayor’s Office thru the Treasurer’s Office dated September 8, 2025.
observations.reason: The parcels of land remained unsupported with TCT in favor of the Municipality. The Municipal Assessor is still in the process of transferring the TCTs under the name of the Municipality. Various documents from the Assessor’s Office to the Mayor’s Office were made; however, titling is now assigned to the Municipal Administrator as per SB Resolution No. 2025-123 dated August 27, 2025 and Office Order No. 23, Series of 2025 dated September 15, 2025. Turnover of pertinent documents from the Assessor's Office to the Mayor's Office thru the Treasurer's Office dated September 8, 2025 (see attached supporting documents).

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) a year-end adjusting entries to revert the unreleased checks totaling P1,979,224.39 to the CIB account and recognize the corresponding liability/payable account;
b. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to comply with the procedures in MNGAS for LGUs pertaining to reporting disbursements/checks issued; and
c. Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that recording of check disbursements will only be done after receipt of RCI with Disbursement Voucher (DV) and supporting documents from the Municipal Treasurer, to avoid inclusion of unreleased checks as reconciling items in the BRS.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant prepared the adjusting entries as supported by Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 100-2024-03000355 dated March 31, 2024.
The Municipal Treasurer reported his disbursement/check issued; and
that they would follow the recommendation for compliance.
observations.reason: For CY 2024, there are four unreleased checks included as Outstanding Checks and as reconciling items in the BRS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor consider undergoing the One-Time Cleansing of PPE account balances as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2020-006 to come up with reliable PPE balances.
observations.reason: For CY 2024, PPE item in the amount of P91,174.64 was still included both in the GL and RPCPPE. Moreover, the Municipality has yet to commence the conduct of one-time cleansing of PPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to remit the balance of P1,000,336.46 to the concerned agency pursuant to COA Circular No. 94-013 so that it can be used to finance other projects or by other agencies needing assistance.
observations.reason: The unexpended/unutilized balance of Trust Funds received from NGAs were still not remitted to their source agencies as at year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to identify the recorded land in the Special Education Fund (SEF) for the fair presentation of the Land account in the financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.reason: Recorded Land under SEF amounting to P61,000.00 was still unidentified.

observations.recommendation: Also, we reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor strictly enforce the immediate settlement of the disallowance pursuant to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006.
observations.reason: Disallowance with COE totaling P879,100.63 was still not settled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor continue to require the Municipal Treasurer and Assistant Municipal Treasurer to strictly monitor the intact deposit of collections daily or on the next banking day in compliance with Sections 32 of MNGAS for LGUs Volume I, to avoid possible misuse of funds.
observations.reason: Deposits of collections were still delayed, ranging from one (1) to 33 days.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Designated MENRO to continually work for the approval of the 10-year SWM Plan (CY 2020-2029) by the Sangguniang Bayan and eventually by the National Solid Waste Management Commission in the compliance with the requirements of RA No. 9003.
observations.reason: The Municipal is still waiting for EMB approval of 10-year SWM Plan.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to request for the authority for the write-off of the dormant and unaccounted balances in the Due to NGAs account with the Commission on Audit. Henceforth, ensure compliance with Sections 111 and 112 of PD No. 1445 and Item 8.2 of COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: Submittal letter to the Commission on Audit for the authority for the write-off of the dormant and unaccounted balances in the Due to NGAs account was prepared. The necessary submissions to COA for approval will be made in CY 2025.
observations.reason: Authority to write off the dormant accounts has been requested from the Sangguniang Bayan; submissions to COA for approval will be made in CY 2025. There are still dormant accounts that remain non-compliant.

observations.recommendation: a. General Services Officer (GSO), through the Appraisal and Disposal Committee, to conduct immediate disposal of all unserviceable properties following the procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 92-386. b. Request assistance from Commission on Audit for proper disposal guidance, if necessary. Henceforth, ensure compliance with PD No. 1445, Manual on Disposal of Government Properties and COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: GSO Designate submitted a request letter to the Commission on Audit to request for assistance regarding proper disposal of unserviceable properties. Request for assistance and procedures drafted; awaiting authority/approval.
observations.reason: Disposal pending issuance of Executive Order for reorganization of the Disposal Committee; Commission on Audit assistance requested; no final disposal made yet.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Accountant to continue their efforts in retrieving the unsubmitted DVs in CY 2017 and oblige the former Municipal Accountant to comply with its submission to the Audit Team for verification and post audit. Consider appropriate legal action, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: Oblige the former Municipal Accountant to comply with its submission to the Audit Team for verification and post audit. The Local Government Unit will also consider appropriate legal action regarding the matter.
observations.reason: Awaiting course of action of the former Municipal Mayor; retrieval and submission of DVs still pending.

observations.recommendation: Continue efforts in establishing the SLF; comply with COA Circular documentary requirements for purchase of lot for the establishment of the Sanitary Landfill.
observations.managementAction: Prepared the documentary requirements for purchase of lot for the establishment of Sanitary Landfill. MENRO still preparing WACS; proposed site acquisition ongoing.
observations.reason: No SLF yet; compliance with NSWMC approval pending; acquisition of proposed site and compliance with geological assessment recommendations still incomplete.

observations.recommendation: GSO through Appraisal and Disposal Committee to conduct immediate disposal following COA Circular No. 92-386. Request COA assistance if necessary. Henceforth ensure compliance.
observations.managementAction: GSO Designate submitted a letter request to COA to request for assistance regarding proper disposal of unserviceable properties. Awaiting COA guidance and approval; Disposal Committee reorganization pending Executive Order.
observations.reason: Disposal pending COA guidance and issuance of Executive Order for Disposal Committee; no final disposal undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to: • Expedite the application of the insurable properties with the General Service Insurance System; and • Prepare and submit the Property Insurance Form to the Supervising Auditor/Audit Team Leader, and the GIF of the GSIS, not later than April 30 of each year. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-MGSO, the Municipal Engineer, and the Municipal Budget Officer to have proper communication regarding the documents needed and budgetary requirements, to facilitate the filing of property insurance of insurable properties as required by the GSIS.
observations.managementAction:
Only the Old Municipal Building, its components, the Multi-purpose (Comelec) Building, and motor vehicles were insured with the GSIS.

The Municipality was able to comply with the application of the insurable properties with the General Service Insurance System for some properties. 

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-MGSO, the Municipal Engineer, and the Municipal Budget Officer to have proper communication regarding the documents needed and budgetary requirements, to facilitate the filing of property insurance of insurable properties as required by the GSIS.
observations.reason: The Municipality was still not able to prepare and submit the complete PIF for all its insurable properties and other Assets. Submission of PIF to the Audit Team was incomplete.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: • Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) to ensure that total appropriation for GAD Fund is at least five percent (5%) of their total Annual Budget; and • GAD Focal Person (GADFP) to inform the DILG Regional Office in writing whenever changes/revisions are made in their DILG-approved GPB.
observations.managementAction: GADFP complied with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: For CY 2024, the MBO was not able to comply with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: • Municipal Accountant to exert effort to identify the unaccounted records for the prior years’ records for the fair presentation of the PPE accounts in the financial statements; and • Municipal Accountant to reconcile records to identify the unaccounted properties from prior years’ records for the disposition and derecognition process as part of the One-Time Cleansing.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant to thoroughly review and reconcile records to identify the unaccounted properties, and to coordinate with the Property Custodian concerned offices to identify and document unaccounted properties, and update the PPE accounts accordingly to ensure fair presentation in the financial statements.
observations.reason: The Municipality has yet to conduct the disposition and derecognition process as part of the One-Time Cleansing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: • Municipal Assessor to facilitate the titling of the subject lots to establish ownership over them and avoid contingent claims that may arise in the future in compliance with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386; and • Municipal Accountant to determine the details of the unidentified land by gathering the relevant supporting documents from the Municipal Treasurer and Assessor’s Office for the fair presentation of the land account in the financial statements pursuant to Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Assessor to facilitate the titling to be expedite the process for the titling to be secure ownership and prevent future claims. Additionally, the Mayor will direct the Municipal Accountant to collect and review relevant documents from the Municipal Treasurer and Assessor’s Office to establish ownership and prevent future claims. This will help in maintaining clear and reliable financial records.
observations.reason: The process of titling was still on going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMO to carefully study the feasibility of the PPAs in accordance with the four thematic areas of disaster risk reduction and management before its inclusion in the LDRRMP to ensure its implementation and utilization of the LDRRMF-MF for the Municipality to reduce disaster risk and be adequately prepared for any imminent disaster/calamity.
observations.managementAction: LDRRMO provided a comprehensive update on the appropriation and utilization of the LDRRMF-MF for the Municipality. They also conducted planning for the PPAs to be included in the LDRRMP.
observations.reason: In CY 2024, 63.23 percent of the total appropriation for the LDRRMF-MF was not utilized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: ➢ Municipal Accountant to: • Maintain subsidiary ledgers for transfers of the Municipality’s unutilized DRRMF to the STF by year of transfer; and • Disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements the available balance of STF as well as its utilization and residual balance per year of transfer as exhibited in Annex D of COA Circular No. 2012-002; and ➢ Municipal Accountant and the LDRRMO to reconcile their records on which year the unutilized balance of DRRMF in the amount of P19,479,176.42 pertains.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: The subsidiary ledgers of DRRMF transfers to the STF were not properly maintained due to incorrect balances recognized as Accounts Payable of December 31, 2023. The Municipality Accountant had started maintaining subsidiary ledgers for DRRMF transfers to the STF and will disclose the balances in the Notes to the Financial Statements; however, full implementation to determine the year to which the unutilized balance pertains is still on-going and records between the Municipal Accountant and the LDRRMO were not yet reconciled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended, and Management agreed to continuously review and update the schedule of all receivables and payables to minimize the further accumulation of dormant balances in the books.

We recommended, and Management agreed to require:
The CAD and HCDRD to coordinate and reconcile their respective records to establish the correct value of properties for the City’s SHP, ensuring proper classification based on intended use. Thereafter, conduct regular monthly reconciliation of accounts and records to prevent future discrepancies and misclassifications;
The CAD to prepare and record the necessary adjusting entries to correctly recognize SHP properties based on intended use by reclassifying them to Finance Lease Receivables, Inventories, or Investment Property, and derecognize from Property, Plant, and Equipment and related Accumulated Depreciation accounts; and
The HCDRD to provide the CAD with complete and updated data/information on all housing projects to ensure accurate and timely recording in the City’s financial records.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the CAD and CGSD to:
Require the concerned departments to submit the RIS, SSMI, distribution lists, and other similar documents for the proper and timely recording of inventory expenses in the books;
Perform periodic reconciliation of property and accounting records to ensure that inventories are properly accounted for; and
Henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of Paragraph 27 of 
IPSAS 1, Paragraph 44 of IPSAS 12 and Section 112, Chapter 7 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I on the proper accounting for acquisition and issuance/distribution of inventories.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the CAD, in coordination with the concerned implementing offices, to:
Devise and implement the necessary recovery procedures to immediately recoup the amount of P173,062,337.99 from the concerned contractors for completed and terminated projects, preventing potential loss of government funds;
Closely monitor the recoupment of these advances to ensure that balances related to completed or terminated projects are refunded to the City; and
Moving forward, establish a sound monitoring system to ensure immediate recoupment of advance payments from initial progress billings, in line with Section 71.1.5 of the IRR of RA No. 12009.

We recommended, and Management agreed to require the CAD to:
Prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the balances of Medical Equipment and its corresponding accumulated depreciation, along with other affected accounts, to ensure the fair presentation of financial statements; and
Strictly adhere to the provisions of IPSAS 1, IPSAS 17, and COA Circular Nos. 2015-009, 2022-004 and 2024-006 for proper recording of transactions in the books.

We recommended, and Management agreed to require:
The CAD to promptly prepare the necessary adjusting entries for the derecognition of disposed properties and coordinate with the CGSD to address and resolve the documentation deficiencies;
The CGSD to ensure timely submission of all pertinent documents to CAD upon disposal of properties for proper derecognition in the books; and
Strictly adhere to Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1, Paragraph 82 of IPSAS 17, and Annexes 50 and 51 of the MNGAS for LGUs.

We recommended, and Management agreed and took action on the following:
The DBO to effectively utilize the fund from the 15% share on Building Permit Fees to further enhance its operations and deliver quality services, in accordance with Section 3.0 of the DPWH-DILG JMC 
No. 01 s. 2013;
The CTO and CAD to correct the overstatement in the collection and remittance of the 5% share of the DPWH from Building Permit Fees;
The CAD prepare an adjusting entry to reclassify the misposted 
15% share of the DBO from Building Permit Fees to the appropriate SL; and
Henceforth, strictly adhere to Sections 2 and 3 of the DPWH-DILG JMC No. 01.

We recommended, and Management agreed that, moving forward, direct the City’s LSB to:
Follow the indicative timeline for the preparation, review and approval of the SEF Budget through an LSB Resolution; and
Strictly comply with the provisions of DepEd-DILG-DBM JC No. 1, 
s. 2017.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Require the Schools Division Office to –
Develop and issue updated guidelines on the proper use of internet connectivity in public schools, and implement measures to ensure strict compliance by end-users;
Instruct the public-school IT personnel to monitor the internet speed regularly and immediately report any instance where connectivity falls below the minimum subscribed speed to the respective ISPs; and
Henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of DepEd Order No. 105, s. 2009, and Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.

We reiterated our recommendation that the CAD immediately enforce the settlement of all audit suspensions, disallowances, and charges in compliance with Sections 5.4 and 7.1.1, Chapter II of COA Circular 
No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009, and Management be more vigilant in the implementation of the existing accounting and auditing rules and regulations in the processing of claims to avoid possible audit suspensions and disallowances.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant continue these efforts in complying the timely preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements for fair presentation of Cash in Bank account of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to re-issue demand letters to all concerned accountable officers for them to liquidate their cash advances by submitting complete and valid settlement documents or require them to refund the cash advances.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the concerned barangays to refund and/or liquidate immediately the funds received by sending demand letters requiring the submission of the Fund Utilization/Liquidation Report to ascertain that the funds granted were utilized in accordance with its intended purpose.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
require the Municipal Accountant to maintain the Report of the Physical Count of Biological Assets (RPCBA) as provided under the Manual and disclose the details of the of biological assets in the Notes to FS; and
require the Municipal Agriculture Officer to provide the inventory of breeding stocks and other biological assets with the prevailing market price for each item as of reporting date.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to disclose the required supporting information on the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with IPSAS No. 1 for the ensuing year.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities to Surplus and disclose the same on the face of the Cash Flow Statement or in the Notes to Financial Statements in compliance with Section 29 of IPSAS No. 2.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal General Services Officer (GSO):
Furnish the Office of the Auditor with a copy of contracts and Purchase Orders (PO), irrespective of amount and each of every supporting document, within five working days from the issuance thereof, in compliance with Items 3.1.1 and 3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001; and
Notify the Office of Auditor of the time and date of every scheduled delivery of procured items within 24 hours from its acceptance, in compliance with Item A (2) of COA Circular No. 96-010.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to (a) strictly comply with the regulation on the rendition of accounts and financial reports in order not to delay in the verification and post-audit activities; and (b) observe the due dates of submission of the required financial reports and documents to the Audit Team by setting timelines of submission from Treasury Office to Accounting Office. Likewise, avoid processing and payment of transactions without complete supporting documents, otherwise this will be the major reason of delays which will affect both the Treasury Office and the Accounting Office.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit intact all her collections as well as all collections turned over to her by the collectors/tellers with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day. She shall record all deposits made in the cashbook and prepare the RCD as required under Section 69 of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445, Section 8 of COA Circular No. 92-382, Local Treasury Operations Manual (LTOM) 2nd Edition and Cash Examination Manual. Likewise, proper handling of collections and its deposits be strictly observed and deposits should be attributed/traced from the collected amounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Local School Board to strictly adhere to the laws, rules and regulations on the formulation, preparation and approval of an SEF Budget to provide funds for the annual budgetary needs for the operation and maintenance of public schools within the Municipality and submit the same for evaluation and examination.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management allocate budget on the conduct of Community Based Monitoring System and consider the DBM DOF DILG Joint Memorandum Circular on the use of 20% Development Fund, for the effective and efficient delivery of necessary services to the constituents.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer rectify the various cracks noted on the above-mentioned projects to avoid further damage or deterioration of the pavements and communicate to the Audit Team upon completion for evaluation purposes. It is further recommended that the stone masonry be repaired immediately to prevent potential damage to the structure.
Additionally, Management submit the accomplishment report on the expiration date (August 22, 2020) of the contract “Construction of Five (5) Rural Health Units” for the computation of the corresponding amount of liquidated damages. Submit also a written justification on the cause of delayed implementation of the project for evaluation of the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the LDRRMO and all his Department Heads in charge to observe the guidelines of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 in formulating PPAs to be funded out of the LDRRMF; and strictly utilize the funds according to the guidelines to achieve the maximum benefits for the Municipality’s constituents.

We recommended that the:
Local Chief Executive ensure the optimum utilization of the 20% DF by promptly implementing the priority development projects as reflected in its duly approved local development plans and establish the effective and efficient implementation of the projects to maximize the developmental benefits for the intended beneficiaries;
MPDC initiate close coordination with the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant for the proper monitoring of project implementation and the provision of all the necessary information in the Status Report of Government PPAs before submission to the Audit Team; and
Municipal Engineer require timely implementation of PPAs in coordination with the MPDC under their responsibility at all times.

We recommended that management expedite the implementation of all projects/programs funded out of the tobacco excise taxes in order to give the constituents early benefits that could be derived therefrom.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer to send demand letters to delinquent taxpayers and conduct an information campaign to all real property owners within the Municipality with emphasis of granting discounts when paid before due dates and the possibility of losing their property for not paying at all.

We recommended that the Municipality create new ordinances to increase its resources and the Municipal Treasurer to devise a plan to facilitate the collection of taxes to increase the revenue for a more efficient and effective implementation of its development plans, program objectives and priorities.

We recommended that:

the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary journal entries to adjust all reconciling items/discrepancies noted herein;

the Municipal Accountant together with the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Budget Officer must reconcile their records and the above-noted variances in the 20% DF and 5% Calamity Fund and LGU-Quezon NV Housing Project appearing in the Financial Statements, and thereafter, undertake the same regularly; and

cash accounts must be monitored by key heads of offices consistently and should be presented fairly in accordance with IPSAS 1 and other accounting rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the noted difference of ₱2,111,237.24 existing on the two receivable accounts and furnish results of reconciliation or adjusting entries made to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended again that management thru the Municipal Accountant verify and validate the existence of these receivables, determine the debtors, and assess their collectability. If deemed uncollectible, request authority to write-off the dormant receivable accounts following COA Circular No. 2023-008; and ensure that subsidiary ledgers are maintained for receivables in accordance with Section 4(e) of the NGAS to provide detailed information on accounts and facilitate collection efforts in the future.

We recommended that Management thru the Municipal Accountant immediately reconcile the discrepancy of ₱1,247,792.60 in the Due to Other Funds and Due from Other Funds accounts and adjust the books accordingly to ensure the accuracy and integrity of the financial statements.

We recommended that Management:
revert the dormant balances of Accounts Payable that have been outstanding for two years or more and lack valid claims or supporting documents to the unappropriated surplus;
Conduct a thorough review of all Accounts Payable account to ensure they meet the criteria for recognition as per the provisions of IPSAS No. 1, namely: a) existence of a present obligation from past events, b) probability of outflow of resources embodying economic benefits, and c) reliable measurement of the obligation's cost/value; and
communicate with potential creditors, especially for accounts that have been outstanding for an extended period. This will help in validating the existence of claims and facilitating their settlement or reversion as appropriate.

We commended management for the on-track implementation of the PPAs in conformity with COA Circular 94-013 dated December 13, 1994. We recommended that management ensure compliance with regulations and prioritize and accelerate the implementation of its ongoing projects funded by the NGAs to ensure that the funds are utilized efficiently and effectively and ensure timely delivery of benefits to the community.

We recommended that management cause the immediate return of all unused balances to source agencies or request authority for the continued use of funds for other identified projects of the Municipality.

We commended management for its high implementation rate of PPAs under the 20% Development Fund, ensuring the optimum utilization thereof, and timely delivery of intended benefits for the constituents of the municipality. Henceforth, management is recommended to continuously ensure that the development projects that may be included under the 20% DF shall be those that are necessary, appropriate, or incidental to efficient and effective local governance, and those that are essential to the promotion of the general welfare of the people.

We recommended that Management:
continuously ensure the allocation and utilization of LDRRMF pursuant to the policies and guidelines primarily embodied in NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1;
ensure efficient and effective implementation of all programmed projects identified during the budget year to optimize the utilization of the fund and to assure that the goals and objectives of reducing disaster risk and enhancing disaster preparedness and response capabilities be achieved; and
continuously comply with pertinent laws, rules and regulations in the recording, utilization and reporting of LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
prepare the necessary adjusting entry to the affected accounts to ensure the fair presentation of financial statements, i.e. – debit Trust Liability – DRRMF amounting to ₱854,453.42; credit – Prior Period Adjustment amounting to ₱854,453.42; and
coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer and the MDRRM Officer to determine the appropriate amount to be transferred from the unexpended balances of Calamity Fund to the STF to be recorded as Trust Liabilities – DRRMF.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
prepare necessary adjusting entry to the affected accounts to ensure fair presentation of financial statements; i.e. – debit Prior Period Adjustment amounting to ₱1,299,241.53; credit – Trust Liability – DRRMF amounting to ₱1,299,241.53; and
coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer and the MDRRM Officer to determine the appropriate amount to be transferred from the unexpended balances of Calamity Fund to the STF to be recorded as Trust Liabilities – DRRMF.

We recommended that the Local School Board sustain its efforts in ensuring that all budgeted projects, programs and activities are optimally and fully implemented. This involves close monitoring on the implementation process, addressing any delays or obstacles promptly, and ensuring that allocated funds are utilized effectively to achieve desired outcomes.

We recommended that the Local School Board, through its Secretariat, continue the practice of furnishing all relevant officials, including the Local Budget Officer, Local Accountant, and Local Treasurer, a copy of the SEF Budget within the stipulated seven-day period in compliance with Item 6.1, DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately submit the Quarterly and Annual Reports on SEF Utilization for the year 2024 to the concerned offices and to strictly follow the submission requirements and deadlines in the future.

We commended the Management for its adherence with the posting of SEF utilization report for CY2024. We recommend that the Management maintain the practice of posting the SEF Utilization Reports in conspicuous locations complying with the pertinent deadline consistent with the Full Disclosure Policy of the DILG and ensure that the reports are up-to-date and regularly updated to guarantee that the public is informed of the most recent financial data related to SEF utilization.

We recommended that:

the Local Chief Executive, being the Head of the Procuring Entity, require the BAC/BAC Secretariat to submit the lacking documents immediately. Otherwise, we will be constrained to issue a Notice of Suspension pursuant to COA rules and regulations;

to ensure that all lacking documents are submitted, the BAC Secretariat should accomplish the attached checklist of documentary requirements and put index tabs/tabbings on the documents to be submitted; and

the Bids and Awards Committee, to ensure that all supporting contract documents are complete in compliance with pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR, and submitted within five (5) days from execution of contracts in accordance with Section 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that Management continuously enforce the settlement of the remaining disallowances and for the Municipal Accountant to properly monitor and reconcile the balance of receivables on disallowances/charges recognized in the financial statements with that contained in the RADC and make the necessary adjustments in consonance with the provisions set forth under COA Circular No. 2009-006.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the
Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their
records at least quarterly to immediately detect any error/discrepancy for
timely adjustment/correction and accordingly, to establish an accurate
and reliable records and reports on Cash in Treasury and Cash in Bank
balances.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the
Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with Section 69 of PD No. 1445,
Section 32 of the New Government Accounting System for LGUs, Vol. I,
and Section 3.2 of Local Treasury Circular No. 3-2022, regarding the
timely deposit of collections within the prescribed period.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the (a)
Municipal Accountant to locate supporting records or documents
pertaining to the investment property and provide impairment loss
thereof; and (b) Inventory Committee to conduct physical count to
validate the existence and correctness of reported Investment Property
account.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the (a)
Inventory Committee to (i) regularly conduct complete physical count of
all PPE of the Municipality; and (ii) submit the related RPCPPE to the
Audit Team on or before January 31 of each year in accordance with the
NGAS Manual; (b) Property Officer to maintain property cards, to
effectively oversee and manage the agency's properties; and (c)
Inventory Committee in prioritizing the one-time cleansing of PPE
account balances as per COA Circular No. 2020-006 to address historical
reliability issues in financial statements and allocate dedicated time and
resources, including relieving the inventory committee of regular duties,
to conduct thorough inventory verification and reconciliation activities
on PPE accounts.

We also recommended the creation of Disposal Committee to facilitate
the immediate disposal of unserviceable transportation equipment
costing ₱2,600,000.00 in accordance with the prescribed guidelines and
procedures.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the (a)
Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of the recorded Biological
Assets to validate existence and reconcile the results with the records of
the Accounting Unit; and (a) Municipal Accountant to (i) locate the
records pertaining to the procured Biological Assets; (ii) maintain
subsidiary ledgers thereof; and (iii) provide adequate disclosure of the
account in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and the Local Chief
Executive agreed to direct the
(a) Municipal Accountant to exert best efforts to trace/locate the
pertinent documents or records on the balances of 18 old accounts
of the Due to NGAs and effect the necessary adjustments/
corrections, as warranted, to reflect the correct balances of Due to
NGAs and other affected accounts in the books of the
Municipality;
(b) Responsible Officer/s of the implementing offices in the
Municipality to coordinate with the Source Agencies (SAs) of the
funds received, for action to be taken and/or cause the immediate
return of the unutilized balances, if warranted.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to comply with
Section 3.2.1 of the DBM Budget Circular No. 2002-01 dated January 14,
2002 and the General Provisions of the General Appropriations Act
(GAA) in the appropriation and prioritization of payment of terminal
leave benefits of the separated officials and employees.

We recommended and Management agreed to continue to comply with
DBM, DOF and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 1, dated
November 4, 2020 on the full implementation of PPAs to ensure the
achievement of desired socio-economic development and environmental
outcomes.

We also recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct:
(a) the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), BAC Secretariat, and
Technical Working Group to: (i) complete and submit to the Audit Team,
the documentary requirements on deficiencies noted in the procurement
of projects through public bidding, totaling ₱24,432,701.79; and (ii)
enhance procurement transparency by complying with the publicity and
notification requirements on projects implemented; and (b) the Municipal
Accountant to (i) strengthen the review of requisite documentation for
procurement and disbursements; and (ii) submit the DVs with supporting
documents for the two implemented projects totaling ₱8,297,073.20.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the
LDRRMO to (a) strengthen the monitoring of LDRRMF utilization
focusing on PPAs that require the highest priority in terms of urgency;
and (b) ensure efficient and effective implementation of all programmed
projects to optimize the fund utilization and achieve the goals and
objectives of reducing risk and enhancing disaster preparedness and
response capabilities.

We recommended and Management agreed to (a) continue its efforts to
substantially comply with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2013-01 on the appropriation, utilization, and reporting of
GAD; and (b) ensure the timely submission to the Audit Team of the
GAD Plan and Budget and Accomplishment Report in compliance with
COA Circular No. 2014-001.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the
LSB to (a) furnish the concerned officials with a copy of the SEF Budget

(DepEd) Central Office, the local sanggunian concerned and Regional

Form No. 1; and (c) post the utilization reports of the SEF Budget for all

conspicuous public places within the indicated timelines, to fully comply
with the requirements of Item 6.1 of the DEPED-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s.
2017.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the (a)
Municipal Accountant to submit the paid DVs and supporting documents,

period; and (b) Municipal Budget Officer to ensure the regular

Obligations to ensure timely financial reporting and compliance with
regulations.

We recommended and Management agreed to continue its good practice
of substantial compliance with the BIR rules and regulations on the
proper withholding and timely remittance of taxes.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Accountant to
