properly and timely submitted to the concerned personnel and pertinent data are
properly recorded in the books of accounts, in accordance with relevant laws, rules,
and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
1. Municipal Treasurer to immediately liquidate all outstanding CAs granted to her;
2. Municipal Accountant to properly record the liquidation of CAs under JEV Nos.
100-2024-10-02187 and 100-2024-10-02191 totaling P220,000.00, the CA for the
sports activity amounting to P64,000.00, and refunds amounting to P4,645.00;
3. Accountable officers to ensure that all cash advances are fully liquidated at
year-end; and
4. Municipal Accountant to ensure that the CAs are properly classified.
Also, ensure that the relevant laws, rules, and regulations pertaining to proper
recording, granting, and liquidation of CAs are adhered to.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
ensure that compensations are properly classified.

Also, ensure that compensation is properly recorded and supported with
documents in accordance with applicable laws, rules, and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:
1. Reclassify the completed projects to the Buildings account; and
2. Record the corresponding depreciation of the completed projects.

Also, ensure that all assets of the Municipality are properly classified in accordance
with the aforementioned criteria.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
1. Designated GSO or the Municipal Accountant to immediately present to the
Audit Team the RIS or acknowledgement receipt of the recipient of the sample
expenses;
2. Designated GSO to ensure that the RIS is prepared and consolidated weekly;
3. Designated GSO to ensure that the SSMI, together with the original copy of the
RIS, is submitted to the Municipal Accountant;
4. Municipal Accountant to compute the cost of supplies issued and ending
inventory using the moving average method; and
5. Municipal Accountant to prepare the journal entry voucher to record the
expenditures using appropriate expenditure accounts based on the submitted
SSMI.

Also, ensure that all relevant laws, rules, and regulations pertaining the issuance
or consumption of supplies and materials, as well as its proper recording, are
adhered to.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
ensure that expenses are properly classified.

Also, ensure that expenses and expenditures are properly recorded in accordance
with applicable laws, rules, and regulations.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to provide the Audit Team with the schedule of the AP account together
with its complete details or breakdown.

Also, ensure that the recorded payables are supported with valid and sufficient
evidence, details, breakdown regarding the claim/obligation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
1. General Services Officer (GSO) and the Municipal Accountant to fully implement
the conduct of one-time cleansing in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-
006, especially the reconciliation of their records with the RPCPPE, to ascertain
the accuracy of the PPE ending balances;
2. Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries relative to the
aforementioned issues;
3. GSO and the Municipal Accountant to include sufficient identification details,
such as, but not limited to, serial number, plate number, chassis number, and
engine number, in their respective records;
4. GSO to include the Concreting of Sitio Santiago Road in Barangay Pansoy in
the RPCPPE;
5. GSO to ensure that all equipment of the Municipality are tagged with property
sticker with legible and accurate details written on it; and
6. GSO to ensure that the RPCPPE is submitted to the Audit Team on or before
January 31 of each year.

Also, ensure that all relevant laws, rules, and regulations pertaining to proper
recording and asset management are adhered to.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
1. Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit to the Municipal Accountant the DVs
and its supporting documents of the aforementioned
check disbursements to facilitate its recording;
2. Municipal Accountant to immediately record the aforementioned
check disbursements once submitted by the Municipal Treasurer;
3. Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the DVs and its supporting
documents are submitted with the Report of Checks Issued as basis of the Municipal
Accountant’s recording in the books of accounts; and
4. Municipal Accountant to ensure that all check disbursements are recorded in
the books of accounts.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor request from the Sangguniang
Bayan an authority to close the bank account pertaining to the KALAHI-CIDSS
program to prevent the incurrence of penalties.

Lastly, ensure that all disbursements made by the Municipality are properly
and timely recorded in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor continue requiring the Municipal
Accountant to review the entries made which affect the erroneously recorded
transactions and make the necessary adjusting entries in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant:
• To prepare the necessary adjusting entries to record all valid reconciling
items and submit to the Audit Team the Journal Entry Vouchers for
validation;
• To present properly the reconciling items in the bank reconciliation
statement;
• To provide the details of all checks presented as Outstanding Checks to
verify if valid reconciling items; and
• To prepare the bank reconciliation statement for all DBP account/s.
Henceforth, ensure that the Municipal Accountant draws journal vouchers to record
all valid reconciling items that require adjustment and corrections in the General
Ledgers.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
• Municipal Accountant and Municipal Agriculturist to continue to locate the
Statement of Receipts and Disbursements (SORDs) copies and submit them to
the Regional Accountant of the Department of Agriculture (DA) for liquidation
and reconciliation of the deficiency amounting to P6,944,478.18; and
• Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the return of any unused balances for
completed projects to the respective source agencies or the National Treasury
in compliance with the above laws and regulations;
Henceforth, ensure that the balances between the source agencies and the
implementing agencies are the same, by strictly complying with the prescribed
period as stated in Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 94-013 dated December
13, 1994.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• Instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
▪ Make necessary adjusting entries to recognize the Semi-Expendable
Machinery and Equipment Expenses, and Semi-Expendable Furniture,
Fixtures and Books Expenses;
▪ Ensure that all acquired and issued tangible items which meet the definition
and recognition criteria of PPE but with cost below P50,000.00, were
accounted as SEPs in the books of accounts of the Municipality in
accordance with Item 3.3 and 4.0 of COA Circular 2024-006;
▪ Prepare, maintain and submit to the Audit Team the Semi-Expendable
Property Ledger Card (SPLC) for validation;
• Instruct the General Service Officer (GSO) to prepare, maintain and submit to
the Audit Team for validation, the Semi-Expendable Property Card (SPC),
Registry of Semi-Expendable Property Issued (RegSPI), Report of Semi-
Expendable Property Issued (RSPI) and Report on the Physical Count of Semi-
Expendable Property (RPCSP) to strengthen control over SEPs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare
the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balance of the affected accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor requires the:

a. Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entry in the books of the Municipality to reflect the collections in the appropriate accounting period; and

b. Municipal Treasurer and the RCOs to remit daily collections at the close of each business day.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all recorded transactions in the Due to NGAs account meets the definition criteria as prescribed by the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. Municipal Engineer to conduct thorough assessment and prepare a valuation report on the extent of damage and the remaining service potential of the affected projects;

b. Municipal Accountant to recognize and record then appropriate impairment loss in the books; and

c. Municipal Engineer to establish regular monitoring and immediate reporting procedures for the projects affected by calamities to ensure accurate financial reporting

Henceforth, comply with Paragraph 26 and 52 of IPSAS 21 and Paragraph 43 of IPSAS 17, ensure the accurate valuation and proper presentation of assets in the financial statements.

We reiterated to recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned employees to:

a. Immediately liquidate the outstanding CAs; and

b. Submit a written explanation/justification for the delay in the liquidation of the outstanding CAs.

Henceforth, ensure compliance with Item 5 of COA Circular No. 97- 002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to perform the procedures for the proper disposition of the dormant Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable account of P2,174,510.18, respectively, as set in COA Circular No. 2023-008.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Trace and analyze the beginning balances of the Due to GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth accounts, reconcile records with the GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth, and identify reconciling items such as over/under remittances or erroneous entries; and

b. Prepare adjusting journal entries or remit to the respective agencies any contributions due to them, if warranted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Make an adjusting entry to recognize the unissued SEPs in the Semi-Expendable
Property inventory accounts of the Municipality; and

b. Record the issued SEPs in the appropriate Semi-Expendable Property Expense
accounts and prepare corresponding ICS and other related documents for the
monitoring of SEPs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MA to:

a. Reconcile the unidentified variance with the SA and submit liquidation report or
make adjustments in the books of accounts, if necessary.

b. Prepare a SORD to show the reversion of unutilized fund for the purchase of
Farm Equipment and Implements and furnish the AT and SA’s accounting a
copy.

c. Prepare a SORD for the liquidation of the portion of Social Pension’s Fund and
furnish the AT and SA’s accounting a copy.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Treasurer to review and ensure the correctness of the individual RAAF prepared by
the COs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer (MT)
to prepare and maintain the SPC, ICS, RegSPI, RSPI, and RPCSP, and Municipal
Accountant (MA) to prepare and maintain the SPLC as prescribed in Section 4 of
COA Circular 2022-004 dated May 31, 2022.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor Instruct the MGSO/Inventory
Committee to:

a. Conduct necessary procedures for the disposition and derecognition of the
Non-Existing/Missing PPEs following the guidelines and procedures under
Circular No. 2020-006; and
b. Ensure that all PPE items counted were tagged with new property stickers
containing the information provided under Paragraph 5.7 of the Circular.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• Instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
▪ Make necessary adjusting entries to recognize the Semi-Expendable
Machinery and Equipment Expenses, and Semi-Expendable Furniture,
Fixtures and Books Expenses;
▪ Ensure that all acquired and issued tangible items which meet the definition
and recognition criteria of PPE but with cost below P50,000.00, were
accounted as SEPs in the books of accounts of the Municipality in
accordance with Item 3.3 and 4.0 of COA Circular 2024-006;
▪ Prepare, maintain and submit to the Audit Team the Semi-Expendable
Property Ledger Card (SPLC) for validation;
• Instruct the General Service Officer to prepare, maintain and submit to the Audit
Team for validation, the Semi-Expendable Property Card (SPC), Registry of
Semi-Expendable Property Issued (RegSPI), Report of Semi-Expendable
Property Issued (RSPI) and Report on the Physical Count of Semi-Expendable
Property (RPCSP) to strengthen control over SEPs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balance of the affected
accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
prepare the necessary adjusting entry in the books of accounts to effect the
recording of the said collections in the appropriate accounting period.

We also recommended the Municipal Mayor to compel the RCOs to strictly comply
with the provisions of Section 29 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
make the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the issued SEPs to the
appropriate SEPs expense accounts for its fair presentation in the year-end
financial statements.

Henceforth, ensure compliance with the provisions of COA Circular 2024-006
particularly on the use of appropriate SEPs accounts in recording SEP
transactions of the Municipality.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
ensure that all CAs are fully liquidated at the end of each year in accordance with
the JMC for Confidential Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to require all officers and employees
to immediately liquidate/return/refund their respective CAs as soon as the
purpose for which the advances were granted has been served as soon as the CA
is no longer needed or has not been used.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
➢ Municipal Accountant to:
• Make the necessary accounting entry to reclassify the various Inventories
Held for Distribution in the amount of P6,851,899.78 that were already issued
to end-users; and
• Prepare the journal entry voucher to record subsequent issuance of supplies
and materials as well as semi-expendable properties, upon receipt of the
Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued and the Report of Semi-
Expendable Property Issued, respectively, from the Acting General Services
Officer. Additionally, reconcile the year-end recorded inventory with the
Report on the Physical Count of Inventories provided by the Acting General
Services Officer.
➢ Acting General Services Officer to:
• Issue a Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) for supplies and materials, and an
Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) for semi-expendable properties to the
respective end-users; and
• Prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI), the Report of
Semi-Expendable Property Issued (RSPI) and the Report on the Physical
Count of Inventories (RPCI) and furnish copies to the Municipal Accountant.
Additionally, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor implement strategies to
strengthen controls over Inventories that will enable the conduct of physical count
of Inventory items, in accordance with Section 124 of the MNGAS, Volume I, and
improved coordination between the Offices of the GSO and the Municipal
Accountant.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
• Prepare the appropriate accounting entries to correct the erroneous
classification and recording of PPEs and accumulated depreciation accounts;
and
• Ensure that PPE accounts and the corresponding depreciation are properly
classified and recorded accordingly.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
draw the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balances of Accounts Payable,
Due to Officers and Employees and Other Payables accounts, if still unpaid.
Henceforth, ensure that liabilities are recorded in the proper accounts, guided by
COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
make the necessary adjusting entries in the TL-DRRMF account in the TF books of
accounts.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to
transfer the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF for CO to the General Fund
amounting to P778,679.00.

Henceforth, comply with the provisions of Section 322 of RA No. 7160 and Item
5.1.11 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 by ensuring that all unexpended/unobligated
balance of LDRRMF for CO shall be made continuing in the General Fund books
until the projects funded therefore are completed and any savings shall be made
available for use in the disaster risk reduction and management activities, subject
to the local budgeting process.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and
the Municipal Accountant to develop and implement appropriate internal control
measures to enable all unreleased or undelivered checks in the custody of
Municipal Treasurer’s Office at year-end are properly communicated to the Office
of the Municipal Accountant. Henceforth, ensure that all valid reconciling items as
of year-end are properly recorded and reported as part of the Cash in Bank account,
in accordance with Item 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
• Submit to the Audit Team proof that goods and services are accepted or
rendered and supplier/creditor bills are received for the remaining claims listed
in the Accounts Payable; and
• Revert to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund those transactions that
cannot be provided with proper documentation to support its validity; and
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to ensure that recording of Accounts Payable is in accordance with
Section 4(s) of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Section 46 of PD No. 1177.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Accountant (MA) to derecognize the recorded liability for Terminal
Leave Benefits in the books of accounts of the Municipality in the amount of
P498,960.90; and
b. Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) to ensure that charges in the
appropriation/allotment are valid.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
record the issued SEPs in the appropriate Semi-Expendable Property Expense
accounts in compliance with Section 4 of COA Circular No. 2024-006.

We also recommended to instruct the Supply and/or Property Custodian to replace
the issued ICS with PAR to record the issuance of the two PPE units in compliance
with Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 2024-006.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
file a request for write-off or derecognition of dormant accounts with/through the
Audit Team Leader and/or Supervising Auditor, depending on the jurisdictional
amount in pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the designated Property/Supply
Officer in coordination with the Accounting Unit to prepare the appropriate
annex/es to support the acknowledgement and issuance of donated properties of
the Municipality in compliance with the Section V Letter B of COA Circular No. 2014-
002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to prepare and maintain the SPC, RegSPI, RSPI, and
RPCSP; and
b. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the SPLC as prescribed in
Section 4 of COA Circular 2022-004 dated May 31, 2022.

We recommended that the:

a. Management (i) direct the Municipal Treasurer and other accountable officers
(AOs) concerned to exert all reasonable efforts to retrieve the source documents
supporting various book reconciling items and submit the same to the
Municipal Accountant; (ii) pursue previous requests with the depository bank
to obtain copies of debit and credit memoranda related to the said reconciling
items for proper recording in the books of accounts; and (iii) initiate the closure
of dormant and unused bank accounts; and

b. Municipal Treasurer (i) perform reconciliation of all Cash in Bank cashbooks
with the corresponding accounting records; (ii) submit a detailed
reconciliation report to the Audit Team; and (iii) ensure strict compliance with the
monthly reconciliation of cashbook balances with subsidiary accounting
records, in accordance with Section 63(F) of the LTOM.

We recommended that the:

a. Municipal Accountant (i) continue sending regular written notices to all AOs
concerned at least quarterly to demand the settlement of their outstanding cash
advances; (ii) ensure that no new cash advance is granted unless previous ones
have been fully liquidated; and (iii) investigate the reasons for negative cash
advance balances and make appropriate adjustments in the books to rectify
discrepancies; and

b. Management (i) initiate the preparation and consolidation of necessary
documents in support of the request for write-off of deceased employees and
those dormant unliquidated cash advances; and (ii) consider the filing of
appropriate legal or administrative action against AOs who have deliberately
failed to liquidate their advances after proper demand, if warranted.

We recommended that Management:

a. Continue in issuing written notices to all AOs concerned, requiring the prompt
settlement of their outstanding cash accountabilities. In cases where no action is
taken within a reasonable period, Management should evaluate the necessity of
initiating appropriate legal measures, in accordance with existing laws and
regulations; and

b. Direct the former Municipal Treasurer to: i) reconcile all existing cashbooks
with the accounting records to establish the accurate amount of her
outstanding accountabilities; and ii) thereafter submit her final accountability
report to the Audit Team pursuant to Section 80 of PD No. 1445.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant make diligent efforts to
reconstruct the SLs pertaining to the former Municipal Treasurer’s cash
accountability, utilizing all available and reliable records. The completed SLs should
then be submitted to the Audit Team for proper verification and audit, in accordance
with Sections 111(1) and 114(2) of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that Management:

a. Instruct the members of the Inventory Committee to promptly conduct physical
count of inventories and prepare the corresponding RPCI in compliance with
existing rules;

b. Facilitate the reconciliation of the results of the physical count with the
accounting records to enable the recording of necessary adjustments and ensure
the accurate valuation of inventories in the books of accounts; and

c. Institute measures to ensure the timely conduct of annual physical inventory
and submission of the corresponding report.

We recommended that the relevant offices (i) revisit the provisions of the
aforementioned Circulars; and (ii) ensure compliance with preparation and
maintenance of the prescribed forms, registry, and reports related to Semi-
Expendable Property.

We recommended that Management:

a. Gradually prioritize the recruitment of additional personnel for the offices
concerned and consider viable options to augment the manpower needs of the
Inventory Committee, in order to improve operational efficiency and provide
adequate support for the conduct of inventory-taking activities;

b. Require the members of the Inventory Committee to prioritize the conduct of a
complete physical inventory, reconciliation with pertinent records, and
preparation and submission of reports, in compliance with COA Circular No.
2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts; and

c. Instruct the Municipal Accountant and the GSO to review and reinforce the
observance of proper accounting and reporting procedures for all PPE-related
transactions (e.g., acquisition, receipt, issuance, transfer, and disposal), in line
with applicable laws and regulations. The Municipal Mayor is likewise
encouraged to regularly monitor and ensure that annual physical inventory is
conducted and the corresponding reports are properly submitted on time.

We reiterated that Management:

a. Require the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal
Engineer and the GSO, to reconcile the RPCLRN with the appropriate records
and prepare the journal voucher to update the Road Networks account in the
books of accounts;

b. Enjoin the Municipal Accountant and the GSO to maintain complete and
updated LRNLC and LRNPC, respectively, in accordance with the provisions
of COA Circular No. 2015-008; and

c. Ensure that a complete physical count of local road networks is undertaken
annually, and the reports thereon are submitted to the Audit Team and the
Municipal Accountant not later than January 31 of each year.

We recommended that the Management:

a. Instruct the Municipal Engineer and the MPDC to provide the Municipal
Accountant with a list of completed projects, duly supported by Certificates of
Completion and Final Acceptance, as basis for adjusting the balances of CIP
and related asset accounts; and

b. Direct the Municipal Engineer to consistently monitor ongoing projects and
promptly submit the required reports and documentation to the Municipal
Accountant to facilitate the timely transfer of completed projects to the
appropriate asset accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (i) conduct a thorough
review and reconciliation of the Due to NGAS account, ensuring that all SLs are
complete, updated, and contain sufficient details to support the recorded balances;
(ii) investigate and resolve the unreconciled balances and make the necessary
adjustments, if warranted, to correct the discrepancies; and (iii) strictly observe the
provisions of Section 111(1) of PD No. 1445 and the applicable standards under
IPSAS 1 in the maintenance of accounting records and preparation of financial
statements to promote fair presentation and accountability.

We further recommended that Management (i) facilitate the preparation of
liquidation reports and ensure the return of unutilized balances from completed
PPAs to their respective source agencies; (ii) give priority to the completion of
PPAs experiencing delays or deferred implementation; and (iii) initiate the
preparation and consolidation of necessary documents in support of the request for
write-off of dormant fund transfers.

We recommended that the:

a. BAC Secretariat immediately submit the lacking contract documents to the
Audit Team to facilitate the necessary auditorial, legal, and technical review; and

b. Management enjoin the BAC Secretariat to strictly adhere with COA Circular
No. 2009-001 on the timely and complete submission of copies of government
contracts to the Audit Team.

We recommended that Management consider the potential benefits of COA
Circular No. 2023-006 by transitioning to the digital submission of ERDs to enhance
document security and accessibility.

Recommendation:
We recommended the following:
The Management to –
Prioritize the review and approval of the results of reconciliation and disposition of Non-Existing/Missing PPEs for 18 PPE accounts already physically counted so that the concerned offices can move forward and complete the one-time cleansing thereof;
The Inventory Committee to conduct the physical inventory of the remaining 18 PPE accounts so that it can finally complete the one-time cleaning of all the PPE accounts of the Municipality. If it is not possible, to identify in its Physical Inventory Plan, the PPE accounts to be physically counted in the current year.
In both cases, to strictly follow the guidelines provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006.
For subsequent transactions, the Office of the Municipal Accountant to –
Ensure that only those properties with a unit cost of P50,000.00 and above are recorded as part of the PPE of the LGU;
Capitalize the expenditures related to an existing property as its additional cost only if it adds economic benefits or service potential to the property, otherwise, record it as an expense for repairs and maintenance.

The Supreme Court in G.R. 
No. 171033 (2010) clarified the distinction between a general law and a special law:

A general statute is one which embraces a class of subjects or places and does not omit any subject or place naturally belonging to such class. A special statute, as the term is generally understood, is one which relates to particular persons or things of a class or to a particular portion or section of the state only. In the present case, R.A. No. 7160 is to be construed as a general law, while 
City Ordinance 
No. SP-91, S-93 is a special law, having emanated only from R.A. 
7160 and with limited territorial application in Quezon City only.

This doctrine reinforces the authority of local ordinances within their locality, so long as they do not contravene national law or constitutional provisions.

For CY 2024, 
the GAD Fund had a utilization rate 
of 98 percent in accordance with Item 2.3 of 
PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC 
No. 2013-01 dated July 18, 2013, as amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC 
No. 2016-01.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Treasurer to require the Special Disbursing Officers to ensure that all cash advances pertaining to the salaries and wages of job orders and contract of service personnel are liquidated within five days from the date of each cash advance and the liquidation documents are submitted to the Office of the City Accountant in compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002.
Also, we recommended the City Mayor to consider the possibility of the full payment of salaries and wages through Auto-Debit Arrangement (ADA) as internal control to prevent delayed liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: The City has fully implemented the payment of salaries and wages through ADA. The City Treasurer’s Office required the Special Disbursing Officers to ensure that all cash advances pertaining to the salaries and wages of job orders and contract of service personnel are liquidated within five days from the date of each cash advance and the liquidation documents are submitted to the Office of the City Accountant in compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002.

observations.recommendation:

The CGSO coordinated with the City Accounting Office to ensure reconciliation of records. Both offices maintained close coordination for the reconciliation of records between their offices.

observations.recommendation:

observations.managementAction: The roads which have been itemized were already included in the submitted RPCPPE prepared by the GSO.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the:
• City Planning and Development Officer (CPDO) to ensure that all funded development projects to be funded out of the 20 percent DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready; and
• OIC-City Engineer to inspect the ongoing infrastructure projects on the expected date of completion and ensure that projects are completed within the dates specified in the contract.
Henceforth, comply with the guidelines set in the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 in the appropriation and utilization of the 20 percent DF of the City.
observations.managementAction: The City Planning Development Officer (CPDO) monitored the projects implementation of the PPAs. The City Engineer commented that some projects were on phasing and that plans and technical designs are prepared by the City Architect Office. The City Accountant imposed liquidated damages against the contractor for delayed projects in CY 2023.
observations.reason: Some of the projects were not well-planned and not procurement-and-implementation ready. There are still projects in the current and continuing appropriations under the 20 percent DF that were not well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the City Mayor instruct the Chairperson of the Gender Focal Point Systems-Technical Working Group to adopt necessary measures to ensure that the LGU’s GPBs are submitted to the Audit Team within five working days from receipt of DILG-approved GPBs.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Head submitted the GPB for CY 2024 on June 4, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the supporting documents of the claims listed in the Accounts Payable totaling P16,488,895.06, together with the contract/Purchase Order if any, for verification; otherwise prepare an adjusting entry debiting the Accounts Payable and crediting the corresponding prior year's adjustment account/s. Henceforth, ensure that the recording of Accounts Payable is in accordance with Section 4(s) of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The City made submissions and revisions: Majority of the recorded accounts payable were supported with documents. Reversions and adjustments were made for some transactions; some transactions were recorded in the General Journal and awaiting submission to the Audit Team. Revision of the schedule of Accounts Payable was made in CY 2024 and reconciled with the financial statements as of December 31, 2024.
observations.reason: There are still Accounts Payable totaling P128,858.24 which were not supported with complete documentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the CPDO to ensure that all development projects to be funded out of the 20 percent DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready; OIC-City Engineer to inspect the ongoing infrastructure projects on the expected date of completion and ensure that projects are completed within the dates specified in the contract; and henceforth comply with the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1.
observations.managementAction: The CPDO monitored projects; the City Engineer commented that plans/designs are prepared by the City Architect Office; some projects not implemented due to right-of-way issues and other constraints; continuous monitoring and evaluation ongoing.
observations.reason: There are still projects in the current and continuing appropriations under the 20 percent DF for CY 2024 that were not well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management instruct the City Accountant to seek or locate the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) entered into with the Source Agencies or any documents stating the purpose of the fund transfer and the provision of the requirement in implementing and reporting of the unutilized PPAs and allocate/unutilize funds accordingly.
observations.managementAction: The Management sent a communication requesting that the unutilized fund be allocated for the purpose of Grants for Local Government Units Best Practices; however, the City was unable to locate copies of the MOA for grants from former president Gloria Macapagal Arroyo fund and DOH Region IV-A Grants for Local Government Units Best Practices. The City submitted SP Resolutions No. 18-92 and 18-93 both dated July 30, 2018 for disposition of DOH unutilized balance; these remain in the books of accounts.
observations.reason: The City Accountant was unable to locate copies of the MOA from the former President Arroyo fund and DOH; compliance could not be validated.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the City Mayor instruct the BAC and the City Engineer to properly plan and schedule all procurement activities pertaining to development projects for their timely implementation and completion; CPDO to fast track and prioritize the full implementation of the development projects as embodied in the 20 percent DF Plan; City Engineer to develop procedures to facilitate preparation of technical designs; and the CPDO to monitor and coordinate with concerned departments to improve planning and programming of projects.
observations.managementAction: The BAC and City Engineer prepared plans and timeline on procurement; CPDO monitored PPAs and is updating the LDIP; City Engineer said technical designs prepared by City Architect Office; some projects on phasing and ROW issues noted. Implementation and monitoring is ongoing.
observations.reason: There are still projects in the current and continuing projects under the 20 percent DF for CY 2024 that were not well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations that the BAC and City Engineer properly plan and schedule all procurement activities; CPDO to fast track plans, designs and implementation of development projects; ensure projects are feasible and can be implemented during the year programmed and that allocated funds are timely and optimally utilized; continuously monitor and evaluate projects funded under the 20 percent DF.
observations.managementAction: CPDO monitored projects; some projects remain unimplemented due to prior year carryovers, documentation and ROW issues, and need for revised plans/ABC/PPMP; CPDO to strategize and coordinate with concerned departments; City Engineer commented that some plans/designs are prepared by City Architect Office; other implementation constraints noted.
observations.reason: There are still projects under continuing appropriations and current appropriations that were not implemented; several requirements (plans, ROW, documents) remain outstanding.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that: a. All details regarding the available funds and its utilization are disclosed in the Notes to FS in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002; b. The utilization portion of the LDRRMF Utilization Report is labeled in accordance with the Annual Budget/LDRRMF Investment Plan.
Additionally, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to: a. Retain in the General Fund (GF) the remaining balances of the capital outlay portion of the LDRRMF; and b. Revert the unexpended LDRRMF for CY 2018 to the unappropriated surplus of the GF, made available for reappropriation to other social services after subsequent enactment by the Sangguniang Bayan.
Lastly, ensure that the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 are adhered to.
observations.managementAction:
All details regarding the available funds and its utilization are disclosed in the Notes to FS in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002.
The labels of the utilization in the LDRRMF Utilization Report are labeled in accordance with the Annual Budget/LDRRMF Investment Plan.
The capital outlay portion of the remaining LDRRMF has been retained and maintained in the GF.
The CY 2018 unexpended LDRRMF has been reverted to the GF, available for reappropriation to other social services after subsequent enactment by the Sangguniang Bayan.
There were no significant findings pertaining to the compliance of Municipality to the aforementioned criteria.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all payments are properly charged to their appropriate funds; b. Municipal Accountant to ensure that all transactions are properly recorded in their proper SLs; and c. Also, ensure that the prescribed managerial policies in the Municipality are thoroughly reviewed as part of the audit which were implemented and fully adhered to.
observations.managementAction: Payments made by the Municipality are properly charged to their appropriate funds. Also, ensure that the prescribed managerial policies in the Municipality are installed, implemented, and fully adhered to. The aforementioned fund was already reprogrammed and implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the return of P4,351,860.00 to the source agencies in compliance with COA letter of demand and the MOA.

Implemented
A total of P4,351,860.00; P4,024,300.00 from the Department of Labor and Employment Regional Office No. 4A (DOLE-4A) and, P327,560.00 received from Department of Science and Technology (DOST) – Calabarzon were returned to their respective source agency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor exercise enhanced prudence in the disbursement of the LDRRMF. Specifically, instruct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) to exert prudence in the disbursement of LDRRMF and immediately stop charging the noted expenses against LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction:
Implemented
The Municipal Mayor exercise enhanced prudence in the disbursement of the LDRRMF. Specifically, the LDRRMC through the MDRRMO to exert prudence in the disbursement of LDRRMF and ensure that all expenses charged against the LDRRMF are closely scrutinized for compliance with the established guidelines, ensuring that only those expenses approved for the LDRRMF are disbursed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that remittances of Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) premium are made within 10 calendar days after the end of each month. We also recommended coordinating with the GSIS to be able to remit the unidentified balance of P23,098.80.
observations.managementAction: On-going tracing and reconciliation in the records of the Office of Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: Unremitted Due to GSIS increased to P338,960.51. Of which P256,656.88 pertains to variance due to non-inclusion in the GSIS bill and P42,662.11 for refund to identified employees. Leaving an unremitted and unidentified balance of P39,641.52 for CY 2024, showing an increase of P16,542.72 from CY 2023 unremitted and unidentified balance as discussed in Observation No. 6, Part III of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: • Use sealed envelopes in transmitting cash advance voucher for Confidential and/or Intelligence Funds including its supporting documents to the Audit Team; and • Ensure that proper authorization is issued to agency personnel transmitting the cash advance voucher for Confidential and/or Intelligence Funds on her behalf.

Use sealed envelopes in transmitting cash advance voucher for Confidential and/or Intelligence Funds including its supporting documents to the Audit Team; and

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: complete and update the PPELC for each category of PPE; and the Municipal GSO and the Municipal Accountant to pursue and complete the reconciliation of the accounting with property records in order to establish the correct PPE balances, to investigate and adjust any discrepancy that will be noted.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: update the PPELC for each category of PPE; and the Municipal GSO and the OIC-Municipal Accountant to pursue and complete the reconciliation of the accounting with property records in order to establish the correct PPE balances, to investigate and adjust any discrepancy that will be noted; and the Disposal Committee to undertake the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties to avoid further deterioration and possible loss and to generate additional income from its disposal.

The Disposal Committee undertook the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Prepare the necessary journal entry to reclassify the transactions to the appropriate accounts and furnish the same to the Audit Team for validation purposes; and
b. Ensure that the Accounts Payable account is only used to record the receipt of goods or services on account in the normal course of trade and business operation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared a Journal Entry Voucher Nos. 70001 and 70005, both dated May 2, 2024 to reclassify the transactions to the appropriate accounts and furnish the same to the Audit Team for validation purposes.
observations.reason: Transactions regarding receipt of goods and services were properly recorded in the Accounts Payable.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to address the weak internal control over the updating of employee records, and in the recording and remittance of employees and government contributions to the GSIS by providing control mechanisms wherein:

a. Changes in the status of employee employment such as separated employees or other changes in employee information will be immediately reported by the Department Heads to the Human Resource Department, through the designated agency authorized officer, to update the records of the GSIS; and
b. Agency Remittance Advice (ARA) for the said changes in status and salary rate will be immediately prepared by the Human Resource Department, through the designated agency authorized officer, to update the records of the GSIS; and
c. Monthly reconciliation of amounts withheld and remitted to GSIS by the Accounting Department to include schedule or breakdown per employee of any unremitted contributions to be sent to the Human Resource Department for ARA, if necessary.

We also recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Inquire with the GSIS the validity of the idle unremitted/unadjusted extra hazard premium totaling P368,354.33 and make necessary remittance/adjustment after account confirmation; and
b. Reconcile the balance of P213,662.52.
observations.managementAction: The Human Resource Management Officer informed the Audit Team that changes in status of employees or other changes in employment information are automatically reported to the Human Resource Department as soon as they retire or separate from service, for the Human Resource Department to prepare the necessary Agency Remittance Advice (ARA). The Municipal Accountant had adjusted the idle unremitted extra hazard premium of P368,354.33, which was charged to Prior Period Adjustment Per JEV No. 70007 dated May 28, 2024.
observations.reason: Not prioritized by the management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to:

a. Ensure that all development projects to be funded out of the 20 percent DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready; and
b. Henceforth, comply with the guidelines set in DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020.
observations.managementAction: Complied. Two projects with appropriations totaling P11,159,859.20 will be funded out of the 20 percent DF are to be funded by the National Government and the Provincial Government of Quezon while the other two projects with appropriations totaling P1,250,000.00 were not implemented due to rainy weather.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to:

a. Submit the consolidated PIF to the Audit Team and the GIF of the GSIS, not later than April 30 of each year; and
b. Henceforth, comply with the guidelines set in COA Circular No. 2018-002 dated May 31, 2018, in insuring the properties with the GIF of the GSIS.
observations.managementAction: Complied. Submitted the consolidated PIF to the Audit Team and the GIF of the GSIS, not later than April 30 of each year. Henceforth, comply with the guidelines set in COA Circular No. 2018-002 dated May 31, 2018, in insuring the properties with the GIF of the GSIS.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources (MENRO)-Designate to coordinate with the National Solid Waste Management Council (NSWMC) regarding the status of review on the Municipality’s 10-Year SWMP in order to know the next steps to be undertaken that will facilitate approval of the said Plan.
observations.managementAction: A one-on-one coaching was conducted on December 11 to 13, 2024 for finalization of the plan. A deliberation to the National Solid Waste Management Commission will be conducted once the information is submitted/completed.
observations.reason: A deliberation to the National Solid Waste Commission will be conducted once the information is submitted/completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and The Municipal Mayor instructed the Municipal Accountant to prepare the following adjusting entries as entry in General Fund evidenced by JEV No. 100 2024-05 50340 and JEV No. 300-2024-05-50039.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared the adjusting entries.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations, and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
 Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain PPELCs to monitor the physical quantity and balances of PPE items, in accordance with Section 114 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and
 Direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and maintain property cards for the PPEs, to monitor the physical quantities and accountabilities for PPE items, in accordance with Section 114 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
Additionally, we recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
 Adopt appropriate strategies to strengthen the internal controls on the PPE, particularly on ensuring that the property officer is able to inspect PPEs prior to the issuance of PPE and pertinent PARs to end-users; 
 Avail and implement the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances, following the guidelines and procedures prescribed by COA Circular No. 2020-006, in order to correct the balances of PPE; and
 Ensure that PPE records of the Accounting Office and Property Unit are reconciled, in accordance with Section 114 of the MNGAS for LGUS, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared and maintained PPELCs to monitor PPE quantities and balances.
observations.reason: No action has been undertaken by the Municipal Treasurer at this time. PCs were not prepared by the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
 Investigate the cause/s of the variance in the Accumulated Depreciation accounts, make the necessary corrections to the lapsing schedule and/or prepare the necessary adjusting entries, if warranted, to obtain faithful representation for the accounts; and
 Ensure that the Accumulated Depreciation reported in the books, lapsing schedule, and PPELCs are reconciled.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has corrected and adjusted the variance in the Accumulated Depreciation.

Timely submission of liquidation reports by the Municipal Treasurer; cash advances are required to be liquidated before granting new cash advances; Municipal Treasurer submitted the liquidation reports within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
 Direct the Municipal DRRM Officer (MDRRMO) and the Municipal Accountant to submit the monthly DRRM Utilization on or before the 15th day of each ensuing month; and
 Enjoin the MDRRMO to provide more specific time frames in the implementation of 'Disaster Prevention and Mitigation' and 'Disaster Preparedness' Programs/Projects/Activities (PPAs).
observations.managementAction:
The monthly DRRM Utilization Reports have been submitted on or before the 15th day of each ensuing month.
The Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) for CY 2024 was approved by the MDRRMC and the Sangguniang Bayan. For the 2025 LDRRMFIP, the PPAs under Disaster Prevention and Mitigation and Disaster Preparedness have been assigned specific time frames.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
 Adopt appropriate internal strategies that shall ensure that the physical count of PPE is conducted by the Inventory Committee on time and not be heavily reliant on the accounting records; 
 Direct the Chairman of the Inventory Committee to inform the Audit Team of the schedule of physical count, at least 10 calendar days before the activity; and
 Direct the Chairman of the Inventory Committee to ensure that moving forward, the RPCPPE is submitted to the Audit Team on or before January 31 of each year, in accordance with Section 57 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II.
observations.managementAction: The audit team was informed about the schedule of the physical count prior to the activity. RPCPPE was submitted on January 24, 2025. Inventory taking was conducted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
 Devise a strategy that will ensure that the individual Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAFs) of Accountable Officers are regularly updated to facilitate the timely preparation and submission of CRAAF; and
 Ensure the timely submission of CRAAF in accordance with Section 51 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II.
observations.managementAction: Regular update of individual Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAFs) is being practiced by the Municipality. Timely submission of CRAAF is being practiced by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the Chairperson, the GFPS to adopt necessary measures to ensure that the Municipality’s GPBs are submitted to the Audit Team within five working days from receipt of Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG)-approved GPBs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality's GPBs were submitted to the Audit Team within the prescribed period.

Henceforth, comply with guidelines set in the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 on the appropriation and utilization of the 20 percent DF of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

Ensure prompt remittance of PhilHealth contributions within the period prescribed by PhilHealth to avoid unnecessary sanctions and penalties.
