observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to ensure the implementation of all planned PPAs funded out of LDRRMF-MF in order for the Municipality to reduce disaster risk and be adequately prepared for any imminent disaster/calamity in accordance with RA No. 10121.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had implemented 84.71 percent of the LDRRMF-MF for CY 2024.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to:
 Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the following adjusting entry: Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 102-2024-05-3091.
Henceforth, ensure that the costs of asphalt overlaying of roads are recorded as Repairs and Maintenance, in accordance with Item IV (7) of COA Circular No. 2015-008.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared the adjusting entries as evidenced by Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 102-2024-05-3091.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
 Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to recognize the projects as PPE, with due consideration to the corresponding depreciation expenses; and
 Enjoin the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC), Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the account codes to be used for the municipality’s PPAs are reviewed based on the nature of the PPAs; and
Henceforth, ensure that items meeting the definition and recognition criteria of PPE, as set forth in paragraphs 13 and 14 of IPSAS 17, are recognized as PPE.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant prepared the adjusting entries to recognize the projects as PPE as evidenced by JEV No. 102-2024-05-3093.
The concerned officials used the account codes based on the nature of the PPAs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to:
 Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the following adjusting entry: Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 102-2024-05-3092.
Henceforth, ensure that the items included in the Construction in Progress (CIP) accounts pertain only to ongoing projects of the municipality, as described in Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared the adjusting entries as evidenced by JEV No. 102-2024-05-3092.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
 Develop internal strategies, such as considering the feasibility of paying JO wages via automated teller machine (ATM), to strengthen internal controls over cash and reduce, if not eliminate, delayed liquidation of cash advances and the occurrence of overlapping cash advances; 
 Direct disbursing officers to ensure that cash advances are liquidated within the prescribed deadlines, in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002; and
 Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to refrain from granting additional cash advances to an accountable officer if he/she has an outstanding cash advance/s, in accordance with Item 4.1.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has already implemented the payment for JO employees via ATM.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
 Implement a more accurate control measure in recording movements of accountable forms to facilitate a more streamlined preparation of the CRAAF; 
 Submit an electronic copy of the CRAAF through email, duly certified by her, within five days of the ensuing month to enable the Audit Team to conduct a preliminary evaluation of the LGU’s Accountable Forms; and
 Thereafter, submit the hard copy of the report upon the LGU’s submission to the Provincial Treasurer’s Office (PTO); and
Henceforth, ensure the timely submission of CRAAFs within five days of each ensuing month, pursuant to Section 51 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality submits an electronic copy of CRAAF through email within five days of the ensuing month and also submits the hard copy with received stamp of PTO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed, to instruct the Designated GAD Focal Person to adopt the necessary measures to ensure that the LGU’s GPBs are submitted to the Audit Team within five working days from receipt of Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG)-approved GPBs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality submitted its approved GPBs to the Audit Team on June 18, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure the proper granting, utilization, and liquidation of all cash advances in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: The accountable officer refunded the amount of P600.00 per Official Receipt (OR) No. 1872962 dated January 20, 2023 and P299,400.00 per OR No. 1872978 dated May 30, 2023.

observations.recommendation:
Henceforth, we recommended compliance with:
 Section 73 of PD No. 1445 on the requirement for the notice of loss and request for relief in connection with the loss of property caused by fire, theft, or other casualty or force majeure; and
 COA Circular No. 92-390 and RA No. 656 on the requirement of insurance coverage of all insurable properties with the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) in order to indemnify or compensate the Government for any damage to, or loss of, its properties due to fire, earthquake, storm, or other casualty.
observations.managementAction: Closed and approved by Regional Director on July 23, 2024, to be excluded in the ensuing year report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of PPEs and submit to the AT the RPCPPE within the prescribed period.
We also recommended the revision of the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) and the submission of the same to the AT at least 10 days before the scheduled start of the inventory taking activities for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts in accordance with Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006.
We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) -Designate to issue and update Property Cards (PCs) and Property Acknowledgement Receipts (PARs) of acquired PPEs and maintain proper and complete file of said documents in compliance with COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction:
The Inventory Committee conducted the physical count of PPEs and submitted the RPCPPE within the prescribed period.
The PIP was submitted to the AT on June 3, 2025.
The MGSO – Designate updated and maintained PCs and PARs for acquired PPEs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
 a. Municipal Treasurer (MT) to submit the certified list of taxpayers to the Municipal Accountant (MA) at the beginning of the year showing the name of the taxpayers and the corresponding amounts due and collectible for the year, so that the Municipal Accountant can set-up the correct RPT and SET Receivables; 
 b. Municipal Accountant to trace the variances for the mentioned accounts made during CY 2023 and make the necessary adjustments; and 
 c. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their balances and maintain subsidiary ledger for monitoring purposes.
observations.managementAction:
A certified list of taxpayers, detailing names and the respective amounts owed and collectible for the year, was provided by the MT to the MA.
The MA and MT were able to maintain and establish the proper balances for RPT and SET Receivables.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to verify the correctness of the amounts included in the schedule of the Due to LGUs account balance of P6,382,390.25 and identify the LGUs to which the Municipality has liability for the said balance.
Henceforth, strictly comply with Section 111 of P.D. No. 1445 in ensuring that the Due to LGUs account is properly supported with details and is accurately recorded.
observations.managementAction: The MA made the necessary adjustments to properly establish the balance of Due to LGUs account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to investigate the cause of discrepancies and make necessary adjusting entries to rectify the irregular figures presented in the books of TF.
Further review and investigation of the account is needed to comply with the said recommendation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made the necessary adjusting entry, JEV Number 100-2025-05-01576.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to confirm with the PCA the breakdown of the received funds to properly account for the share of the Municipality and Barangay and to remit the share of the Barangays.
Subsequently, we also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entry in the books of accounts to reclassify the aforementioned balance of Due to NGAs totaling P726,869.44 to Trust Liabilities and/or Due to LGUs account/s, upon confirmation of the Municipal and Barangay shares with the PCA.
observations.managementAction: Verification and confirmation of received funds have been completed, allowing for the proper implementation of the barangay and municipal share allocation in accordance with the RA and remit the share of the Barangays. Subsequently, the required adjustments have been made through Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) Number 300-2024-03-00291.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the following adjusting entry in the corresponding books of accounts to properly classify the accounts.
observations.managementAction: The transactions were no longer adjusted since the obligations were already paid.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MA to make adjusting entries in the books of General Fund (GF) and Special Education Fund (SEF).
observations.managementAction: The MA made the adjustments supported by JEV Number 100-2024-04-01320 and 200-2024-04-00120.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to submit the year-end reports together with the necessary schedules in compliance with the above-cited law, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality ensured timely compliance by submitting the required financial reports and documents to the Audit Team in compliance with the above-cited law, rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDC and the MPDC to: a. Ensure that all projects to be funded out of the 20 percent DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready; and b. Henceforth, comply with guidelines set in DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 in the appropriation and utilization of the 20 percent DF.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2024, the Municipality effectively utilized approximately 73 percent of the appropriated funds for the implementation of the projects, programs, and activities under the 20 percent DF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal DRRM Officer to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the Audit Team within the prescribed period which is on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality ensured timely submission of the required utilization reports for CY 2024, meeting the prescribed deadline and reinforcing transparency in fund allocation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GAD Focal Person to submit immediately to the Audit Team the Accomplishment Report for GAD Fund for CY 2023 and henceforth, strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2014-001 on the timely submission of the GAD Accomplishment Report.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person submitted the Accomplishment Report for CY 2023 and 2024 to the AT.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the titling of the five parcels of land to avoid contingent claims that may arise in the future.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor was able to acquire Certificate of Title for one of the five parcels mentioned. Moreover, follow-up letters were also sent to concerned agencies/individuals to be able to secure titles for the remaining land parcels.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to make a constant follow-up of their request to Land Bank of the Philippines (LBP) regarding the particulars of various debit/credit memos and consequently effect the adjustment immediately; and b. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to exert more efforts to locate and submit to the Audit Team the remaining unsubmitted Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) for CYs 2016 and 2017 and their supporting documents for evaluation and verification.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant exerted extra effort to locate and submit to the Audit Team the remaining unsubmitted DVs of CYs 2016 & 2017 and their supporting documents. The Municipal Accountant made the necessary adjustment with JEV# 100-2025-05-01849.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MT to adopt strict collection measures and strategies in collecting the outstanding loan balances of P142,141.12 from the retired/resigned employees in order for the Municipality to have additional funding for its livelihood projects.
observations.managementAction: The outstanding balance of Microfinance Loan is only P138,141.12 as per schedule of loan receivable account. The MT informed that the remaining balance will be collected from the respective individuals.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. MA with the help of Human Resource Management Officer to gather all relevant documents for the reconciliation of the aforementioned Inter-Agency Payables and make necessary adjustments, as needed; b. MT to subsequently remit to the respective agencies or refund to the employees the balances identified by the Municipal Accountant, if warranted; and c. MA to prepare adjusting journal entries to establish the correct and proper balances of the accounts, if necessary.
observations.managementAction: The MA, MT and HRMO are coordinating to reconcile unpaid contributions and establish breakdowns. The MT committed to comply once MA identified and established the breakdown.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Make the adjusting entry by debiting the CIB – LCCA and crediting the AP accounts both by the amount of checks still unreleased; and
b. Strictly comply with Section 43 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I by ensuring that only checks duly released to the claimants are recorded in the Check Disbursements to avoid understating both the CIB-LCCA and the AP accounts.
observations.managementAction: All unreleased checks noted in the observation have already been released; hence, no further adjustments were deemed necessary. Nevertheless, the Municipal Accountant complied with the recommendation and applied it in CY 2024 transactions.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the following adjusting entry in the General Fund books of accounts:
Accounts Payable 910,550.65
Leave Benefits Payable 833,586.46
Due to LGUs 45,340.00
Other Payables 31,624.19
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made an adjusting entry through Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 100-2024-04-100152 dated April 15, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to draw the adjusting entries to reclassify the Due to PhilHealth account of P36,400.20 to Other Payable account and initiate the refund of the said premium contributions to the JOs. We also recommended the Human Resource Management Officer and the Municipal Accountant to identify the breakdown of the remaining unaccounted balance in the Due to PhilHealth account amounting to P12,400.25. Henceforth, strictly comply with the above-mentioned guidelines.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made a reclassification entry through JEV No. 100-2024-04-100151 dated April 15, 2024. Moreover, her office has initiated the refund of the said premium contribution to JO. Henceforth, ensure timely remittance of Premium Contributions. Premium Contributions were remitted on the date prescribed by Philhealth.
observations.reason: The Municipality is still in the process of identifying the breakdown of the remaining unaccounted balance of P12,400.25.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Development Council and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to ensure that all development projects to be funded out of the 20 percent DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready; and Henceforth, comply with guidelines set in the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 in the appropriation and utilization of the 20 percent DF of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Management assured that the implementation of the planned projects will be prioritized. The projects were reprogrammed to Continuation of Motorpool through Municipal Ordinance No. 21-2025 enacted on April 21, 2025.
observations.reason: There were still projects that were not well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal General Service Officer (MGSO) to:
a. Comply with the preparation and submission of the Consolidated PIF to the Audit Team not later than April 30 of each year; and
b. Ensure that all insurable properties of the Municipality are adequately covered with the GIF of the GSIS.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-MGSO prepared and submitted the Consolidated PIF and has ensured that insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with insurance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Immediately remit the identified unremitted premium contribution of P64,575.00 to PhilHealth and ensure that no penalty or fines will be charged against the budget of the Municipality; and
b. Analyze and identify the breakdown of the remaining unaccounted/unreconciled balance of P10,471.60. Henceforth, ensure timely remittance of premium contributions on the date prescribed by PhilHealth.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already remitted the identified unremitted premium contribution to PhilHealth. The Municipality remitted the unremitted contribution on the date prescribed by PhilHealth.
observations.reason: The Municipality is still in the process of tracing and identifying the breakdown of the unaccounted balance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments to reclassify the eight (8) completed projects totaling P7,490,183.96 to their respective PPE accounts. Henceforth, ensure that all completed and paid projects funded by budget appropriation are closed or reclassified to the appropriate asset account pursuant to the provisions of MNGAS for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted to the Audit Team JEV Nos. 2024-05-003343 and 3344 both dated May 31, 2024, reclassifying the eight completed projects totaling P7,490,183.96 to their respective PPE accounts. Recording and reclassification of all completed and paid projects to the appropriate asset account is now being observed by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the following adjusting entries in the General Fund books of accounts to correct the misclassification of the recorded transactions, to wit: Dr. Other Structures P1,876,770.23 Dr. Prior Period Adjustment P1,876,770.23 Cr. Buildings P1,876,770.23 Cr. Land 12,600.00 Cr. Other Land Improvements 496,779.50 Cr. Road Networks 81,182.80 Cr. Flood Control Systems 1,744,676.65 Cr. Other Structures 76,600.00 Henceforth, ensure that recording of the transactions under the 20% Development Fund is properly made in accordance with the provisions of the COA Circular No. 2015-009.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared and submitted to the Audit Team JEV No. 2024-05-003104 dated May 6, 2024 to correct the misclassification of the recorded transactions in the General Fund books of accounts. The Municipal Accountant implemented the recording of transactions in the books of accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009.
observations.reason: The recording of completed projects funded by the 20% Development Fund was not properly recorded prior to adjustment; now adjusted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:  Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare a year-end adjusting entries to revert the unreleased checks totaling P3,233,874.22 to the cash in bank account and recognized the corresponding liability/payable account;  Direct the Municipal Treasurer to comply with the procedures in MNGAS for LGUs pertaining to reporting of disbursements/checks issued; and  Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that recording of check disbursements will only be done after receipt of the Report of Checks Issued (RCI) with DV and supporting documents from the Municipal Treasurer, to avoid inclusion of unreleased checks as reconciling items in BRS.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared and furnished the Audit Team with a copy of JEV No. 2024-05-003536 dated May 31, 2024 for the adjustments made reverting the unreleased checks totaling P3,233,874.22 to the cash in bank account and recognized the corresponding liability/payable accounts. The Municipal Treasurer observed the procedures in MNGAS for LGUs pertaining to reporting of disbursements/checks issued. The Municipal Accountant implemented the recording of check disbursements after receipt of RCI with DV and supporting documents from the Municipal Treasurer, to avoid inclusion of unreleased checks as reconciling items in BRS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:  Immediately submit the remaining subsidiary ledgers and/or provide complete details or information supporting the Due to NGAs account with total amount of P102,516.25 as of December 31, 2023, for further evaluation of the Audit Team; and  Strictly observe the provisions stated in Section 111 (1) of PD No. 1445 on keeping of accounts in details to meet the needs of the agency and at the same time be adequate to furnish the information needed by fiscal or control agencies of the government.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted on May 18, 2024 the remaining subsidiary ledgers supporting the Due to NGAs account with total amount of P102,516.25 as of December 31, 2023, for further evaluation of the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:  Analyze and reconcile the Due to Other Funds and Due from Other Funds accounts to determine whether all transfers of funds are authorized and received by other funds and to ensure the accuracy of its reciprocal balances;  Effect the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances of said accounts in the financial statements; and  Submit the corresponding JEV and other supporting documents to the Audit Team for further evaluation thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant reviewed the entries made and identified erroneous entries that caused the non-reconciliation of both accounts. The JEV was submitted by the Municipal Accountant on May 31, 2024 which was verified and found in order by the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to observe timely submission of the monthly report of utilization of DRRMF to the Municipal DRRM Officer (MDRRM) and submit the same to the Audit Team not later than the 15th day after end of each month.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant already submitted the reports to the Audit Team for CY 2024 within the prescribed period. The MDRRMO complied with the timely submission of the reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Accountable Officers to ensure that cash advances are liquidated within five days from the date of each cash advance. Also, an official receipt was prepared and issued in case of any unclaimed salaries.
observations.managementAction: The payment of salaries and wages to job orders and contract of service pertaining to the salaries and wages of job orders and contract of service are liquidated within five days from the date of cash advance. Also, an official receipt was prepared and issued in case of any unclaimed salaries. Compliance was observed pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer (MT) to strictly ensure that the collecting officers remit their daily collections at the close of each business day. Also, ensure compliance to relevant rules and regulations pertinent to collections, remittance, and deposit per aforementioned criteria.
observations.managementAction: The MT issued a memorandum dated April 19, 2024 to the municipal collectors regarding the rules on collection, remittance, and deposit per aforementioned criteria. With this, he implemented a cut-off time for remittance of the collectors which ensured timely remittance and deposit of their collections, therefore, complying with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Development Council, through the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC), to henceforth ensure that the projects funded out of the 20 percent DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready.
observations.managementAction: As of year-end of CY 2024, all projects funded under the 20 percent DF are either on-going or have already been implemented. Meanwhile, funds allocated to development projects from prior years under the same fund have either realigned to the other eligible projects or are currently on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the following adjusting entries in General Fund (GF) books of accounts:
observations.managementAction: An adjusting entry was made by the Municipal Accountant through Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 100-2406-128 dated June 30, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the return of the unused balance of fund transfers totaling P947,984.07 to the respective source or National Treasury in compliance with the above laws and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The return of unused balance amounting to P10,484.07 covered by Check No. 211973 was deposited on April 12, 2024 to the account of Bureau of Treasury (3402-2844-20). Elevated to the Sangguniang Bayan the resolution relative to the remaining P937,500.00. The said amounts were endorsed to the Treasurer for their action since there is an existing resolution that the fund shall be used as revolving fund. Relatively, the balance amounting P937,500.00 is currently being processed for return.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor in coordination with the Municipal Accountant and the Officer-in-Charge (OIC)- Municipal Engineer impose the liquidated damages of P193.33/day from the defaulting contractor for the delays in the completion of the project without duly approved suspension order.
observations.managementAction: Final payment to the contractor covered under Disbursement Voucher (DV) No. 24071938 and Check No. 90261310 includes deduction of liquidated damages amounting to P23,392.93.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Chairperson, GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) Technical Working Group (TWG) to ensure that the GPB submitted to the Audit Team is duly approved by the DILG. In addition, we also recommended that the approved GPB be submitted to the Audit Team within five working days from receipt by the Municipality and/or by the Chairperson, GFPS TWG.
observations.managementAction: The GPB duly approved by the DILG together with its Certificate of Review and Endorsement, were submitted to the Audit Team within the reglementary period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: • Instruct the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to submit the list of all on-going government PPA and those that are to be implemented during the year; and • Thereafter, submit the above required list within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: The list of all on-going government PPA for CY 2024 and those that are to be implemented for CY 2025, were submitted to the Audit Team within the reglementary period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Prepare the journal entry to reclassify the transactions to appropriate accounts and furnish the Audit Team with a copy of the adjusting entries made for validation purposes; and
b. Ensure that the Accounts Payable account is only used to record receipt of goods or services on account in the normal course of trade and business.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 601 dated March 19, 2024 reclassify the transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer (MT) to monitor the maintenance of the CO’s cashbook conforming to Items a, b, and c of Section 181 of GAAM, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The COs maintained separate cashbooks.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Secure a copy of the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) or any document related to the utilization of the fund transfers and make representation with the SAs to determine whether the aforementioned funds can still be utilized by the Municipality according to its intended purposes. Otherwise, instruct the Municipal Accountant and MT to facilitate the return of the unused balance of the fund transfers to the respective SAs; and
b. Henceforth, ensure compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 94-013 on the utilization and liquidation of fund transfers.
observations.managementAction: Fund from the Department of Agriculture Region IV-A for the Concreting of Farm to Market Road amounting to P939,881.63 was neither implemented nor refunded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instructs the MDRRMO to furnish the OCD with a copy of the annual LDRRMFIP and/or the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF as required in Section 5.1.6 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: MDRRMO furnished the OCD with a copy of the LDRRMFIP and/or the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF as required.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to: • Submit certified true copy of Cashbook-Cash in Treasury; and • Reconcile the cashbook balances with the balances in the books of accounts of the Municipal Accountant at least quarterly and adjust any discrepancies that will be noted. We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer verify and reconcile the unidentified balance of P41,825.00 and make the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts and/or cashbook.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Accountant agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) for the unrecorded cleared checks totaling P19,694,260.63 to the Office of the Municipal Accountant. The Municipal Accountant prepared the adjusting entries as evidenced by JEV Nos. 100-2024-06-02550 and 200-2024-06-01013 dated June 30, 2024. The balance of P41,825.00 was traced to account for 20 percent Special Education Fund (SEF) and was adjusted in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to draw the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balances of Accounts Payable, Due to Officers and Employees and Other Payable accounts, if still unpaid. Henceforth, ensure that liabilities are recorded in their proper accounts, guided by COA Circular No. 2015-009.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared the adjusting entries as evidenced by JEV No. 100-2024-06-02550 dated June 30, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to verify and reconcile the cashbook and unidentified balances and make the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts and/or cashbook.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant reconciled the variance of P7,954,103.79 between the two reciprocal accounts and the adjustments were made in the books of accounts per JEV Nos. 100-2024-07-3203 and 300-24-08-00012 dated July 31, 2024 and April 30, 2024, respectively.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: • Revert to any unutilized Unappropriated Surplus of the GF for other social services by the local sanggunian; • Verify the cause/s of the inconsistencies in the Notes and utilization reports and effect the necessary adjusting entries; and • Ensure that the unexpended/unobligated balance of LDRRMF for Capital Outlay is presented in the Notes to Financial Statements (FS). Henceforth, ensure that the Notes to FS and utilization reports are accurately presented and reported, in accordance with Items 5.1.16 and 5.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made the necessary adjusting entries to correct the affected accounts amounting to P1,959,162.80 per Journal Entry Voucher Nos. 100-25-003-1290 and 300-25-003-083, both dated March 31, 2025. The Municipal Accountant reverted the Unappropriated Surplus of the GF to be programmed for other social services per JEV No. 300-24-01-00045 dated December 29, 2024. The Municipal Accounting Office is now conducting an intensive review of the accounts. The unexpended/ unobligated balances were adjusted in the books of accounts and are to be disclosed correctly in the Notes to FS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Acting Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the JEVs supported by proof that goods and services are accepted or rendered and received, together with the contract/Purchase Order if any, for our verification. Otherwise, prepare an adjusting entry debiting the Accounts Payable and crediting the corresponding account/s. Henceforth, ensure that the recording of Accounts Payable is in accordance with Section 4(s) of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was able to submit the supporting documents showing that goods and services are received and accepted as of December 31, 2023, to justify inclusion in the 2023 Accounts Payable balance. The Municipal Accountant prepared the adjusting entries as evidenced by JEV Nos. 100-2024-06-02551 and 200-2024-06-01014, both dated June 30, 2024. The Municipality has nominated an accounting clerk to monitor the processing of Accounts Payable for timely payment. All DVs as of December 31, 2024 were submitted to the Municipal Accountant’s Office and recorded in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that issued checks are monitored and written notices are sent to suppliers at least one month before their checks become stale, in accordance with Section 59 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer is already sending written notices to the suppliers with unclaimed checks at least one month before the checks become stale. The Municipal Treasurer provided the required written notices to suppliers and the Acting Municipal Accountant. The written notices were revised and approved by the Regional Director on August 1, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GSO to: • extract complete data in the RPCPPE and include all properties as defined in Section 4 (c) of Republic Act (RA) No. 656 and the Property Floater, in the PIF to enable the GSIS to completely determine all the insurable properties of the Municipality; • instruct the Municipal Engineering Office to submit the required as-built floor plans to GSIS to enable the completion of PIF; and • submit the PIF to the GSIS and the Supervising Auditor/Audit Team Leader on or before April 30 of each year.
observations.managementAction: In preparation of PIF, the GSO extracted the complete data in the RPCPPE to facilitate the assessment and determination of insurable properties of the Municipality. The GSO already submitted the required as-built floor plans to GSIS. The PIF was submitted to the Audit Team on March 5, 2025.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Gender and Development (GAD) Focal Person to adopt necessary measures to ensure that the Municipality’s GPBs are submitted to the Audit Team within five working days from receipt of DILG-approved GPBs.
observations.managementAction: The GAD TWG and the Municipal Gender and Development Focal Person submitted the GPB for FY 2025 to the Audit Team on September 12, 2024, the same date when the Certificate of Review and Endorsement was released to the Municipality by the DILG.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to: • request for the copy of released checks from the claimants; • provide the copies of stale checks to the Acting Municipal Accountant; • ensure monitoring of issued checks and sending of written notices to payees whose checks are about to become stale; and • submit the DVs for the unrecorded cleared checks totaling P1,098,058.41 to the Municipal Accountant’s Office.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer requested copies of released checks from the claimants. The Municipal Treasurer was able to provide copies of stale checks to the Acting Municipal Accountant. The Treasurer issued the required written notices to suppliers at least one month before checks become stale. All DVs as of December 31, 2024 were submitted to the Municipal Accountant’s Office and were recorded in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the JEVs supported by proof that goods and services are received and accepted for the claims listed in the Accounts Payable totaling P44,585,497.28 together with the contract/Purchase Order if any, for our verification. Otherwise, prepare an adjusting entry debiting the Accounts Payable and crediting the corresponding accounts. Henceforth ensure that recording of Accounts Payable is in accordance with Section 4(s) of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Accounts Payable as of December 31, 2024 were supported with documents evidencing receipt and acceptance of goods and services. The Municipal Accountant prepared JEVs supported by documents evidencing the receipt and acceptance of goods and services as of December 31, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer observe timely submission of the monthly CRAAF using the prescribed format and accompanied by the individual Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) of the Accountable Officers.
observations.managementAction: The CRAAF was submitted to the Audit Team within the prescribed period and accompanied by the individual RAAFs of the Accountable Officers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that the disposal of property either through public bidding or negotiated sale is properly documented and duly authorized.
observations.managementAction: The disposal of unserviceable properties was made through public bidding on April 19 and August 9, 2024.

observations.recommendation:
a.Municipal Accountant to make an adjusting entry and recognize an obligation for the stale checks by debiting CIB-LCCA and crediting Accounts Payable in the amounts of P3,693.56 and P73,000.0, in the General Fund (GF) and Trust Fund (TF) books, respectively.
b.Municipal Treasurer to mark cancelled unclaimed stale checks and return the original Disbursement Voucher/s (DV/s) and supporting documents to the Accounting Office pursuant to the provisions set forth in the Section 59 of the manual.
c. Municipal Treasurer to send a written notice to the payee/s of the subject check/s for them to present the checks for replacement.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant has made the necessary adjustments to correct the misclassification.
The Municipal Treasurer has adhered to the Auditor’s recommendation regarding the marking of unclaimed stale checks and the return of the original disbursement vouchers and supporting documents to the Accounting Office.
During the year, all stale checks were already reissued with the new check.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the following adjusting entry in their books of accounts: DuetoNGAs (Dr.)TrustLiabilities (Cr.) in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has already made the necessary adjusting entry in their books of accounts: reclassification related to this transaction.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the following adjusting entry in the GF books of accounts: AccountsPayable (Dr.) DuetoNGAs (Cr.) DuetoLGUs (Cr.) OtherPayables (Cr.) to correct the misclassification.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has already made the necessary adjusting entries related to these transactions.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
a.Instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary entries for the reversion of the total balance of P184,675.02 to the Unappropriated Surplus of the Municipality, with a condition that appropriation shall be made available in the event that claim arises in the future, and
b.Instruct the Municipal Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) and Municipal Accountant to submit the supporting documents pertaining to the details of the wages of Job Order’s (JO) Wages amounting to P1,074,491.76. Otherwise, make the necessary adjusting entry.
Henceforth, ensure compliance with the above-cited provisions of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal HRMO submitted certification that there’s no more unprocessed payrolls related to the JO’s. Hence, the Municipal Accountant made the necessary adjusting entry for this account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:
a.Adjust the amount to be returned to the Department of Agriculture (DA) pertaining to the erroneous charging of P42,346.72 of one DA project to another project; and
b.Ensure proper recording of the receipt and transfers of disbursement of funds of completed projects and project with lapse implementation period. Afterwards, we recommended the concerned Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Treasurer to remit the adjusted excess fund transfers of completed projects and project with lapse implementation period to the concerned agencies.
Henceforth, we recommended to strictly comply with the guidelines set in the above-mentioned Circular.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has already made the necessary correcting entries related to the misstatement noted. Additionally, the Municipal Treasurer has already returned the excess fund noted. Henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer will remit the adjusted excess fund transfers of completed projects and project with lapse implementation period to the concerned agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit the SORDs of the projects mentioned above to the SAs. Henceforth, ensure that SORDs for all fund transfers are prepared and submitted to the SAs in accordance with the COA Circular.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer have already ensured the timely submission of SORDs. SORDs are regularly submitted to the COA and Source Agency.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a.Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all CAs pertaining to the salaries and wages of JOs are liquidated within five days from the date of each CA in compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002; and
b.To consider the possibility and necessity of payment of salaries and wages through ATM.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer is strictly implementing controls to ensure that liquidations are made on time. Moreover, the Municipality of Mulanay has already started paying the salary of JO’s through ATM.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to issue AF No. 56 solely for acknowledging payment of RPT and ensure sufficient stocks of all AFs at all times. Henceforth comply with provisions of LTOM 2nd Edition in the issuance of AFs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer has already ensured AF No. 56 is issued solely for acknowledgement of payment of RPT. Henceforth comply with LTOM 2nd Edition in the issuance of AFs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Development Council (MDC) and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to ensure that all development projects to be funded out of the 20 percent DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready. Henceforth, comply with DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 in the appropriation and utilization of the 20 percent DF of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor directed the MDC and Municipal Planning and Development Officer to ensure all development projects to be funded out of the 20 percent DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready. Henceforth, comply with guidelines set in the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 in the appropriation and utilization of the 20 percent DF of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a.Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustment for the remaining unissued inventory; and
b.Municipality DRRM to submit at year-end inventory of undistributed food supplies.
Henceforth, strictly comply with the regulations on the proper recording of inventory for distribution.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has already made the necessary adjusting entry for the related transaction. Additionally, the inventory of undistributed food supplies at year-end were submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to instruct the OIC-General Service Officer (GSO) to:
a.Comply with the preparation and submission of the Consolidated PIF to the Audit Team not later than April 30 of each year; and
b.Ensure that all insurable properties of the municipality are adequately covered with the GIF of the GSIS.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-GSO was able to submit the Consolidated PIF to the Auditor before the prescribed deadline. However, she is still in the process of completing the documents required to insure all government properties.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct:
a.The Municipal HRMO to use the correct premium rate as stipulated in the above-mentioned Act; and
b.The Municipal Accountant:
   (1) To recompute the compensation and deduction recorded in the payroll to ensure the proper recording of accounts; and
   (2) To submit documents to support the claim that remittance for the January premiums was made on time and that the oversight lies with the bank; and
   (3) To subsequently ensure that remittances were posted by the bank at the time of the transaction.
c.The concerned offices to strengthen their internal control measures to establish effective checks and balances.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal HRMO ensured that the correct rates are used in remitting the PhilHealth Premiums.
The Municipal Accountant already conducted the recomputation of the compensation and the deductions of all employees and submitted the supporting evidence for this matter. Additionally, she ensured that remittances were posted by the bank at the time of the transaction.
All concerned offices implemented ways to strengthen their internal controls to establish effective checks and balances such as thorough checking/review of the financial transactions before processing the same with the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality Mayor direct the designated GSO to regularly monitor the expiration of all motor vehicles registration and its timely renewal/registration to avoid penalties and fines as well as undue risk. Otherwise, any surcharges, interests, penalties and expenses that may be incurred will be the personal liability of the GSO who is responsible for the delay or non-registration of the vehicles.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-GSO exhausted all efforts to retrieve any, if any, related to the unregistered vehicles and in continuous coordination with the Land Transportation Officer (LTO).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the following adjusting entry in the General Fund (GF) books of Accounts: AccountsPayable 578,519.37 LeaveBenefitsPayable 99,225.82 OtherPayables 479,293.55
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made the following adjusting entry in thru Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 100-2024-04-00528 dated April 11,2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. Municipal Treasurer to immediately transfer from GF to TF P125,728.62 representing 20 percent LGU share from fire code fees from the GF to the appropriate TF account; and b. Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjusting entries on the GF and TF of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Transfer of fund account was made on April 24, 2024. Adjusting entry was made thru JEV No. 300-2024-04-00072 dated April 15, 2024. The Municipality complied with the aforementioned laws, rules and recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and maintain the Cashbook - Cash Advances and ensure to accomplish certification in all cashbooks at the end of each month. Henceforth, strictly comply with Sections 26, 27 and 28 of the MNGAS, Volume II.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer maintains Cashbook - Cash Advances and accomplish its monthly certification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Treasurer require the Collecting Officer to: (a) Ensure that cashbook maintained by Accountable Officer/COs conform with the prescribed format and instructions provided in the LTOM and (b) Accomplish the certification in cashbook at the end of each month. Henceforth, strictly comply with Item C.2a, Section 90 of Local Treasury Operations Manual 2nd Edition.
observations.managementAction: The Collecting Officers accomplish their Cashbook with the necessary details and certify it every month.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• consider the One-time Cleansing of PPE accounts instead pursuant to procedural guidelines prescribed by Commission on Audit (COA) Circular 2020-006, specifically, Item 6.3.2 e of the said Circular, to write-off/drop from the books of accounts the remaining balances which were created to facilitate the conversion of accounts from the Old Government Accounting System to New Government Accounting System (NGAS) to come up with reliable PPE balances;
• instruct the Municipal Treasurer to notify/inform the Audit Team on the schedule of the physical inventory count of the Municipality to be able to allow the AT to witness/attest the conduct of the physical inventory count; and
• instruct the Municipal Treasurer to refrain from using the forms prescribed by Government Accounting Manual (GAM) for Local Government Units (LGUs) until such time that the deferral has been lifted.
observations.managementAction:
Notification was made through letter dated November 29, 2024 requesting authorize representative to witness physical count.
The submitted Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) on February 5, 2025 adheres to the prescribed format of Manual on New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for LGUs, Volume II.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Accountable Officers to ensure that all cash advances pertaining to the salaries and wages of Job Orders and Contract of Service are liquidated within five days from the date of each cash advance in compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002.
We also recommended the Municipal Mayor to revisit the possibility and necessity of payment of salaries and wages of Job Orders and Contract of Service through Automated Teller Machine (ATM) as internal control to prevent delayed liquidation or unliquidated cash advances.
observations.managementAction:
Monitoring of the liquidation of cash advances was made. Relatively, the liquidation period for cash advances ranges from three to five days.
Payments of salaries and wages for job orders and contracts of service were made through an Auto-Debit Arrangement (ADA) with the Authorized Government Depository Bank (AGDB), except for the four noted retired uniformed personnel who received their compensation via cash payroll since an ATM cannot be issued to them due to their ineligibility under the current banking policies and account requirements of the AGDB.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Human Resource Management Officer to coordinate with the PhilHealth Regional Offices (PROs) for the enrollment of JOs/COS employed by the Municipality under Group Enrolment Scheme.
observations.managementAction: A contract agreement dated February 5, 2025, was signed for the enrollment, registration, orientation of JOs/COS personnel under a Group Enrollment Scheme. Consequently, for CY 2025, deductions and remittances were made and are being regularly transmitted to PhilHealth in accordance with the prescribed guidelines.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• Direct the Municipal Treasurer to comply with the procedures in MNGAS for LGUs pertaining to reporting of disbursements/checks issued; and
• Develop and implement internal control measures to enable all undelivered checks in the custody of the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to be communicated to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer.
Henceforth, ensure that unreleased checks as of year-end are reported as part of the Cash in bank account in compliance with the existing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Implemented The Municipal Treasurer complied with the procedures on the reporting of checks issued.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entry to capitalize the fabrication and installation of glass partition and swing door in the amount of P50,000.00; and
• Chairperson of the Committee on Inventory for the Physical Count of PPE to:
  ➢ Report all PPE items counted physically and owned by the Municipality;
  ➢ Prepare the RPCPPE using the prescribed format; and
  ➢ Notify/inform the Audit Team on the schedule of the physical inventory count of the physical inventory of the Municipality to be able to allow the Audit Team to witness/attest the conduct of the physical inventory count.
Henceforth, ensure the reconciliation of the PPE balances presented in the RPCPPE and the accounting records.
observations.managementAction: Implemented The Municipal Accountant recorded the capitalization of PPE through Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 2024-01-05369 dated . The RPCPPE for CY 2024 was prepared using the prescribed format. The RPCPPE committee have no alternate from the Supply Office, which greatly affects the implementation of the inventory activity.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry to be able to present the correct Cash-Local Treasury and Cash in Bank balances at year-end; and
• Direct the Municipal Treasurer to observe proper reporting of deposits in the Cashbook.
observations.managementAction:

• JEV No. 100-2024-01-05397
• JEV No. 200-2024-01-00270
• JEV No. 300-2024-01-00182
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to observe proper reporting of deposits in the Cashbook.

observations.recommendation:

Debit: Accounts Payable – 108,275.00
Credit: Other Payables – 108,275.00

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
• Submit to the Audit Team proof that goods and services are accepted or rendered and supplier/creditor bills are received for the remaining claims listed in the Accounts Payable under the General Fund (GF) and Special Education Fund (SEF) amounting to P325,276.11 and P72,789.00, respectively; and
• Provide the breakdown/schedule of the Accounts Payable Beginning Balance- Municipal Treasurer under the SEF to determine its particularity and validity. Otherwise, revert the same to the unappropriated surplus of the GF as it constitutes accounts payable with no actual claim filed or not covered by perfected contracts.
Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that recording of Accounts Payable is in accordance with Section 4(s) of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Section 47 of PD 1177.
observations.managementAction: Implemented The Municipal Accountant was able to submit proof that goods and services were accepted or rendered. The Municipal Accountant was able to provide the schedules for the balance of Accounts Payable under SEF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer to provide the Audit Team with justification for the disbursements totaling P175,041.24 as well as for any Activity Designs for any Activity linked to programs/projects/activities allowed to be charged against the 20% Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: Implemented The Municipal Budget Officer was able to provide the Audit Team with justification for the disbursement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Head of the Requesting Units and the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that the Obligation Requests are prepared in the prescribed format and are provided with all the necessary details.
observations.managementAction: Implemented The Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to:
• Submit the monthly CRAAF using the prescribed format and accompanied by the individual RAAF of the Accountable Officers; and
• Report all procured Accountable Forms Receipts.
observations.managementAction: Implemented The Municipality complied with the submission of the monthly CRAAF using the prescribed format accompanied by the individual RAAF of the Accountable Officers; and reported all procured Accountable Forms Receipts. The Municipal Treasurer complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• Require the Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) to execute Income Payor/Withholding Agent’s Sworn Declaration and submit the same to the BIR, and ensure compliance with execution of Income Payor/Withholding Agent’s Sworn Declaration (Annex 'C') stating the number of payees who shall not be subjected to withholding taxes and have duly submitted their income payee’s sworn declarations and copies of COR, and submit the same to the concerned BIR office on or before the last day of January of each year or on the fifteenth (15th) day of the following month when a new income recipient submitted the payee’s sworn declaration; and
• Require the Municipal Accountant to withhold the prescribed withholding tax from all JOs/COS earning P250,000.00 and below in any 12-month period who was unable to comply with the requirements for tax exemption.
observations.managementAction: Implemented The Management complied with the execution and submission to the HRMO of all JOs/COS income payee’s sworn declaration of gross receipts in accordance with the format per Annex B-2 of Section 3 of RR No. 11-2018. The Municipal Accountant complied with the recommendation to withhold the prescribed withholding tax where applicable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Chairperson, GAD Focal Point System to adopt necessary measures to ensure that the Municipality’s GPBs are submitted to the Audit Team within five working days from receipt of DILG-approved GPBs.
observations.managementAction: Implemented The Municipality was able to submit the GPB for CY 2025 within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation:

• GAD Focal Person to monitor the implementation of the DILG-approved GAD Plan and Budget as well as the utilization of the Fund of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Implemented The Municipal Budget Officer complied with the recommendation. The GAD Focal Person complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
• Make the necessary entries for the reversion of the total balance of P837,220.64 to the Unappropriated Surplus of the GF of the Municipality with a condition that appropriation shall be made available in the event that claim arises in the future; and
• Provide the details of the Accounts Payable recorded under SEF.
Henceforth, ensure proper monitoring of the Accounts Payable account so that claims nearing the maturity of two years are communicated to the respective payees for the processing of their claims.
observations.managementAction: Not Only Implemented P760,177.97 was reverted to the Unappropriated Surplus of the GF per JEV No. 100-2023-03-1050 dated March 31, 2023. The Municipal Accountant provided the details of the Accounts Payable recorded under SEF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer to continue exerting extra effort to facilitate the immediate titling of the four parcels of lot valued at P301,100.00 in the name of the Municipality and to determine/identify the unidentified municipal lots worth P5,801,641.81 either through the reconciliation of the accounting records and the property records as well as with the records of the Municipal Assessor to avoid possible third party claims that may arise in the future, and for the fair presentation of the Land account in the financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: Not Only Implemented The old cemetery valued P2,539,631.81 remained untitled aside from the four newly purchased parcels of lot totaling P1,227,720.00 in CY 2023. The Municipal Assessor, the Municipal General Services Officer and the Assessor's Office continued exerting extra effort to facilitate immediate titling of the four parcels of lots and property records determination/identification of unidentified municipal lots. The full implementation of this recommendation will be determined at year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: • Municipal Treasurer to prepare the necessary adjusting entries in his cashbook; and • Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries for the erroneous recording of Check Number 2773098 in the General Fund book by debiting Prior Period Adjustment and crediting Cash in Bank by P30,000.00.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer prepared the adjusting entries in his cashbook. The Municipal Accountant prepare the adjusting entry in the General Fund book by debiting Prior Period Adjustment and crediting Cash in Bank by P30,000.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: • Make the necessary adjustments to reclassify 15 completed projects totaling P2,245,672.33 to their respective PPE accounts; and • Coordinate with the Municipal Engineer in verifying the status of the three projects with total amount of P770,040.94, for its proper disposition in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already reclassified the 15 completed projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MPDO to ensure that all development projects to be funded out of the 20% DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready.
observations.managementAction: Henceforth, comply with guidelines set in the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: • Ensure timely submission of all DV for Confidential and/or Intelligence Funds within seven (7) days from release of the check, in accordance with Item 6.1.8.4 of COA-DBM-DILG-DND-GCG Joint Circular No. 2015-01; and • Submit the 4th Quarter DV for Confidential and/or Intelligence Fund with complete required supporting documents to COA Audit Team Leader (ATL).
observations.managementAction: Timely submission of DVs for Confidential/Intelligence Funds was observed for CY 2024. The 4th Quarter DV for Confidential and/or Intelligence Fund with complete supporting documents was already submitted to COA ATL.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to submit the CRAAF within the prescribed period which is not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month.

We reiterated our recommendation that the former Municipal Treasurer to submit the Disbursement Vouchers and supporting documents and Reports of Disbursements to facilitate reconciliation and to be able to account, once and for all, the correct amount due from them.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: • MGSO - Designate and the Municipal Accountant to pursue the reconciliation of the accounting with property records in order to establish the correct inventory balance and adjust any discrepancy that will be noted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MA to draw an adjusting entry to reclassify the semi-expendable properties to the related PPE accounts and submit to the Audit Team for validation. Henceforth, follow the new capitalization threshold of newly acquired PPE and other guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 2022-004 in the issuance of semi-expendable items and its recording in the books of accounts and other records.

Henceforth, follow the new capitalization threshold of newly acquired PPE and other guidelines set forth in the COA Circular No. 2022-004 in the issuance of semi-expendable items and its recording in the books of accounts and other records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MT to: a. Compel the MT to prepare the Reports on Collections and Deposits (RCDs) pursuant to the provisions set forth in Section 49 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II; and b. Provide the Collecting Officers a separate cashbook in accordance with Items a, b, and c of Section 181 of GAAM, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The MT had already prepared the Reports on Collections and Deposits as set forth in Section 49 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II. A separate cashbook was provided to Collecting Officers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant to: a. Provide the breakdown of the unreconciled balance totaling P316,968.41 which relates to the rejected remittances in the GSIS System; 2) the list of employees with paid loans wherein deductions should have been stopped but were not refunded; and 3) the unremitted contributions totaling P13,281.39 for those employees with no Business Partner (BP) Number with the GSIS System, to the HRD for the preparation of the ARA, and immediately remit the same to the GSIS; and b. Refund the balance to concerned employees for the incorrect deductions.
observations.managementAction:
a. The schedule of remittances is prepared by the HRMO, as they are responsible for managing payroll and are knowledgeable about employees with active or paid loans to avoid over deduction of loan payments from employees.
b. The Human Resource Department (HRD) will promptly create a schedule for the fully paid loans to stop loan payment deductions; and will immediately refund any over deduction to employees.
c. Agency Remittance Advice (ARA) for the said changes in status and salary rate will be immediately prepared by the HRD, through the designated agency authorized officer, to update the records of the GSIS.
d. Monthly reconciliation of amounts withheld and remitted to the GSIS by the Accounting Department to include schedule per employee of any unremitted contributions to be sent to the HRD for ARA, if necessary.
We also recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant to: a. Provide the breakdown of the unreconciled balance totaling P316,968.41 which relates to the rejected remittances in the GSIS System; 2) the list of employees with paid loans wherein deductions should have been stopped but were not refunded; and 3) the unremitted contributions totaling P13,281.39 for those employees with no Business Partner (BP) Number with the GSIS System, to the HRD for the preparation of the ARA, and immediately remit the same to the GSIS; and b. Refund the balance to concerned employees for the incorrect deductions.
observations.reason: Partial schedule was prepared by the HRMO. Not Tracing of documents Implemented is ongoing. Refunds and remittance of contributions have not yet been made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to monitor the maintenance of the CO’s cashbook conforming to Items a, b, and c of Section 181 of GAAM, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The COs maintain separate cashbooks.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to: a. Ensure that all development projects to be funded out of the 20 percent DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready; and b. Henceforth, comply with the guidelines set in Department of Budget and Management-Department of Finance-Department of the Interior and Local Government– (DBM-DOF-DILG) Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 1 in the appropriation and utilization of the 20 percent DF of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality prioritized the implementation of projects funded from continuing appropriations.
observations.reason: Some projects were implemented in the succeeding year, while other funds were reverted and reprogrammed for other projects. The MPDC will ensure that the LGU will abide with JMC No. 1

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Accountant to exert efforts in identifying the PPE without details from the records and Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) of prior years and coordinate with the Municipal Engineer for the identification of the recorded infrastructure assets; b. Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit to the AT a complete reconciled Schedule of Accumulated Depreciation corresponding the balances in the General Ledger (GL) for review; c. Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the regulation on depreciation; and d. Municipal Accountant to maintain complete PPELC of the recorded PPE.
observations.reason: Maintenance of PPELC was not prioritized. Depreciation for CY 2024 was recorded only at year-end instead of monthly. Completion of details of PPE is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: • Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer to continue their efforts in locating and securing the documents that will support the land ownership of the Municipality; • Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the titling of the lots once ownership by the Municipality is determined to avoid possible third-party claims and incurrence of litigation costs in the future; • Municipal Assessor to facilitate the transfer of tax declaration of the seven purchased lot from former owner and ensure that all lots owned by the Municipality have tax declaration in its name, after its title is transferred in the name of the Municipality; and • Appraisal Committee/Team to properly appraise all lots that were acquired through donation as to their fair values at the time of acquisition.
observations.reason: Requested the assistance of the LRA for the status of various parcels; titling in process; appraisal of donated parcels was not prioritized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Officer in Charge (OIC) - Municipal Agriculturist to facilitate the implementation of the project and ensure its completion. We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer facilitate the reversion or remittance of unutilized funds and ensure proper recording of shares and fees to the appropriate income accounts.
observations.managementAction: The fund for the project 'Establishment of the Demo Site-Organic Farming: amounting P100,000.00 was reverted to the Department of the Agriculture Region IV-A while the project 'Dairy Production' amounting to P300,000.00 was implemented this CY 2024. Building permit/other fees were addressed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Agriculturist, to plan and adopt effective collection strategies to recover the loaned funds from the borrowers such as offering payment alternatives adjusted to fit the borrowers’ financial capacity and continuous monitoring and follow-up; and b. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records to establish the correct balance of the Other Receivables account.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to maintain a supplementary record that will facilitate the monitoring of individual loan balance and preparation of supporting schedule at any given time.
observations.managementAction: a. Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with Municipal Agriculturist, to plan and adopt effective collection strategies to recover the loaned funds from the borrowers such as offering payment alternatives adjusted to fit the borrowers’ financial capacity and continuous monitoring and follow-up; and b. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records to establish the correct balance of the Other Receivables account. Maintained a supplementary record.
observations.reason: Receivables totaling P2,341,807.42 are still uncollected as of December 31, 2024 due to the insolvency of most of its borrowers. The Municipal Accountant already traced the discrepancy from their office records and submitted the working paper to the Mun. Treasurer for the reconciliation on their records. No update from the Mun. Treasurer on the status of their reconciliation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entry in the books of the Municipality for CY 2023 by debiting cash and crediting the appropriate income/liability/equity accounts totaling P93,680.09; and
b. Municipal Treasurer and the RCOs to remit daily collections at the close of each business day.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already prepared the necessary adjusting entries in the books of the Municipality for CY 2023 by debiting cash and crediting the appropriate income/liability/equity accounts totaling P93,680.09. The Municipal Treasurer already issued a memorandum to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to remit daily collections at the close of each business day and to strictly adhere to Section 29 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office (MSWDO) to ensure the timely liquidation of the funds received from the National Government Agencies and comply with the provisions of the Memorandum of Agreement.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive (LCE) Instructed the Municipal Accountant will oversee all matters related liquidation of funds from NGAs.

For Calendar Year (CY) 2024 the LGU Plaridel have no disbursements pertaining to CF.
