observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year audit recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Development Council (MDC) and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to:
a. Ensure that all development projects to be funded out of the 20 percent DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready; and
b. Henceforth, comply with guidelines set in the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 in the appropriation and utilization of the 20 percent DF of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: All projects were implemented as of December 31, 2024. The MDC and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer ensures compliance with guidelines set in the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 in the appropriation and utilization of the 20 percent DF of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRMO) to carefully study the feasibility of the PPAs in accordance with the four thematic areas of disaster risk reduction and management before its inclusion in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan to fully utilize the appropriated amounts, reduce disaster risk and adequately prepare for any imminent disaster/calamity; and
b. Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) to regularly monitor and evaluate the implementation of the LDRRMFIP and instruct the MDRRMO for its revision to include alternative PPAs for those that cannot be implemented during the year, if necessary.
observations.managementAction: Appropriations for the LDRRMF for CY 2024 was fully utilized. The MDRRMO prepares and submits monthly LDRRMF utilization report to the LCE for review.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: • Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare year-end adjusting entries to revert the unreleased checks totaling P4,363,442.54 to the cash in bank account and recognize the corresponding liability/payables account; • Direct the Municipal Treasurer to comply with the procedures in MNGAS for LGUs, pertaining to reporting of disbursements/ checks issued; and • Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that check recording of check disbursements will only be done after receiving RCI with DVs and supporting documents from the Municipal Treasurer, to avoid inclusion of unreleased checks as reconciling items in the Bank Reconciliation Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared the adjusting entries through Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) Nos. 100-2024-01-00121 and 100-2024-01-00130 both dated January 30, 2024, to revert the unreleased checks to the cash in bank account and recognize the corresponding liabilities/payables account. The Municipal Treasurer ensured to comply with the procedures in MNGAS for LGUs. The Municipal Accountant ensured that check recording of check disbursements only be done after receiving RCI with DVs and supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct: • The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the bank for possible identification of the source of the identified reconciling items and for its proper recording in the books of accounts maintained by the Municipal Accountant, and in the cashbooks maintained by the Municipal Treasurer; and • The Municipal Accountant to record the check reorder fee amounting to P15,000.00 upon securing the supporting document from the Municipal Treasurer.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer coordinated with the bank for the identification of the source of the identified reconciling items and, upon confirming the source, secured the supporting documents to ensure validity of the transactions. The necessary adjusting entries were prepared: JEV No. 100-2024-03-00701 to record the deposit amounting to P389.82 and JEV No. 100-2024-03-00598 to record the check reorder fee amounting to P15,000.00. Henceforth, ensure that all valid reconciling items reflected in the bank reconciliation statements were properly supported with documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: • Prepare the necessary adjusting entries in the books of accounts for the misclassified liabilities and provide the Audit Team with copies of the Journal Entry Vouchers, for verification in audit; and • Review the status of the noted transactions totaling P181,554.00 recorded in Other Payables account, and make the necessary adjusting entries for its proper disposition and/or classification in the books of accounts and financial statements of the Municipality. (Specific reclassification JEV entries shown in prior AAR were to be made for General Fund and Special Education Fund.) Also, instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the supporting documents for the above-mentioned claims/transactions for further validation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant immediately prepared the necessary adjusting entries, excluding those which are already paid as of April 25, 2025, through various JEVs. Copies of which were provided to the Audit Team, with list of payables already paid as of that date, for verification. Regarding the transactions for transfer to Trust Liabilities account and other items, adjusting entries were prepared and JEVs provided to the Audit Team for verification. The Municipal Accountant submitted supporting documents via electronic mail on April 26, 2024, which were verified and found in order.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain a special account for 20% Development Fund separately under the General Fund in compliance with the provisions of RA No. 7160 and Chapter 6 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I and to facilitate monitoring of the utilization of the fund.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already maintained separate subsidiary account for 20% Development Fund and submitted the corresponding reports/records. The corresponding reports were submitted to the Audit Team and the consolidated financial statements of the Municipality were provided in compliance with the provisions of RA No. 7160 and Chapter 6 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Acting Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to expedite the preparation of all necessary documents, proceed with the appraisal and prioritize the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties to prevent further deterioration of the same and to realize additional income from the proceeds of saleable items. Afterwards, provide the Municipal Accountant with the copy of completed Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) to support the dropping of disposed PPEs in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Out of the balance of unserviceable properties from CY 2023 AAR totaling P1,149,531.93, PPE items with total book value of P1,030,777.10 were already disposed of through sale and destruction in CY 2024 per copies of IIRUP provided to the Municipal Accountant and to the Audit Team for verification. PPE items not yet disposed of amounting to P13,466.59 are still pending disposal and are currently in the custody of the end-user.
observations.reason: PPE items not yet disposed of amounting to P13,466.59 remain undisposed and are currently in the custody of the end-user.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant (MA) to record unpaid terminal leave benefits as at year-end in the Leave Benefits Payable account and correct the error in recording terminal leave benefits as AP. Moreover, furnish the Audit Team copy of the adjusting entries made for validation purposes.
observations.managementAction: The MA had adjusted the same under Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 100-2024-01-09001 dated March 31, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: a. Make the adjusting entry by debiting the CIB – LCCA and crediting the AP accounts both by the amount of checks still unreleased; and b. Strictly comply with Section 43 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I by ensuring that only checks released to the claimants are recorded in the Check Disbursements to avoid understating both the CIB-LCCA and the AP accounts.
observations.managementAction: The unreleased checks as of December 31, 2023 were all claimed at the end of the 1st quarter. The Management ensured compliance with Section 43 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was able to immediately reclassify Municipal Construction in Progress (CIP) items to their appropriate asset accounts and provide the related depreciation expense. • Draw the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balance of CIP accounts;

• Ensure that Depreciation expenses are properly recognized in accordance with PAG 3 of IPSAS 17.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• Enjoin the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to avoid including PPAs under the 20 percent Development Fund with potential issues especially on right-of-way (ROW);
• Direct the MPDC to indicate the target implementation schedule of PPAs under the 20 percent Development Fund to increase organizational coordination, effectiveness and efficiency; and
• Ensure that PPAs funded by the 20 percent Development Fund are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready, in accordance with Item 3.2.3 of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1, s. 2020.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has created a Land Acquisition Committee to avoid potential issues on ROW under the 20 percent Development Fund. The Municipality has provided the schedule for the implementation of PPAs under the 20 percent Development Fund. The Municipality has created the Land Acquisition Committee to avoid potential issues on ROW under the 20 percent Development Fund. The Municipality provided the schedule for the implementation of PPAs under 20 percent DF.
observations.reason: Projects for CY 2024 funded by the 20 percent projects with total appropriations of P18,100,000.00 were non-procurement-and-implementation ready.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• Ensure that a more robust policy on the payment of JO wages is developed and strictly implemented, to enable the timely CA liquidation; 
• Instruct the Municipal Accountant to adopt appropriate strategies to ensure that new CAs are not granted to officials with unliquidated CAs; and
• Consider the payment of JO wages via ATMs, to decrease, if not eliminate, the need for multiple CAs to be drawn by disbursing officers.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had formulated an internal policy over granting cash advances and avoid the incurrence of overlapping CAs; The Municipality is now paying their JO wages through ATMs, to decrease, if not eliminate, the need for multiple CAs. The Municipality had formulated an internal policy over granting cash advances and avoid the incurrence of overlapping CAs. The Municipality is now paying their JO wages through ATMs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
• Submit the supporting documents submitted the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents submitted the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents of Accounts Payable settled in 2024, to justify inclusion in the Accounts Payable balance; 
• Draw the necessary adjusting entries to revert Accounts Payable totaling P2,250,336.90 to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund; and
• Adopt appropriate internal strategies and mechanisms that would facilitate the timely completion of documents by end-users and their submission to the Accounting Office for processing the payment of end Accounts Payable and a more effective monitoring of Accounts Payable, to minimize, if not eliminate, the existence of Accounts Payable outstanding for more than two years; and
• Ensure that the JEVs prepared to record year-end Accounts Payable are supported with documents evidencing the receipt and acceptance of goods and services.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already submitted the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents of Accounts Payable settled in 2024. The Municipal Accountant already submitted the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents of Accounts Payable settled in 2024. The Municipal Accountant secured duplicate copies of Obligation Requests, Inspection and Delivery Receipts, Purchase Orders of transactions which were attached to the JEV of Accounts Payable for year-end accruals.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• Municipal DRRM Officer (MDRRMO) to include the list of PPAs charged to Special Trust Fund (STF) in LDRRMFIP under a separate caption, in compliance with Item 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002; 
• MDRRMO to indicate a more specific implementation schedule in LDRRMFIP; and 
• Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to adopt appropriate strategies that would minimize, if not eliminate, the need for reconciliation of budget and accounting records that may affect the timeliness of the preparation of monthly utilization reports; and ensure that the monthly utilization reports are submitted within 15 days of the ensuing month, in accordance with Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has provided a more specific implementation schedule in LDRRMFIP. Reconciliation of budget and accounting records are done monthly by the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer. The monthly utilization reports were submitted within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended, and the Municipal Mayor and the Gender and Development (GAD) Focal Person agreed, to adopt necessary measures to ensure that the LGU’s GPBs are submitted to the Audit Team within five working days from receipt of DILG-approved GPBs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has submitted the DILG-Approved GAD Plan and Budget for CY 2025 on August 5, 2024, three days after its receipt.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit to the Audit Team the CRAAF for CY 2022 accompanied by the individual RAAFs of the Accountable Officers. observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer was able to immediately submit to the Audit Team the CRAAF for CY 2022 accompanied by the individual RAAFs. Henceforth, the Office of the Municipal Treasurer submitted the CRAAF for CY 2024 within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Agriculturist to:
• Put into writing the change in the agreement as regards payment schedule to protect the interest of the Municipality and ensure collectability of the amount of P51,000.00 from the farmer-beneficiaries due at the planting time; and
• Continue to monitor the collection of the fifty percent of the price per seedlings due for payment at planting time.
observations.managementAction: The Agricultural Office has been continuously monitoring and reminding the beneficiaries as to their obligation to pay their respective purchases of macapuno seedlings. From 2022 to 2024, there was a total collection paid to the Municipal Treasurer amounting to Thirty-Nine Thousand Five Hundred Pesos (P39,500.00). The Municipal Agriculturist will request also this for collection. The Municipal Agriculturist put into writing the change in the agreement as regards payment schedule and will continue to monitor the collection. The Municipal Agriculture Office is continuously monitoring and reminding the beneficiaries as to their obligation to pay their respective purchases of macapuno seedlings. As of December 2024, the total number of macapuno trees left is One Hundred Eighty Two (182) trees.
observations.reason: For first recommendation, the farmer beneficiaries already signed a new agreement last September 2022, stating that they will start paying once their macapuno trees start to bear fruits. As of date, some collections were made; remaining balance uncollected.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that
(a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entries to revert the unreleased checks totaling P2,724,429.57 to recognize the payees account;
(b) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that only those checks released to claimants shall be reported in the Report of Checks Issued (RCI); and
(c) Develop and implement internal control measures to enable all reconciling items in the custody of the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to be communicated to the Office of the Municipal Accountant, to avoid inclusion of unreleased checks as reconciling items in the BRS.
observations.managementAction:
a) The Municipal Accountant did not make the necessary adjustments as these were corrected when the checks were issued to the payees and cleared in the bank.
b) The Municipal Treasurer ensured that only checks released to payees were recorded in the RCI.
c) Unreleased checks were not included in the BRS but were recorded by the Municipal Accountant in the payable accounts as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Planning and Development (MPDC) to ensure that all development projects to be funded out of the 20% Development Fund are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready in accordance with Item 3.2.3 of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1.

observations.managementAction: All projects were implemented for CY 2024. Compliance with guidelines set in DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 has been observed; implementation of projects was undertaken in 2024.

observations.recommendation: a. Immediately prepare the CDP and LDIP showing the linkage of the listed PPAs in the AIP/budget, which include the proposed 20 percent DF PPAs for CY 2023, and submit the same to the Audit Team; and b. Henceforth, comply with guidelines set in the DILG-NEDA-DBM-DOF Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 dated November 18, 2016.
observations.managementAction: Submitted CDP and LDIP showing the LDIP and linkage to AIP and budget for CY 2024 and submitted same to the Commission on Audit for CY 2024.

observations.recommendation: Require the MDRRMO to carefully study the feasibility of the projects/activities before its inclusion in the LDRRMF Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) to ensure its implementation for the Municipality to be adequately prepared on any upcoming disaster/calamity and thereby reduce its risk. Henceforth, ensure the implementation of all planned disaster PPAs included in the MDRRMP particularly those included in the 70 percent LDRRMF-MF in accordance with RA No. 10121.
observations.managementAction: More than 90 percent was implemented for CY 2024. Implementation of planned disaster PPAs included in the MDRRMP, particularly those in the 70 percent LDRRMF-MF, was carried out.

observations.recommendation: a. To undertake disposition procedures for the non-existing/missing assets prescribed in Section 7.0 of COA Circular No. 2020-006.
observations.managementAction: Conducted investigation and procedures of disposition for the non-existing/missing assets.

observations.recommendation: a. Determine whether the amount of P5,307,261.16 are valid claims of the suppliers, otherwise make the necessary entries for its reversion to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund, with condition that General Fund appropriation shall be made available in the event that claim arises in the future.
observations.managementAction: Determined the validity of the claims and made necessary adjustments for reversion to the Unappropriated General Fund (CY 2023).

observations.recommendation: Municipal Treasurer and the RCOs to remit daily collections at close of each business day as per Section 29 of MNGAS for LGUs. Municipal Mayor requires the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entry.
observations.managementAction: Remittance directives issued; verification indicates some collectors were unable to remit regularly; actions to ensure remittance have been undertaken and will be evaluated in subsequent audit.
observations.reason: Some collections were still not deposited intact with authorized depositories for CY 2022; untimely remittances occurred.

observations.recommendation: a. Liquidation of the Advances to Special Disbursing Officer amounting to P24,450.00; b. Concerned Accountable Officers and Employees to immediately liquidate the long outstanding CAs totaling P235,768.88; and ensure that each CA is reported as soon as the purpose for which it was given has been served and that all CAs are fully liquidated at the end of each year in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: Liquidation of long outstanding CAs: Concerned employees have already liquidated their CAs totaling P108,748.88. The Municipal Accountant issued certifications and adjustments; one employee has not yet liquidated her account and explanation requested; some balances proposed for write-off pending revision of SB Resolution 66-2024.
observations.reason: Some long outstanding CAs remain unliquidated; pending explanations and proposed write-offs awaiting Sangguniang Bayan action.

observations.recommendation: a. Municipal Assessor to hasten the titling of the 26 parcels of land in the name of the Municipality of San Francisco; and b. Henceforth, comply with Section 50 of the MNGAS for LGU, Volume I and Section 449 of Government Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM), Volume II.
observations.managementAction: Three (3) out of the twenty-six (26) untitled LGU owned real properties are currently under processing for land titling. Retrieval of documents is still ongoing for the remaining parcels.
observations.reason: Three out of 26 parcels are being processed; retrieval of documents for remaining parcels still ongoing; full compliance not yet realized.

observations.recommendation: Require MDRRMO to carefully study feasibility of projects/activities before inclusion in MDRRMP to ensure implementable projects and reduce disaster risk. Henceforth, ensure implementation of planned disaster PPAs.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2024, more than 95 percent of projects were implemented. MDRRMO implemented planned projects included in the MDRRMP.

observations.recommendation: Municipal Mayor direct MDC and MPDC to: a. Ensure that all PPAs funded out of 20 percent DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready; b. Henceforth, comply with DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 in appropriation and utilization of 20 percent DF.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2024, all PPAs were implemented; compliance with DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 observed.

observations.recommendation: Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile with the Provincial Accounting Office the balances of the Due from LGUs and Due to LGUs accounts between the Municipality and the Provincial Government and make the adjusting entries in the books of accounts of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Reconciled accounts with the Provincial Government; adjustments made as necessary and entries made in the books.

observations.recommendation: Municipal Mayor instruct Municipal Accountant to establish breakdown of the aforementioned Inter-Agency Payables accounts and remit due amounts to BIR, Pag-IBIG, GSIS and PhilHealth; instruct HRMO to secure necessary GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth employee numbers for remittance of new employees.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant established breakdown for Inter-Agency Payables and continues reconciliation; coordination with GSIS Lucena branch ongoing to obtain historical billings; necessary adjusting entries will be prepared to correct unremitted balances; HRMO secured GSIS, Pag-IBIG, PhilHealth employee numbers for remittance.
observations.reason: Reconciliation with GSIS ongoing; awaiting historical billings from GSIS Head Office; some accounts require write-off requests or further reconciliation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Require the preparation of RCD pursuant to the provisions set forth in Section 49 of MNGAS, Volume II; and
b. Monitor the maintenance of the collector’s cashbook conforming to items in Section 181 of GAAM, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor required the COs to prepare their reports on time. Cashbooks were prepared by the COs and monitored by the liquidating officer.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry in the books of the Municipality for CY 2023 by debiting cash and crediting the appropriate income/liability/equity accounts totaling P1,233,393.81; and
b. Municipal Treasurer and the RCOs to strictly comply with Section 29 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I and to remit daily collections at the end of each work day.
observations.managementAction: The proper adjusting entry was made in January 2024. The Municipality developed proper internal control measures to make sure that collections are remitted to the Municipal Treasurer (MT) at the end of each work day.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the following adjusting entry in the General Fund books of accounts:
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entry was made on March 2024. Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 101-2024-03-0348

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Development Council and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to:
a. Ensure that all development projects to be funded out of the 20 percent DF are well-ready, planned and procurement-and-implementation ready; and
b. Henceforth, comply with guidelines set in the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 in the appropriation and utilization of the 20 percent DF of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: In 2024, the Municipality was able to implement 75 percent of their 20 percent DF and the remaining unimplemented were understandably justified.
observations.reason: In 2024, the Municipality was able to implement 75 percent of their 20 percent DF and the remaining unimplemented were understandably justified.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMO to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF to the Audit Team within the prescribed period which is on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO complied with the submission of the Report on Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF to the Audit Team within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MT to proceed with the titling of the land using the documents available on file that was presented to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The MT coordinated with the concerned National Government Agencies (NGAs) in updating documents and requirements.
observations.reason: Not Due to difficulty in gathering documents for the titling.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to commence and undergo the One-Time cleansing of PPE account balances as prescribed by Commission on Audit Circular No. 2020-006 to come up with reliable PPE balances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality created the Inventory, Disposal and Appraisal Committee under Executive Order (EO No. 60-2022). One-time cleansing of PPE commenced on July 23, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) to ensure that all development projects to be funded out of the 20% Development Fund are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready. Henceforth, comply with DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% DF of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: For Calendar Year (CY) 2024, the MPDO and Municipal Engineer were able to plan and implement the development projects under 20% DF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Accountable Officers to ensure that all cash advances pertaining to the salaries and wages of JOs, and COS are liquidated within five (5) days from the date of each cash advance in compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: The Accountable Officers were able to liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GAD Focal Person to adopt necessary measure to ensure that: • Municipality’s GPBs are submitted to the Audit Team within five working days from the receipt of the DILG-approved GPBs; and • GAD ARs are submitted to the Audit Team within five working days after the end of January of the preceding year.
observations.managementAction: Municipality’s GPBs are submitted to the Audit Team within five (5) working days from the receipt of the DILG-approved GPBs. GADFP submitted the GAD AR to the Audit Team within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: • instruct the Municipal Engineer (ME) and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to submit the list of all on-going government PPA and those that are to be implemented during the year and its monthly monitoring report within the prescribed period; • inform the Audit Team of posting of appropriate project signboards and/or public notices 10 days upon award of infrastructure projects and start of the program/activity; and • Henceforth, submit the above required list within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality submitted the List of on-going PPAs and those to be implemented during the year within the prescribed period. The Municipality was able to comply with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entry in the books of accounts to reclassify the aforementioned balance totaling P1,843,568.35 as Due to GOCCs.
observations.managementAction: Reclassified the accounts with Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 2024-300 02-0001 dated 02/13/2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to reclassify the accounts with JEV No. 2024-300-02-0002 dated February 28, 2024 and confirm with the PCA the breakdown of the received funds from January 2016 to February 2018 to properly account for the share of the Municipality and Barangays and to incorporate the supposed municipal share to fund intended projects of the RA and remit the share of the Barangays.
observations.managementAction: Received the confirmation reply from PCA and confirm with the PCA the breakdown of the received funds from January 2016 to February 2018 to properly account for the share of the Municipality and Barangays. Subsequently, we also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entry in the books of accounts to reclassify the aforementioned balance of Due to NGAs totaling P524,460.00 to the Trust Liabilities and/or Due to LGUs account/s, upon confirmation of the Municipal and Barangay shares with the PCA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer (MT)/Special Disbursing Officer to observe the limit in the granting of CAs for the CF which should not be more than the three-month requirement. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant or his authorized representative to submit all the DVs for Confidential Fund CAs with the complete supporting documents on the grant of CA for CF for the 2nd and 4th quarters of CY 2023 to the COA Audit Team Leader within seven days from release of the check, in accordance with Item 6.1.8.4 of COA-DBM-DILG-DND-GCG Joint Circular No. 2015-01.
observations.managementAction: Rules and requirements with regard to granting CAs for the CF were observed and implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) to: a. Ensure that all development projects to be funded out of the 20 percent DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready; and b. Henceforth, comply with guidelines set in the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 in the appropriation and utilization of the 20 percent DF of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: MPDO issued justification on the delayed implementation of the project. Reported and recorded as completed project on 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MT to remit the funds to the source agencies.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality of Tagkawayan already remitted the unexpended balance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: • Direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to comply with the procedures in MNGAS for LGUs pertaining to reporting of disbursements/checks issued; and • Develop and implement internal control measures to enable all undelivered checks in the custody of the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to be communicated to the Office of the Municipal Accountant. Henceforth, ensure that undelivered checks as of year-end are reported as part of the Cash in bank account in compliance with the existing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Reiterated with modifications in Observation No. 5, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: • Acting General Services Officer (GSO) and the Municipal Accountant to include in their records the parcel of land and the motor unit costs/values as prescribed on Item 6.2.12 of COA Circular 2020-006; • Acting GSO to exert effort in the verification of ownership on the six parcels of land located in Barangay Quipot, Tiaong, Quezon; and • Inventory Committee to tag all counted PPE items with new property stickers containing the Property Number and all vital information on the PPE item as provided under Paragraph 5.7 of COA Circular No. 2020-006.
observations.managementAction: Implemented
observations.reason: A copy of Deed of Donation from the donor to the Municipality of Tiaong was provided to the Audit Team confirming that the six parcels of land are owned by the Municipality. The Inventory Committee is currently encoding in their system all the vital information relating to PPE items to generate new property stickers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Accountable Officers (AOs) to ensure all cash advances pertaining to the salaries and wages of JOs and COS are liquidated within five days after the pay period in compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor revisit the possibility and necessity of payment of salaries and wages of job orders and contract of service through Automated Teller Machine (ATM) as internal control to prevent delayed liquidation or unliquidated cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Implemented
observations.reason: Payments for wages of JOs and COS were made through ATM starting the payroll period of August 15, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Acting GSO to: • Expedite the application of the insurable properties with the GSIS; and • Facilitate the proper procedures in transferring the ownership of properties to its end-users including the cancellation of issued Acknowledgment Receipt for Equipment (ARE) to the Barangay pertaining to motor vehicle and furnish the Municipal Accountant all the necessary documents supporting such transfers.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Not all insurable properties were insured during CY 2024. The Acting GSO submitted the PIF to the Audit Team on April 30, 2024. AREs issued to Barangay pertaining to motor vehicle and other properties are not yet cancelled and transferred in the respective RPCPPE and PPE ledgers during CY 2024. All accounting entries relating to transfers of properties are to be recorded once the pertinent documents supporting the same are received.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that the recommendations of the DILG be complied with to secure a DILG-approved GPB and to submit the DILG-approved GPB to the Audit Team within five working days from receipt thereof.
observations.managementAction: Implemented
observations.reason: The GFPS TWG was able to secure the Certificate of Review and Endorsement from DILG on August 2, 2024 and correspondingly sent to the Audit Team on the same day. It was acknowledged on August 9, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor adopt necessary measures to ensure that the duly signed GAD AR is submitted to the Audit Team within five working days after the end of January of the succeeding year.
observations.managementAction: Implemented
observations.reason: The Accomplishment Report of GAD Funds for CY 2024 was already submitted to the Audit Team within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the recording of Accounts Payable is in accordance with Section 4(s) of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Implemented
observations.reason: Due to the absence or loss of supporting documents. The amount of P4,932,813.63 was reverted to the equity accounts under JEV No. 100-2023-06-1445 and 100-2024-12-1492, on June 30, 2023 and December 27, 2024, respectively.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the issuance of a Blacklisting Order disqualifying the erring contractor from participating in the bidding of all government projects in conformity with Appendix 17 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: On-going
observations.reason: Not Implemented during CY 2024. Notices of Cancellation/Blacklisting are to be issued; copies of notices were furnished to the Audit Team during exit conference on May 21, 2024, but full blacklisting action not yet completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to ensure that all development projects to be funded out of the 20% Development Fund are procurement-and-implementation ready.
observations.managementAction: Implemented
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2024, the implementation of development projects funded out of 20% Local Development for CY 2024 has been 93.70%.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Appraisal Committee to immediately appraise the two Motor Vehicles as this is needed for its disposal and the Disposal Committee to proceed with the disposal of the 11 Motor Vehicles in accordance with existing laws, rules and regulations. Thereafter, instruct the Municipal Accountant to drop the disposed properties from the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: On-going
observations.reason: No unserviceable motor vehicle was disposed during CY 2024. Appraisal and public auction were conducted; declared failure. Management intends to proceed with negotiated sale upon re-assessment of values.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to maintain SL for each collecting officer pursuant to the provisions set forth in Item 2 of Section 114 of the Presidential Decree.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has established and maintains a subsidiary ledger for each collecting officer, ensuring proper monitoring and accountability of their collections.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to prepare the SORD for the purchase of Equipment for Calamansi Concentrate Juice making and submit the same to the DOST; and b. Municipal Agriculturist to secure the copy of MOA for the said grant to verify if the remaining balance of P141,600.00 is already due for return to the SA.
observations.managementAction: The SORD was submitted to the SA by the Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer, with a copy furnished to the Audit Team. The Municipal Agriculturist has secured a copy of MOA. Henceforth ensure that SORDs for all fund transfers are prepared and submitted to the SAs in accordance with item 6.5 of COA Circular No. 94-013. For CY 2024, all fund transfers were duly supported by SORD and submitted to the SAs within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal DRRM Officer (MDRRMO) to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the Audit Team within the prescribed period which is on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2024, the Municipality has submitted the utilization reports within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Gender and Development (GAD) Focal Person to submit the DILG-approved GPB for CY 2023 to the AT. Henceforth, strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2014-001 on the timely submission of the GAD Accomplishment Report.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2024, the Municipality was able to submit the GAD Accomplishment Report within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
 a. Mun. Treasurer (i) strictly observe the provisions of Section 63(F) of the LTOM on the monthly reconciliation of cashbook balances with subsidiary accounting records as this is an important control tool for cash; and
 b. Mun. Mayor (i) require the Mun. Treasurer and the Mun. Accountant to immediately reconcile their records and submit a reconciliation statement thereon and take-up the necessary adjusting entries in their respective records; (ii) demand from/require the Mun. Treasurer and other accountable officers (AOs) concerned to immediately submit to the Mun. Accountant the source documents of the unrecorded disbursements, credit memorandum, deposits, and other book reconciling items, after which, the same shall be submitted to the Audit Team for audit purposes; and (iii) make representations with the bank to furnish the Municipality with copies of debit and credit memos of said reconciling items for recording in the books of accounts.

observations.reason: The requested reconciliation statements were not yet submitted for validation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
 a. Mun. Accountant (i) continue sending regular written notices to all AOs concerned at least quarterly to demand the settlement of their outstanding cash advances; (ii) ensure that no new cash advance is granted unless previous ones have been fully liquidated; and (iii) investigate the reasons for negative cash advance balances and make appropriate adjustments in the books to rectify discrepancies; and
 b. Mun. Mayor (i) cause the request for write-off of the accounts of deceased employees and those dormant unliquidated cash advances pursuant to the guidelines prescribed under COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023; and (ii) initiate the filing of appropriate action against the erring AOs who still failed to settle their accounts after demand, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: Notices were served to AOs concerned. The Mun. Accountant exerted efforts in reconstruction of the SLs. Some demand letters were prepared and issued; majority scheduled for release starting second quarter. Some AOs subject to ongoing monthly payroll deductions. Management to prepare list of deceased employees and supporting documents for write-off; follow-up actions to be undertaken.
observations.reason: Some AOs settled their unliquidated advances through salary deduction and internal controls were not strictly enforced for advances granted to SDOs; required documents for write-off still unavailable.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
 a. Mun. Mayor ensure the timely submission of the required documents on the grant of CF to the Audit Team and that the same is contained in a sealed envelope; and
 b. Mun. Accountant (i) submit the lacking supporting documents; and (ii) ensure the completeness of documentation on the grant of CF and its confidentiality.
observations.managementAction: Management ensured confidentiality and timely submission of CF required documents to the Audit Team. Mun. Accountant submitted lacking documents and ensured completeness of documentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. follow up on the demand to all AOs concerned for the full settlement of their cash accountabilities and initiate the necessary steps to pursue legal action against them, if deemed necessary, and (ii) enjoin the former Mun. Treasurer to produce cashbook for SEF, reconcile all her cashbooks with accounting records, and thereafter submit to the Audit Team her final report pursuant to Section 80 of PD No. 1445; and b. Mun. Accountant continue reconstructing the SLs of the former Mun. Treasurer pertaining to her cash accountability and submit the same to the Audit Team for verification and audit.
observations.managementAction: Management has served demand letters to all AOs concerned. One AO is subject to fixed monthly payroll deduction. Efforts are ongoing to retrieve data from old files to reconstruct unsubmitted SLs. Mun. Accountant has already exerted efforts in reconstructing the SLs; could not complete due to incomplete documents turned over.
observations.reason: Reconciliation is still in progress due to incomplete SLs from previous years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Prioritize the filling-up of vacant positions and provision of funds for newly-created positions in the offices concerned to enhance the delivery of its services and further assist the Inventory Committee in the conduct of inventory-taking activities; b. Require the members of the Inventory Committee, in coordination with the personnel concerned, to prioritize the conduct of complete inventory-taking activities, reconciliation with pertinent records, and preparation and submission of reports, for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006; and c. Mun. Accountant prepare the journal entries necessary to derecognize the items below the ₱50,000.00 capitalization threshold and the Acting General Services Officer (GSO) carry out control procedures for the maintenance of semi-expendable property records, pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2022-004 and 2024-006; and d. Mun. Accountant and the Acting GSO ensure that proper accounting and reporting procedures for all acquisition/receipt/issue/transfer/disposal of PPE pursuant to the relevant laws, rules and regulations on property and supply management are strictly followed. The Mun. Mayor should likewise ensure that physical inventory is undertaken annually and reports thereon are submitted accordingly.
observations.managementAction: Additional personnel were appointed to the General Services Office in CYs 2024 and 2025. A one-time data cleansing activity was initiated but remains incomplete due to unforeseen circumstances. Reconciliation between the Accounting and GSO records is still in progress. Journal entries to derecognize items below capitalization threshold were prepared. Acting GSO to implement control procedures; Mun. Mayor to ensure annual physical inventory.
observations.reason: One-time cleansing started but not yet finished; insufficient manpower and incomplete reconciliation with relevant records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Require the Mun. Accountant, in coordination with the Mun. Engineer and the Acting GSO, to reconcile the RPCLRN with the appropriate records and prepare the journal voucher to update the Road Networks account in the books of accounts; b. Enjoin the Mun. Accountant and the Acting GSO to maintain complete and updated LRNLC and LRNPC, respectively, in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-008; and c. Ensure that the physical count of local road networks is undertaken annually, and the reports thereon are submitted to the Audit Team and the Mun. Accountant not later than January 31 of each year.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of the Local Road Network records is ongoing. The annual physical count update is underway and will be submitted to the Municipal Accountant before the end of November 2025. The Mun. Accountant and GSO to maintain complete and updated LRNLC and LRNPC; Mun. Mayor to assign focal person for annual physical count.
observations.reason: Final RPCLRN was belatedly submitted; reconciliation with appropriate records is still in progress.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mun. Accountant: a. Verify the validity of dormant and unsubstantiated payables and confirm the existence of actual claimants. Legitimate claims should be settled promptly, while unresolved payables may be subject to reversal if found to lack substantiation; and b. Investigate the reasons for negative balances and make appropriate adjustments in the books of accounts to rectify discrepancies.
observations.managementAction: The Mun. Accountant conducted regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of dormant fund transfers; partial adjustments in the books were recorded. Investigation and review of negative balances ongoing; bulk transactions being reviewed and necessary adjustments effected.
observations.reason: Verification of the validity of dormant and unsubstantiated payables is still in progress; some bulk transactions require further review.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mun. Accountant (i) conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of all dormant fund transfers; (ii) effect adjustment in the books for the correction of identified errors/misstatements in the said account; (iii) ensure the reconciliation of the balances reported between the SLs and the Notes to Financial Statements; and (iv) cause the submission of liquidation reports to the source agency for all fully implemented projects, programs and activities (PPAs). We also recommended that Management (i) cause the return of unexpended balances of completed PPAs to its source agency; (ii) prioritize the completion of PPAs with delayed or postponed implementation; and (iii) consider filing a request for authority to write-off dormant fund transfers to this office following the guidelines and procedures under COA Circular No. 2023-008.
observations.managementAction: Mun. Accountant conducted regular and periodic verification of fund transfers; recorded partial adjustments in the books; reconciled balances reported in SLs and Notes to Financial Statements; submitted liquidation reports to source agencies for fully implemented PPAs. Management caused return of unexpended balances for some completed PPAs. Preparation and consolidation of necessary documents for write-off pending; required documents not yet available.
observations.reason: SL for Beginning Balances was still used in recording some transactions; documents required for write-off not yet available.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Requisitioning Units and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) (i) provide justification for their non-reference in the DPRI as the ceiling price in the PRs and POs, and (ii) ensure compliance with RA No. 9502 and DOH AO No. 2019-0040; and b. Mun. Accountant (i) coordinate with the offices concerned relative to the submission of the lacking documentary requirements, (ii) ensure that disbursements are supported with complete documentations, and (iii) strictly observe the perpetual inventory method of accounting for inventories.
observations.managementAction: Management provided justifications for non-reference to DPRI; complied with RA No. 9502 and DOH AO. Mun. Accountant coordinated with concerned offices to submit lacking documentary requirements; ensured disbursements supported with complete documentation. Continued efforts to strictly observe perpetual inventory method; some adjustments and reconciliations still ongoing.
observations.reason: Some inventory-related adjustments and reconciliations remain ongoing as part of implementation and data cleansing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Finance Committee (LFC), in coordination with the Mun. Accountant and the Mun. Engineer, immediately prepare the quarterly report on fund utilization and status of program/project implementation as of December 31, 2023 for posting and submission to the offices concerned for information purposes and transparency, copy furnished the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: Quarterly report on fund utilization and status of program/project implementation as of December 31, 2023 was posted and submitted to the offices concerned for information and transparency purposes, copy furnished the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Review the Municipality's organizational structure and staffing pattern to ensure alignment with service requirements and financial capabilities, while complying with CSC guidelines; b. Develop and implement a strategic human resources plan focused on recruiting and retaining personnel to fill vacant plantilla positions, while deploying COS personnel strategically to support critical service areas; c. Conduct regular assessments of staffing needs and establish mechanisms to address gaps and optimize human resources utilization; and d. Create avenues for COS personnel to obtain the required eligibility qualifications and improve their pertinent skills, ensuring fair treatment and equal access to career advancement opportunities for all.
observations.managementAction: Management made modifications of position titles through SB Resolutions/Ordinances. Reviewed and included CSC inputs to the draft RSP prior to finalization and resubmission to said agency. Regular coordination with Heads of Departments/Offices. Provided avenues for COS personnel for learning and development and continued support to COS employees’ learning and development.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Cause the immediate formulation and execution of a comprehensive and detailed catch-up plan for the full implementation of all priority development projects at the soonest possible time; b. Require the Office of the Mun. Engineer to prepare design plans and program of work for all unimplemented projects; c. Require the MPDC, in coordination with the Mun. Engineer, to conduct a re-assessment of the selected mode of implementation of projects in the AIP to identify the most efficient and appropriate mode for the Municipality; and d. Ensure that all budgets for each project are supported with a program of work (POW); and e. Ensure that the status report of government PPAs is completely provided with the required information and reconciled with the records of the offices concerned.
observations.managementAction: Management reprogrammed/realigned funds for 92 development projects totaling ₱50,949,065.15. MEO to prepare design plans and programs of work; MPDC and MEO to conduct reassessment of implementation modes. Regular monitoring and inspection of projects and profiling is ongoing. Status reports being updated and reconciled with concerned offices.
observations.reason: Lack of technical personnel and continued monitoring and profiling ongoing; some projects reprogrammed/realigned.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management ensure in the succeeding years that the transfer of actual cash pertaining to the unexpended balance of current year DRRM to the STF and the reversion of the unutilzed STF balance for more than five years to the unappropriated surplus of the GF be made immediately, pursuant to the provisions of RA No. 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: Transfer was already carried out in the succeeding period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Mun. Mayor (i) observe the balanced implementation of activities addressing the four thematic areas of DRRM by making sure that implementation of prevention and mitigation projects and activities are also given utmost priority; and (b) expedite the prompt implementation and completion of DRRM PPAs, in management coordination with the Mun. DRRM Council and other offices concerned; and b. Mun. Accountant and the Mun. DRRM Officer reconcile their records and make adjustments on the identified discrepancies and exert more effort to trace the variances to reflect the correct amount of DRRM balances at year- end.
observations.managementAction: Relevant offices including LPMC committed to conduct comprehensive review; MPDC, Municipal Engineer, barangay officials to update and reconcile project implementation reports; instituted regular monitoring; Mun. DRRM Officer and Mun. Accountant reconciled records and adjusted reports. Adjusted and correct data reflected in reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, in coordination with the members of the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) and other stakeholders, develop robust monitoring and reporting mechanisms to track the progress of GAD PPAs. Implementing regular reviews and evaluations will allow for ongoing assessment of performance against objectives and identification of areas for improvement, ultimately facilitating the successful completion of GAD PPAs.
observations.managementAction: Convene GFPS; discuss unimplemented projects; assess feasibility and recommend strategies for augmentation or realignment. Conduct regular monitoring and reporting.
observations.reason: Process of reviewing the GAD PPAs is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that at the close of each business day, the collectors/tellers shall accomplish the Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD) in four copies. The original and two copies, together with the duplicate of the official receipts issued, shall be submitted to the treasurer/cashier to whom the cash collected shall be turned over.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit intact all her collections as well as all collections turned over to her by the collectors/tellers with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day. She shall record all deposits made in the cashbook and prepare the RCD as required under Section 69 of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445, Section 8 of COA Circular No. 92-382, Local Treasury Operations Manual (LTOM) 2nd Edition and Cash Examination Manual. Likewise, proper handling of collections and its deposits be strictly observed and deposits should be attributed/traced from the collected amounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the 

(a) Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit supporting schedule or specific details on the erroneous deposit of General Fund collections amounting to ₱273,885.82 to support the adjustments in the Cash in Local Treasury account; and 

(b) Municipal Accountant to ensure that the required documents for all type of transactions are submitted/attached to the disbursement vouchers before the processing and eventual recording in the books.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to 

(a) reconcile their records at least quarterly to immediately detect any error/discrepancy for timely adjustment and accordingly, to establish an accurate and reliable records and reports on Cash in Treasury and Cash in Bank balances; and 

(b) effect the necessary adjustment in the books and cashbook as indicated in the bank reconciliation and reconciliation statement of accountability.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the responsible officer to submit the pertinent documents relative to the Water System in order to substantiate the recorded income and expense in the books of the Municipality.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive and responsible municipal offices agreed to put serious efforts and time in resolving the perennial issues on PPE. We likewise reiterated that 

(a) Inventory Committee should (i) conduct physical inventory of all PPE of the Municipality on or before January 31 of each year as and immediately submit the related reports to the Office of the Auditor; and (ii) reconcile the resultant inventory reports with the Accounting records; and 

(b) designated Property Officer and Municipal Accountant should maintain complete PPE records such as PPE ledger cards and property cards for the proper control and monitoring of the agency properties. We further recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and Property Officer to consider the guidelines on one-time cleansing of PPE accounts to render a reliable PPE information.

We recommended and the Management agreed to continue complying with DBM, DOF and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 1, dated November 4, 2020 on the implementation of PPAs to ensure that the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes are achieved.

We also recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the 

(a) Bids and Awards Committee; BAC Secretariat, and Technical Working Group to (i) work on the completion of the documentary deficiencies and submit the same to the Audit Team; (ii) enhance the transparency on procurements by complying the publicity and notification requirements; and 
(b) Municipal Accountant to strengthen her review function on the requisite documentation of disbursements for procurements.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the LDRRMO to ensure efficient and effective implementation of programmed projects identified during the budget year to optimize the utilization of the fund and to warrant that the goals and objectives of reducing disaster risk and enhancing disaster preparedness and response capabilities are achieved. 

We further recommended and Local Chief Executive also agreed to require the HRM Officer to comply with the filling up of the vacant positions in the LDRRMO in accordance with NDRRMC, DILG, DBM and CSC JMC No. 2014- 1 to effectively carry out the DRRM activities and programs.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the submission within the prescribed period as stated in COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Accountant to 

(a) maintain the Special Accounts for the economic enterprises in the General Fund; and 

(b) prepare the corresponding schedules to support the financial statements which will serve as tools in informing Management in evaluating the viability of the economic enterprise operated by the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Market Administrator to require the full payment of goodwill from stallholders in compliance with Chapter 5 Section 2A of the Market Code and Section 3 of the lease contracts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to direct the:

HRMO to (i) assess prudently the need to engage the services of consultants and in case there is really a need, engage the experts in their respective fields and submit the Curriculum Vitae, Terms of Reference and Accomplishment Reports of the hired consultants for reference in audit; and (ii) reassess its existing organizational structure to upgrade the plantilla of personnel requirement for possible budgeting, thereby, lessening the hiring of job-order personnel; and

Municipal Accountant to support disbursements of funds for services rendered by Consultants, COS and job order personnel with the necessary and appropriate supporting documents to establish validity and propriety of payment.
We recommended and the Management agreed to continue complying with the BIR rules and regulations on the withholding and remittance of withheld taxes.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to submit to the Audit Team proof that goods and services are accepted or rendered, and supplier/creditor bills are received for claims listed in the Accounts Payable. Otherwise, prepare an adjusting entry debiting the Accounts Payable and crediting the corresponding prior year’s adjustment account/s. Henceforth, we recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to ensure that the recording of Accounts Payable is in accordance with Section 4(s) of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Accounts Payable totaling P12,025,566.25 were already paid in CY 2023. Hence, payable totaling P4,463,328.81 remained outstanding as of December 31, 2023. The City Accountant submitted disbursement vouchers and supporting documents for payments made in CY 2023; however, this is still an ongoing practice and the City has yet to submit the complete documentation for the remaining accounts.
observations.reason: The City Accountant was still not able to submit the complete documentation for the accounts as of December 31, 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the: • CPDO to ensure that all development projects to be funded out of the 20% DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready; and henceforth comply with guidelines set in the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% DF of the City.
observations.managementAction: The management reorganized the Local Project Monitoring Committee and instructed the CPDO and other departments to comply with the guidelines stated in JMC No.1. The CPDO commented that they were compliant with the JMC but implementation problems were due to bulk of projects; they will focus on implementation of current year projects and require Barangays to complete necessary documents.
observations.reason: This observation has been a recurring observation hence, the Management has yet to observe the thorough planning of the 20% DF to address this observation. The management has not yet complied with the guidelines as of December 31, 2023 due to various unimplemented projects.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations that the City Mayor instruct the: • Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and the City Engineer to properly plan and schedule all procurement activities pertaining to development projects for their timely implementation and completion; • CPDO to strategize and coordinate with all concerned Departments to improve the planning and programming of projects, fast track and prioritize the full implementation of the development projects as embodied in the 20% DF Plan within the year these were programmed; and • City Engineer to develop other procedures and method to facilitate the preparation of plans and technical design of all projects to be implemented.
observations.managementAction: The CPDO prepared a schedule of the development projects for implementation and coordinated with concerned departments to improve planning and implementation. The City Mayor committed to comply with recommendations in CY 2024.
observations.reason: This observation has been a recurring observation; the Management has yet to observe thorough planning of the 20% DF. Some developmental projects were implemented by the City, but many remain unimplemented.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations that the City Mayor instruct the: • BAC and the City Engineering to properly plan and schedule all procurement activities pertaining to development projects for their timely implementation and completion; • CPDO to fast track and prioritize the full implementation of the 47 development projects under the current appropriation, 29 projects under continuing appropriation, and the remaining 26 projects programmed in CY 2019; • CPDO to strategize and coordinate with all concerned Departments to improve the planning and programming of projects and see to it that the projects to be programmed were feasible and can be implemented during the year it was programmed; and • the City Engineer to develop other procedures and method to facilitate the preparation of technical design of all projects to be implemented.
observations.managementAction: The CPDO coordinated with concerned departments and prepared schedules; the City committed to comply with recommendations in CY 2024. Some developmental projects were implemented by the City.
observations.reason: This observation has been recurring; the Management has yet to fully comply. Some developmental projects were implemented by the City but many remain unimplemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
Municipal Mayor instruct the
Municipal Treasurer to
henceforth require all MCs
to remit their collections
daily.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer issued
an office memorandum to
prioritize the collectors to
ensure proper turnover and
verification of daily collections.
observations.reason: Despite actions taken, some MCs were not able to compliance with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
Municipal Mayor and the
SWM Board provide the
needed resources and
assistance to expedite the
completion and submission to
the NSWMC of the 10-Year
SWM Plan of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
The MENRO – Designate attended a seminar which aimed to provide a framework to facilitate the Waste Analysis and Characterization Study (WACS) of the 10-Year SWM Plan. The MENRO – Designate has also coordinated with the Provincial Environment and Natural Resources Officer (PENRO) with regard to the completion and conduct of the WACS.

By June of 2024, the supplemental budget will be allotted for the WACS. Upon budget approval, a schedule will be set by the PENRO and then proceed with the conduct and completion of the WACS.
observations.reason: The WACS, as a preliminary activity

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Direct the Municipal HRMO to publish the vacancy and expedite the hiring of the mandatory positions; and b. Fill out the vacant positions, as prescribed in Section 443 (a) (b) of RA No. 7160 and in accordance with the existing guidelines and rules and regulations of the Civil Service Commission (CSC), for more effective administration of the said offices which in turn will benefit the Municipality, as a whole.
observations.reason: Seven positions were not yet posted as of December 31, 2023. Five positions were scheduled already approved by Human Resource Merit Promotion and Selection Board.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to adopt remedial measures in enforcing the collection of the Due from Officers and Employees and Other Receivables accounts in compliance with Section 2 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Management collected P10,001.00 out of P26,761.00 Due from Officers and Employees on January 2024 while Other Receivables amounting to P6,089.44 remained uncollected as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor strictly monitor the implementation of all planned LDRRM PPAs to ensure achievement of the Municipality’s goal and objectives in mitigating the impact of disaster.
observations.managementAction: Some PPAs for procurement were already submitted to Bids and Awards Committee on the latter part of the year, however, no prospective suppliers submitted their bid.
observations.reason: Likewise, the LDRRMO cited various reasons for the non-implementation of several projects such as: • Non-availability of instructors for the two proposed training and seminar; • The LDRRMO team Environmental Planners (ENP) and Geographic Information System (GIS) were not able to attend a workshop, hence, the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan (LDRRMP) and Local Climate Change Action Plan (LCCAP) were not formulated; • Non-availability of raw materials for warning sign projects; • Insufficiency of budget in the procurement of tactical uniforms; • Bidder has no license from the National Telecommunications Commissions (NTC) for the procurement of handheld radios.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Environmental Waste Management Officer (MENRO) to submit its MSWMP to the NSWMC for review and approval as required in Section 16 of RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: According to Waste Analysis Study (WACS) which would be the basis of projects in the plan, is scheduled tentatively in February 2024 to determine the percentage of different compositions of the waste.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal Assessor to expedite the titling of the 14 parcels of land in the name of the Municipality to avoid third party claims in the future and possible land disputes.
observations.managementAction: The name titled one lot in the Municipality.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2023, the remaining 13 lots were still on process.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert more efforts in requiring the liquidation of the funds transfer to the Barangay. Likewise, instruct the Municipal Treasurer to demand for the 30 percent of income of the livelihood projects from the concerned Barangays, as stipulated in the MOA. In case the said funds were not utilized, demand for the return of the unused funds and institute proper charges in accordance with the MOA.
observations.managementAction: The three Barangays submitted their AR and fully liquidated the fund transfer to the barangay as of year-end.
observations.reason: The remaining 10 Barangays have not submitted their AR and have not fully liquidated the fund transfers as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MT to consider revising/ updating the rates in the Local Revenue Code (LRC) in compliance with RA No. 7160 to maximize its taxing power and to provide more funds for its operations.
observations.managementAction: The LRC has been drafted, orientation/ seminar for the toolkit has been conducted but the consolidation is still ongoing. Its target date of submission is May 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor file a legal action against the contractor to demand the refund of the advance payment made to them to avoid loss of government funds.
observations.managementAction: The Contractor paid P50,000.00 every quarter of the succeeding year leaving a balance of P299,999.98 as of year-end. The balance will be refunded to the Municipality a total amount of P352,779.00, until it becomes fully paid. The last payment per the agreement of both parties will be on March 31, 2025.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to ensure the implementation of all planned programs, projects, and activities funded out of the LDRRMF-MF in order for the Municipality to reduce disaster risk and be adequately prepared for any imminent disaster/calamity in accordance with RA No. 10121.
observations.reason: For CY 2023, 60.45 percent or P3,379,347.47 of the appropriations for LDRRMF-MF was not utilized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Development Council and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to: a. Ensure that all development projects to be funded out of the 20 percent DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready; and b. Henceforth, comply with the guidelines set in the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 in the appropriation and utilization of the 20 percent DF of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Four development projects for CY 2023 with a total appropriation of P4,035,216.40 were not implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to remind the contractors to submit the documents pertaining to their claims. If they are unable to support their claims with the necessary documents, revert the account to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund, subject to an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan through a Resolution with a condition that appropriation shall be made in the event that claim arises in the future pursuant to the provisions of Section 98 of PD No. 1445.
observations.reason: The contractors/suppliers were contacted and informed about their claims in prior years but they did not respond.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality continue to exert effort in securing the approval of its 10-year SWMP from the NSWMC and continue to exert effort in securing the necessary information to comply with RA No. 9003 so that once its establishment is completed they can operate the sanitary landfill and ensure projects are aligned.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC stated that the Plan was already submitted to the NSWMC and will be deliberated on June 2024. They are waiting for the results of the deliberation.
observations.reason: The MPDC stated that the Plan was submitted to the NSWMC and will be deliberated on June 2024. They are waiting for the results of the deliberation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MGSO - Designate and the Municipal Accountant to pursue and complete the reconciliation of the property records with the accounting records to establish the correct PPE balances. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MGSO - Designate to prepare and complete the prescribed RPCBS and submit it to the Audit Team, for verification in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Account and GSO managed to reconcile a significant amount of previously unreconciled balance. RPCBS has been submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Of the variance identified amounting to P23,813,400.14, pertaining to Information and Communication Technology Equipment remains unreconciled. This item has been identified and is awaiting verification of its existence.
