observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MGSO - Designate and the Municipal Accountant to pursue and complete the reconciliation of the property records with the accounting records to establish the correct PPE balances.
observations.managementAction: PPELCs were prepared and maintained by the Municipal Accountant and submitted to the Audit Team on May 4, 2023.
observations.reason: Of the variance identified and of the previously unreconciled balance, an amount totaling P324,000.00 pertaining to Information and Communication Technology Equipment remains unreconciled. This item has been identified and is pending verification of their existence.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: • Strictly comply with all other prescribed procedures and guidelines under RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: The municipality has submitted a new application for the issuance of ECC. However, certain requirements, specifically the Land Plan, must be retraced back to the original owner of the land designated for the sanitary landfill. The Land Plan is an essential prerequisite for obtaining CENTRO Certification.
observations.reason: No/pending approval of ECC for the sanitary landfill.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Treasurer to monitor the daily remittance of collectors and likewise, monitor the issuance of accountable forms to collecting officers, by preparing the Individual Report/s of Collections and Deposits and the Consolidated Report of Collections and Deposits.
observations.managementAction: The former Municipal Mayor conducted an investigation on this matter; however, no subsequent action has been taken.
observations.reason: The former Municipal Mayor conducted an investigation on this matter; however, no subsequent action has been taken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to conduct an investigation and file appropriate charges against the accountable officers for the alterations of documents in violation of the provision of Article 171 of the Revised Penal Code, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: The former Municipal Mayor conducted an investigation on this matter; however no subsequent action has been taken. Also, the accountable officers were no longer in the service.
observations.reason: Also, the accountable officers were no longer in the service.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer be required to produce the missing accountable forms or submit proper accounting thereof.
observations.managementAction: The former Audit Team who conducted the cash examination instructed the Municipal Treasurer to complete the missing ORs before submitting to COA for validation. Also, the accountable officers were no longer in the service.
observations.reason: The accountable officers were no longer in the service.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to expedite the immediate cleansing of property tax records to have a reliable RPT Assessment Roll and consequently, a reliable RPT and SET Receivables accounts.
observations.managementAction: Not Cleansing of property records is still on-going.
observations.reason: Cleansing of property records is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the: • Appraisal Committee/Team to properly appraise the donated land as to their fair values at the time of acquisition and apply the same to the purchased land if the acquisition costs cannot be determined reliably; • Municipal Accountant to record the land once the fair value and copy of titles are received; • Municipal Tax Assessor to ensure that all land owned by the Municipality are declared in the name of the Municipality; and that the Municipal General Services Office (MGSO) request from the Registry of Deeds the owner’s copy of title for the six parcels and expedite the titling of eight parcels of land owned by the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The head of the Appraisal Committee tried to secure the Deed of Conveyances. Not Implemented Reiterated per Observation No. 1, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor file appropriate charges against the concerned persons, otherwise require the Municipal officials who authorized the use of Municipal funds for payment of personal loans of employees to settle/refund the same.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality will send a demand letter to the concerned person.
observations.reason: The concerned persons have either died or have not been located.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Disaster Risk Management Officer to ensure the implementation of all planned PPAs funded LDRRMF-MF in order for the Municipality to reduce the disaster risk and be adequately prepared for any imminent disaster/calamity in accordance with RA No. 10121.
observations.reason: 15.38 percent or P74,329.81 of the LDRRMF-MF were not implemented in CY 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDC and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to: a. Ensure that all development projects to be funded out of the 20 percent DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready; and b. Henceforth, comply with guidelines set in the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 on the appropriation and utilization of the 20 percent DF of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 6, (Part II of this AAR).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the General Services Officer to dispose the unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445 to avoid further deterioration and earn at least the salvage value from the sale of these assets. Likewise, create an Appraisal and Disposal Committee who will be responsible in appraising the scrap/salvage value of those unserviceable properties.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented The appraisal committee is still finalizing the documents to support the disposal of unserviceable properties.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor require the:
• Municipal Treasurer to continue to exert extra effort to collect from the identified debtors and/or gather all the required documents required under COA Circular No. 2016-005 and re-file the request for write-off to COA; and
• Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to identify the particular account/s for which the collections of partial payments for Loans Receivable-Others were taken up and prepare the necessary adjusting entries.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer exerted efforts to collect from identified debtors by sending demand letter to their last known address and to file request for write-off to COA. The Municipal Accountant coordinated with Municipal Treasurer and identified the particular accounts from which collections of partial payments for Loans Receivable were taken up and prepared the necessary adjusting entries. The Municipal Accountant made the necessary adjusting entries under JEV no. 2022-11-6705 dated November 15, 2022.
observations.reason: The municipality has not yet gathered the complete documents necessary, and is yet to submit the request for write-off to the Audit Team for evaluation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to verify the correctness of the recorded amounts included in the Schedule of the Due to LGUs account balance of P4,473,680.51 and identify the LGUs to which the Municipality has liability for the said balance. Henceforth, strictly comply with Section 111 of P.D. No. 1445 in ensuring that the Due to LGUs account is properly supported with details and is accurately recorded.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant stated that she was not able to focus on the said tracing of account due to the voluminous workload and lack of turnover by the former Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Development Council (MDC) and Municipal Planning and Development Council (MPDC) to:
a. Ensure that all development projects to be funded out of the 20 percent DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready; and
b. Henceforth, comply with guidelines set in the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 in the appropriation and utilization of the 20 percent DF of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Management implemented some of the development projects included in total appropriation of P12,700,000.00 were implemented.
observations.reason: Not Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 4 , Page Nos. 37 to 38

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor include in the proposed budget the amount required for the purchase of land and utilize the funds intended for the construction of the Gumaca Municipal Nursery.
observations.managementAction: The Management was able to locate potential lot for the construction of the Municipal Nursery. The Nursery will commence in First Quarter of 2023.
observations.reason: The Management identified lot for the construction of the nursery but was not able to purchase the identified lot hence fund intended for this was not utilized.

observations.managementAction:
The Management submitted the latest copy of RPCPPE to the Audit Team. Reiterated and presented anew in Audit Observation No. 1, Page Nos. 35 to 39, Part II of this Report
Also, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to submit all the schedules and the MGSO to:
• Cause the immediate appraisal or costing of the PPEs that have zero values in RPCPPE; 
• Adhere on the provisions of COA Circular No. 2022-004 on the PPE threshold for the inclusion of items in the RPCPPE; 
• Reclassify the noted small tangible items included in the RPCPPE to excluded in the appropriate inventory class or semi- expendable assets in the Report on Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI); and 
• Transfer the noted road networks line items from RPCPPE to Report on Physical Count of Local Road Networks (RPCLRN), henceforth, refrain from including the Road Networks in the RPCPPE, instead, include the same in the RPCLRN as prescribed in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2015-008.
Necessary schedules were provided by the Municipal Accountant to the MGSO for the preparation and execution of the one-time cleansing of PPE balances. Items falling below the threshold of P50,000.00 were excluded in the RPCPPE and reported in their appropriate inventory reports.
The noted small tangible items were already excluded in the RPCPPE and was transferred to a separate report for inventories with high/low value.
The transfer and adjustment of road networks line items from RPCPPE to RPCLRN was indicated; however, the noted road networks line items are still included in the RPCPPE instead of RPCLRN.
observations.reason: No reconciliation of records yet between the Office of the Municipal Accountant and Municipal General Services Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert effort in tracing the records to provide the adequate information on the balances of receivables reflected in the schedule and to make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balance of the account for the fair presentation in the financial statements in accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant is still tracing the records to verify and to correct the negative and unreconciled balances, and to correct the balances and to correct the balances and to correct the balances, and the related adjustments were not yet made.
(As stated in the table: The Municipal Accountant is still tracing the records to verify and to correct the negative and unreconciled balances, and the related adjustments were not yet made.)
observations.reason: The Management was still not able to identify the causes of the negative balances and the related adjustments were not yet made

observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant is still tracing the records for the possible verification and checking in the records for the possible adjustments relative to the recommendations. Henceforth, ensure that there is proper monitoring of the Payables account in the books of accounts to fairly present the balances in accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1. The Municipal Accountant is still in the process of tracing of records.
(As stated in the table: The Municipal Accountant is still tracing the records and in the process of verification and tracing of records.)
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant was still in the process of tracing of records and was not able to furnish the Audit Team with the results of the record tracing and validation of claims.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: • Municipal Treasurer /General Services Officer (GSO) Designate to submit to the Audit Team the RPCPPE for CY 2022 and the complete and updated Property Cards being maintained; • Municipal Accountant to furnish/present the Audit Team with PPE Ledger Cards prepared and maintained by the Accounting Office; and Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 1, Page Nos. 25 to 26 • Municipal Treasurer/GSO and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records regularly in order to establish with certainty, the valuation, completeness and existence of the PPE. Henceforth, submit the corresponding inventory report within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: No action was undertaken by the Management in CY 2023.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer/GSO Designate was not able to submit the RPCPPE for CY 2022. Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 1, Page Nos. 25 to 26 The Municipal Accountant was able to submit only some of the PPE Ledger Cards. The Municipal Accountant was able to present some of the PPE Ledger Cards to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: • Reconcile the variance of P1,391,149.99 between the two reciprocal accounts; and • Prepare Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) and submit to the Audit Team the proof of reconciliation and adjustments made.
observations.managementAction: No action was undertaken by the Management in CY 2023.
observations.reason: A net balance of P220,103.99 between the affected accounts in CY 2023. Nonetheless, the unreconciled net balance of P220,103.99 is still a material discrepancy, hence subject for reconciliation by the Municipal Accountant through her records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: • Retrieve the necessary documents to support the unaccounted cash advances balance of P840,825.66, for our verification; otherwise prepare an adjusting entry debiting the Prior Period Adjustments and crediting the corresponding cash advances account/s; • Submit the JEV/s for any adjustment/s made; and • Ensure that recorded cash advances are supported with complete details and documentation in accordance with the above-stated provisions.
observations.managementAction: No action was undertaken by the Management in CY 2023.
observations.reason: The unaccounted cash balance was included in the schedule of cash advances as of December 31, 2023. However, the said balance was included in the long outstanding cash advances without supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: • Submit to the Audit Team proof that goods and services are accepted or rendered, and supplier/creditor bills are received for claims listed in the Accounts Payable balances for CYs 2021 and 2022 totaling P3,465,525.48, together with the contract/Purchase Order if any, for our verification. Otherwise, prepare an adjusting entry debiting the Accounts Payable and crediting the corresponding account/s; • Make the necessary entries for the reversion of the CY 2019 to 2020 outstanding balance totaling P621,062.82 to the Unappropriated Surplus of the GF of the Municipality with a condition that appropriation shall be made available in the event that claim arises in the future; and • Make the necessary adjusting entries to exclude in the Accounts Payable the claims of the Municipal Treasurer of Jomalig under JEV account totaling P374,000.00. Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the recording of Accounts Payable is in accordance with Section 4(s) of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: No action was undertaken by the Management in CY 2023.
observations.reason: There are still transactions recorded in accounts payable with no supporting documents. The claims of the Municipal Treasurer totaling P374,000.00 were reversed in the Accounts Payable per JEVs dated January 9 and February 23, 2023. Hence, some adjustments were made but many accounts payable remained unsupported.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: • Municipal Treasurer to: ➢ Prepare schedule of debtors and beneficiaries of livelihood program for the Receivable accounts totaling P495,987.87; and ➢ Adopt efficient collection strategies to enforce collection from the employees and concerned beneficiaries to ensure the recovery of the amount released to the beneficiaries. • Municipal Accountant to: ➢ Reconcile the records with the Municipal Treasurer’s Office; and ➢ Conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of the receivables and determine the concerned debtors to ensure that these are collected when these become due and demandable in compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: No action was undertaken by the Management in CY 2023.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer did not prioritize the submission of the schedule of debtors and beneficiaries of the livelihood program for the Receivable accounts. The Municipal Accountant did not perform reconciliation; hence receivables remained uncollected as of December 31, 2023.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: • Coordinate with the concerned offices to seek or locate the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) entered into with the Source Agencies or any documents stating the purpose of the fund transfer and the requirements in implementing and reporting of the unutilized PPAs totaling P6,886,466.25; • Department Heads concerned to secure copies of MOA from the Source Agencies and determine if these projects are still valid for implementation, otherwise remit the amount to the concerned sourced agency; and • Municipal Treasurer to remit the unexpended fund balance of P7,400.00 to the source agency in compliance with the MOA. Henceforth, ensure compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994 on the utilization and liquidation of funds transfers.
observations.managementAction: No action was undertaken by the Management in CY 2023.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant was not able to locate the MOA entered with the source agencies. The concerned department heads were not able to secure copies of MOA. The unexpended balances remained unremitted to source agencies.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: • Accountable Officers to immediately liquidate their cash advances, otherwise, consider deducting the corresponding amounts from the money due them; and • Municipal Accountant: ➢ Not to grant additional cash advance to those with outstanding balance and ensure that advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year; ➢ Review the cash advances of those employees who were no longer connected to the Municipality, assess the possibility of requiring them to liquidate their cash advances, and take appropriate action thereon. Henceforth, ensure that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of each year.
observations.managementAction: No action was undertaken by the Management in CY 2023.
observations.reason: The cash advances remained unliquidated as of December 31, 2023. The Municipal Accountant continued to grant additional cash advances to employees with outstanding balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: • Instruct the Municipal Engineer (ME) and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to submit the list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year; • Inform the Audit Team thru the Supervising Auditor of posting of appropriate project signboards and/or public notices 10 days upon award of infrastructure projects and start of the program/activity; and • Instruct the ME and the MPDC to ensure that signboards of all infrastructure projects of the Municipality are provided with the relevant information in compliance with Annex A of COA Circular No. 2013-004. Henceforth, submit the above required list within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: No action was undertaken by the Management in CY 2023.
observations.reason: The list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during CY 2023 and its monthly reports were not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct MPDC to ensure that all development projects to be funded out of the 20% DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready. Henceforth, comply with guidelines set in the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% DF of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: No action was undertaken by the Management in CY 2023.
observations.reason: Most of the 20% DF projects were still unimplemented as of year-end due to backlogs and failed biddings; Municipality not compliant with JMC No.1.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the LDRRMO to submit the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF for CY 2022 on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and the Local Development Council in compliance with Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002. Henceforth, ensure that the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF are submitted to the Audit Team on time.
observations.managementAction: No action was undertaken by the Management in CY 2023.
observations.reason: The monthly reports for CY 2023 were submitted in bulk via electronic mail only on March 15, 2024; reports for CY 2023 were not submitted within prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to remit the tax withheld of P483,953.89 from officers/employees, contractors/suppliers and other entities to the BIR. Henceforth, ensure prompt and full remittance of taxes withheld pursuant to existing rules.
observations.managementAction: No action was undertaken by the Management in CY 2023.
observations.reason: Taxes withheld in CY 2023 were not fully remitted to the BIR as of December 31, 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that the succeeding reports and documents are submitted within the prescribed period pursuant to existing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: No action was undertaken by the Management in CY 2023.
observations.reason: Financial statements and related reports were not submitted within the prescribed period; RPCPPE and other reports were not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: • Municipal Treasurer/GSO Designate to submit the RPCPPE to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year in compliance with Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I; • Municipal Accountant to furnish/present the Audit Team with PPE Ledger Cards prepared and maintained by the Accounting Office and the complete and updated Property Cards prepared and maintained by the Municipal Treasurer/GSO Designate; and • Municipal Treasurer/GSO Designate and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records regularly in order to establish with certainty, the valuation, completeness and existence of the PPE. Henceforth, submit the corresponding inventory report within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: No action was undertaken by the Management in CY 2023.
observations.reason: No RPCPPE was submitted for the inventory of PPE in CY 2023. Municipal Accountant submitted some PPE Ledger Cards only. No reconciliation made due to absence of stock cards and RPCPPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: • Coordinate with concerned offices to seek or locate the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) entered into with the Source Agencies or any documents stating the purpose of the fund transfer and the requirements in implementing and reporting of the unutilized PPAs totaling P6,886,466.25; • Department heads concerned to secure copies of MOA from the Source Agencies and determine if these projects are still valid for implementation, otherwise remit the amount to the concerned sourced agency; • Municipal Engineer and Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to facilitate the implementation of the specific projects with funds received from NGAs totaling P5,971,851.86 in accordance with the provision/s stated in the MOA; and • Municipal Treasurer to remit the unexpended fund balance of P92,432.01 to the source agencies in compliance with the MOA. Henceforth, ensure compliance with COA Circular No. 94-013.
observations.managementAction: No action was undertaken by the Management in CY 2023.
observations.reason: Municipality was not able to locate MOAs; funds remained unutilized/unremitted; no action by concerned departments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: • Submit to the Audit Team copies of JEVs and necessary supporting documents of the claims listed in the Accounts Payable totaling P3,153,848.64, if there is any, for our verification; and • Ensure that all claims against government funds are properly supported with complete supporting documentation in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: No action was undertaken by the Management in CY 2023.
observations.reason: There were claims listed in the Accounts Payable account as of December 31, 2023 without supporting documents; no JEVs/supporting documents submitted to Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC to strictly adhere and comply with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 on the prompt submission of perfected contracts and its supporting documents, to ensure timely review, evaluation and verification thereof.
observations.managementAction: No action was undertaken by the Management in CY 2023.
observations.reason: Copies of perfected contracts and supporting documents were not submitted; Municipality did not comply with prompt submission requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to: • Submit the list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year and its monthly monitoring report within the prescribed period; and • Inform the Audit Team within 10 days after award of project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted to facilitate validation.
observations.managementAction: No action was undertaken by the Management in CY 2023.
observations.reason: The list of all on-going government PPAs and monthly monitoring reports were not submitted to the Audit Team; Audit Team not informed of posting of project signboards.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to remit the tax withheld of P543,073.60 from officers/employees, contractors/suppliers and other entities to the BIR. Henceforth, ensure prompt and full remittance of taxes withheld pursuant to Section 2.58(a) of BIR RR No. 2-98, to avoid incurrence of interest as defined under Section 2.80(c) of BIR RR No. 11-2018.
observations.managementAction: No action was undertaken by the Management in CY 2023.
observations.reason: The remaining taxes withheld were not fully remitted to the BIR as of December 31, 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MDRRMO to submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRM Council and the Local Development Council in compliance with Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002. Henceforth, ensure strict compliance with the reporting requirements of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: No action was undertaken by the Management in CY 2023.
observations.reason: The Municipality still not compliant with the reporting requirements; monthly report for CY 2021 not submitted within prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor require the: • Accountable Officers (AOs) to liquidate their cash advances, otherwise consider deducting the corresponding amounts from the money due them; • Municipal Accountant: ➢ Not to grant additional cash advance to those with outstanding balance and see to it that advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year for fair presentation of financial statements and to allow the granting of succeeding cash advance only when the previous one is fully liquidated; ➢ To exert extra effort to locate supporting documents to support the unaccounted cash advances balance of P653,241.66; ➢ To further review the cash advances of those employees who were no longer connected to the Municipality, assess the possibility of requiring them to liquidate their cash advances, and take appropriate action thereon. Henceforth, ensure proper granting, utilization and liquidation of all cash advances within the prescribed period in accordance with existing rules and regulations and for the fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: No action was undertaken by the Management in CY 2023.
observations.reason: The outstanding cash advances remained unliquidated as of December 31, 2023. Supporting documents were not retrieved; some cash advances were not reconciled.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to make a detailed review of the accounts to determine the cause/s of the unreconciled balances and make the appropriate adjustments in the books of accounts to present the correct balances of the affected accounts for their fair presentation in the financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: No action was undertaken by the Management in CY 2023.
observations.reason: There is still a significant amount of unreconciled balances. The Municipal Accountant has not yet identified the discrepancies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: • Require the Municipal Treasurer to pursue the collection of P188,339.25 by sending demand letters to the identified debtors; • Instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to locate the supporting documents to identify the debtor and enforce collection; • Make representation with the Sangguniang Bayan to pass a Resolution authorizing him to file the request for the write-off of the dormant accounts to the COA in case actions taken to implement recommendations a and b, above, failed and proved to be futile, by following the procedures prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 16, 2016.
observations.managementAction: No action was undertaken by the Management in CY 2023.
observations.reason: The supporting documents to identify the debtor and enforce collection were not located; no request for write-off filed to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: • Instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to continue the regular reconciliation of their records and adjust any discrepancies that will be noted in order to establish the correct cash account balances in the financial statements; • Require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with Item 33(a) of the Revised Cash Examination Manual by depositing all collections promptly and intact to avoid possible loss or misuse of funds.
observations.managementAction: No action was undertaken by the Management in CY 2023.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer still was not compliant with depositing all collections promptly and intact; no reconciliation made between Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer resulting in unreconciled balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: • Municipal Planning and Development Office, with the assistance of the Municipal GAD Focal Point System to: ➢ Institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation; ➢ Undertake gender analysis to identify and confirm existing gender issues and ensure that the programs, projects and activities are responsive to the said issues.
observations.managementAction: No action was undertaken by the Management in CY 2023.
observations.reason: Information on the GAD database and gender analysis was not submitted to the Audit Team; report or proof of gender analysis not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Environmental and Natural Resources Office and the Solid Waste Management Board to: • Develop and operate a sanitary landfill as disposal site for wastes of the Municipality which shall be designed and operated in accordance with the guidelines specified under RA No. 9003; • Provide the necessary support in enjoining the barangays to establish their own MRFs and to strictly implement waste segregation; • Secure the approval of its 10-year SWMP from the National Solid Waste Management Commission; and • Strictly comply with all other prescribed procedures and guidelines under RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: No action was undertaken by the Management in CY 2023.
observations.reason: No report submitted regarding action taken; no reason provided for non-implementation; SWMP not submitted/approved.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Agriculturist to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer, OIC- Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and source agencies for the implementation of the projects.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Agriculturist was able to communicate with the aforementioned agency regarding the implementation of the projects. Moreover, the fund received for the Construction of the Community Fish Landing Center amounting to P3,000,000.00 was reverted back to the National Treasury due to difficulty in securing environmental compliance.
observations.reason: The Municipality is yet to implement the aforementioned projects which were subject to the extension approval of the source agency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: • instruct the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to submit the list of all on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year; and • inform the Audit Team thru the SA and the ATL of posting of appropriate project signboards and/or public notices 10 days upon award of infrastructure projects and start of the program/activity. Henceforth, submit the above required list within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer and the Municipal MPDO submitted the list of all on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented; and informed the Audit Team thru the SA and the ATL of posting of appropriate project signboards and/or public notices 10 days upon award of infrastructure projects and start of the program/activity.
observations.reason: The Quarterly Reports were submitted to the Audit Team within the period. However, the Annual Report for the succeeding year (CY 2024) were not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the person in-charge for the titling of parcels of land to: • request/ask help from other government agencies on how to secure the titles for the Items 1 to 3 of Annex J wherein no documents can be found in the Municipality; • support the claim that the Municipality does not own Item recorded in the books of accounts; • expedite the processing of land records on file and for title for Items 21 to 27 of Annex J. Items 25 and 26 refer to elevated water tank of the Municipality with on-going process of titling.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality have requested/asked help from other government agencies on how to secure the titles for Items 1 to 3 of Annex J wherein no available documents can be found in the Municipality. The Municipality have exerted effort to secure the titles for required documents pertinent to land titling/Special Patent Application.
observations.reason: On-going gathering of documents for land titling/Special Patent Application.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to expedite the gathering of pertinent documents required under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 16, 2016 so that authority from the Sangguniang Bayan through a Resolution authorizing him to file the request for write-off of uncollectible receivables totaling P250,000.00 is secured/obtained and eventually, the write-off of these accounts be undertaken upon approval by the Commission on Audit.
observations.reason: On-going gathering of the required documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
● Secure a copy of the Memorandum of Agreement was refunded to its respective agencies.
● Henceforth, ensure compliance with the provisions of the COA Circular on the utilization and liquidation of fund transfers.
observations.managementAction: A total of P4,989,950.95 was refunded to its respective agencies.
observations.reason: A copy of the MOA for the program Local Government Support for Local Infrastructure with a total amount of P500,000.00 was not secured.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMO to carefully study the feasibility of the projects/activities before its inclusion in the Municipal Disaster Risk and Management Plan (MDRRMP) to ensure its implementation in order for the Municipality to be adequately prepared for any upcoming disaster/calamity and reduce its risk. Henceforth, ensure the implementation of all planned disaster PPAs included in MDRRMP, particularly those included in the 70 percent LDRRMF-MF in accordance with RA No. 10121.
observations.managementAction: A total of P4,161,248.89 MF was utilized.
observations.reason: Various programmed PPAs were not implemented.

observations.managementAction: Submitted a copy of the annual LDRRMFIP and/or the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF for the months of January and February 2023.
observations.reason: Submission of copy of LDRRMFIP and monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF for the remaining months of CY 2023 was not made.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO)-Designate and all other members of Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (MSWMB) expedite the: ● Preparation of the revised 10-Year SWMP and submit the same to the National Solid Waste Management Council (NSWMC) and to the Environment Management Bureau (EMB) to ensure that programs/projects for SWM are consistent with the National Solid Waste Management Framework (NSWMF); and ● Process the documentary requirements regarding the construction of sanitary landfill.
observations.managementAction: Noted. The Waste Analysis and Characterization Study was conducted in the last quarter of 2023; hence, the report has not yet been completed. The title of the land, which is a prerequisite for submitting the application for the construction of a sanitary landfill was not secured.
observations.reason: The Waste Analysis and Characterization Study report has not yet been completed. The title of the land required for the sanitary landfill application was not secured.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor send a final written notice to the contractor, otherwise, take legal actions to compel the contractor to refund the un-recouped advance payment of P7,384,897.05.
observations.managementAction: Noted. The Municipal Mayor has already sent a written notice and taken legal steps, and the contractor has been blacklisted. However, because the contractor cannot be located, the refund of the amount is still pending.
observations.reason: Because the contractor cannot be located, the refund of the amount is still pending.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MPDO and Municipal Engineer to:
 a. Ensure that all on-going projects and those that are to be implemented during the year are submitted at the beginning of each year.
 b. Submit the monthly monitoring report and ensure that it is as current and updated as possible.
 c. Inform the Auditor within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices were already posted for validation; and
 d. Henceforth, strictly comply with items 2.1, 3.1 and 3.2 of COA Circular No. 2013-004.
observations.managementAction:
The responsible OIC-officers will ensure continuous and compliance with the mentioned.
MPDC d three projects 2024 2024 three projects was implemented while remaining two going.
observations.reason: No further compliance. To be determined during the audit of the CY 2024 transactions on the first half of CY 2025.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDC and the MPDO to ensure that all development projects to be funded out of the 20 percent DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready.
Henceforth, comply with guidelines set in the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 in the appropriation and utilization of the 20 percent DF of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer, in a letter dated May 6, 2024, stated that they agree to comply with the recommendation which is to ensure all development projects funded from 20 percent Municipal Development Fund (MDF) are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready to achieve desired socio-economic outcomes. They also pledge to comply with the guidelines of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1.
observations.reason: Three development projects with a total appropriation of P3,000,000.00 from CY 2023 were not well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready; reiterated in Audit Observation No. 10, Page Nos. 57 to 59.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the designated GSO to regularly monitor the expiration of all motor vehicles registration and its timely renewal/registration to avoid penalties and fines as well as undue risk. Otherwise, any surcharges, interests, penalties and expenses that may be incurred will be the personal liability of the GSO who is responsible for the delay or non-registration of the vehicles.
observations.managementAction: The newly designated OIC-GSO is currently exhausting all efforts to retrieve the documents, if any, related to the unregistered vehicles. (OIC GSO/LCRO I) The OIC GSO is currently exhausting all efforts to retrieve the documents, if any, related to the unregistered vehicles. For those vehicles with unrenewed license, the OIC GSO will coordinate with the LTO for the computation of the penalties related to non-renewal of registration.
observations.reason: The OIC GSO is facing challenges retrieving the documents since the related transactions happened in CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards Committee and the Municipal Accountant to exert effort to fast track and exhaust all possible means to submit the information/data together with the status of these projects and for the immediate recovery of the long outstanding advances to contractors.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is still in the process of retrieving information related to this said advances to contractors. The Municipal Accountant will continue to exhaust all possible means to submit the information related to the said advances to contractors and recover the outstanding amounting to P755,877.93.
observations.reason: The Municipality was still not able to gather the documents/information needed to establish accuracy and validity; thus the said amount P755,877.93 recorded in CY 2018 and prior years, remains unrecouped.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality comply with the documentary requirements in filing for the request for the write-off of receivables with the Commission on Audit (COA) in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is still in the process of completing all the necessary supporting documents. The Municipal Accountant has initially requested the write off of these accounts together with the submission of various documents supporting the claim that these accounts are already unrecoverable.
observations.reason: The Municipality is still in the process of completing the necessary supporting documents required for the write-off request.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer to continue their effort in facilitating the immediate titling of the subject lots in the name of the Municipality to avoid contingent claims that may arise in the future in compliance with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality is still facilitating the remaining lands. The Municipal Assessor was able to secure the titling of one parcel of land during this year and the payment of the necessary duties and taxes has been made. The Municipal Assessor coordinated with the Municipal Budget Officer to allot the corresponding budget for such.
observations.reason: Out of the five parcels, three remain untitled as at year end of CY 2023.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the GSO to ensure that all insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with appropriate insurance with the GSIS in compliance with the provision of COA Circular No. 92-30 and RA No. 656. Considering the list of insurable properties identified by the COA based on AOM 2024-013.
observations.managementAction: The designated GSO is still in the process of completing the required documents for the application of insurance to GSIS. The OIC-GSO coordinated with the GSIS regarding the new guidelines for application of insurance with the GSIS, while considering the list of insurable properties identified by the COA based on AOM 2024-013.
observations.reason: No additional property was insured due to ongoing retrieval and completion of required documents for the GSIS application.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer/GSO-Designate to institute measures to properly and completely dispose of all fully-depreciated and unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445 and Sections 379 to 381 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: Not implemented
observations.reason: Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) was prepared and submitted, however actual disposal was not prioritized by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the immediate titling of subject lots in the name of the Municipality and continuously coordinate with other government agencies to avoid contingent claims that may arise in the future in compliance with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: Not implemented
observations.reason: The holder of the mother title is still unknown and the rightful heir is not yet identified. Various individuals are claiming to be heirs, but no documents have been presented to support their claims. The Municipality was not able to facilitate the transfer of title for the remaining untitled lots due to issues with identifying the rightful owner of the land.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Acting Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor to coordinate with the Bids and Awards Committee to expedite the surveying activity and the completion of the titling of the four lots in the name of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Two out of four parcels of land in the lot numbers stated on the titles were supported by TCT in the name of the Municipality.
observations.reason: There is a difference between the lot numbers stated on the titles between the Certificate of Registration from Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) and the Municipality. Moreover, the large amount needed for titling of the lot acquired in CY 2022 for Multi-purpose Hall and Evacuation Center and lot acquired in CY 2023 for Bantigue Elementary School affects the interest of contractors to participate bidding for titling. In view thereof, a budget allocation for the titling of lands will be proposed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer to assist the Acting Municipal Treasurer in the providing the necessary supporting documents required by the GSIS to immediately process their application for property insurance of all insurable properties of the Municipality. Henceforth, ensure that all insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with existing appropriate insurance for the GSIS, to insure against any insurable risk the significant/most used properties, and proposal for the allocation of budget.
observations.reason: List of Uninsured Properties with the status/reason for non-implementation): • School Buildings with intention to donate where not used properties, donated (with reassessment of application to GSIS); • Donated Health Center Building (attached in an Building); and • Construction and Heavy Equipment (for inquiry to GSIS if for application).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to establish the availability of the cash pertaining to these unutilized trust funds and after which, require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit to the accounts of the source agencies the unexpended/unutilized balances of the trust funds or to the National Treasury for unidentified source agencies.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant coordinated with the Municipal Treasurer to establish the availability of the cash pertaining to these funds. However, the total amount of P1,419,928.22 remains unremitted to the Treasury.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the former Municipal Treasurer to submit the accounts, reports and accounting records prior to August 2004 in order to ascertain the correct extent of their accountabilities. We also reiterated the recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the former Municipal Treasurer to submit the Disbursement Vouchers and supporting documents and the Reports of Disbursements to facilitate reconciliation and to be able to account, once and for all, the correct amount due from them.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor filed a complaint-affidavit to the Office of the Ombudsman against the former Municipal Treasurer and the former Assistant Municipal Treasurer and the report was received by the Ombudsman for Luzon on August 30, 2023. The recommendation was no longer doable hence it was recommended to be closed.
observations.reason: Despite repeated follow up letters and actions, the former accountable officers did not submit the required records; most of the necessary documents were lost due to passage of time.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the relieved Municipal Treasurer (Mr. Leovino V. Ayuma) to deposit intact with authorized government banks (AGDBs) all undeposited collections of P3,094,631.96 as prescribed by Section 69(4) of PD No. 1445 and Section 32 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor sent follow-up letters to the relieved Municipal Treasurer (Mr. Leovino V. Ayuma) instructing him to deposit intact with authorized government banks (AGDBs) all undeposited collections. A Demand Letter was issued. Until the current period, no comments, action or explanations were heard from the former Municipal Treasurer. The Municipal Treasurer sent follow-up letter.
observations.reason: The collections remained undeposited and the former Municipal Treasurer did not respond; the Audit Team issued a Demand Letter.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant: a. to identify the details of unaccounted Advances to Contractors totaling P208,902.20; and b. Demand for the immediate refund of the unrecouped advances from the contractor of the completed projects.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was not able to identify the details of the unaccounted Advances to Contractors due to passage of time. The Municipal Accountant failed to demand the immediate refund of the unrecouped advances.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant was not able to identify the details of unaccounted balances and did not demand immediate refund; records missing due to passage of time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: • instruct the Municipal Engineer (ME) and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to submit the list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities (“PPA”) and those that are to be implemented during the year and its monthly reporting within the prescribed period; and • inform the Audit Team of posting of appropriate project signboards and/or public notices 10 days upon award of infrastructure projects and start of the program/activity. Henceforth, submit the above required list within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: The MPDO, Municipal Engineer and others provided reasons for non-submission; MPDC submitted the list for CYs 2023 and 2024 on April 23, 2024. Some monitoring reports were submitted quarterly rather than monthly. Notification to Audit Team was made only quarterly. Municipality intends to submit required lists within prescribed period.
observations.reason: MPDO was not able to submit the list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented within the prescribed period. Monthly monitoring reports were not submitted to the Audit Team; notification was only on a quarterly basis.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to send written notices or demands to the Accountable Officers to immediately settle the unliquidated cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Out of P300,260.11, the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant to send written notices or demands to the Accountable Officers to immediately settle the unliquidated cash advances. In CY 2023, cash advances of P60,430.00 were granted and remain unliquidated at year-end. The Municipal Accountant has undertaken actions to request settlement.
observations.reason: Several cash advances remain unliquidated; former Municipal Treasurer long outstanding issues persist.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer to verify the details of the unremitted amounts or the accumulated/unreconciled discrepancies in the Due to BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth accounts, and reconciliation for their fair presentation in the financial statements in accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1, and remit whatever balances due to the agencies concerned to avoid incurrence of penalties and surcharges.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer are to verify the details and reconciling items of the Due to BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth accounts; remittances were made for various amounts and some balances were remitted in early 2024. Reconciliation is still on-going.
observations.reason: Verification of balances to the BIR, GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG were still on-going; reconciliation of records still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MT to strictly adhere with the regulations and deposit all municipal collections promptly and intact in order to protect municipal funds against risk of loss and possible misuse.
observations.reason: The MT was not able to make her collections deposited promptly and intact as of year-end due to its geographical location which is an island Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor should require the Municipal Assessor to continue exerting efforts in securing all requirements/documents needed for the titling of the said lots in the name of the Municipality to avoid third party claims in the future and possible land disputes.
observations.reason: Titling process is still on-going as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipality, through the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (MSWMB), work for the completion of their 10-year SWMP and secure the approval of their 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan from the NSWMC. Also, we recommended to expedite the closure of their open dump site as of year-end.
observations.reason: The 10-year SWMP is still in the process of revision/approval and the open dump site closure was on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to: a. Ensure that all development projects to be funded out of the 20 percent DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready; and b. Henceforth, comply with the guidelines set in Department of Budget and Management-Department of Finance-Department of the Interior and Local Government–(DBM-DOF-DILG) Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 1 in the appropriation and utilization of the 20 percent DF of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: MPDC-Head were amenable to the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Three projects with appropriations totaling P7,162,895.60 were not implemented this CY 2023 due to two failed biddings of the first and second project and resolving the discrepancy of account title of the third project between the Annual Budget and Annual Investment Program

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Acting Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to expedite the preparation of all necessary documents, proceed with the appraisal and prioritize the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties to prevent further deterioration of the same and to realize additional income from the proceeds of saleable items. Afterwards, provide the Municipal Accountant with the copy of completed Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) to support the dropping of disposed PPEs in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Resolutions were created relative to the disposal activities, and the Disposal Committee activities, and the Disposal remain undisposed of as of December 31, 2023.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal Assessor to: a. accelerate the titling of the two remaining lots whose titling process were already commenced in CY 2018; and b. immediately start the process of the titling for the six lots where legal documents were found, continue exerting more efforts in locating the documents of the five lots and make all the necessary actions /representations with the DENR and RD in the titling of the lots.
observations.reason: The titling process of the two lots are still on-going as of year-end. The Municipality will coordinate with and seek assistance from the Community Environment and Natural Resources Office (CENRO) for the processing of the titling of the six lots where legal documents were found. Meanwhile, the Municipal Assessor will continue its effort in locating the necessary documents for the titling of the remaining five lots.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO)- Designate to work on securing all the requirements needed for the issuance of the ECC and establishment of the proposed Sanitary Landfill pursuant to RA No. 9003. We also recommended that the Municipality, through the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, follow-up the status of the review of the revised plan and secure the approval of its 10-year SWMP from the NSWMC pursuant to the provisions under the above cited regulations.
observations.reason: The MENRO was still not able to secure the requirements needed for the issuance of the ECC and establishment of the proposed Sanitary Landfill. The 10-year SWMP of the Municipality is still subject for revision.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous years’ recommendations that the Municipal Mayor: • require the MGSO and the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with each other and complete the reconciliation of the affected PPE accounts to establish the correct PPE balances, and to investigate and make necessary adjustments in their respective records, if warranted. • Instruct the MGSO to prepare an updated Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and submit a copy to the Audit Team; and • cause the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties in accordance with existing rules and regulations to avoid further deterioration of the assets and generate additional income for the Municipality. Additionally, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor: • Review and revise, if necessary, the composition of the Inventory Committees, and facilitate the conduct of One-time cleansing of PPE accounts, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006; and • Adopt internal strategies that shall cause the periodic reconciliation between accounting and GSO records to ensure the accuracy of the PPE balances, in accordance with Section 114 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the MGSO have not yet reconciled their respective PPE records. With the new developments, the audit observation was updated and presented anew in Audit Observation No. 1, Page Nos. 29 to 31, Part II of this report. The Municipality is preparing for the conduct of One-Time Cleansing to reconcile their records and to facilitate disposal of all unserviceable properties of the Municipality.
observations.reason: The MGSO was not able to prepare and submit an updated IIRUP. An Executive Order No. 017, s. 22 was issued by the Municipal Mayor to create an Asset Disposal Committee in the Municipality with the primary task of facilitating the disposal of unserviceable PPEs. The Municipality is preparing for the conduct of One-Time Cleansing to reconcile their records and to facilitate disposal of all unserviceable properties of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit to the Audit Team the CRAAF for CY 2022 accompanied by the individual RAAFs of the Accountable Officers. Henceforth, ensure that the CRAAF is submitted within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer incurred delays ranging from 27 to 299 days in the submission of CRAAF due to the overwhelming volume of work during the reporting period and the distance between the Municipality and the Audit Team’s office. Copy of CRAAF for CY 2022 was submitted to the Audit Team on August 16, 2024.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer incurred delays in the submission of the CRAAF due to the overwhelming volume of work during the reporting period and the distance between the Municipality and the Audit Team’s office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MGSO to complete the filing of the application for the insurance coverage of the public market and slaughterhouse and ensure that all insurable properties owned by the Municipality are covered with appropriate property insurance with the GSIS in strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 and RA No. 656.
observations.managementAction: The insurance for the Public Market and slaughterhouse was paid under OR Nos. 5650014897 and 5650014898, respectively. In addition, seven motor vehicles remaining insurable were insured in 2023. The Municipality is in the process of identifying the properties for proper turnover to the respective end-users/beneficiaries. Due to budget limitations, filing for insurance for remaining insurable properties of the Municipality was not performed.
observations.reason: The Municipality is in the process of identifying the properties for proper turnover to the respective end-users/beneficiaries. Also, due to budget limitations, filing for insurance for remaining insurable properties of the Municipality was not performed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: • Require the Municipal Accountant to allocate time for the preparation of the necessary documentation to support the request for authority to write-off dormant receivables totaling P1,280,833.21 for its filing with the COA through the Audit Team following the guidelines and procedures provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and • Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to intensify efforts to collect receivables by constant follow-up and by sending collection notice to make the accounts current and to collect receivables totaling P218,760.00 from the beneficiaries of the Livelihood Programs.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the Municipality. The balance P110,000.00 remained uncollected. The Municipality was not able to allocate time for the preparation of the necessary documentation to support the request for authority to write-off dormant receivables. No request for write-off has been filed with the COA.
observations.reason: The Municipality did not allocate time to prepare the necessary documentation for the write-off request due to COVID-19 restrictions and volume of work; hence, no request was filed with COA and follow-up collection efforts were not fully carried out.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Agriculturist to: • Put into writing the change in the agreement as regards payment schedule to protect the interest of the Municipality and ensure collectability of the amount of P51,000.00 from farmer-beneficiaries due at the planting time; and • Continue to monitor the collection of the fifty percent of the price per seedlings due for payment at planting time.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the Municipality. There was no collection for the year, and it was disclosed that most beneficiaries are no longer capable to settle their accountabilities due to financial difficulty. However, no request for write-off was filed with the COA.
observations.reason: Beneficiaries were financially incapable to settle their accounts; no request for write-off was filed with COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MPDC to ensure that all development projects to be funded out of the 20% DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready. Henceforth, comply with guidelines set in the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% DF of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 2.
observations.reason: The management has not yet complied with the guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% DF due to various unimplemented projects as of December 31, 2023.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor to continue exerting extra effort in retrieving the documents required by the Register of Deeds to facilitate the immediate titling and registration of the lots in the name of the Municipality to avoid contingent claims that may arise in the future and in compliance with Section 39(2) of PD No.1445 and Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386 and for the fair presentation of the Land account in the financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.reason: The parcels of land remained unsupported with TCT in favor of the Municipality. The Municipality needs extra time to do the surveying of the actual property lines. Various titles were with pending court orders, hence cannot be transferred immediately to the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the balances of the individual TF bank accounts and other assets with its corresponding liabilities to establish the correct individual TF account balances in the books of accounts.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary corrective actions or adjustments after reconciliation to reflect the correct individual TF account balances in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Supporting documents such as Statement of Receipts and Disbursements pertaining the TF transactions are yet to be located, the adjustments remain unimplemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC – Municipal Treasurer to henceforth require all MCs to remit their collections daily.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: The MCs’ collections remittance was not prioritized.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC – Municipal treasurer to revise/update the rates in the Local Revenue Code subject for deliberation of the Sangguniang Bayan in compliance with RA No.7160 to maximize its taxing power and to provide additional funds for its operations.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: The Municipality is still in the process of revising the 2008 Local Revenue Code. They requested assistance from the Bureau of Local Government Finance regarding training related to the matter.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) and the Municipal Agriculturist ensure that the aforementioned development projects with a total appropriation of P300,000.00 are implemented.
Henceforth, ensure that all development projects funded of the 20 percent DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: The development projects were cancelled as these were no longer feasible considering the changing weather conditions and time constraints which may affect its overall execution. Its corresponding 20 percent fund is subject to reappropriation for other development projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the LDRRMO- Designate to:
a. Ensure that the Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (DRRMF) is submitted within the required period to facilitate its timely review; and
b. Henceforth, ensure that the LDRRMF is fully utilized according to plan in order to achieve the objectives of RA No. 10121.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: The CY 2023 LDRRMF was prepared and submitted quarterly and upon demand. The CY 2023 LDRRMF was substantially utilized. The reports on Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF for CY 2023 were only prepared quarterly and submitted to the Audit Team upon demand.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to expedite the titling process of the four identified parcels of land.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: The titling process is still ongoing. The LGU has already started in preparations for the titling of the subject properties and coordinated with the different government agencies to facilitate the titling process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor initiate the filing of claim against the estate of the deceased former municipal official, through his legal heirs, for the refund of the unliquidated cash advance amounting to P156,240.00; and request for authority to write-off dormant accounts of P54,500.00; and to correct the recording of CAs as appropriate.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Despite the Demand letter has been served to the legal heirs of the deceased former mayor, the unliquidated CA is yet to be collected from the legal heirs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to remit the balance of P1,000,336.46 to the source agencies pursuant to COA Circular No. 94-013 so that it can be used to finance other projects or by other agencies needing assistance.
observations.reason: The unexpended/unutilized balance were still not remitted/deposited to the source agency since it was still for reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to identify the recorded land in the Special Education Fund for the fair presentation of the Land account in the financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is still searching for the available documents pertaining to this lot.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant is still searching for the available documents pertaining to this lot.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor strictly enforce the immediate settlement of the disallowance pursuant to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and COA Order of Execution.
observations.managementAction: ND No. 16-040-101-(15) was settled on February 15, 2022 per NSSDC No. 2022-01 amounting to P125,000.00 while the disallowance amounting to P879,100.63 was still not settled by the absconding former Municipal Accountant to date.
observations.reason: The disallowance amounting to P879,100.63 was still not settled by the absconding former Municipal Accountant to date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and Assistant Municipal Treasurer to strictly monitor the intact deposit of collections daily or on the next banking day in compliance with Section 32 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I, to avoid possible misuse of funds.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer is planning to negotiate with the bank to cater the pick-up of daily deposits of collections to comply with the recommendation, however for CY 2023, collections were still not deposited intact on daily basis or the following day ranging from one to 48 days.
observations.reason: For CY 2023, collections were still not deposited intact on daily basis or the following day ranging from one to 48 days.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Designated MENRO to continually work for the approval of the 10-year SWM Plan (CY 2020-2029) by the Sangguniang Bayan and eventually by the National Solid Waste Management Commission in the compliance with the requirements of RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: The MENRO is continuously facilitating the plan which is now for the period of CYs 2023 to 2032 and already submitted to the EMB-Calabarzon for final checking.
observations.reason: The approved 10-year SWM Plan was still not approved; the MENRO is continuously facilitating the plan which is now for the period of CYs 2023 to 2032 and already submitted to EMB-Calabarzon for final checking.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile with the Provincial Accounting Office the balances of the Due from Local Government Units and Due to LGUs accounts between the Municipality and the Provincial Government and make the adjusting entries in the books of accounts of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Determination and reconciliation of accounts is ongoing; Municipal Accountant continues efforts to reconcile with Provincial Government.
observations.reason: Reconciliation is still on-going.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Development Council (MDC) and the OIC-MPDC to:
a. Ensure that all development projects to be funded out of the 20 percent DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready; and
b. Henceforth, comply with guidelines set in the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 in the appropriation and utilization of the 20 percent DF of the Municipality.
