and submit the same to the DOST Accountant.

b. Municipal Agriculturist to secure the copy of the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA)
for the said grant to verify if the remaining balance of P141,600.00 is already due for
return to the SA.

Henceforth ensure that SORDs for all fund transfers are prepared and submitted to the
SAs in accordance with item 6.5 of COA Circular No. 94-013.

We recommended the following –
The Management to –
prepare the necessary documents to formally turn-over the above identified projects to the Quezon Water District;
always provide a copy of MOA to the MACCO so that it can properly record the transaction based on the stipulations of the MOA.
The Municipal Accountant’s Office to make the necessary adjustments on the affected accounts, supported with working papers and necessary documents, and submit the same to this office.

We recommended to the Municipal Accounting Office to make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the misstatements in the financial statements as of December 31, 2023, supported with working papers and all necessary documents, and submit the same to this office for evaluation.

We recommended to the management to do the following:
Instruct the Municipal Enterprises Management Officer and the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant with a list of uncollected revenues as of year-end; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record in the appropriate income and receivable accounts the accrued revenues as of year-end.

We recommended to the management to do the following:
Submit the herein-identified lacking supporting documents to the disbursements; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to check the completeness of the documents supporting the disbursement following the relevant COA Circular on Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transactions before approving the disbursement voucher.

We recommended to the management to –
Revisit SB Resolution No. 14th SB 2019-69 and make amendments to ensure that it is in line with DBM’s Compensation Policy Guideline No. 98-1 dated March 23, 1998 and also indicate the monthly honorarium to be given to the entitled national officials assigned in the LGU.
Starting January 2024, pay only to those who are entitled to receive additional honorarium and the rate thereof must be based on the amended SB Resolution;
Ensure that all honoraria payments to personnel assigned in the LGU are in accordance with DBM Compensation Policy Guideline No. 98-1 dated March 23, 1998.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive advise the Officer in Charge of Quezon Water System to furnish the Municipal Accountant a copy of the Monthly Billing, Reading, and Payment Monitoring Report and to advise the Municipal Accountant to strictly follow the accrual basis of accounting in accordance with IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: The Officer in Charge already submitted the monthly report to the accounting office for recording purposes.

observations.recommendation: In view of the foregoing, we reiterated our recommendation that the Management advise the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher on the transfer of the unexpended balance of QRF and DRRMF-MOOE amounting to ₱996,169.94 and submit immediately to the Office of the Auditor a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made a journal entry on the transfer of the unexpended balance of QRF and DRRMF-MOOE amounting to ₱996,169.94 and submitted to the Office of the Auditor a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive advise the Municipal Accountant to explain why he did not record the tax revenue from fines and penalties amounting to ₱885,818.43 and why he did not reconcile with the Municipal Treasurer to ascertain the amount collected as Tax Revenue.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already explained the reason for the non-recording of fines and penalties.

observations.recommendation: We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw the necessary adjusting entries to record the correct balances of the Tax Revenue, Real Property Tax Receivable and Deferred Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax Receivable and Deferred Special Education Tax.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant recorded the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balances of Tax Revenue, Real Property Tax Receivable and Deferred Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax Receivable and Deferred Special Education Tax.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management justify/explain the incurrence of cash overdraft.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer already explained the incurrence of cash overdraft.

observations.recommendation: We further recommended to the Management to require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly monitor the balance of cash in bank prior to disbursing of funds so as not to incur cash overdraft.
observations.managementAction: The Management already monitored the fund balance before the disbursement of the fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management advise the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) that in case of two failed biddings, the BAC shall invite at least three (3) suppliers or contractors, including those disqualified in previous biddings for the project, for negotiations to ensure effective competition.
observations.managementAction: The Management already complied with our audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: Likewise, we recommended management to immediately submit to the Office of the Auditor the minutes of the meeting on the discussion and negotiation with Bulanit General Construction and revision made on the terms, conditions, specifications, and cost estimates of the project, if any.
observations.managementAction: The Management already submitted the necessary documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management thru the Local Chief Executive the following courses of action:
Require the Municipal Development Council to specify the details of the identified priority projects to be charged under the 20% Development Fund including project location, specific implementation schedule, and physical target to avoid generic or lumpsum projects.
observations.managementAction: The Management complied with our audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: Stop the practice of implementing generic or lumpsum projects without further authority from the Sanggunian to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Management complied with our audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management submit immediately a distribution list duly acknowledged by the recipients of cement and the original copy of delivery receipts to support the submitted distribution list of the procured cement for CYs 2021 and 2022.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted delivery receipts to support the delivery of cement.

observations.recommendation: We further recommended the Management to explain or justify why there were still undelivered cement despite the full payments made by LGU Quezon to CAPCON Builders.
observations.managementAction: The Management already explained why there was still undelivered cement despite the full payment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management impose liquidated damages to CAPCON Builders due to non-delivery of the procured cement within the allowed period of delivery schedule and the remaining undelivered cement totaling 9,691 bags.
observations.managementAction: The Management already imposed liquidated damages to the contractor and the latter already made payment amounting to ₱420,723.45.

observations.recommendation: We further recommended the Management to exercise due diligence in the planning and implementation of projects, programs and activities in order not to compromise the delivery of services to its constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Management complied with our audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management fully implement/utilize the planned programs, activities and projects under the DRRM Fund so that the intended benefits would be fully achieved.
observations.managementAction: The Management fully utilized their 5% LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management advise the Municipal Treasurer to enforce a more vigorous and effective tax campaign, employ a realistic and effective collection strategies, and institute legal remedies pursuant to pertinent provisions of RA 7160 in order to persuade the Taxpayers to pay their tax on time and to attain one hundred per cent 100% collection efficiency so that more programs, projects and activities can be implemented for the benefit of the Municipality’s constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Management complied with our audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management advise the Municipal Accountant to liquidate immediately the amount received from Bureau of Animal Industry and submit the liquidation report to the source agency and to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the liquidation report pertaining to the amount received from Bureau of Animal Industry.

observations.recommendation: Likewise, return/refund the unutilized balances of the fund transfers to the Provincial Government of Isabela and Department of the Interior and Local Government amounting to ₱85,615.72 and ₱15,297.50, respectively.
observations.managementAction: The Management made a communication to the Provincial Government of Isabela and DILG to use the unutilized balance for projects that will benefit its constituents.

observations.recommendation: In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Management advise the Municipal Accountant to withhold taxes on payment of gasoline to Golden Oil Exchange in compliance with Revenue Regulation No. 02-98 to avoid the possible imposition of sanction set by the NIRC.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant complied with our audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer to stop the practice of accommodating private checks of employees and private individuals using her collections.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer already stopped the accommodation of private checks.

observations.recommendation:
submit a written explanation for the incurrence of average. Otherwise, the amount shall be forfeited in favor of the government pursuant; and

properly monitor her accountabilities and observe proper safekeeping of collections by separating personal monies from the municipal funds and comply with the provision of Section 101(1) of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Accountable Officer made the necessary adjusting entries in her cashbook and reconciled the balance of cash overage.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management continue the practice of depositing collections regularly to comply with the rules and regulations as mandated by Section 69 of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 32 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I on collections and deposits
observations.managementAction: Management has committed to continue the practice of depositing collections regularly and intact as prescribed by regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accountable Officer submit the monthly Report on Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) to the COA office at the end of each month in compliance with the above-cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Accountable Officer maintained the monthly RAAF and has submitted the same to the Auditor as recommended.
The Accountable officer restituted the cash shortage and committed to adhere with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the management require the Accounting and Treasury Offices to conduct monthly reconciliation of cashbook and accounting records to ensure timely discovery of errors and to facilitate early preparation of applicable adjustments.
observations.managementAction: The Accountable Officer and the accounting unit reconciled the cashbooks and accounting records as recommended.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous audit recommendation that the Municipal Accountant thoroughly review the transactions which resulted in the negative balances of accounts and accordingly prepare adjustments to establish the accuracy and valuation thereof.
observations.managementAction: Management has complied the recommendations and the necessary adjustments were made in the books.

observations.recommendation: We commended management for its consistent adherence to Section 287 of R.A. No. 7160 by providing the required amount out of its IRA for development projects, as well as compliance to DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1, s. 2020 by including only eligible projects, all for the benefit of its constituents.
observations.managementAction: The management committed to continue the best practices of the LGU to implement PPAs effectively and efficiently in accordance with audit recommendations.
The management committed to continue the best practices of the LGU to implement PPAs effectively and efficiently in accordance with audit recommendations.
Management complied with the recommendation.
The management has complied the recommendations and instructed the concerned offices and personnel to abide with their duties and responsibilities in accordance with regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and reiterated that the members of the GAD Focal Point System use monitoring tools such as the HGDG and PIMME in the GAD planning and budgeting as well as in the preparation of the accomplishment report.
observations.managementAction: Management has complied with the recommendation.
Management has complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that within a period not exceeding ten (10) calendar days from the receipt of Notice of Award by the bidder with the LCRB, HRRB, SCRB, or SRRB, the HoPE or his duly authorized representative shall enter into a contract agreement with the winning bidder to ensure the early implementation of projects.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management file a motion for reconsideration to the Sangguniang Panlalawigan to justify the Supplemental Budget for the grant of the CNA Incentives, otherwise the paid benefits will be disallowed in audit due to defect in the legality and validity of the Appropriation Ordinance No. 14, series of 2022.
observations.managementAction: The Management through the Sangguniang Bayan made a follow-up on their motion for reconsideration regarding on the status of their budget disallowance in the Sangguniang Panlalawigan thru resolution no. 31, Series 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to be prudent not only in signing of turnover or transfer documents but also of other documents/reports detailing the transferred accountabilities for cash, cash items and accountable forms so as not to distract the integrity of the succeeding accountability reports.
observations.managementAction: Turnover of OIC – Municipal Treasurer in CY  2023 is properly documented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the Section 69 of PD No. 1445, Section 32 of the New Government Accounting System for LGUs, Vol. I and Section 30 of the Joint Circular (JC) No. 1-81 of Commission on Audit (COA) and Ministry of Finance (MOF) on depositing collections within the prescribed period and frequency in accordance with existing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The new OIC-Municipal Treasurer was able to deposit the collections within the prescribed period and frequency

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer to increase the amount of Fidelity Bond of the five accountable officers to fully cover their maximum cash accountability.
observations.managementAction: The accountable officers are now adequately bonded

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to refrain from accepting post-dated checks, in compliance to the Section 77 of the GAAM Volume II.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer prepare and submit regularly to the Office of the Auditor the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) With and Without Money Value in compliance with and Sec. 50 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units (MNGAS), Volume II.
observations.managementAction: The new OIC-Municipal Treasurer was able to prepare and submit the RAAF regularly.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior years recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to revisit the prior year transactions and conduct the reconciliation of the balances of Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts and effect the necessary adjustments as warranted to bring their balances into agreement.
observations.managementAction: The Accountant  was able to reconciled the balances of Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior years recommendation that the Management continue its effort to observe prudence in cash management by introducing austerity measures on its daily operations and providing sufficient cash back-up for due and demandable obligations.
observations.managementAction: Cash deficiencies were eliminated

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the:

Municipal Treasurer to (i) strictly enforce the remedies for the collection of delinquent RPT as expressly provided in Section 256 of R.A. No. 7160; (ii) prepare a complete Certified List of Taxpayers, bearing the names, amount due and collectibles during the year, and submit the same to the accounting unit as the basis in setting up of RPT/SEF tax receivables in the succeeding year; and (iii) institute appropriate strategies to further improve collection efficiency; 

b) Municipal Accountant to (i) set-up the RPT/SEF tax receivables at the beginning of the year based on the Certified List of Taxpayers and Tax Due prepared by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer; and (ii) maintain the corresponding subsidiary ledgers (SLs) in support of the RPT and SET Receivable balances; and

c) Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to regularly conduct reconciliation of their records.

We recommended that the 

(a) Local School Board (i) ensure that all SEF disbursements are supported with appropriate DepEd certification on the 
non-budgetary provision for the PPAs under consideration; (ii) follow the guidelines on budgeting process as prescribed by the applicable DepEd, DBM, and DILG Joint Circulars and (iii) request the Inventory Committee of the LGU to conduct physical inventory of all properties booked under SEF and prepare a Report thereon; 

(b) Local Chief Executive instruct the Accounting and Property Offices to install proper asset management controls over the SEF properties including the maintenance of appropriate records and report; and 

(c) Local Chief Executive and the Local School Board to refrain from charging ineligible expenditures to SEF pursuant to Section 4.0 of DepEd, DBM, and DILG Joint Circular (JC) No. 1, s. 2017 dated January 19, 2017.
observations.managementAction: The management already conducted inventory count on SEF PPE

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management henceforth strictly comply with the provisions of the laws on net take home pay of the employees; and refrain from entering into any contract that would result in unauthorized deductions from the monthly pay of the employees.

We recommended and the Management agreed to continue complying with the GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG regulations on the withholding and remittance of employees’ and employer contributions and loan amortizations of employees to the respective agencies.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the: • Acting City Accountant to prepare and maintain the required PPELCs and coordinate closely with the CGSO to ensure that PPELCs agree with the PCs. Henceforth, ensure strict compliance with the provisions of MNGAS for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: Not specified in the City Accountant and OIC-CGSO indicated Implemented Implemented For preparation of Recommendations Tracking Sheet (RTS) to the Regional Office with recommendation for closure for the recommendation has been reiterated with modifications. For preparation of RTS for the above recommendation considered closed for having been reiterated with modifications. Henceforth, ensure strict compliance with the provisions of MNGAS for LGUs. The Office of the City Accountant and CGSO maintained close coordination for the reconciliation of records between their offices.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor: • Instruct the Acting City Accountant and Treasurer to facilitate the return of the funds from the source agencies; • Coordinate with the Department of Social Welfare and Development Regional Office IV-A regarding the utilization/implementation of the Sustainable Livelihood Program and/or the disposition of funds for the intended purpose in case the activity could no longer be materialized. Henceforth, ensure compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994, on the utilization and liquidation of funds transfers.
observations.managementAction: Unused balance totaling P3,872,893.11 have already been returned/transferred to their respective source agencies, including the DSWD Regional Office IV-A for the Sustainable Livelihood Program since the same could no longer be implemented. JEVs were prepared and adjustments made (e.g., JEV No. 200-2023-03-200056 dated 03/14/2023 amounting to P49,692.30; JEV No. 200-2023-03-200058 dated 03/15/2023 amounting to P23,819.63; JEV No. 200-2023-03-200063 dated 03/16/2023 amounting to P892.40; JEV No. 200-2023-03-200067 dated 03/21/2023 amounting to P142.78; and JEV No. 200-2023-04-200095 dated 04/03/2023 amounting to P3,798,346.00). Coordinate with the Department of Social Welfare and Development Regional Office IV-A regarding the utilization/implementation of the Sustainable Livelihood Program and/or the disposition of funds for the intended purpose could no longer be materialized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the CGSO and the City Accountant to reconcile the variance of P19,947,621.53 between the GL and RPCI; and • City Accountant to prepare Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) and submit to the CGSO; and any adjustments, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: The inventory accounts per GL has a variance of P19,947,621.53, (CGSO) and the City Accountant to reconcile the variance of P19,947,621.53 and make the necessary adjustments to their respective records and henceforth, regularly reconcile their records and strictly comply with the MNGAS for LGUs.
observations.reason: The inventory accounts per GL and RPCI were reconciled as of December 31, 2023. The City Accountant and CGSO made regular reconciliations of their records in CY 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries for the reversion of the total balance of P1,122,210.48 to the Unappropriated Surplus of the City with a condition that appropriation shall be made available in the event that claim arises in the future. Henceforth, ensure proper monitoring of the Accounts Payable account so that claims nearing the maturity of two years are communicated to the respective payees for the processing of their claims.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant made the necessary adjusting entries for the reversion of the outstanding Accounts Payable totaling P1,122,210.48 under JEV Nos. 101-2023-3-137 and 221-2023-3-008, both dated March 28, 2023.
observations.reason: Accounts Payable monitors the Accounts Payable so that claims nearing the maturity of two years are communicated to the respective payees for the processing of their claims.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Engineer and the City Planning and Development Officer (CPDO) to: • Submit the list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year and its monthly monitoring report within the prescribed period. • Ensure that signboards of all infrastructure projects of the City are provided with the relevant information in compliance with COA Circular No. 2013-004.
observations.managementAction: The CPDO regularly submits the government PPAs report within the prescribed period. The City Engineer ensured that the infrastructure projects of the City are provided with relevant information.
observations.reason: The CGSO and City Accountant reconciliations were made and signboards of all infrastructure projects were provided with relevant information.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to record in the books of accounts the remaining 149 vials of COVID-19 vaccines and various ancillary supplies based on the fair value at the date of acquisition.
observations.managementAction: The City sent communication letter to the Office of the President on March 28, 2023 requesting that the unutilized fund be allocated for the livelihood assistance to the indigent constituents. The remaining 149 vials of COVID-19 vaccines were consumed in CY 2023.
observations.reason: The remaining vials were already consumed in CY 2023. Hence, no adjustment was made, and no inventory balance remains in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations that the Management require the CGSO and the City Accountant to coordinate for the reconciliation of their records and make the necessary adjustments to their respective records, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: The inventory accounts per GL and RPCI were reconciled and the City Accountant and CGSO made the necessary adjustments to their respective records.
observations.reason: Inventory accounts per GL and RPCI were reconciled as of December 31, 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management instruct the City Accountant to seek or locate the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) entered into with the Source Agencies or any documents stating the purpose of the fund transfer and the provision of the requirements in implementing and reporting of the unutilized PPAs totaling P12,607,562.15.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant submitted to the Audit Team copies of the Sangguniang Panlungsod (SP) Resolutions No. 18-92 and 18-93, both dated July 30, 2018, for the disposition of the unutilized balance from the Department of Health (DOH) totaling P200,000.00. The City submitted copies of MOA for former president Gloria Macapagal Arroyo fund and DOH grants as instructed by the City Mayor.
observations.reason: The City submitted to the Audit Team copies of the MOA and SP Resolutions; however, some balances remain in the books of accounts as of December 31, 2023.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations that the City Mayor require the CGSO and the City Accountant to reconcile their records regularly and make the necessary adjustments in their respective records, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: The inventory accounts per GL and RPCI were reconciled as of December 31, 2023.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
Municipal Mayor instruct the
Officers to prepare and
submit their monthly RAAF
using the prescribed format
to the Municipal Treasurer to
facilitate the preparation of
monthly CRAAF.

Henceforth, to strictly
comply with Sections 50 and
51 of MNGAS for LGUs.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer issued memorandum to the collectors and discussed with them the importance of preparing the RAAF and CRAAF.

The compliance in the preparation and submission by the collectors is continuous.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
Municipal Mayor instruct the
Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator
(MPDC) to ensure that the
aforementioned development
projects with a total
appropriation of P400,000.00
are implemented.

Henceforth, ensure that all
development projects funded
out of the 20 percent DF are
well-planned and
procurement-and-implementation-ready.
observations.managementAction:
In CY 2023, the P400,000.00 was reapproriated through Supplemental Budget No. 01 Ordinance No. 2023-01 dated June 26, 2023.

The whole amount of P400,000.00 was used for the Extension of Elias Salvador National High School Road project which has a total cost of P529,606.43.
observations.reason:
Unutilized 20 percent DF from CY 2023 P3,000,000.00 was not implemented for any development project due to lack of well-planning.

Reiterated in Observation No. 3, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
Municipal Mayor require the
Local Disaster Risk Reduction
and Management Officer
(LDRRMO) – Designate to
henceforth ensure that the
LDRRMF is fully utilized
according to plan in order to
achieve the objectives of RA
No. 10121.
observations.managementAction:
The Mayor facilitated the discussion regarding the LDRRMF-MF implementation and full utilization for CY 2023.

As committed by the LDRRMO – Designate, the Municipality has implemented and utilized the fund according to its plans which resulted to an increased LDRRMF Utilization compared to CY 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer’s Office (MTO) and other departments concerned to conduct periodic review of the Quarterly Report of Income to ascertain whether collection targets are attained to provide assurance of improved finances for the operations of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Management conducted its periodic review of the Quarterly Report of Income. Moreover, the MTO reached 101.23 percent of their target this CY 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to consider revising/updating the rates in the Local Revenue Code in compliance with RA No. 7160 to maximize its taxing power and to provide more funds for its operations.
observations.managementAction: The Management has already drafted its revised tax code and submitted it to the oversight committee for final review.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) to: a. Enact an SB Resolution for the creation of a separate LDRMMO; and b. Instruct the Head of the Human Resource Management Office to immediately fill-up the four mandatory positions of the LDRRMO as required under Section 5.2 of the NDRRMC, DILG, DBM, and CSC JMC.
observations.managementAction: The Management created a separate LDRRMO Resolution No. series of 2023 for the creation of LDRMMO dated June 26, 2023. The Management already filled-up the four mandatory positions of the LDRRMO as required under Section 5.2 of the NDRRMC, DILG, DBM, and CSC JMC.
observations.reason: The Management raised concern on the capacity of the Municipality to deliver efficiently and effectively the necessary services to ensure the safety of its constituents in the event of disaster, calamity and other fortuitous events.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Development Council (MDC) and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to:
a. Ensure that all development projects funded out of the 20 percent DF are well-planned and procurement-and- implementation ready; and
b. Henceforth, comply with the guidelines set in the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 in the appropriation and utilization of the 20 percent DF of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality re-aligned projects which were not well-planned and procurement-and- ready; and
The Management complied with the guidelines set in DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Human Resource and Management Officer of the Municipality to refrain from designating JO employees in the collection of fees and other charges of the Municipality. Henceforth, strictly comply with Section 170 of RA No. 7160 and Section 101 of P.D. No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Management complied with the rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Assistant Municipal Treasurer (MT) in coordination with the Human Resource Management Officer-Designate to stop the practice of designating JO employees in the collection of fees and other charges of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality stopped the practice of designating JO employees in the collection of fees and other charges.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MT to direct the AOs to prepare and submit the individual RAAF Forms of the AOs and their monthly RAAF to the MT using the prescribed format to facilitate the preparation of the monthly CRAAF. Henceforth, to strictly comply with Sections 50 and 51 of MNGAS for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The MT submitted the individual RAAF of the AOs to the Audit Team. Henceforth, to strictly comply with Sections 50 and 51 of the MNGAS for LGUs.
observations.reason: Sections 50 and 51 of the MNGAS for LGUs were complied by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: • Municipal Treasurer to transfer the fund to the TF; and • Municipal Accountant to make the necessary JEV to record such transfer to the TF.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant informed the Audit Team that the fund was transferred to the TF on March 1, 2023, and was recorded only on March 31, 2023, thru JEV No. 100-2023-03-0945.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Accountable Officers to immediately liquidate their outstanding CAs. Henceforth, ensure that all CAs are fully liquidated at the end of each year.
observations.managementAction: Accountable Officers submitted documents for the liquidation of their outstanding CAs. Henceforth, ensure that all CAs are fully liquidated at the end of each year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: • instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the return of the unused balance of fund transfers to the respective source agencies for the completed/implemented programs and projects; and • provide the Audit Team the MOA for various funds received from 2015 to 2017 which were not implemented as of December 31, 2022, to determine the duties and responsibilities of the parties in handling the said funds. In the absence of such MOA, comply with the provision under COA Circular 94-013 that any unused balance shall be returned to the Source Agency.
observations.managementAction: A total of P2,336,762.73 of unused balance of transfers from various source agencies were returned to the Bureau of Treasury. Available MOA has been provided to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: • Municipal Treasurer to exert effort to collect from the identified debtors and/or gather all the required documents required under COA Circular No. 2016-005 and submit the request for write-off to the COA.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer to collect from the identified debtors and coordinate with the concerned offices and gather all the required documents necessary for the request of write-off for the uncollectible accounts.
observations.reason: Lack of the available MSWDO records/document gathering on file.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: • Justify the certification that all documents are complete, despite the absence of the signature/certification of the Municipal Budget Office on the Disbursement Voucher; and • Ensure that disbursements or disposition of government funds or property shall invariably bear the approval of the proper officials, in compliance with the provisions of Section 4(5 and 6) of PD No. 1445 and Section 138 of GAAM, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Accountant commented due to its urgency, she certified as to the completeness of the attachments despite the absence of the signature of the Municipal Budget Office on the Obligation Request (OBr) since there was an attached List of Continuing Appropriations and Approved Annual Budget for CY 2021 which showed that there was an available appropriation for that object of expenditure. Also, on December 4, 2023 the Municipal Accountant submitted OBr for the Disbursement Vouchers noted bearing the signature of the next in rank of the MBO on his absence.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor, being a part of the GAD Focal Point System, ensure the implementation of the GAD Plan and Budget in order that the Municipality constituents benefit from these GAD PPAs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality appropriated P8,418,000.00 for the GAD Fund for CY 2023. Out of this, a total of P5,277,786.43 or 62.70% was utilized by the Municipality for gender-responsive projects, programs and activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Work, Other Animals and Breeding Stocks Ledger Card (WOABSLC) for each type of livestock and require the designated GSO to prepare the corresponding stock cards and reconcile the same to establish the correct balances and to investigate and adjust any discrepancy that will be noted. Furthermore, request/apply for Relief from Accountability on the dead/missing animals in accordance with COA Memorandum No. 92-751, Documentation on Petition/Requests for Relief from Accountability.
observations.managementAction: Requests for Relief from Accountability have been filed to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer to see to it that all the insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with insurance with the GSIS in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 and RA No. 656.
observations.managementAction: The Management was able to apply for insurance for all insurable properties of the Municipality with the GSIS except for the Municipal Building due to the non-availability of required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile her records with the Municipal Treasurer and make necessary adjustments in the appropriate record. Henceforth, ensure compliance with Section 111 of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445 and Section 10 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGU).
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: a. Prepare an adjusting entry to reclassify the payables totaling P2,761,794.92 to the correct accounts; and b. Henceforth, ensure that all recorded transactions in Due to LGUs account meets the definition criteria as prescribed by the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The office of the Municipal Accountant prepared JEV No. 100-2023-04-01804 dated 4/28/23 to reclassify the said payables.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDC and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDC) to: a. Ensure that all development projects to be funded out of the 20 percent DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready; and b. Henceforth, comply with guidelines set in the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 in the appropriation and utilization of the 20 percent DF of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMO to carefully study the feasibility of the projects/activities before its inclusion in the MDRRMP to ensure its implementation for the Municipality to be adequately prepared for any upcoming disaster/calamity to reduce its risk. Henceforth, ensure the implementation of all planned disaster PPAs included in the MDRRMP particularly those included in the 70 percent LDRRMF-MF in accordance with RA No. 10121.
observations.managementAction: 83.98 percent of the budgeted amount for the MF was utilized by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to: a. Direct the Accountable Officers to prepare and submit on time their monthly RAAF using the prescribed format to the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to facilitate the preparation of monthly CRAAF; b. Ensure that the CRAAF tally with the individual RAAF submitted by the Accountable Officers; c. Observe timely submission of the monthly CRAAF every 5th day of the month using the prescribed format accompanied by the individual RAAF of the Accountable Officers; d. Henceforth, comply with the provisions of Sections 50 and 51 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume II, to facilitate the proper monitoring and reporting of the accountable forms.
observations.managementAction: a. Direct the Accountable Officers to prepare and submit on time their monthly RAAF using the prescribed format to the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to facilitate the preparation of monthly CRAAF; b. Ensure that the CRAAF tally with the individual RAAF submitted by the Accountable Officers; c. Observe timely submission of the CRAAF every monthly using the prescribed format accompanied by the individual RAAF of the Accountable Officers; d. Henceforth, comply with the provisions of Sections 50 and 51 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume II, to facilitate the proper monitoring and reporting of the accountable forms.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor immediately fill-up the three mandatory positions of the LDRRMO as required under Item 5.2 of the NDRRMC, DILG, DBM, and CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2014-1.
observations.managementAction: The three mandatory positions were already filled up per Office Order Nos. 2024-01A, 01-B and 01-C.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to: a. Facilitate the titling of the cemetery in Barangay Sta. Maria and the parcel of land in Barangay Pinagkamaligan; and b. Make the necessary follow-ups regarding the titling of the market site.
observations.managementAction: a. NOT IMPLEMENTED
observations.reason: Pending petition for the Issuance of 3rd owners duplicate copy of title of the original owner (seller). b. the petition of the 3rd owner's duplicate copy of title made by original owner for the reason that the original owners duplicate copy is missing/lost and was not in possession of the original owner. Upon verbal conversation and verification to one of the heirs of the property, they are amenable for the responsibility. However, due to untimely death of the last Immediate heir who happened to be the administrator of the property and that they are experiencing financial difficulties, they could not immediately facilitate the said petition in court. On the proposed filing of adverse claim, pending the Issuance of 3rd original owners copy of title, there was no employed legal counsel to represent the municipality proceedings, in the commissioning is still to be processed. b. A follow-up letter by the LCE dated May 28, 2024 has been personally sent and received on May 30 to DENR, Management Regional Land Services, Technical Director Office regarding the respond letter by the office dated May 18, 2023 for the verification and correction for the cited discrepancy and submission to the LRA office, the LCE requested the photocopy of reply letter and a copy of plotting they made less. the copy of plans PSU-134036-amd and TS-4A-000006. Upon completion of this map, this office will coordinate with the LRA. for the confirmation whether the discrepancies are already addressed and for the issuance of corresponding certifications needed (a copy of which is hereto attached.)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:  Municipal Accountant to derecognize the carrying amount of the PPE items;  Municipal Accountant to complete and update the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card (PPELC) for each category of PPE; and  Municipal General Services Officer (GSO) and the Municipal Accountant to pursue and complete the reconciliation of the accounting with property records in order to establish the correct PPE balances, to investigate and adjust any discrepancy that will be noted. Prepared Recommendation Tracking Sheet (RTS) for approval of the Regional Director.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant derecognized the carrying amount of the PPE items which were disposed by the Municipality through Journal Entry Voucher Number 100-2023-03-01121 dated March 30, 2023. However, the Municipality was not able to implement the recommendations on the completion and updating of the PPELC for each category of PPE, and the reconciliation of the accounting records with the property records to establish correct PPE balances.
observations.reason: The PPE accounts in the financial statements for CY 2023 amounting to P1,068,434,709.38 do not reconcile with the RPCPPE as of December 31, 2023, submitted by GSO totaling P1,069,517,770.94, thus the variance amounts to P2,269,108.44.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:  Reclassify the completed projects to proper Infrastructure Asset account and calculate and record its depreciation;  Verify the validity on the recording of the Construction of Water Supply System Cylinder Plunger Pumps in Brgy. Bukal Sur amounting to P168,200.00 and prepare and adjustments, if any; and  Prepare the necessary journal entries to correct the erroneous credit made in the CIP account in the amount of P593,023.44 and furnish the Audit Team copies of the adjusting entries made for validation purposes. Thereafter, ensure that proper recording of entries was made in the books of accounts for the fair presentation in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant reclassified the completed projects to proper Infrastructure accounts and recorded its depreciation through JEV No. 100-2023-03-01109 dated March 30, 2023. The Municipal Accountant verified the validity of the recorded construction of Water Supply System Cylinder Plunger Pumps in Brgy. Bukal Sur and prepared an adjusting entry and through JEV No. 100-2023-12-05521 dated December 31, 2023. The Municipal Accountant corrected the erroneous credit to the CIP account in the amount of P593,023.44 through JEV No. 100-2023-03-01111 dated March 30, 2023. All completed projects were reclassified to proper infrastructure accounts for the fair presentation in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:  Instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the return of the unused balance of P21,500.00 to the respective source agency;  Coordinate with the DSWD Regional Office IV-A regarding the utilization/implementation of the Cash Incentives to Day Care Workers and/or the disposition of funds should the intended purpose could no longer be materialized; and Henceforth, ensure compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994 on the utilization and liquidation of funds transfers.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer facilitated the return of the unused balance of P21,500.00 to the respective source agency. The Municipal Accountant coordinated with the DSWD Regional Office IV-A regarding the utilization/implementation of the Cash Incentives to Day Care Workers and found that the fund received was an incentive and not required to be liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit to the Audit Team the BRS for CY 2022. Henceforth, ensure that the BRS is submitted within the prescribed period, and that all bank accounts are provided with BRS.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has submitted the Audit Team the BRS for CY 2022.
observations.reason: BRS for CY 2023 were still not submitted within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact all collections with the authorized government depository banks (AGDBs) daily or not later than the next banking day as prescribed by Section 69(4) of PD No. 1445 and Section 32 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The RPT collections were deposited promptly and intact with the AGDBs.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations that:  Municipal Accountant to update the PPELC by category of PPE;  Municipal GSO and OIC-Municipal Accountant to pursue and complete the reconciliation of the accounting with property records in order to establish the correct PPE balances, to investigate and adjust any discrepancy that will be noted; and  Disposal Committee to undertake the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties to avoid further deterioration and possible loss and to generate additional income from its disposal.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant derecognized the carrying amount of PPE items amounting to P2,465,830.00 which were disposed by the Municipality through Journal Entry Voucher Number 100-2023-03-01121 dated March 30, 2023. The Municipal GSO provided the RPCPPE as of December 31, 2023 which is equal to the PPE accounts in the financial statements for CY 2023. Prepared Recommendation Tracking Sheet (RTS) for approval of the Regional Director.
observations.reason: The PPE accounts in the financial statements for CY 2023 amounting to P1,068,434,709.38 do not reconcile with the RPCPPE as of December 31, 2023, submitted by GSO totaling P1,069,517,770.94, thus the variance amounts to P2,269,108.44. There were still unserviceable properties which were not disposed of as of year-end. Prepared RTS for approval of the Regional Director.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update Subsidiary Ledgers (SL).
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office has maintained subsidiary ledger based on the reports submitted by the Treasurer’s Office of the collectible from RPT for the year.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor:  Require the Municipal Accountant to check the validity of non-moving prior years’ balances of Inter-Agency Payables account, complete the reconciliation of the account, adjust those without supporting documents for fair presentation in the financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1; and  Instruct the Municipal Treasurer for the prompt remittance of the provincial shares from property, special education, professional and transfer tax and barangay shares from RPT and Community Tax Certificate (CTC).
observations.managementAction: The non-moving prior years’ balances to BIR was remitted on September 7, 2023 amounting to P2,008,479.95. The Municipal Treasurer for the prompt remittance of Barangay share from RPT and CTC for CY 2023.
observations.reason: The non-moving prior years’ balances of Due to GSIS, Due to GOCCs, and Due to Pag-IBIG were not completely reconciled. Due to voluminous vouchers and work during the year-end closing of books, some reconciliations were submitted late.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal General Services Officer to ensure that all the insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with appropriate insurance with the GSIS and to expedite the completion of necessary documents required by the GSIS to facilitate the application for property insurance of all the properties of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: There are twelve (12) properties that are on process for GSIS insurance. With the new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew under audit observation no. 9, Part II of this Report.
observations.reason: Some insurable properties of the Municipality are still not yet covered with appropriate insurance with the GSIS. With the new developments, the Municipal Engineer is to expedite the completion of necessary documents required by the GSIS to facilitate the application for property insurance of all the properties of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor make representation with the source agency, whether the afore-mentioned funds can still be utilized by the Municipality according to its intended purpose. While, otherwise, instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the return of the unused balance of the fund transfers to the respective SAs. Henceforth, ensure compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 94-013 on the utilization and liquidation of funds transfers.
observations.managementAction: Upon inquiry by the Municipal Health Officer from the source agency, it was confirmed that the municipality can be utilized by the Municipality. While a total of P869,629.84 was refunded by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to revise/update the rates in the LRC for deliberation of the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) in compliance with RA No. 7160 to maximize its taxing power and to provide more funds for its operations.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) and the Municipal Engineer to submit to this Office the list of all on-going government PPAs, as well as those that are to be implemented for the year together with the monthly monitoring report on implementation of PPAs. Henceforth, ensure compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to submit relevant supporting documents for the request for write-off.
observations.managementAction: Dormant receivables were requested for write-off. A total of P81,229.00 for the receivables were requested for write-off. The remaining amount is pending approval by the COA Regional Office.
observations.reason: The remaining amount is pending approval by the COA Regional Office.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the MENRO to: heighten its efforts to expedite the drafting and/or approval/review of the 10-year SWMP of the municipality; coordinate with the National Solid Waste Management Council (NSWMC) regarding the status of review on the Municipality’s 10-Year SWMP in order to know the next steps to be undertaken that will facilitate approval of the said Plan; and provide assistance for the establishment of MRF in two barangays to ensure protection of public health and environment.
observations.managementAction: The MENRO has been designated to coordinate with the National Solid Waste Management Council (NSWMC) regarding the status of review on the Municipality’s 10-Year SWMP in order to know the next steps to be undertaken that will facilitate approval of the said Plan; and provide assistance for the establishment of MRF in two barangays to ensure protection of public health and environment. The MENRO has sent the draft of the municipality’s 10-year SWMP to the PGENRO for its review and endorsement to the EMB.
observations.reason: The 10-year SWMP is still for review.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to: • Prepare and maintain PPE Ledger Cards by the Municipality. (PPELCs) and Property Cards (PCs), respectively. Reiterated in Observation No. 2, Part II, Page 32 to 34 of this Report. • Complete the reconciliation of the accounting with RPCPPE and property records in order to establish correct PPE balances; and • Investigate and adjust any discrepancy that will be noted. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert effort to identify the unaccounted properties from prior years’ records for the fair presentation of the PPE in the FS.
observations.managementAction:
No action taken by the Municipality.
The total balance per FS was reconciled as of December 31, 2023.
The variance of P1,844,973.74 noted in the CY 2022 AAR was accounted for.
observations.reason: PPELCs and PCs are still not maintained by the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer, respectively.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to exert efforts in the reconciliation of the cash accounts at least quarterly and adjust any discrepancies that will be noted.
observations.managementAction: The total balance per FS was reconciled as of December 31, 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee to immediately act on the disposal of unserviceable properties to avoid further deterioration and to generate additional income from their disposal.
observations.managementAction:
The Inventory Committee reconciled the RPCPPE with the records of the Municipal Accountant.
No action taken by the Municipality.
observations.reason: Disposal was not undertaken in CY 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Local Development Council to ensure that development projects included in the 20 percent development plan/annual investment plan are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready, in accordance with DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was able to utilize 97.84 per cent of GPPAs funded out of the CY 2023 20% Development Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: • instruct the Municipal Treasurer to intensify efforts to collect P489,336.00 from the Capital Assistance Program beneficiaries by constant follow-up and sending collection notice to borrowers and to make the accounts current; • require the Municipal Accountant to allot time for the preparation of the necessary documentation to the Sangguniang Bayan to secure the authority for the filing of the request for write-off of the receivables to the COA through the Audit Team following the guidelines and procedures provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005; • In the meantime, require the Municipal Accountant to set up the provision for Allowance for Impairment in order to present fairly the receivables accounts in the financial statements at net realizable amount consistent with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 and for the fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements in accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Mayor directed the Municipal Treasurer to intensify efforts to collect the remaining receivables on January 9, 2023.
An Allowance for Impairment was set-up for Receivables.

observations.recommendation: Henceforth, ensure the submission by the Municipal Accountant of all disbursement vouchers for Confidential/Intelligence Fund cash advances, with complete supporting documents to the concerned COA Audit Team Leader within seven working days from release of the checks. We also reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor exert more effort to locate the liquidation reports and complete the supporting documents pertaining to prior years’ cash advances totaling P265,750.00 and submit the same to the COA ICFAU, in order that expenses and/or refunds relative thereto are taken up for the fair presentation of the receivables and government equity accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
No action taken by the Municipality.
The Municipal Mayor exert more effort to locate the liquidation reports and complete the supporting documents pertaining to prior years’ cash advances totaling P265,750.00 and submit the same to the COA ICFAU.
observations.reason: The Municipality was not able to prioritize the implementation of the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MT to direct the AOs to prepare and submit their monthly RAAF using the prescribed format to the MT to facilitate the preparation of monthly CRAAF. Henceforth, strictly comply with Sections 50 and 51 of MNGAS for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The CRAAF and the RAAF were submitted to the Audit Team by the MT. Henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of Sections 50 and 51 of MNGAS for LGUs. The Municipality complied with the provisions of Sections 50 and 51 of MNGAS for LGUs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Human Resources Management Officer to ensure that an employee’s net take home pay shall not be less than the mandated minimum take home pay of P5,000.00. Henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of the GAA.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor has ensured compliance of the employees. Henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of the GAA. The Municipality complied with the provisions of the GAA.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
• In-Charge-of-Office (ICO)-Municipal Accountant and the ICO- General Services Officer (GSO) to reconcile the net variance between the FS and RPCPPE; 
• ICO-Municipal Accountant to prepare Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) and submit to the Audit Team the proof of reconciliation and any adjustments, if warranted; and
• ICO-Municipal Accountant to ensure that PPE Ledger Cards (PPELCs) for each category of PPE are prepared and being maintained.
Henceforth, strictly comply with the MNGAS for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality conducted the One-Time Cleansing of PPE accounts. The records of the Accounting and GSO were reconciled, except for missing PPE items, which were disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements. The Municipal Accountant prepared the necessary accounting entries. The Municipal Accountant prepared the PPELCs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• require the accountable officer to immediately liquidate the cash advance in the amount of P300,000.00; and
• ensure the proper granting, utilization, and liquidation of all cash advances in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: The accountable officer refunded the amount of P600.00 per Official Receipt (OR) No. 1872962 dated January 20, 2023 and P299,400.00 per OR No. 1872978 dated May 30, 2023.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
• Municipal Health Officer to provide the ICO-Municipal Accountant with the inventory reports on receipt, utilization and distribution of donations related to its COVID-19 Vaccination Program as basis in the recording in the books of accounts; and
• ICO-Municipal Accountant to record in the books of accounts of the Municipality the remaining 1,583 vials of COVID-19 vaccines and various ancillary supplies based on the fair value at the date of acquisition.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Health Officer submitted the supporting documents to the ICO-Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• instruct the Municipal Engineer (ME) and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to submit the list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year within the prescribed period;
• inform the Audit Team of posting of appropriate project signboards and/or public notices 10 days upon award of infrastructure projects and start of the PPAs; and
• instruct the ME and the MPDC to ensure that signboards of all infrastructure projects of the Municipality are provided with relevant information in compliance with Annex A of COA Circular No. 2013-004.
observations.managementAction: The ME was able to prepare and submit the report on PPAs. The Municipality informed the Audit Team within 10 days of the award. Signboards of infrastructure projects of the municipality are provided with relevant information.

observations.recommendation:
Henceforth, we recommended compliance with:
• Section 73 of PD No. 1445 on the requirement for the notice of loss and request for relief in connection with the loss of property caused by fire, theft, or other casualty or force majeure; 
• Section 125 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Section 79 of PD No. 1445 on the proper disposal procedures of unserviceable properties; and
• COA Circular No. 92-390 and RA No. 656 on the insurance coverage of all insurable properties with the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) in order to indemnify or compensate the Government for any damage to, or loss of, its properties due to fire, earthquake, storm, or other casualty.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has conducted the disposal of unserviceable properties. The Municipality has conducted the necessary procedures and coordinated with the Regional Director of COA Office No. IV-A.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to allocate enough time for the reconciliation of the affected accounts for the fair presentation of these accounts in the financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1. We also recommended that henceforth, all contributions and taxes withheld from its employees, suppliers and contractors are remitted intact to the respective government agencies.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant coordinated with the GSIS for the arrears and already remitted the arrears for CY 2007-2022 last October 6 and December 1, 2023. The Municipality is now compliant with the remittance requirements on withholding taxes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the ICO-GSO to continue exerting efforts to ensure that all the insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with appropriate insurance with the GSIS in compliance with the existing regulations by expediting the gathering of all the supporting documents required by the GSIS for the immediate processing of their application for property insurance. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer to assist the ICO-GSO by providing the necessary supporting documents required by the GSIS to immediately process their application for property insurance of all the insurable properties of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality allocated funds for the insurance of the property. The ICO-General Services Officer is in coordination with the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer to determine the details of all their properties and to complete the documents required by the GSIS for insurability. The Municipality is still in the process of determining the details of all insurable properties.
observations.reason: The Municipality is still in the process of determining the details of all insurable properties and was not able to have all properties insured due to budgetary constraints.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee composed of adequate number of members to conduct the physical inventory in three months or less and require the Inventory, Appraisal and Disposal Committee to prepare and submit the approved Physical Inventory Plan to the AT (Audit Team) at least 10 days before the scheduled start of the inventory taking activities in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts. We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MGSO-Designate to issue and update Property Cards (PCs) and Property Acknowledgement Receipts (PARs) of acquired PPEs and maintain proper and complete file of said documents in compliance with COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: Furthermore, N o t the Inventory Committee has not yet conducted the Physical Inventory due to lack of manpower. Also, PCs and PARs were issued, and the AT were given copies of previously acquired PPE.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to reverse the adjusting entry pertaining to the PPE amounts with a total cost of P8,991,728.38 until such time that the PPE balances could already be identified on a per item basis. Henceforth, expense only issued tangible PPE items below P50,000.00 in compliance with COA Circular No. 2022-004 dated May 31, 2022.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made the appropriate reversal of the adjusting entry amounts with a total cost of P8,991,728.38 and submitted the copy of JEV No. 100-2023-03-01457 dated March 31, 2023 as proof that the recommendation was implemented.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 7, Part II of this AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Accountable Officers to immediately liquidate their outstanding CAs. Henceforth, ensure that all CAs are fully liquidated at the end of each year in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: All outstanding CAs were liquidated and were included in the First Quarter Ending Report 2023. The Statement of Receipts and Disbursements (SORDs) were also submitted to the respective fund-source agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Treasurer to submit the certified list of taxpayers to the Office of the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year to recognize the RPT/SET Receivable at the Office of the Municipal Accountant; b. Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I for the recording of RPT/SET collections.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer (MT) stated that they are still in the process of creating a Database Build-up which will eventually provide the Certified List of Taxpayers.
observations.reason: Due to on-going Data Buildup (RPTAR v4)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: a. Review and reconcile the negative balances of the Due to NGAs and Due to Government-Owned and Controlled Corporations (GOCCs); b. Confirm the balance of the Adaptation and Mitigation Initiative in Agriculture (AMIA) project from the Source Agency; and c. Consequently, prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the accounts’ balances. Henceforth, ensure that all effects of transactions, other events, and conditions are faithfully represented to achieve fair presentation of the FS in compliance with IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant stated that upon review, the Due to NGAs- AMIA has been fully liquidated and the negative balance is due to duplication of entries. Furthermore, reversal of JEV Nos. 1466 to 1477 has been made to correct the said accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Instruct the Municipal Planning and the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Development Coordinator (MPDC) to submit the list of all on-going government PPA and those that are to be implemented during the year as well as its monthly monitoring reports to the AT within the prescribed period; b. Inform the AT thru the Supervising Auditor (SA) and AT Leader of posting of appropriate project signboards and/or public notices 10 days upon award of infrastructure projects and start of the program/activity; and c. Henceforth, ensure compliance with Items 2.1, 3.1 and 3.2 of COA Circular No. 2013-004.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC and the Municipal Development Engineer submitted a written response stating that they have submitted the list of on-going government PPA and the list of on-going government PPA and that are to be implemented during the year, more so reporting is usually done on a quarterly basis. The management adheres with this recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to: a. Transfer the unutilized CY 2017 and prior years unutilized appropriation for DRRMF totaling P1,293,608.36 to the GF which will be available for other social services; and b. Ensure that all unutilized DRRMF be reverted back to the GF after the lapse of the five-year period in accordance with Paragraph 3, Section 21 of RA No. 10121.
observations.managementAction: On March 21, 2023, the unutilized CY 2017 and prior years’ appropriation for DRRMF were reverted back and transferred to GF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MGSO-Designate to expedite the completion of the necessary documents needed to dispose the unserviceable properties in order to facilitate the immediate disposal thereof in compliance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445 and Sections 379 to 381 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: Written response was sent to the AT stating that GSO is now ready for disposition in any manner in the presence of concerned authorities and in compliance with the law.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the titling of the five parcels of land to avoid contingent claims that may arise in the future.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor already sent follow-up letter to concern agencies to be able to secure titles of the four (4) land parcels. One (1) land parcel (Municipal Building) has already an issued Certificate of Title.
observations.reason: Titling of properties are still being processed

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MGSO-Designate to: a. Facilitate the transfer of registration of the above stated ambulance to the Municipality’s name and consequently, update its registration; and b. Ensure that all vehicles owned by the Municipality are registered with the Land Transportation Office (LTO) and supported with OR/CR under the name of the Municipality and a photocopy of which is carried inside the vehicle at all times while the original copies should be filed and updated in the Office of the MGSO for monitoring of its yearly registration.
observations.managementAction: In a written response sent to the AT dated June 24, 2021, the GSO-Designate stated that OR/CR of Motor Vehicle totaling to P3,177,143.00 is now in their safekeeping.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to make a constant follow-up of their request to Land Bank of the Philippines (LBP) regarding the particulars of various debit/credit memos and consequently effect the adjustment immediately; and b. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to exert more efforts to locate and submit to the Audit Team the remaining unsubmitted Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) for CYs 2016 and 2017 and their supporting documents for evaluation and verification.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant continuously exerted extra effort to locate and submit to the AT the remaining unsubmitted DVs of CYs 2016 & 2017 and their supporting documents. These findings are part of an on-going case filed against the Former Municipal Accountant. To date, she remains at-large, hence this matter is yet to be resolved.
observations.reason: Bank debits/credits from prior years are still unidentified. The unsubmitted DVs are still being located. The Former Municipal Accountant is at-large.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to adopt strict collection measures and strategies in collecting the outstanding loan balances of P142,141.12 from the retired/resigned employees in order for the Municipality to have additional funding for its livelihood projects.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer added that they will re-evaluate the list of debtors and see if there are some who will qualify for write-off and will request the same to the AT.
observations.reason: The outstanding collection from retired/resigned employees is on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to gather all relevant documents for the reconciliation of the aforementioned Inter-Agency Payables and make necessary adjustments, as needed. We further recommended compliance with the RA Nos. 8281, 9679 and 7875 and PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was able to partially determine that P44,073.86 of the said amount is due to excess withholding of percentage of tax made to suppliers. The remaining P444,705.90 due is due to lack of time and manpower. On the other hand, the year-end balance of employee-employer contribution for social security and mutual funds were already remitted.
observations.reason: Lack of time and manpower. Related to Observation No. 8, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to:
• Take up the necessary adjusting/correcting entries in the books on the collections due and prepared adjusting/ correcting entry thru Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 100-2023-03-100122.
