• Henceforth, follow the correct accounting entries regarding the collections due and advance payments of RPT and SET in order to reflect the correct Deferred RPT/SET Receivable and RPT/SET Income to attain fair presentation of the financial statements in compliance with IPSAS 1 and Section 112 of PD 1445.
b. Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the records submitted to the Municipal Accountant are accurate and with complete details as required in Section 111 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Accountant followed the correct accounting entries in recording Deferred RPT/SET, RPT/SET Receivable and RPT/SET Income.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to confirm the validity of the claims with the owners/suppliers. Otherwise, make necessary entries for reversion to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund, with a condition that appropriation shall be made available in the event that claim arises in the future. Henceforth, ensure proper monitoring of liability accounts so that Accounts Payable nearing the maturity of two years are communicated to the respective payees to verify its validity; and comply with Section 98 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality sent letters to suppliers to confirm the existence of claims. Otherwise, claims were paid while invalid claims were reverted to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund per Sangguniang Bayan (SB) Resolution No. 694-2023 thru JEV No. 2023-12-100224.
The Municipal Accountant implemented the monitoring of liability accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Make the necessary adjusting journal entries in the books to present the correct balance of the Trust Liabilities – DRRM account; and
b. Henceforth, ensure that all transactions are computed and recorded accurately in its proper account.
observations.managementAction:
The Office of the Municipal Accountant prepared adjusting/correcting entry thru JEV No. 300-2023-03-100.
The Municipal Accountant ensured that all transactions are computed and recorded accurately in its proper account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GSO to prepare and submit the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and to undertake the necessary action for the disposal of the said properties in accordance with applicable rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted IIRUP and undertake disposal of unserviceable properties.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the immediate titling of lots in the name of the Municipality and continuously coordinate with other government agencies to avoid contingent claims that may arise in the future in compliance with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: The remaining unaccounted/unknown land account amounting to P259,892.08 was identified and transferred the Land Account to Gumaca Integrated School per Local School Board No. 3 s. 2023 through JEV No. 200-2023-05-05114.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the MGSO to: 
• Coordinate for the reconciliation of their records and make the necessary adjustments in their respective records, if warranted. Also, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: 
• MGSO to prepare and submit the RPCI to the Audit Team; and 
• Municipal Accountant to maintain supplies ledger cards for the purpose of inventory monitoring. Henceforth, observe the provisions of Section 124 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I on the prescribed timeline on submission of RPCI.
observations.managementAction:
The MGSO prepared and submitted the RPCI to the Audit Team on June 1, 2023.
The Municipal Accountant maintained supplies ledger cards for the purpose of inventory monitoring which was validated by the Audit Team on June 1, 2023.
Henceforth, observe the provisions of Section 124 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I through submission of the RPCI to the Audit Team within the prescribed timeline.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
• Municipal Treasurer to stop the practice of direct cash payments of the salaries of the job order workers thru the Disbursing Officer; instead, draw cash advances for the salaries of the JO workers, liquidate the same within five days and refund the remaining amount in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002; and
• Municipal Accountant and the Department Heads to immediately submit the accomplishment reports of the job order workers in accordance with Section 1.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality implemented the process by introducing the ATM Payroll System (LandBank) and payments are now being made through Automated Teller Machine or List of Due and Demandable Accounts Payable – Advice to Debit Accounts (LDDAP-ADA) .
The accomplishment reports were submitted to the Audit Team on April 28, 2023. In CY 2023 onwards, the accomplishment reports were already being attached in the Disbursement Vouchers as supporting documents.

The MGSO and the supply officer already stopped the practice of drawing cash advances for payments to suppliers relative to the ICT Equipment acquisition. Payments are now being made through issuance of checks directly to suppliers. The lacking documents were submitted to the Audit Team on April 28, 2023. The BAC and MGSO already complied with the recommendation. Alternative modes of procurement are now being employed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
• Municipal Health Officer (MHO) to provide the Municipal Accountant with the inventory reports on the receipt, utilization and distribution of donations in-kind related to its COVID-19 Vaccination Program as basis in the recording in the books of accounts and submit to the Audit Team the report on the same; and
• Municipal Accountant to record in the books of accounts of the Municipality the remaining doses of COVID-related vaccines and various ancillary supplies thru JEV No. 2023-01-002010 dated 01/03/2023 amounting to P4,229,693.38.
observations.managementAction:
The MHO provided the Municipal Accountant the list needed for recording in the books of accounts. Also, the Audit Team was provided with entries made.
The Municipal Accountant recorded in the books of accounts the remaining doses of COVID- related vaccines and various ancillary supplies thru JEV No. 2023-01-002010 dated 01/03/2023 amounting to P4,229,693.38.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) to expedite the identification of the employees with unremitted balances and to reflect the correct balance due to BIR and Due to GSIS to avoid possible penalties and surcharges. Also, we recommended the Municipal Accountant to monitor the taxes withheld from various suppliers/contractors and to remit the same within the prescribed period as provided under Section 5 (A) of RR No. 11-2018 to avoid penalties and surcharges. Further, ensure that the Due to GSIS balance reflects the correct balance for fair presentation in the financial statements in accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted a list of identified employees with unremitted balances to the Audit Team on May 24, 2024, via electronic mail. The taxes withheld were remitted to the BIR within the prescribed period. The Municipal Accountant also undertook measures to reflect the correct balances and to reconcile the Due to GSIS account. The amounts were remitted accordingly and follow-up reconciliation is being undertaken with the respective agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor request the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts of the Municipality, and provide the Audit Team with copy of the adjustments made, for verification in audit. Also, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor request the Municipal Treasurer to effect the cancellation of the stale checks, and ensure monitoring of issued checks and sending of written notices to payees whose checks are about to become stale; and to coordinate closely with the Municipal Accountant for the reconciliation of cashbook balances.
observations.managementAction: The stale checks were coordinated to the Municipal Treasurer to the Municipal Accountant. Hence, these were already adjusted in the accounting records. The Municipal Accountant prepared various JEVs for the adjustment of stale checks in the books of accounts of the Municipality on December 29, 2023.
observations.reason: The stale checks were included in the submitted Bank Reconciliation Statements as of December 31, 2023 and the Municipal Accountant prepared the necessary JEVs for the adjustment of stale checks in the books of accounts dated December 29, 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to transfer the cash corresponding to the unexpended balance of LDRRMF - QRF and MOOE for CY 2022 to the STF account and record the transfer/adjustments in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002. In addition, we recommended that the savings from the completed projects be made available for use in the disaster risk reduction and management activities in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The unexpended LDRRMF balance for CY 2022 were transferred to the Trust Fund per JEV No. 12299 dated February 27, 2023. The savings from completed projects were included in the STF.
observations.reason: The unexpended balances were transferred to STF per JEV dated February 27, 2023 and the savings from completed projects were included in STF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer to provide the Municipal Accountant with the inventory reports on the receipt, utilization and distribution of donations in-kind related to its COVID-19 Vaccination Program as basis in recording in the books of accounts; Municipal Accountant to record in the books of accounts of the Municipality the inventory remaining 312 vials of COVID-19 vaccines and various ancillary supplies based on the fair value at the date of acquisition.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Health Officer provided the Municipal Accountant the inventory report as of December 31, 2022. The Municipal Accountant recorded the inventory per JEV No. 2023-01-000003 dated January 1, 2023 by debiting Drugs and Medicines Inventory, and Medical, Dental and Laboratory Supplies Inventory amounting to P211,108.80 and P26,339.97, respectively, and crediting to Prior Period Adjustments account. The copy of JEV was submitted to the Audit Team on May 15, 2023.
observations.reason: Inventory was recorded and JEV submitted to Audit Team on May 15, 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to revise/update the rates in the Local Revenue Code subject for deliberation of Sangguniang Bayan (SB) in compliance with RA No. 7160 to maximize its taxing power and to provide additional funds for its operations.
observations.managementAction: The revisions to Revenue Code of 2014, otherwise known as The Revised Revenue Code of 2014, was deliberated by the SB up to the second public hearing as of writing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the disposal the Unserviceable properties following the guidelines provided under Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory and Inspection Committee/ GSO Report of IIRUP was submitted by the Municipality to the Audit Team. Disposal of unserviceable fully depreciated properties was made on July 25, 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Computer Programmer to continue fixing the computer system defects by requiring the Service Provider to complete its overdue obligation and also providing funds for upgrading and preventive maintenance.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality provided fund for the Computerization Program which will also facilitate its upgrade. They also availed free services to fix/update their information system.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: • Municipal Health Officer to provide the Municipal Accountant with the inventory reports on the receipt, the inventory utilization and reports on the distribution of donations in-kind related to its COVID-19 Vaccination Program as basis in the recording in the books of accounts; and • Municipal Accountant to record in the books of accounts of the Municipality the remaining 708 vials of COVID-19 vaccines based on the fair value at the date of acquisition.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal provided the Municipal Accountant with the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 100-2312-316 to 317 to record the utilization, and the remaining COVID-19 vials as of December 31, 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the LDRRMO to immediately submit to the Audit Team the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF for CY 2022. Henceforth, ensure that the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF is submitted within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMO submitted to the Audit Team the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF for CY 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to refrain from designating JO employees in the collection of fees and other charges of the Municipality pursuant to Item A, Section 21 of Local Treasury Operations Manual (LTOM) in order to safeguard the funds and protect the interest of the government.
observations.managementAction: Complied.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) and the Municipal Engineer to submit to this Office the list of all on-going government PPAs, as well as those that are to be implemented for the year together with the monthly monitoring report on the implementation of PPAs. Thusforth, ensure compliance with the provision of COA Circular No. 2013-004.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to ensure that all development projects to be funded out of the 20 percent DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready; Henceforth, comply with the guidelines set in Department of Budget and Management-Department of Finance-Department of the Interior and Local Government–(DBM-DOF-DILG) Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 1 in the appropriation and utilization of the 20 percent DF of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: All of the projects funded out of the 20 percent DF were implemented.

observations.recommendation: We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the preparation of tax declaration and the immediate titling of the lots once ownership by the Municipality is determined to avoid possible third party claims and incurrence of litigation costs in the future.
observations.managementAction: Noted. The Municipality is contemplating to sell the parcel located in Barangay Pajarillo as the land is no longer needed. An annotation is required on the land title of the ecopark. While a Deed of Donation has not yet been executed for the school site.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMO to facilitate the immediate implementation and completion of the crucial PPAs of DRRMF within the budget year in order to be adequately prepared for any upcoming disaster/calamity and hence reduce its risk.
observations.managementAction: Complied.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
Municipal Mayor direct the
Municipal Accountant to:
a. Make the following necessary adjustments to correct the misclassification of the vehicle charged under LDRRMF to its proper account under Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) no. 100-2023-03-00868 dated March 21, 2023.
b. Reclassify all rescue vehicles charged under the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) and recorded under Motor Vehicles to Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment account.
Henceforth, strictly adhere with the accounting and reporting guidelines for LDRRMF or COA Circular No. 2012-002, dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality made the necessary adjusting entries to correct the misclassification of the vehicle charged under LDRRMF to its proper account under Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) no. 100-2023-03-00868 dated March 21, 2023. The Municipality adhered with the accounting and reporting guidelines for LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary entries for the reversion of the total balance of P699,542.67 to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund, with a condition that appropriation shall be made available in the event that claim arises in the future.
Henceforth, ensure proper monitoring of liability accounts so that Accounts Payable nearing the maturity of two years are communicated to the respective payees to verify its validity; and comply with Section 98 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant traced back the previous recording and found out that the amount of P699,542.67 pertains to previous years’ Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) Contribution withheld from the employees recorded under this account. The Municipal Accountant has ensured that proper monitoring of liability accounts so that monitoring is communicated to respective payees.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
 a. Keep adequate permanent record books with complete details and evidence of the receipt and subsequent issuance of the AFs, in accordance with Section 86D, Book II Local Treasury Operations Manual;
 b. Restrict access of AFs and the permanent records to authorized Accountable Officers (AOs); and
 c. Strictly implement procedures to strengthen their internal control over accountable forms in accordance with Section 123 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer maintained permanent record books with complete details and evidence of the receipt and subsequent issuance of the AF. The Municipal Treasurer assigned one AO. Internal control was strengthened by the Municipality over AF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary entries in the books to present the correct balance of the Trust Liabilities - DRRM account and to ensure that all transactions are computed and recorded accurately in its proper account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made the necessary adjusting journal entries under JEV no. 300-2023-03-00018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to verify from the Provincial Accountant the discrepancies of P1,349,056.47 and P75,105.26 between the recorded balances of the reciprocal accounts reflected in the books of the Province and adjust the same to present the correct balances of the said accounts in the Municipality’s financial statement.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant coordinated with the Provincial Office of the Provincial Accountant relative to this matter and made the necessary reconciliation process.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
 a. Require the responsible departments to utilize and implement the related projects; and
 b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to return the unused balance of P18,283.97 for the projects completed projects to the source agency.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was able to utilize the fund transfers from NGAs and any corresponding unused balance were returned to source agency. The Municipality was able to utilized the fund transfers from NGAs and any corresponding unused balance were returned to source agency.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Designated General Services Officer (GSO) to: a. Pursue and complete the reconciliation of the accounting records with property records in order to establish the correct PPE balances, to investigate and adjust any discrepancy that will be noted; b. Maintain complete and updated property cards, as a counter-check to the correctness of the accounting records; and c. Prepare and maintain Property Acknowledgment Receipt (PAR) with complete details and ensure that all properties were properly tagged/labeled.
We also recommended One-time conducting One-Time Cleansing of PPE accounts pursuant to Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006.
Henceforth, strictly comply with Section 114 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I, Section 57 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II.
observations.managementAction:
The Management completed the reconciliation of records and made the necessary adjusting entries.
The Management completed and updated their property card.
PARs were prepared and properties were labeled.
One-time cleansing of PPE account were conducted.
The Agency complied with Section 114 of the MNGAS.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Assessor to prepare and submit to the Municipal Treasurer a complete assessment roll including the list of all persons whose real properties have been newly assessed and the values of such properties; and b. Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of taxpayers showing the name of the taxpayers and amount due and collectible at the start of each year to be used as basis of the Municipal Accountant in setting up the RPT/SET Receivables.
Henceforth, strictly comply with Section 20 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction:
Assessment Roll were prepared and submitted to the concerned office.
The Municipal Treasurer prepared the certified list of taxpayers and submitted the same to the Municipal Accountant.
Strict compliance were made by the Management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. Make the following necessary adjustments to correct the misclassification of the account; and b. Reclassify all rescue vehicles charged under the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) and recorded under the Motor Vehicles to Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment account.
Henceforth, strictly adhere with the accounting and reporting guidelines for the LDRRMF or COA Circular No. 2012-002, dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction:
Adjustments were made to correct the misclassification of the account.
The Management reclassified all rescue vehicles charged under the DRRM Fund to Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment account.
Strict compliance of the guidelines was adhered to.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: • Municipal Health Officer to provide the Municipal Accountant with the inventory reports on the receipt, the utilization and distribution of donations in-kind related to its COVID-19 Vaccination Program as basis in the recording in the books of accounts; and • Municipal Accountant to record in the books of accounts of the Municipality the remaining vials of COVID-19 vaccines based on the fair value at the date of acquisition.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Health Officer provided the Municipal Accountant with the inventory reports on the receipt, the inventory utilization and reports on the distribution of donations in-kind related to its COVID-19 Vaccination Program as basis in the recording in the books of accounts; and The Municipal Accountant provided the Audit Team the Journal Entry Voucher No. 7648 to record the utilization and the remaining COVID-19 vials as of December 31, 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GAD Focal Point Person to strictly observe the deadline to the COA Circular No. 2014- 001 dated March 14, 2014.
observations.managementAction: GAD Focal Point Person submitted GPB for CY 2023 to the Audit Team on March 6, 2023 in compliance with AOM 2023-004 (23) dated February 28, 2023, consequently, the GPB for CY 2024 was submitted to the Audit Team on May 18, 2023.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor: • instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to continue pursuing the collection of the receivables from the beneficiaries of the livelihood project who are able to pay by sending demand letters; and • refile the request for write-off of uncollectible receivables of P45,325.00 due to insolvency and insolvency and was granted as per COA Decision No. 2023-001 dated August 2, 2023 recorded thru JEV No. 4821 dated August 31, 2023.
observations.managementAction: Partial collection of P41,600.00. Refiled the request for write-off of uncollectible receivables of P45,325.00 and was granted as per COA Decision No. 2023-001 dated August 2, 2023 recorded thru JEV No. 4821 dated August 31, 2023. Moreover, the P5,600.00 representing loans granted to beneficiaries who are already deceased was collected per Official Receipt (OR) No. 2854906 amounting to P2,700.00 and OR No. 3984253 amounting to P2,900.00.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
• Make the necessary entries for the reversion of the total balance of P837,220.64 to the Unappropriated Surplus of the GF of the Municipality with a condition that appropriation shall be made available in the event that claim arises in the future; and
• Provide the details of the Accounts Payable recorded under Special Education Fund (SEF).
Otherwise, revert the same to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund as it constitutes accounts payable with no actual claim filed or not covered by perfected contracts.
Henceforth, ensure proper monitoring of the Accounts Payable account so that claims nearing the maturity of two years are communicated to the respective payees for the processing of their claims.
observations.managementAction:
Not Only Implemented P760,177.97 was reverted to the Unappropriated Surplus of the GF of the Municipality with a condition that appropriation shall be made available in the event that claim arises in the future; and
The Municipal Accountant provided the details of the Accounts Payable recorded under Special Education Fund (SEF).
The Management established proper monitoring to ensure that claims nearing the maturity of two years were communicated to the respective payees for the processing of their claims.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry in the books of accounts of Trust Fund and furnish the Audit Team a copy for validation purposes.
Likewise, prepare and maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for cash donations received amounting to P100,000.00 and both amounting to P100,000.00 were maintained in a single subsidiary ledger.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared the corresponding adjusting entry in the books of accounts of Trust Fund. Likewise, prepare and maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for cash donations received amounting to P100,000.00. Cash donations received from the LGU-Mauban and the BLGU-Brgy. Cagsiay, Mauban above per donor per purpose were established in accordance with the existing rules and regulations. Cash donations received from the LGU-Mauban and BLGU-Brgy. Cagsiay, Mauban both amounting to P100,000.00 were maintained in a single subsidiary ledger.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry in the books of accounts and furnish the Audit Team a copy for validation purposes.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared the corresponding adjusting entry in the books of accounts and furnished the Audit Team a copy for validation purposes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer to continue exerting extra effort to facilitate the immediate titling of the four parcels of lot valued at P301,100.00 in the name of the Municipality and to determine/identify the unidentified municipal lots worth P5,801,641.81 through the reconciliation of the accounting records and the property records as well as with the records of the Municipal Assessor to avoid possible third party claims that may arise in the future, and for the fair presentation of the Land account in the financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction:
The Inventory Committee will conduct the One-Time Cleansing of PPE account balances as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2020-006. Preparatory activities are being conducted by the GSO-Designate.
(As to facilitation of immediate titling and identification of lots: The Municipality continues exerting extra effort to facilitate the immediate titling of parcels and to determine/identify the unidentified municipal lots through reconciliation of records.)
observations.reason: Only the old cemetery valued at P2,539,631.81 remained untitled aside from the newly purchased parcels of lot totaling P1,227,720.00 in CY 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the ICO-General Services Officer to ensure that all the insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with appropriate insurance with the GSIS in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 and RA No. 656 by requiring the Municipal Engineer to submit the building plans and other documents in order to facilitate the application for property insurance of all the properties of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The GSO Designate has submitted a justification stating that the Municipality which belongs to the fourth-class is not required by the RA No. 656 to insure its properties with the GSIS. The Municipality which belongs to fourth-class appropriate insurance with the GSIS is not required by RA No. 656.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to achieve the Municipality’s sanitary landfill full operation.
observations.managementAction: The MENRO has coordinated with the Sangguniang Bayan for the creation of the said ordinances. As regards the other comments and recommendation of the EMB, the MENRO is still in the process of complying.
observations.reason: However, the barangays now had established their own Material Recovery Facility (MRF).

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor continually oblige the former Municipal Treasurer to submit the accounts, reports and accounting records prior to August 2004 as basis to determine the correct balances of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor filed a complaint-affidavit to the Office of the Ombudsman against the former Municipal Treasurer and the former Assistant Municipal Treasurer and the report was received by the Ombudsman for Luzon on August 30, 2023. The recommendation was no longer doable hence it was recommended to be closed.
observations.reason: The former Municipal Treasurer was still unable to submit accounts, report, and accounting records prior to August 2004 despite the effort exerted by the Office of the Municipal Mayor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: • Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) - Designate to complete the RPCPPE and submit the same to the Audit Team; and • MGSO - Designate and the Municipal Accountant to pursue the reconciliation of the accounting with property records in order to establish the correct PPE balances and adjust any discrepancy that will be noted.
observations.managementAction: MGSO-Designate submitted the RPCPPE to the Audit Team. The Municipal Accountant pursued reconciliation of the accounting with property records; prepared and transmitted Recommendations Tracking Sheet (RTS) dated April 22, 2024 for approval of the Regional Director. The recommendation tracking and reconciliation are ongoing.
observations.reason: The Statement of Reconciliation of PPE accounts were submitted to the Audit Team. However, variance remains and further reconciliation of PPE accounts is still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: • MGSO - Designate to complete the RPCI and submit the same to the Audit Team; and • MGSO - Designate and the Municipal Accountant to pursue the reconciliation of the accounting with property records in order to establish the correct inventory balance and adjust any discrepancy that will be noted.
observations.managementAction: The MGSO - Designate completed the RPCI and submitted the RPCI to the Audit Team. The Municipal Accountant pursued reconciliation of inventory records with accounting; reconciliation is still on-going.
observations.reason: Verification of balances of inventory accounts is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the LDRRMO to immediately submit to the Audit Team the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF for CY 2022. Henceforth, ensure that the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRM is submitted within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMO submitted the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF for CY 2022 to the Audit Team on May 5, 2023. For CY 2023, the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilizations were submitted on April 4, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person to submit to the Audit Team the GAD Plan and Budget duly approved by DILG and its corresponding Accomplishment Report for CY 2022. Henceforth, furnish the Audit Team with GAD reports in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person submitted to the Audit Team GAD reports in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014. The GAD Plan and Accomplishment Report for CY 2022 were furnished.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor: • require the Designated MGSO to determine necessary personnel who will/can attend to the insurance coverage of properties of the Municipality in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 and Republic Act No. 656.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality indicated that being a fifth class municipality it is not required under RA No. 656 to insure their insurable properties. The MGSO designated personnel to determine insurance matters where applicable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Development Council (MDC) and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to: a. Ensure that all development projects to be funded out of the 20 percent DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready; and b. Henceforth, comply with guidelines set in the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 in the appropriation and utilization of the 20 percent DF of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality implemented all its 17 development projects. 14 PPAs were completed while the remaining three PPAs were on-going as of year-end.
observations.reason: The Municipality strictly complied with the regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer (MT) in coordination with the HRMO to refrain from designating JO employees in the collection of fees and other charges of the Municipality in order to safeguard the funds and protect the interest of the government.
observations.managementAction: The MT in coordination with the HRMO reviewed and reorganized the contracts and designations of the Job Order personnel and assigned the Accountable Officers as Collector/Tellers to collect fees and other charges of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MT to direct all collectors/tellers to prepare individual RCD for their daily collections.
observations.managementAction: The MT have been supervising the daily preparation and submission of individual RCD of all collectors/tellers for their daily collections.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor strictly monitor the implementation of all LDRRM Project/Program/Activity (PPA) to ensure the achievement of the Municipality’s goal in mitigating the impact of disaster.
observations.managementAction: The materials were already delivered, and the construction is being monitored by the Municipal Engineering Office. The Deep Well Irrigation Supply project is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the General Service Officer (GSO)/Appraisal and Disposal Committee to proceed with the appraisal and disposal of the unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 79 of P.D. No. 1445 to avoid further deterioration and earn at least the salvage value from the sale of these assets.
observations.managementAction: The GSO prepared and submitted their Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and completed disposal the said properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor conduct the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts by requiring the Inventory Committee to complete the physical inventory in three months or less and require them to prepare and submit the approved Physical Inventory Plan to the AT at least 10 days before the scheduled start of inventory taking activities in accordance with Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006. Henceforth, submit to the AT the RPCPPE of the Municipality not later than January 31 of each year in compliance with Section 124 of the Manuals on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the RPCPPE to the AT.
observations.reason: Not so

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to: • Review and reconcile the balances of RPT and SET Receivables with their respective SLs and schedules; and • Henceforth, ensure that their records are reconciled so that the accuracy and completeness of the balances are reliable, in compliance with Section 111 of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made an adjustment to the General Ledgers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to stop the practice of designating JO employee in the collection of fees and other charges of the Municipality in order to safeguard the funds and protect the interest of the government. Henceforth, ensure compliance with Item A of Section 21 of the Local Treasury Operations Manual (LTOM) and Section 101 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Complied.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of PPE taking into consideration the guidelines prescribed in COA Circular No. 2020-006, for the one time cleansing of PPE account balances.
observations.managementAction: Conducted the inventory of PPE.
observations.reason: Conduct of PPE was not made due to non-prioritization of inventory of infrastructure projects and work/zoo animals

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to submit a request for write-off and attach the relevant supporting documents required under COA Circular No. 2016-005 for evaluation of the Audit Team since its collectability is remote.
observations.managementAction: Submitted a request for write-off.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) to ensure that the Funds received from the National Government was utilized.
observations.managementAction: No fund was received from the National Government in CY 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct the actual physical count of the Municipality’s property, and prepare and submit the RPCPPE and RPCLRN to the Audit Team (AT) not later than January 31 of each year, including the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP); b. Constantly monitor the status of physical inventory taking to ensure its completion and submission of complete inventory reports within the deadline. We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Accountant to exert efforts in identifying the PPE without details from the records and Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) of prior years and coordinate with the Municipal Engineer for the identification of the recorded infrastructure assets; b. Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit to the AT a complete Schedule of Accumulated Depreciation corresponding the balances in the General Ledger (GL) for review; c. Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the regulation on depreciation; and d. Municipal Accountant to maintain complete PPELC of the recorded PPE.
observations.managementAction: Conducted a partial inventory of PPE. Submitted the RPCPPE to the AT. Two Office failed to submit the complete inventory.
observations.reason: PPE was not made due to non-prioritization of inventory of infrastructure projects and work/zoo animals

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer to continue their efforts in locating and securing the documents that will support the land ownership of the Municipality; b. Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the titling of the lots once ownership by the Municipality is determined to avoid possible third-party claims and incurrence of litigation costs in the future; c. Municipal Assessor to facilitate the transfer of tax declaration of the seven purchased lot from former owner and ensure that all lots owned by the Municipality have tax declaration in its name, after its title is transferred in the name of the Municipality; and d. Appraisal Committee/Team to properly appraise all lots that were acquired through donation as to their fair values at the time of acquisition.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor has assigned a specific person made by the gathering and researching the documents to support the land Office and Assessor's Office. Requested the assistance of the Land Registration Authority for the status of various parcels of land. The Municipal Assessor & previous coordinating person (Mayor’s Office, Assessor’s Office and Treasurer’s Office) The appraisal of the donated parcels was delayed due to a miscommunication between the assessor's office and the mayor's office. However, this issue has now been clarified, and the mayor's office will issue an executive order for the land appraisal.
observations.reason: No proper turn-over of documents has been made by the previous concerned person

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC - Municipal Agriculturist to facilitate the implementation of the project and ensure its completion.
observations.managementAction: No fund was received from the National Government in CY 2023. Project for the establishment of the Demo Site-Organic Farming amounting P100,000.00 will be requested for extension and the refund of the Dairy Production amounting to P300,000.00 is on hold because the Treasurer's Office are waiting for the new checks with the updated format to be released.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Agriculturist, to plan and adopt effective collection strategies to recover the loaned funds from the borrowers such as offering payment alternatives adjusted to fit the borrowers’ financial capacity and continuous monitoring and follow-up; and b. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records to establish the correct balance of the Other Receivables account. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to maintain a supplementary record that will facilitate the monitoring of individual loan balance and preparation of supporting schedule at any given time.
observations.managementAction: Dec. 2024 The balances are already reconciled. Fully Implemented The Mun. Accountant will submit the supplementary record to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: The Mun. Accountant will submit the supplementary record to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the submission of request for write-off and the relevant supporting documents for evaluation of the Audit Team since its collectability is remote.
observations.managementAction: Submitted a request for write-off.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to complete the conduct of CBMS and consolidate its results with the other SDDs already gathered to establish the Municipal GAD database.
observations.managementAction: Conducted CBMS. Completed the conduct of CBMS and consolidated its results with the other SDDs already gathered to establish the Municipal GAD database.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MENRO-Designate to make the follow-ups regarding the review of the EMB Region IV-A of the Municipality's 10-Year SWMP and/or coordinate with the National Solid Waste Management Council (NSWMC) in order to know the steps to be undertaken for the approval of the plan.
observations.managementAction: Communications with EMB were made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Create an Inventory Committee and instruct the conduct thereon prepare the RPCI; and b. Enjoin the GSO Designate, Municipal Accountant, and Inventory Committee to conduct reconciliation of records/reports and effect adjustment in the appropriate record to arrive at a reconciled balance of Inventories as of a given period. Henceforth, ensure compliance with Section 490 of GAAM, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee was established through Executive Order No. 19 Series of 2023. The Municipality conducted the year-end physical count of inventory and reconciled the records/reports. The Municipality complied with Section 490 of GAAM, Volume I.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: a. Make the necessary journal entry to correct the balance of the Due from Other Funds and Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management accounts; b. Ensure that the correct amounts are reported in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Utilization Report; and c. Henceforth, comply with the provisions of PPSAS 1 and COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared JEV No. 100-2023-04-00606 and JEV No. 300-2023-04-041 both dated April 24, 2023, to reflect the necessary adjustments. The Municipal Accountant ensured that the correct amounts are reported in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Utilization Report. The Municipal Accountant complied with the provisions of PPSAS 1 and COA Circular No. 2012-002.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Require all the end-users or implementing units to prepare their respective PPMP and submit the same to the Budget Officer for evaluation and inclusion in the Municipality’s budget proposal and to the BAC for consolidation in the APP; b. Instruct the BAC to ensure that no procurement shall be undertaken unless included in the APP; and c. Require the BAC to prepare the PMR using the prescribed format and submit the duly approved report to the GPPB in printed and electronic format within 14 calendar days after the end of each semester. Henceforth, carefully plan its procurement activities to conform with the provisions of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer issued a memorandum dated October 12, 2023, stating that no procurement will be undertaken unless included in the APP; The BAC submitted the PMR to the Audit Team. Henceforth, carefully planned its procurement activities to conform with the provisions of RA No. 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer (ME) and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to: a. Submit the list of all on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year; b. Ensure that the Monitoring Report for the GPPAs are maintained as current as possible and updated; c. Inform the Audit Team thru the SA and ATL of posting of appropriate project signboards and/or public notices 10 days upon award of infrastructure projects and start of the program/activity; and d. Henceforth, comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004.
observations.managementAction: List of all on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year are submitted to the Audit Team. The Monitoring Report for the GPPAs are maintained as current as possible and updated. The Municipality complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Require the GSO to renew the PAR every January of the third year after its issuance or whenever there is a change in the custodianship; and b. Henceforth, comply strictly with Section 492 of the GAAM, Volume I to ensure the accountability of the property issued.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality updated the PAR on CY 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to verify with the Provincial Accountant the remaining unidentified discrepancies between the recorded balances of the reciprocal accounts reflected in the Provincial books and adjust the same, if warranted to present the correct balances of the said accounts in the Municipality’s financial statement.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared and submitted the Statement of Receipts and Disbursements to the Province of Quezon for the unreconciled balance of P9.00.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to continue in exerting extra efforts to complete the titling process of the aforementioned parcels of land.
observations.managementAction: Not Two of the parcels of land are already in the titling process while the remaining two are still on documentation phase.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the department heads of other concerned offices to coordinate with the MPDC in providing additional SDD to expedite the establishment of a GAD Database that will assist the gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC has submitted the GAD Database to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• Instruct the Municipal Engineer (ME) and the Local Chief Executive Officer (MPDO) to submit the list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year;
• Inform the Audit Team thru the SA and ATL of posting of appropriate project signboards and/or public notices 10 days upon award of infrastructure projects and start of the program/activity; and
• Instruct the ME and MPDO to ensure that signboards of all infrastructure projects of the Municipality are provided with the relevant information in compliance with Annex A of COA Circular No. 2013-004.
observations.managementAction:
An Office Order was issued by the Municipal Engineer and the Local Chief Executive instructing the Municipal Engineer to comply with the recommendations of the Audit Team.
The Municipal Engineer regularly and timely submits the GPPA monitoring reports to the Audit Team, as well as the proofs of posting of the appropriate signboards.
Engineer issued reminder letters to the winning contractors to ensure the completeness of information in the project signboards.
Henceforth, submit the above required list within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Development Council (MDC) and the Officer-In-Charge – Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (OIC-MPDC) to: a. Ensure that all development projects to be funded out of the 20 percent DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready; and b. Henceforth, comply with guidelines set in the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 in the appropriation and utilization of the 20 percent DF of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Not Reiterated in audit observation no. 5, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer (MT) to exert effort and adopt remedies to enforce collection of delinquent RPT and SET in accordance with RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Management issued demand letters to delinquent taxpayers that caused the significant increase in the collection of RPT/SET.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-General Service Officer (GSO)/Inventory Committee Chairperson to prepare Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and to facilitate the disposal of the unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 79 of P.D. No. 1445 to avoid further deterioration of the same and earn at least the salvage value from the sale of these assets.
observations.managementAction: The IIRUP was already submitted to the Audit Team including documents related to the disposal of the said properties indicated in the IIRUP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to accelerate the modification of the construction layout/plan so that the said project will be implemented for the benefit of the constituents.
observations.managementAction: Already submitted the final layout plan to Department of Environment and Natural Resources (DENR) based on the cadastral survey.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately transfer the unexpended balances of the Due to NGAs held in Trust to the General Fund (GF).
observations.managementAction: The Municipality refunded the source agency.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to prioritize the remittance of the amount of GSIS contributions amounting to P1,985,119.65 to GSIS.
observations.managementAction: Successfully remitted the contributions amounting to P1,985,119.65 to GSIS upon reconciliation/updating of the employee's records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and MGSO to: • Complete the reconciliation of the accounting with property records in order to establish correct balances for the inventories; and • Investigate and adjust any discrepancy that will be noted.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was able to resubmit reconciled inventory accounts as of December 31, 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to revert back to Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund any unutilized LDRRMF in the Trust Fund books.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer was able to submit reconciled CRAAF for CY 2022 accompanied by the individual RAAFs of the Accountable Officers. JEV Nos. 300-2023-05-00101 to 100-2023-05-01134 dated May 11, 2023 were posted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to: • Prepare an adjusting entry to correct the recording of impaired KKK Receivables in the amount of P2,315.00; and • Identify the transactions comprising the beginning balance of Government Equity account of P133,195.00, prepare the necessary journal entries and furnish the Audit Team in copies for validation purposes. Henceforth, ensure that proper recording of entries in the Trust Fund books of accounts for the fair presentation in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made the necessary correcting entries booked in CY 2023 under JEV No. 300-2023-05-00102 dated May 11, 2023. Identified the transactions comprising the beginning balance of Government Equity account and corrective entry was made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to allocate enough time to verify the details of the unremitted amounts or the accumulated/unreconciled discrepancies in the accounts and reconciliation for their fair presentation in the financial statements in accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1, and remit the balances due to the agencies concerned to avoid incurrence of penalties and surcharges.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already remitted the balances for Due to GSIS, Due to Pag-IBIG and Due to PhilHealth. P1,365,614.22 of the P1,404,691.61 balance of the Due to GSIS account as of December 31, 2023, were remitted on January 5, 2024. The Municipal Accounting Office is reconciling the remaining P39,077.39 account balance with the GSIS due to salary differential premiums.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, the Municipal Engineer and the Bids and Awards Committee to prioritize and expedite the implementation of the PPAs for the 20% Development Fund to ensure that intended beneficiaries could avail of the maximum benefits that will be derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was able to fully utilize the 20% Development Fund appropriated in 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team proof that goods and services are accepted and that supplier/creditor bills are received for claims listed in the Accounts Payable together with the contract/Purchase Order if any, for our verification; otherwise prepare an adjusting entry debiting the Accounts Payable and crediting the corresponding account/s.
Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure the recording of Accounts Payable is in accordance with Section 4(s) of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared and submitted to the Audit Team Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 100-2023-04-1216 dated April 13, 2023, adjusting the unclaimed accounts payable totaling P1,196,974.66 as of December 31, 2022. The Municipal Accountant ensured the recording of Accounts Payable in accordance with the MNGAS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: • Municipal Health Officer to provide the Municipal Accountant with the inventory reports on the receipt, utilization, and distribution of COVID-19 vaccines and various ancillary supplies based on the fair value at the date of acquisition. • Municipal Accountant to record in the books of accounts of the Municipality the remaining 2,517 vials of COVID-19 vaccines and various ancillary supplies based on the fair value at the date of acquisition.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Health Officer provided the Municipal Accountant with the inventory reports on the receipt, utilization, and reports of COVID-19 vaccines and ancillary supplies. The Municipal Accountant recorded them in the books of accounts per Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 100-2023-04-1309 dated April 19, 2023, amounting to P887,920.66.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• Instruct the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to submit the list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year and its monthly monitoring report within the prescribed period; and
• Inform the Audit Team thru the Supervising Auditor of posting of appropriate project signboards and/or public notices 10 days upon award of infrastructure projects and start of the program/activity.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer submitted the list of all ongoing government PPAs in CY 2023. The Municipal Engineer complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
• Ensure that the recording of liabilities is in accordance with Section 4(s) of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and
• Ensure that all claims against government funds are properly supported with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant ensured the recording of Accounts Payable in accordance with the MNGAS. The Municipal Accountant ensured that all claims against government funds are properly supported with complete documentation.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to continue adopting efficient and aggressive collection strategies and enforce the repayment terms offered by the Municipality to ensure recovery of the amounts from the existing beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer adopted efficient and aggressive collection strategies and was able to collect amount from debtors during CY 2022.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Inventory Committee to:
a. Submit the RPCPPE and Inventory reports of the physical count pertaining to the PPEs of the Municipality were submitted to the Audit Team.
b. Conduct the one-time cleansing in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006; and
c. Henceforth, ensure the timely submission of the RPCPPE and RPCLRN to the Municipal Accountant and to the Audit Team for validation and evaluation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Administrator is amenable to the audit recommendations and committed to its compliance.
observations.reason: Other documents pertaining to the preliminary steps in conducting the one-time cleansing are yet to be gathered/prepared. The inventory reports of the physical count pertaining the PPEs were only submitted on February 23, 2024.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the OIC – Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Reconcile their respective records; and
b. Ensure that subsidiary ledgers and the cashbooks are reconciled in accordance with Section 181 of GAAM, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant reconciled her records with the Cashbooks.
observations.reason: The reconciliation of subsidiary ledgers and the Cashbooks were only done once as at year-end of CY 2023, instead of quarterly.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC - Municipal Treasurer to direct the Accountable Officers to prepare and submit their monthly RAAF using the prescribed format to the OIC - Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the preparation of monthly CRAAF.
Henceforth, to strictly comply with Sections 50 and 51 of MNGAS for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented
observations.reason: The provisions of the MNGAS criteria were complied with. Supporting RAAFs were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: • Head of the Inventory, Appraisal and Disposal Committee to complete the RPCPPE and submit the same to the Audit Team; and • Municipal General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to pursue the reconciliation of the accounting with the property records in the order to establish the correct PPE balances and adjust any discrepancy that will be noted.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory, Appraisal and Disposal Committee completed the RPCPPE and submitted the same to the Audit Team; and the Municipal General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant pursued the reconciliation of the accounting records with the property records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to transfer the unobligated balance of LDRRMF from General Fund to Trust Fund.
observations.managementAction: The unexpended balance for CY 2022 has been transferred to the Trust Fund account and for CY 2023 unexpended/ MOOE remain as continuing for the period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary entries for the reversion of the total balance of Accounts Payable P468,599.56 to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund with a condition that are communicated to the respective payees for the processing of their claims. Henceforth, ensure proper monitoring of the Accounts Payable account so that claims nearing the maturity of two years are communicated to the respective payees for the processing of their claims.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made the necessary entries and continuously monitored the account so that claims nearing the maturity of two years are communicated to the respective payees for the processing of their claims.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: • instruct the Municipal Engineer (ME) and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to submit the list of all on-going government PPA and those that are to be implemented during the year and its monthly monitoring report within the prescribed period; • inform the Audit Team of posting of appropriate signboards and/or public notices 10 days upon award of infrastructure projects and start of the program/activity; and • instruct the ME and MPDC to ensure that signboards of all infrastructure projects of the Municipality are provided and with all the relevant information in compliance with Annex A of COA Circular No. 2013-004. Henceforth, submit the above required list within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality posted the appropriate signboard, however submission of notices has been made to Audit Team on a quarterly basis. Infrastructure Projects are provided with signboards with all relevant information.
observations.reason: Monthly monitoring reports were not submitted to the Audit Team within the prescribed period; submission was made quarterly instead.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to: • direct the Accountable Officers to prepare and submit their monthly RAAF using the prescribed format to the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to facilitate the preparation of monthly CRAAF; and • observe timely submission of the monthly CRAAF using the prescribed format and accompanied by the individual RAAF of AOs.
observations.managementAction: The Accountable Officers prepared and submitted the monthly RAAF to the Municipal Treasurer. Monthly CRAAF, accompanied by the individual RAAF of Accountable Officers were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: • Municipal Accountant to record the purchases of supplies for stock under the Inventory account and the issuances thereof shall be recorded as expense as they take place under the appropriate expense accounts; • Inventory Committee/Acting Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit monthly Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) based on the Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) to the Municipal Accounting Office in support to the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) recognizing the issued materials as expenses; • Municipal Accountant to maintain Supplies Ledger Cards (SLCs) for each commodity/stock to facilitate the reconciliation and for verification of the year-end balances; and • Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of all supplies and materials inventory of the Municipality every semester and to prepare and render a complete Report on Physical Count of Inventory (RPCI) and submit to the Audit Team not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for first and second semesters, respectively.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant recorded the purchases of supplies and materials under the Inventory account and the issuances thereof shall be recorded as expense as they take place under the appropriate expense accounts. The Municipal Treasurer started the preparation and submission of the SSMI based on RIS. The Municipal Accountant maintained the Supplies Ledger Cards (SLC). The Inventory Committee conducted inventory and submitted the RPCI.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to remit the tax withheld of officers/employees, contractors/suppliers and other entities to the BIR.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer remitted the tax withheld of officers/employees, contractors/suppliers and other entities to BIR, as evidenced by JEV No. 100-2022-03-00274 dated March 9, 2022 and JEV Nos. 100-2022-04-00451 and 100-2022-04-00447 both dated April 7, 2022 totaling P198,403.16. The remaining P90,409.89 pertains to tax refund to retired employees and suppliers, therefore it was reclassified as payables per JEV No 100-2022-07-02063 dated July 1, 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to act on the insurance of the buildings as well as the heavy equipment with GSIS.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer acted on the insurance of the buildings as well as the heavy equipment with GSIS.

The Municipal Accountant reclassified the PPEs to the appropriate accounts. The accounting and property records are already reconciled. Property Stickers are already attached to the PPE items which showed the vital information of the PPE. Inventory Committee, Municipal Accountant, and Property Unit reconciled.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Prepare and maintain SLs for the Accounts Payable account to easily monitor the liabilities to suppliers;
b. Determine whether the amount of P5,307,261.16 are valid claims of the suppliers, otherwise make the necessary entries for its reversion to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund, with a condition that appropriation shall be made available in the event that claim arises in the future; and
c. Henceforth, comply with the provisions of Sections 111 and 98 of PD No. 1445 and Section 10 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II.
observations.managementAction: SLs are already maintained by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the:
a. Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical count of inventories, and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) in the prescribed format; and
b. Supply Officer Designate, Municipal Accountant, and Inventory Committee to conduct reconciliation of records/reports and effect the appropriate record to arrive at a reconciled balance of Inventories as of a given period.
Henceforth, ensure compliance with Section 490 of GAAM, Volume I and Sections 11 and 44 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II.
observations.managementAction: Also, the Inventory Committee conducted the physical count of inventories and submitted the RPCI. The Supply Officer Designate, Municipal Accountant, and Inventory Committee conducted reconciliation of records/reports and effected adjustments in the appropriate records to arrive at a reconciled balance of inventories as of the period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry in the books of the Municipality for CY 2022 by debiting cash and crediting the appropriate income/liability/equity accounts; and
b. Municipal Treasurer and the RCOs to remit daily collections at close of each business day.
Henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of Section 29 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I to ensure that all collections are recorded in the appropriate reporting period.
observations.managementAction: Adjustments were made by the Municipal Accountant in the books, hence collections were recorded in the appropriate reporting period. The Municipal Treasurer and the RCOs will remit daily collections at close of each business day.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
a. The Municipal Accountant to account the Advances to Special Disbursing Officer amounting to P24,450.00;
b. The concerned Accountable Officers and Employees to immediately liquidate the CAs totaling P235,768.88; and
c. The Municipal Accountant to ensure that all CAs are recorded in the proper receivable account in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009.
Henceforth, ensure that each CA is reported on as soon as the purpose for which it was given has been served and that all CAs are fully liquidated at the end of each year in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant records all CAs to appropriate receivable account.
observations.reason: Not all long outstanding CAs were liquidated; some remain unliquidated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balance of the Land account;
b. Municipal Assessor to hasten the titling of the 26 parcels of land in the name of the Municipality of San Francisco; and
c. Henceforth, comply with Section 50 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Section 449 of GAAM, Volume II.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already prepared the adjusting entry to correct the Land account. One parcel already covered with land title, four were already in process with the Land Registration Authority, while retrieval of documents for the remaining parcels of land is still on going.
observations.reason: Retrieval of documents for the remaining parcels of land is still on going.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Health Officer (MHO) to provide the Municipal Accountant with the inventory reports on the receipt, utilization and the distribution of donations in-kind related to its COVID-19 Vaccination Program as basis in the recording in the books of accounts; and
b. Municipal Accountant to record in the books of accounts the remaining 791 vials of COVID-19 vaccines and various ancillary supplies based on the fair value at the date of acquisition.
Henceforth, ensure compliance with Paragraph 96 of IPSAS 23, Revenue from Non-Exchange Transactions (Taxes and Transfers) and COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020.
observations.managementAction: The MHO submitted the inventory reports. The Municipal Accountant prepared Journal Entry Voucher No. 46 dated August 7, 2023 to record the remaining 791 vials of COVID-19 vaccines and ancillary supplies.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to provide the accumulated depreciation, accumulated impairment losses and the carrying amount of the unserviceable PPE per IIRUP;
b. Appraisal and Disposal Committee to update the IIRUP to include the garbage truck costing P820,000.00 and set the final appraised value of all unserviceable properties; and
c. General Services Officer, through the Appraisal and Disposal Committee, to conduct immediate disposal of all unserviceable properties following the procedures set forth in COA Circular 92-386.
Henceforth, ensure compliance with Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445, Manual on Disposal of Government Properties and Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: The IIRUP is already updated which includes the garbage truck. The Appraisal and Disposal Committee is awaiting approval of the new management.
observations.reason: Conduct of the disposal is still not made pending approval of the new Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMO to carefully study the feasibility of the projects/activities before its inclusion in the LDRRM Plan to ensure its implementation in order for the Municipality to be adequately prepared for any upcoming disaster/calamity to reduce its risk. Henceforth, ensure the implementation of all planned disaster PPAs included in the MDRRMP particularly those included in the 70 percent LDRRMF-MF in accordance with RA No. 10121.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO examines the undisbursed funds in the LDRRMF and is taking actions to implement planned projects. The MDRRMO remains committed to providing services and striving to make the Municipality disaster-resilient.
observations.reason: There are ongoing procurements; some appropriations remain unutilized.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDC and the MPDC to:
a. Ensure that all development projects to be funded out of the 20 percent DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready; and
b. Henceforth, comply with guidelines set in the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 in the appropriation and utilization of the 20 percent DF of the Municipality.
