observations.managementAction: The Designated Municipal Engineer/MPDC submitted Sangguniang Bayan approved AIP under 20% development fund and committed to formulate well-planned and procurement-ready projects.
observations.reason: Ten PPAs were not implemented; three were not well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor Require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and maintain Property Cards, and reconcile their records regularly with the Accounting Office.
observations.managementAction: The Property Custodian already maintains Property Cards and reconciles her records regularly with the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor demand the concerned officials and employees to liquidate immediately the remaining unliquidated CAs of prior years. Otherwise, implement the withholding of their salaries in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant continued efforts to reconcile and will demand liquidation; some long outstanding CAs have been liquidated.
observations.reason: There are still long outstanding CAs that remain unliquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to fast-track the securing of the required clearance/permit from the DPWH for the installation of the streetlights.
observations.managementAction: Signboards and/or public notices are already posted and submitted to the Audit Team for validation; copies of contracts and purchase orders already submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Human Resource Management to immediately fill up the afore-mentioned positions of the LDRRMO as required under Item 5.2 of the NDRRMC, DILG, DBM, and Civil Service Commission (CSC) Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2014-1.
observations.managementAction: The mandatory positions are already filled up.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to establish the breakdown of the afore-mentioned Inter-Agency Payables accounts, make adjusting entries after reconciliation and remit the amounts due to the BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth. We also recommended to instruct the Human Resource and Management Officer to secure the necessary GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth employee numbers needed in the availment of benefits and for proper recording.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant continued efforts to reconcile the balances and make necessary adjustments and remittances where due; coordination with agencies ongoing.
observations.reason: Prior year balances of the Due to GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth accounts remained unaccounted/unreconciled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to have their records updated and reconciled and subsequently make the necessary adjustments for the discrepancies in their records. Furthermore, require the Municipal Accountant to continue exerting efforts in retrieving relevant documents such as the Statement of Receipts and Disbursements (SORDs) and reconcile the unaccounted balances between the GL and SL totals of the CIB accounts in the TF.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer are continuing efforts to reconcile records and retrieve supporting documents. Adjustments were made where possible.
observations.reason: Difficulty in retrieving documents from prior years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to provide the details or breakdown of the aforementioned unidentified/unaccounted balances and make necessary adjustments or refund to employees the over deduction of loan payments in salary payrolls after verification of supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant continues retrieval of documents and adjustments; recovery/refund actions being undertaken.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of accounts is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to continue his effort in retrieving all relevant documents such as SORDs for all funds received from the National Government to establish the details or breakdown of the aforementioned unidentified balances and make necessary adjustments, if any. Furthermore, request the COA for authority to write-off the dormant/unaccounted balances if warranted.
observations.managementAction: Despite efforts, the amounts remained unaccounted; actions were initiated to request write-off authority from COA where applicable.
observations.reason: Records remain unaccounted; awaiting COA action for write-off.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Accountant continue their efforts in retrieving the unsubmitted DVs in CY 2017 and oblige the former Municipal Accountant to comply with its submission to the Audit Team for verification and post audit. We also recommended to consider appropriate legal action, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: The Local Government Unit will continue efforts to retrieve and submit the unsubmitted DVs and consider appropriate action as necessary.
observations.reason: Awaiting course of action of the Municipal Mayor; retrieval efforts ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer submit to the Audit Team the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for each motor vehicle duly supported with Driver’s Trip Tickets issued during the month for review and evaluation. Henceforth, see to it that the Driver’s Trip Tickets were properly accomplished.
observations.managementAction: Reports and Driver’s Trip Tickets were prepared and submitted; Driver’s Trip Tickets attached to the DVs.
observations.reason: Some reports were not submitted within the deadline but were later provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat to submit to the Audit Team the copies of the contracts and purchase orders as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2009-001 together with supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: Copies of contracts and purchase orders were already submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer/BAC Chairperson to:
a. Inform the Audit Team within 10 days after the award of the PPAs or before its start that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices were already posted for validation; and
b. Regularly submit the monthly monitoring report.
observations.managementAction: Quarterly monitoring reports on PPAs were submitted to the Commission on Audit through email and regular submission practices were instituted.
observations.reason: Some monitoring reports were submitted; periodic reporting still being improved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MENRO to complete the conduct of WACS and subsequently secure the approval of the 10-year SWM Plan from the NSWMC. Furthermore, continue efforts in establishing the SLF including acquisition of the proposed site and compliance with the geological assessment recommendations.
observations.managementAction: The Local Government Unit has prepared a SWM Plan and continues efforts to establish SLF; MENRO to complete WACS and pursue NSWMC approval.
observations.reason: Establishment of SLF was not prioritized; site acquisition and compliance activities ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry in the books of the Municipality for CY 2022 by debiting cash and crediting the appropriate income/liability/equity accounts totaling P1,058,181.27; and
b. MT and the RCOs to strictly comply with Section 29 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I and to remit daily collections at the end of each working day.
observations.managementAction:
a. The Municipal Accountant made an adjusting entry in the Cash Receipts Journal for the month of January 2023.
b. The Municipality has issued memorandum to concerned Revenue Collection Clerks (RCCs) and Liquidating Officers dated May 25, 2022 to regularly monitor the accountable forms issued to RCCs.
observations.reason: Lack of Proper Monitoring.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MT to immediately remit the balance of P429,294.18 to the respective Barangays. Henceforth, strictly comply with Section 271 (d) of R.A. No. 7160 in order for the Barangays to utilize the funds for priority PPAs.
observations.managementAction: Barangay shares were remitted quarterly.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MT to:
a. direct the RCC and Accountable Officers to prepare and submit their monthly RAAF using the prescribed format to the Municipal Treasurer’s Office (MTO) to facilitate the preparation of the monthly CRAAF; and
b. submit the monthly CRAAF accompanied by the individual RAAF of the Accountable Officers as prescribed in the above-mentioned Manual.
observations.managementAction:
a. The RCC and Liquidating Officers were directed to prepare and submit monthly RAAF.
b. The said reports accompanied by the individual RAAF of the Accountable Officers were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MT in coordination with the Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) to stop the practice of designating JO employees in the collection of fees and other charges of the Municipality in order to safeguard the funds and protect the interest of the government. Henceforth, strictly comply with Section 170 of R.A. No. 7160 and Section 101 of P.D. No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor through Office Order No. 6-2023 dated March 7, 2023, directed the MT to immediately stop the practice of designating JO Workers in the collection of fees and other charges of the Municipality. Further list of duties and functions of JO workers assigned in the MTO were submitted to the Office of the HRMO for daily monitoring.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MT to proceed with the titling of the land using the documents available on file that was presented to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The MT coordinated with concerned National Government Agencies (NGAs) in updating documents and requirements.
observations.reason: To date, no documents pertaining to this recommendation were submitted to the Audit Team by Municipality

observations.managementAction:
The MGSO and the Municipal Accountant pursued and completed the reconciliation of the property records with the accounting records showing no variance for CY 2023.
Municipal Accountant to exert effort to identify the unaccounted properties from prior years’ records for the fair presentation of the PPE accounts in the financial statements.
observations.reason: Despite the effort exerted by the Municipal Accountant to locate/identify the unaccounted properties. details of unaccounted properties from prior years, it remain unaccounted as of December 31, 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary entries for the reversion of the total Accounts Payable as evidence by JEV No. 100-2023-04-1732. Henceforth, ensure proper monitoring of the Accounts Payable account so that claims nearing the maturity of two years are communicated to the respective payees for the processing of their claims.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant monitored the Accounts Payable account so that claims nearing the maturity of two years are communicated to the respective payees for the processing of their claims.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• instruct the Municipal Engineer (ME) and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to submit the list of all on-going government PPA and those that are to be implemented during the year and its monthly monitoring report within the prescribed period;
• inform the Audit Team of posting of appropriate project signboards and/or public notices 10 days upon award of infrastructure projects and start of the program/activity; and,
• instruct the ME and the MPDC to ensure that signboards of all infrastructure projects of the Municipality are provided with the relevant information in compliance with Annex A of COA Circular No. 2013-004.
Henceforth, submit the above required list within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction:
The ME submitted to the Audit Team the monitoring reports quarterly.
The ME and the MPDC ensured that signboards of all infrastructure projects of the Municipality are provided with the relevant information in compliance with Annex A of COA Circular No. 2013-004.
Submitted the required list quarterly.
observations.reason:
Monthly monitoring reports were not submitted to the Audit Team, instead submission was made quarterly.
Henceforth, submit the above required list within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to make payments thru issuance of checks directly to suppliers. In instances when it may be very difficult, impractical or impossible to make payments by checks, payments may be made by the OIC-Municipal Treasurer or a designated Special Disbursing Officer in the form of cash through his cash advance.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer made payments thru issuance of checks directly to suppliers. In instances when it may be very difficult, impractical or impossible to make payments by checks, payments may be made by the OIC-Municipal Treasurer or a designated Special Disbursing Officer in the form of cash through his cash advance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer (MT) to remit the funds to the source agencies. Henceforth, comply with COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994 on the utilization and liquidation of funds from source agencies.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality conformed with COA Circular No. 94-013.
observations.reason: The remaining amount of P157,452.65 represents balance for ongoing activity: Vermin-composting (shed house) for Organic Fertilizer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MT to direct the Accountable Officers to prepare and submit their monthly RAAF using the prescribed format to the MT to facilitate the preparation of monthly CRAAF. Henceforth, to strictly comply with Sections 50 and 51 of MNGAS for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The MT submitted the monthly CRAAF and RAAF to the Audit Team. Henceforth, to strictly comply with Sections 50 and 51 of MNGAS for LGUs. The MT complied with the submission under Sections 50 and 51 of MNGAS for LGUs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Instruct the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to submit the list of all on-going government PPA and those that are to be implemented during the year as well as its monthly updated and completely filled-out monitoring reports to the Audit Team within the prescribed period;
b. Inform the Audit Team thru the Supervising Auditor and Audit Team Leader of posting of project signboards and/or public notices 10 days upon award of infrastructure projects and start of the program/activity; and
c. Henceforth, ensure compliance with Items 2.1, 3.1 and 3.2 of COA Circular No. 2013-004.
observations.managementAction: Concerned Officials were able to submit the reports and were able to submit the reports to the Audit Team as well as its updated completely filled-out monthly monitoring reports. The Audit Team Leader was properly informed of the posting of appropriate project signboards. The Municipality was able to comply with the COA Circular No. 2013-004.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to facilitate the return of the unused balance of P6,000.00 to the respective source agency for the completed/implemented program/project; and

b. Coordinate with the DBM Regional Treasurer Office IV-A regarding the utilization/implementation of the Rescue Equipment and School Furniture and/or the disposition of funds should the intended purpose could no longer be materialized.

Henceforth, ensure compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994 on the utilization and liquidation of funds transfers.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant facilitated the return of the balance of P6,000.00 to its source agency. The Municipal Treasurer returned P1,353,165.00 to its source agency.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
• Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer (GSO) and the Municipal Accountant to determine/identify the unidentified municipal properties; and
• Disposal Committee to undertake the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties to avoid further deterioration and possible loss and to generate additional income from its disposal.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has undertaken the one-time cleansing which resulted to the reconciliation of records of the Accounting and GSO. The Disposal Committee disposed most of the unserviceable properties of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Appraisal Committee to immediately appraise the two Motor Vehicles as this is needed for its disposal and the Disposal Committee to proceed with the disposal of the 11 Motor Vehicles. Thereafter, instruct the Municipal Accountant to drop the disposed properties from the books of accounts for the fair presentation of the PPE accounts in the financial statements pursuant to Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: Appraisal/Disposal proceeding is on-going. The Disposal Committee proceeding is on-going. The Resolution adopted by the Appraisal Committee together with the other supporting documents were submitted to COA on August 28, 2024.
observations.reason: However, its disposal is yet to be undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor conduct the One-Time Cleansing requiring the Inventory Committee to complete the physical inventory in three months or less and require them to prepare and submit the approved Physical Inventory Plan to the AT at least 10 days before the scheduled start of inventory taking activities in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006. Henceforth, submit to the AT the RPCPPE of the Municipality not later than January 31 of each year in compliance with Section 124 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee conducted the One-Time Cleansing of PPE accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: a. Make an adjusting entry for the unreleased checks by debiting Cash in Bank-Local Currency, Current Account and crediting Accounts Payable in the amount of P2,693,233.14 in the GF and P40,386.24 under the TF; and b. Strictly comply with Section 43 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I by ensuring that only checks included in the Report of Checks Issued (RCI) are recorded in the Check Disbursements Journal.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made the necessary accounting entry. The agency has corrected its practice regarding this matter.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Instruct the Municipal Engineer (ME) and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to submit the list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year to the AT; b. Ensure that all on-going projects and those that are to be implemented are completely reported in the monthly monitoring report and ensure that it is as current as possible and updated; and c. Inform the AT thru the Officer-in-Charge-Supervising Auditor (OIC-SA) and AT Leader (ATL) of posting of appropriate project signboards and/or public notices 10 days upon award of infrastructure projects and start of the program/activity in compliance with COA Circular No. 2013-004.
observations.managementAction: The ME and MPDC has submitted the list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year to the AT. The ME has submitted the monthly monitoring report of all on-going projects and those that are to be implemented. The ME has informed the AT thru the OIC-SA and/or ATL of posting of appropriate signboards and/or public notices 10 days upon award of infrastructure projects and start of the program/activity.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the ICO-Municipal Accountant to: a. Make the following necessary adjustments to correct the misclassification of the account. Account Debit (Dr.) Credit (Cr.) Disaster and Response Rescue Equipment P Dr. 2,476,998.00 Motor Vehicles Cr. 1,348,000.00 Power Supply Systems Cr. 960, 000.00 Other Cr. 168,998.00 b. Reclassify all equipment charged under the LDRRMF to Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment account. Henceforth, strictly adhere with the accounting and reporting guidelines for the LDRRMF or COA Circular No. 2012-002, dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made the necessary accounting entry. The Municipal Accountant made the necessary reclassification of accounts. The agency has corrected its practice regarding this matter.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the titling of the three parcels of land to avoid contingent claims that may arise in the future and in compliance with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor has processed the re-application for the titling of the remaining two parcels of land. The Community Environment and Natural Resources Office (CENRO) also provided the AT a certification that the said lots are being applied for titling under (Special Patent) by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following –

a. The Municipal Accountant’s Office make the necessary adjustments on the affected accounts and record properties funded out of the financial assistance only when the documents evidencing transfer of ownership could be obtained from the source agency.

b. The Management to –

Prepare the donation documents and submit the same to the Provincial Monitoring Office so that the donation will be facilitated;

Once the Deed of Donation was obtained, forward it to the Municipal Accountants Office for the recording of the assets in the General Fund of the LGU.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant’s Office made the necessary adjusting entries to derecognize the assets from the LGU’s books until the Deed of Donation are obtained.

Processing of Deed of Donations are still pending at the Provincial Government.

Recording of semi-expendables per ICS and PAR monitoring report of the GSO is in accordance with the thresholds under COA Circular No. 2022-004, however, the forms and reports as required by the COA Circular were not yet maintained by the PSMD. Issuances of semi-expendables were reflected in the SSMI instead of the RSPI and RegSPI.

We have not yet received a copy of the following requested documents:

Semi-Expendable Property Card (PSMD)
Semi-Expendable Property Ledger Card (MACCO)
Registry of Semi-Expendable Property Issued (PSMD)
Report of Semi-Expendable Property Issued (RSPI)
Report on the Physical Count of Semi-Expendable Property (Inventory Committee)
Estimated useful life of each semi-expendable property

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to: 

Record subsequent receipts of the ER No. 1-94 funds in the Share from National Wealth account; and 

Record earned interests of the ER No. 1-94 funds as Interest Income.
observations.reason: Transactions were still recorded in the Due to NGAs Account.

observations.managementAction:
The LGU prepared a timeline and the necessary steps to accomplish the preparation of PPMP for consolidation to APP. Since the process is still on-going, some offices have already submitted their PPMPs and the others are still preparing the mentioned documents.

The management together with all the department/office heads conducted a meeting regarding the GPPB prescribed format and were instructed to follow and properly fill-up the required information.
observations.reason: The BAC did not furnish our office with a copy of the PPMPs in CY 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to require the BAC Secretariat to prepare the APP using the format prescribed by GPPB Circular No. 07-2015, herein attached as Annex E. A softcopy of the prescribed APP format in excel file was also sent to the BAC Secretariat through electronic mail on February 20, 2023.
observations.reason: The BAC did not follow the prescribed format of the APP in CY 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to require the BAC to follow strictly follow the guidelines on alternative methods of procurement under Rule XVI of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.reason: The BAC Resolution states “the following Purchase Requests (PR) were submitted before the BAC for evaluation and determination as to the mode of procurement, and upon validation, the following items as indicated in the Purchase Request are not considered ordinary supplies and equipment commonly used goods for the government” but the mode of procurement undertaken by the LGU was not reflected in the APP per Section 48.3 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA. No. 9184.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to do the following:

Require the Municipal Budget Office (MBO) to submit the legal basis for continuing the unexpended priors’ unexpended 20% LDF-MOOE. Otherwise, if there is no legal basis, revert the same to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund; and 

Require the MBO to refrain from continuing any appropriations for MOOE.
observations.reason:
Upon checking the SAAOB, there are still MOOE as of December 31, 2023 that were carried over to the next calendar year.

The Budget Officer will revert all the continuing MOOE this CY 2024.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to do the following:

Provide appropriations for the MOOE of the MDRRMO from the General Fund of the LGU; and

Ensure that the LDRRMF is only allocated PAPs which are in line with the four thematic areas of the fund.
