observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to ensure that before withdrawal of fuel or delivery thereof is made, the required procurement process was undertaken and the Purchase Order as well as the Notice to Proceed are already executed and received by the supplier.

We recommended to require the Municipal Accountant to continue the reconciliation of land reported in lump amount and disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements necessary information on these real properties.

Require the Municipal Assessor to constantly follow up the status of the submitted documents (for titling of the real properties of the municipality at the Registry of Deeds and DENR provincial office at Malaybalay City to ensure titling of all the lands owned by the municipality in compliance to Section 47 (C) of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Local Government Code of 1991 and Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.   

Direct the General Services Officer to keep a complete record of all real properties owned by the LGU in compliance to Articles 122 and 440 of the IRR of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction:
The land account has been reconciled. Information about the land account has been disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements since 2018. 

The process of titling of the untitled land owned by the LGU Quezon through Land Administration Program (LAMP) is ongoing.
observations.reason:
Two (2) titles have been released.

18 applications for title issuances (Public Schools) are still pending to date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Reconciliation Committee, composed with the accounting and property unit personnel, to continue the reconciliation of reports per inventory count, per property and per accounting records by taking into consideration the clear-cut guidelines provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, in order to analyze and review the existence, validity and accuracy of PPE items which comprised the bulk of unreconciled beginning balances. Effect necessary adjusting entries after reconciliation, if warranted.
observations.managementAction:
The Inventory Committee is still in the process of reconciling the beginning balances.

The LGU will continue the reconciliation efforts already started by the Committees concerned and implement COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

Adjusting entries, supported with working papers and other pertinent documents, shall thereafter be effected as warranted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to reassess the programmed projects whether to be charged to MOOE or Capital Outlay by taking into consideration the criteria laid out in IPSAS No. 17 and Section 4.2 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 on the determination whether to capitalize the cost or to expense outright. 
Moreover, directed the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to recognize the PPE accounts and the depreciation expense, if any, to fairly present the affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Adjustments were made by the Municipal Accountant’s Office for all of the projects in this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to require the Municipal Accountant to make adjusting entries to record the transferred projects pending compliance with the above-mentioned lacking documents and to ensure that all transferred assets are duly approved in accordance with Section 381 of Republic Act 7160, in order to present fairly the financial statements of the LGU. Henceforth, the Municipal Accounting Office to properly record capitalizable projects undertaken by LGU Quezon as assets and not as direct expense. For a more convenient and expeditious manner, the management may resort implementing the proposed projects thru financial assistance to be implemented by the component barangays and subject for subsequent liquidation.

We recommended to direct the Municipal Accountant to reissue demand letters and exhaust all possible remedies to collect the receivables and to demand liquidation of cash advances and fund transfers. Conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of these accounts. Comply with all the conditions and requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 for the possible request for the write off of the specified dormant receivables and cash advances accounts to the Commission on Audit. Provide allowance for impairment thereafter and reconcile the accounts with unidentified/ unreconciled subsidiary ledgers from prior years in order to have a fair presentation of affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
Demand Letters have been reissued last October 2023 to accountable officers for the liquidation of the accounts.
Request for write-off will be made after reconciliation of receivable accounts. 
Allowance for impairment of accounts to be provided. 
Ongoing reconciliation and analysis of the unidentified accounts.
observations.reason:
Accountable officers are already disconnected with LGU due to resignation, retirement and/or end of term.

Remaining balance per unliquidated cash advances report as of CY 2024 1st Quarter is P48,016.52 for the Due to Officers and Employees Account. 

No summary report of unreconciled balances was submitted by the Accounting Office as of 12/31/23 for the following accounts:
Due from NGAs
Due from GOCCs
Due from LGUs
Receivables – Disallowances/Charges
Other Receivables

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to require the Local Chief Executive to create an Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of all local roads and prepare Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System.

Direct the GSO to maintain a Local Road Inventory, Road Map, Local Road Network Property Card, and prepare Report on Local Road Network for all roads and its components annually. Then, instruct the Municipal Engineer to render complete description and cost segregation of all road components, serving as basis for the Municipal Accountant to prepare Journal Voucher to record the costing of local road network in the general ledger and in the Local Road Network Ledger Card.
observations.managementAction:
Inventory Committee reconstituted through Executive Order.

Physical Inventory of all LRNs to be conducted.

Final reports for submission to COA.
observations.reason: Inventory Committee was reconstituted but not functional. Management action needs the coordination with MPDO and Engineering Office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

a.	require the consultants / to pay to the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) the amount of deficiency withholding tax and then submit proofs of payment to the auditor; otherwise, it shall be disallowed in audit; and

b.	to comply with the withholding tax rate of ten percent (10%) for management and technical consultants; otherwise, penalties and interest shall be personally liable to those responsible for withholding and remittance as stated in Section 247(b) of the 1997 NIRC.
observations.managementAction:
The contractor failed to pay the tax due to the BIR. 

The Municipal Accountant averred that he will withhold the final payment for their existing contract if the contractor will not pay the amount of tax including penalties and other charges.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous audit recommendation that:

the Accounting Office comply with the provision Section 114 of P.D. No 1445 and Section 4 (e) of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I, so that all transactions would be properly documented and recorded. With this, all transactions can be readily verified and reconciled with the amount appearing in the financial statements. Thus, the accuracy and reliability of the financial statements will be achieved; and

the Management consider hiring more personnel to augment the staff requirements of the Accounting Office.
observations.managementAction: Management averred that they are waiting for the Organizational Structure of the LGU to be approved to hire additional personnel to do the tasks
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 24, page 65, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management through:

a.	the Bids and Awards Committee to attach all the documentary requirements as provided by COA Circulars 2012-001 and 2009-001 in the Disbursement Vouchers;

b.	the BAC secretariat to submit copies of the bidding documents of all perfected contracts for FY 2021; and

c.	the BAC and the Municipal Accountant, ensure that all perfected contracts and their respective supporting documents including those required in auditorial/technical review be submitted to the Audit Team within five (5) days from the execution thereof to facilitate immediate review and evaluation as required by COA Circulars No. 96-010 and 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: No management action.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 35 and 36, pages 87 and 90, respectively, Part II of this AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive advise the Officer in Charge of Quezon Water System to furnish the Municipal Accountant a copy of the Monthly Billing, Reading, and Payment Monitoring Report and to advise the Municipal Accountant to strictly follow the accrual basis of accounting in accordance with IPSAS 1.

In view of the foregoing, we reiterated our recommendation that the Management advise the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher on the transfer of the unexpended balance of QRF and DRRMF-MOOE amounting to ₱996,169.94 and submit immediately to the Office of the Auditor a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher.

In view of the foregoing, we reiterated our recommendation that the Management advise the Municipal General Services Officer to submit the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) for the result of inventory of supplies to the Auditor concerned not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive advise the Municipal Accountant to explain why he did not record the tax revenue from fines and penalties amounting to ₱885,818.43 and why he did not reconcile with the Municipal Treasurer to ascertain the amount collected as Tax Revenue. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw the necessary adjusting entries to record the correct balances of the Tax Revenue, Real Property Tax Receivable and Deferred Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax Receivable and Deferred Special Education Tax.

In view of the foregoing, we reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant and GSO to prepare and maintain the PPELC and PC, respectively, and regularly reconcile the balances of the PPE.

We recommended that the Management justify/explain the incurrence of cash overdraft. We further recommended to the Management to require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly monitor the balance of cash in bank prior to disbursing of funds so as not to incur cash overdraft.

We recommended that the Management advise the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) that in case of two failed biddings, the BAC shall invite at least three (3) suppliers or contractors, including those disqualified in previous biddings for the project, for negotiations to ensure effective competition. Likewise, we recommended management to immediately submit to the Office of the Auditor the minutes of the meeting on the discussion and negotiation with Bulanit General Construction and revision made on the terms, conditions, specifications, and cost estimates of the project, if any.

We recommended that the Management ensure that all development projects are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready in order to complete them within the target completion date to maximize the benefit for its intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Management thru the Local Chief Executive the following courses of action:
Require the Municipal Development Council to specify the details of the identified priority projects to be charged under the 20% Development Fund including project location, specific implementation schedule, and physical target to avoid generic or lumpsum projects.
Stop the practice of implementing generic or lumpsum projects without further authority from the Sanggunian to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the Management submit immediately a distribution list duly acknowledged by the recipients of cement and the original copy of delivery receipts to support the submitted distribution list of the procured cement for CYs 2021 and 2022. We further recommended the Management to explain or justify why there were still undelivered cement despite the full payments made by LGU Quezon to CAPCON Builders.

We recommended that the Management impose liquidated damages to CAPCON Builders due to non-delivery of the procured cement within the allowed period of delivery schedule and the remaining undelivered cement totaling 9,691 bags. We further recommended the Management to exercise due diligence in the planning and implementation of projects, programs and activities in order not to compromise the delivery of services to its constituents.
We recommended that the Management fully implement/utilize the planned programs, activities and projects under the DRRM Fund so that the intended benefits would be fully achieved.

We recommended that the Management advise the Municipal Treasurer to enforce a more vigorous and effective tax campaign, employ a realistic and effective collection strategies, and institute legal remedies pursuant to pertinent provisions of RA 7160 in order to persuade the Taxpayers to pay their tax on time and to attain one hundred per cent 100% collection efficiency so that more programs, projects and activities can be implemented for the benefit of the Municipality’s constituents.

We recommended that the Management advise the Municipal Accountant to liquidate immediately the amount received from Bureau of Animal Industry and submit the liquidation report to the source agency and to the Office of the Auditor. Likewise, return/refund the unutilized balances of the fund transfers to the Provincial Government of Isabela and Department of the Interior and Local Government amounting to ₱85,615.72 and ₱15,297.50, respectively.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Management advise the Municipal Accountant to withhold taxes on payment of gasoline to Golden Oil Exchange in compliance with Revenue Regulation No. 02-98 to avoid the possible imposition of sanction set by the National Internal Revenue Code.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer to stop the practice of accommodating private checks of employees and private individuals using her collections.

We recommended that at the close of each business day, the collectors/tellers shall accomplish the Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD) in four copies. The original and two copies, together with the duplicate of the official receipts issued, shall be submitted to the treasurer/cashier to whom the cash collected shall be turned over. We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit intact all her collections as well as all collections turned over to her by the collectors/tellers with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day. She shall record all deposits made in the cashbook and prepare the RCD as required under Section 69 of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445, Section 8 of COA Circular No. 92-382, Local Treasury Operations Manual (LTOM) 2nd Edition and Cash Examination Manual. Likewise, proper handling of collections and its deposits be strictly observed and deposits should be attributed/traced from the collected amounts.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management through the Municipal Accountant submit the necessary reports on timely basis and strictly comply with the provisions in COA Circular No 2009-006 and PD No. 1445 in order to avoid imposition of the sanctions provided therein.

We recommended the Accountable Officer to:
submit a written explanation for the incurrence of overage. Otherwise, the amount shall be forfeited in favor of the government pursuant; and
properly monitor her accountabilities and observe proper safekeeping of collections by separating personal monies from the municipal funds and comply with the provision of Section 101(1) of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that management continue the practice of depositing collections regularly to comply with the rules and regulations as mandated by Section 69 of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 32 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I on collections and deposits.

We recommended that the Accountable Officer submit the monthly Report on Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) to the COA office at the end of each month in compliance with the above-cited regulation.

We recommended the Accountable Officer to:
Immediately restitute the cash shortage;
Stop the unsound practices of handling cash; and
Comply with the aforementioned provisions of P.D. No. 1445.

We recommended the management require the Accounting and Treasury Offices to conduct monthly reconciliation of cashbook and accounting records to ensure timely discovery of errors and to facilitate early preparation of applicable adjustments.

We reiterated our previous audit recommendation that the Municipal Accountant thoroughly review the transactions which resulted in the negative balances of accounts and accordingly prepare adjustments to establish the accuracy and valuation thereof.

We recommended that management ensure that trust funds are used exclusively to the purpose/s for which they were granted following the provisions of Section 4(3) of P.D. No. 1445 and Sections 305(e) and 309(b) of R.A. No. 7160.

that after the complete physical inventory of municipal properties and the preparation of RPCPPE, the same must be reconciled with the subsidiary ledger cards and property cards. Thereafter, the reconciled report must be submitted to the audit team for evaluation; and
the Municipal Accountant and Municipal General Services Officer to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledger cards and property cards, respectively.
We recommended management to submit the lacking documentary requirements. Otherwise, we will be constrained to issue a Notice of Suspension on the above-stated transaction.

We recommended that:
the Management create a committee/team to:
a.1 facilitate the titling of 4 parcels of land in the name of the Municipality and thereafter provide the Accounting Office with copies thereof for recording in the books of accounts; and
a.2	determine the mode of acquisition of the 5 unrecorded lots of the Municipality. In case of donation, the committee/team should determine the fair market value thereof which will be recorded in the books of accounts; and
the Municipal Accountant record the parcels of land owned by the Municipality following the provisions of IPSAS 17 and to provide detailed schedule for verification, monitoring and reconciliation purposes.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
update and provide depreciation to the affected PPE accounts in order to present their reasonable book values in the financial statements in conformity with the provisions of IPSAS 17; and
provide Subsidiary Ledgers of PPEs which includes relevant information such as date of purchase or availability for use, salvage values, useful life, etc. to facilitate the computation and verification of depreciation expenses.

We recommended that Management:
expedite the implementation of all on – going programmed and funded projects under the 20% DF to optimize utilization thereof and to ensure timely delivery of intended benefits for the constituents of the municipality;
continuously ensure that the development projects that may be included under the 20% DF shall be those that are necessary, appropriate, or incidental to efficient and effective local governance, and those which are essential to the promotion of the general welfare of the people; and
ensure that the development projects to be funded out of the 20% DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready.

We recommended that management:
conduct regular analysis on the causes of delay in the implementation of projects and to formulate specific, achievable and time-bound measures to ensure timely implementation of the projects; and
continuously monitor and ensure that the transferred funds are used only for the purpose/s for which they were granted.

We recommended that management cause the immediate return of all unused balances to source agencies or request authority for the continued use of funds for other identified projects of the municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
locate available records/documents to determine the source agency and corresponding amount of fund transfers from NGAs of the unknown balance;
maintain Subsidiary Ledgers for the Due to NGAs Account; and
comply with the provision of Section 2 and 111 of P.D. No. 1445.

We recommended that Management:
continuously ensure the allocation and utilization of LDRRMF pursuant to the policies and guidelines primarily embodied in NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1;
ensure efficient and effective implementation of all programmed projects identified during the budget year to optimize the utilization of the fund and to assure that the goals and objectives of reducing disaster risk and enhancing disaster preparedness and response capabilities be achieved; and
continuously comply with pertinent laws, rules and regulations in the recording, utilization and reporting of LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
prepare the necessary adjusting entry to the affected accounts to ensure fair presentation of financial statements;
transfer unexpended balance of DRRMF other than those appropriated for capital outlays to the Trust Fund and recognized as Trust Liabilities – DRRMF; and
abide with the guidelines set forth under Items 5.1.10 and 5.1.11 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
prepare necessary adjusting entry to the affected accounts to ensure fair presentation of financial statements;
cause the transfer of unexpended balance of DRRMF other than those appropriated for capital outlays to the Trust Fund, and record the same as Trust Liabilities – DRRMF; and
abide with the guidelines set forth under Items 5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We reiterated our previous audit recommendation that:
the Accounting Office comply with the provision Section 114 of P.D. No 1445 and Section 4 (e) of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I, so that all transactions would be properly documented and recorded. With this, all transactions can be readily verified and reconciled with the amount appearing in the financial statements. Thus, the accuracy and reliability of the financial statements will be achieved; and
the Management consider hiring more personnel to augment the staff requirements of the Accounting Office.

We reiterated our previous audit recommendation that:
the Municipal Accountant conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of the receivables and determine the concerned debtors to ascertain their collectability. If proven to be no longer collectible, request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts and attach required documents prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005; and
in the future, for transactions involving receivables, the Municipal Accountant should maintain subsidiary ledgers in accordance with Section 4 (e) of the NGAS to ensure complete information on the accounts and to facilitate the collection thereof.

We reiterated our previous audit recommendation that the Municipal Accountant:
analyze the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts, trace back differences and the nature of the accounts;
prepare the necessary adjusting entries to bring the correct balances of the accounts; and
maintain subsidiary ledger as necessary and include disclosure in the Notes to FS for fair presentation of Financial Statements

We recommended and reiterated that the members of the GAD Focal Point System use monitoring tools such as the HGDG and PIMME in the GAD planning and budgeting as well as in the preparation of the accomplishment report.

We recommended that management further strengthen its internal control by instructing all concerned to properly accomplish all accounting and procurement forms by strictly following the instructions in the MNGAS for LGUs and procurement laws and regulations to ensure propriety and propriety regularity of its transactions.

We recommend that Management direct the BAC/BAC Secretariat to prepare and submit the PMR and APCPI Results to the GPPB and comply with the posting requirements as required in R.A. No. 9184 and GPPB Circular No. 02-2020. Thereafter, please furnish the Office of the Auditor of a copy of each report.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to order the responsible personnel to immediately submit the lacking documents. Otherwise, we will be constrained to issue a Notice of Suspension pursuant to COA rules and regulations. To ensure that all lacking documents are submitted, we urge the responsible personnel to accomplish the attached checklist of documentary requirements and to put index tabs/tabbings on the documents to be submitted.
Henceforth, we recommended Management thru the Bids and Awards Committee and the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all supporting contract documents are complete and submitted on time in accordance with pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR, Section 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 and COA Circular No. 2012-001

We recommended that within a period not exceeding ten (10) calendar days from the receipt of Notice of Award by the bidder with the LCRB, HRRB, SCRB, or SRRB, the HoPE or his duly authorized representative shall enter into a contract agreement with the winning bidder to ensure the early implementation of projects.

We recommend the Accounting Office to require the submission of the lacking documentary requirements and forward the same to the audit team for evaluation. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant should strictly comply with his/her function under Section 474(b)(5) of R.A. No. 7160 to ensure the completeness of supporting documents before payment to contractors/suppliers.
To ensure that all lacking documents are submitted, we recommend that the Accounting Office accomplish the attached updated checklist of documentary requirements and attach in the disbursement vouchers.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC/BAC Secretariat to immediately submit the documents lacking. Otherwise, we will be constrained to issue a Notice of Suspension pursuant to COA rules and regulations.
To ensure that all lacking documents are submitted, we urged the responsible personnel to accomplish the documentary requirements checklist and put index tabs/tabbings on the documents to be submitted.
Henceforth, we recommended Management, thru the Bids and Awards Committee, to ensure that all supporting contract documents are complete in compliance with pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR, and submitted within five (5) days from execution of contracts in accordance with Section 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC/BAC Secretariat to immediately submit the documents lacking. Otherwise, we will be constrained to issue a Notice of Suspension pursuant to COA rules and regulations.
To ensure that all lacking documents are submitted, we urged the responsible personnel to accomplish the documentary requirements checklist and put index tabs/tabbings on the documents to be submitted.
Henceforth, we recommended Management, thru the Bids and Awards Committee, to ensure that all supporting contract documents are complete in compliance with pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR, and submitted within five (5) days from execution of contracts in accordance with Section 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that Management file a motion for reconsideration to the Sangguniang Panlalawigan to justify the Supplemental Budget for the grant of the CNA Incentives, otherwise the paid benefits will be disallowed in audit due to defect in the legality and validity of the Appropriation Ordinance No. 14, series of 2022.

We recommend that Management revisit the grant of financial assistance to other LGUs especially to those belonging to higher income classification considering the financial capacity of the LGU.  If warranted, Management may also make proper representation with the other LGUs in the Province for the evaluation of the practice on exchange of financial assistance during the celebration of annual town fiesta, and if warranted, discontinue the same.

We recommended the following –
The Municipal Accountant’s Office make the necessary adjustments on the affected accounts and record properties funded out of the financial assistance only when the documents evidencing transfer of ownership could be obtained from the source agency.
The Management to –
Prepare the donation documents and submit the same to the Provincial Monitoring Office so that the donation will be facilitated;
Once the Deed of Donation was obtained, forward it to the Municipal Accountants Office for the recording of the assets in the General Fund of the LGU.

We recommended to the Management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Record subsequent receipts of the ER No. 1-94 funds in the Share from National Wealth account; and
Record earned interests of the ER No. 1-94 funds as Interest Income.

We recommended to the management to require the BAC Secretariat to prepare the APP using the format prescribed by GPPB Circular No. 07-2015, herein attached as Appendix E. A softcopy of the prescribed APP format in excel file was also sent to the BAC Secretariat through electronic mail on February 20, 2023.
We recommended to the management to require the BAC to follow strictly follow the guidelines on alternative methods of procurement under Rule XVI of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended to the management to do the following:
Require the Municipal Budget Office (MBO) to submit the legal basis for continuing the unexpended priors’ unexpended 20% LDF-MOOE. Otherwise, if there is no legal basis, revert the same to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund; and
Require the MBO to refrain from continuing any appropriations for MOOE.

We recommended to the management to do the following:
Provide appropriations for the MOOE of the MDRRMO from the General Fund of the LGU; and
Ensure that the LDRRMF is only allocated PAPs which are in line with the four thematic areas of the fund.

We recommended to the management to ensure that before withdrawal of fuel or delivery thereof is made, the required procurement process was undertaken and the Purchase Order as well as the Notice to Proceed are already executed and received by the supplier.

observations.managementAction: The Management already implemented our audit recommendations except for maintaining the Property Ledger Cards and Stock Cards.
observations.reason: The Management was not able to implement the maintenance of Property Ledger Cards and Stock Cards, hence reiterated this year’s Annual Audit Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management advise the Municipal Accountant report immediately the unreconciled variance amounting to ₱751,361.69 between the reported Cash in Bank, LCCA (SEF) amounting to ₱2,882,545.91 in the SEF Books and the adjusted Cash in Bank, LCCA (SEF) amounting to ₱2,131,184.22 per Bank Reconciliation Statement and submit to the Auditor the adjusting entry made, if any, to adjust the amount reported as at year-end.
observations.managementAction: The Management had already reconciled the differences noted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct:

The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer reconcile the difference of the eleven (11) outstanding checks between the amount recorded in the books and the amount as per Report of Checks Issued; 

The Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries after reconciling the amount and submit a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to the Office of the Auditor; and

The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant immediately verify the Special Savings Account and recognize the same on the books of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Management had already reconciled the differences in the amount of outstanding checks per RCI and per books and it was due to error in inputting amount of checks in the RCI which was gross of tax, hence, already adjusted in the RCI. The special savings account was already closed and the balance was transferred to the general fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management of LGU Quezon advise:

The Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Municipal Assessor prepare and furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of taxpayers showing the names and the amounts due and collectible based on updated Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR)/ Taxpayer’s Index Card to provide an accurate and reliable basis in setting up the Receivables from RPT and SET;

The Municipal Accountant draw a Journal Entry Voucher to record the RPT/SET Receivables established at the beginning of the year based on the submitted duly certified list of taxpayers with the amount due and collectible for the year and maintain the corresponding subsidiary ledgers (SL) to monitor tax collections and delinquencies; and to use the correct account title in setting up the RPT/SET Receivables for CY 2022; and

The Municipal Assessor undertake thorough review on the assessments of real properties within the Municipality for taxation so that the Accounting Department could take up a more realistic RPT/SET Receivables in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Management already implemented our audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

advise the Municipal Accountant stop the practice of recording immediately the procured supplies and materials to expense account; instead he should record all purchases  of supplies and materials regardless of whether or not they are consumed within the accounting period as an Inventory, except those purchased through petty cash fund, following the perpetual inventory method and an inventory control account should be maintained for each inventory item pursuant to Section 114 of the Manual on NGAS, Volume I; likewise, he shall maintain Supplies Ledger Cards per stock number; Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards for each category of assets; and Real Property Ledger Cards for land;

advise the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) number each type of supplies and maintain Stock Cards per stock number; he shall consolidate weekly the RIS for which supplies and materials were issued using the SSMI. The SSMI together with the original copy of the RIS shall be submitted to the Municipal Accountant, who shall compute the cost of supplies issued and ending inventory using the moving average method as provided under Section 115 of the same Manual and prepare the JEV to record the expenditures using the appropriate expense accounts; and

require the MGSO submit the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) and Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) for the result of inventory of supplies or property and physical count of PPE, respectively, to the Auditor concerned not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year.
observations.managementAction: The Management already adopted the perpetual inventory method. However, they were not been able to conduct physical count of inventories in CY 2022.
observations.reason: The Management has not been able to conduct physical count of inventory, hence, reiterated to this year’s Annual Audit Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management advise:

The Municipal Accountant (1) draw a Journal Entry Voucher on the transfer of the unexpended balance of QRF and DRRMF-MOOE; (2) to disclose in the CY 2021 and succeeding years Notes to Financial Statements the details on the receipt and utilization of LDRRMF of the Municipality using the format as required under Item 5.1.16 of COA Circular 2012-002; and (3) to maintain a separate RAAO for QRF, MOOE and CO of LDRRMF; and

The LDRRMO use the required format on the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF as required under Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002 and submit the report on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and the Local Development Council to the COA auditor of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: The Management had already drawn Journal Entry Voucher on the unexpended DRRMF for year 2021 and disclosed the details of receipt and utilization of LDRRMF. The LDRRMO had already used the required template on the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF.
observations.reason: The Management failed to transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance of Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (DRRMF)- Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) and Quick Response Fund (QRF) as at December 31, 2022, amounting to ₱3,514,669.94 to the Special Trust Fund under the account Trust Liability-DRRM in the Trust Fund books. Hence, reiterated to this year’s Annual Audit Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant maintain a special account in the general fund for the operation of the Local Economic Enterprises which can be used to formulate Management decisions whether to continue the operations of their respective LEEs.
observations.managementAction: The Management commented that there was no Sangguniang Bayan Resolution/Ordinance declaring the Water System, Public Market and Slaughterhouse as Local Economic Enterprises (LEEs). Also, they will review the operations of the said revenue sources if they need to be declared LEEs of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management justify/explain the incurrence of cash overdraft on the Trust Fund of the Municipality. Likewise, we recommend the prompt remittance of the unutilized trust funds due to other government agencies to avoid misappropriation thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Management had already reconciled the cash overdraft and it was due to unrecorded intra-agency receivable.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

Require collectors turn over/remit all collections daily and intact to the Municipal Treasurer in compliance with Section 69 of P.D. 1445 and Item A of Section 60 of LTOM;

Require the Municipal Treasurer to closely supervise the collecting officers and monitor daily collections and remittances; and

Strengthen internal control over collections and deposits. To avoid holding big amount of money in the hands of Collectors, consider the following:

Promulgate and implement a policy on the turn-over of daily collections by the collectors to the Municipal Treasurer. A cut-off may be established on the time of turn-over that will give the Municipal Treasurer ample time to deposit the turned-over collections;

Likewise, established a cut-off in depositing daily collections to the bank by the Municipal Treasurer. This is to avoid having large balances of daily collections in the hands of Municipal Treasurer; and

Promulgate and implement a policy that will penalize holding of collections for several days in the hands of either the collectors or the Municipal Treasurer.
observations.managementAction: The Management had already implemented our audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management justify/explain why the above-mentioned expenditures were charged to the financial assistance from the Office of the President. Likewise, we recommended that Management thoroughly review all Memorandum of Agreement wherein the Municipality is a signatory and ensure that both parties affixed their signature thereon, in order to bind each party in accordance with the provisions in the MOA.
observations.managementAction: The Management had already implemented our audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management adhere with the provisions of COA Circular 2004-006 and COA Circular 2021-001 on the use of RER, otherwise, payments that are supported with RER instead of OR will be suspended/disallowed in audit, if applicable. Likewise, refrain from entering into contract with suppliers that are unregistered to BIR in order to support the campaign of the National Government on tax collection.
observations.managementAction: The Management had already implemented our audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management submit justification/explanation on why there was no separate bank account for SFP and why the Management opted to disburse the amount received from DWSD by distributing cash to DCWs instead of conducting a competitive bidding on the procurement of goods. Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant should observe the proper recording of transactions pertaining to the liquidations of fund transfer from NGAs.
observations.managementAction: The Management had already adhered with the provision of the MOA, likewise, they conducted public bidding on the procurement of goods to be distributed to the beneficiaries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management advise the Municipal Accountant ensure that a copy of an approved POW be attached to the disbursement voucher to support the processing of the release of the funds to the implementing agency to establish monitoring of the proper utilization of the funds. Further, we recommended the Management to refrain from transferring of funds to Barangays if the latter do not have the capacity to hold competitive bidding as required under Section 10 of the Revised IRR of R.A No. 9184. Moreover, advise the Municipal Engineer and the MPDC to coordinate and to establish proper monitoring on the different projects/programs/activities implemented or monitored by the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Management had already implemented our audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management refrain from charging the wages of job order workers to the 20% DF. However, for CY 2022, we recommended to charge the wages of job orders of greening program to the Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) under the General Fund of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The wages of job order workers charged to 20% Development Fund were attributed to labor portion of program under environmental greening and it was supported by a program of work.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

Refrain from appropriating funds to unrelated projects and activities of disaster risk reduction and management and adhere to the provisions of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013;

Revise the LDRRMP and AIP for CY 2022 to reprogram the above mentioned PPAs to disaster preparedness related projects, programs and activities and submit to the Auditor’s Office the copy of the revised LDRRMP and AIP for CY 2022; and

Explain/ justify why the above mentioned PPAs were funded under the LDRRMF to forestall the issuance of Notice of Disallowance on the amount disbursed for CY 2021.
observations.managementAction: The Management had already implemented our audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the Accounting Department to ensure that taxes are withheld and deducted from payment of honoraria of BAC members, TWG and BAC Secretariat in accordance with the Revised Withholding Tax Table and the same amount are remitted to the BIR within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: The Management had already implemented our audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Management through the Municipal Accountant submit the necessary reports on timely basis and strictly comply with the provisions in COA Circular No 2009-006 and PD 1445 in order to avoid imposition of the sanctions provided therein.
observations.managementAction: The Management failed to submit some documents within the prescribe period.
observations.reason: There are some documents that were not submitted on time, hence, reiterated to this year’s Annual Audit Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management advise the Municipal Accountant strictly deduct the loan amortization due from each employee based on GSIS billings. Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant explain/justify this basis in recording the account Due to GSIS and why the amount of ₱340,982.45 was not reflected in the Due to GSIS account as at December 31, 2021 despite the fact that billing statement from GSIS were received monthly by the Office of the Municipal Accountant.
observations.managementAction: The Management stopped the practice of accommodating loans of employees who have a net income below the threshold.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Management prioritize the production of the Electrical Plan required by ISELCO for the immediate installation of the additional transformer necessary for the operation of said X-ray machine. We further recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer adopt proper planning including all the necessary measures before any procurement to ensure that anything procured would be immediately utilized.
observations.managementAction: The X-ray machine was already in operation.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that Management establish specific guidelines in the grant of financial assistance for uniformity and fairness. Likewise, the members of the Sangguniang Bayan should stop the practice of giving financial assistance to its constituents regardless of source of fund as it is not among their mandated powers, duties and functions under Section 447 of RA 7160 as well as to avoid possible misuse of government funds.
observations.managementAction: The Management had already implemented our audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to Management that the Petty Cash Fund be maintained under the imprest system in compliance with Section 48 of NGAS, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Management adhered to the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer provide the necessary information in the Cash Disbursement Books and Report of Disbursements as prescribed by the Manual on the New Government Accounting System, Volume II.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer has provided the essential information in the cashbook and adhered to the recommendations

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management continue the practice of depositing collections regularly to comply with the rules and regulations as mandated by Section 69 of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 32 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I on collections and deposits.
observations.managementAction: Management adhered to the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accountable Officer accomplish properly and completely the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms to ensure accuracy thereof and to promote accountability.
observations.managementAction: RAAF was maintained and submitted to the Auditor at the end of the month.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous audit recommendation that the Municipal Accountant thoroughly review the transactions which resulted in the negative balances of accounts and accordingly prepare adjustments to establish the accuracy and valuation thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office is continuously reviewing the transactions which resulted to the negative balances of accounts
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 6, page 31, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to Management the following:

that after the complete physical inventory of municipal properties and the preparation of RPCPPE, the same must be reconciled with the subsidiary ledger cards and property cards. Thereafter, the reconciled report must be submitted to the audit team for evaluation; and

the Municipal Accountant and Municipal General Services Officer to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledger cards and property cards, respectively.
observations.managementAction: Management has conducted physical count of PPEs but reconciliation of amounts with the accounting unit is ongoing.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 8, page 34, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

update and provide depreciation to the affected PPE accounts in order to present their reasonable book values in the financial statements in conformity with the provisions of IPSAS 17; 

provide Subsidiary Ledgers of PPEs which includes relevant information such as date of purchase or availability for use, salvage values, useful life, etc. to facilitate the computation and verification of depreciation expenses; and

abide with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 and follow the procedures in the derecognition of non-existing PPEs without available record of accountability.
observations.managementAction: Management has updated their records and provided the depreciation on the affected PPE  accounts but management failed to provide SL of PPEs.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 11, page 39, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated to Management our previous audit recommendation that:

a.	the Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer with the complete description and segregation of road components for those with data on construction cost. For those not posted in the registry but without identified cost and those included in the RPCRNS but not in the registry, the fair value must be used in determining the cost of road components and thereafter shall provide the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects;

b.	the Municipal Accountant depreciate the carrying amount of existing roads and its components to reflect the asset in its used condition and accordingly adjust the amount recorded in the Road Network account supported by the RPCRNS, Registry Infrastructure for Roads and working paper on the determination of the depreciated replacement cost per registry. Subsequently, at the end of each year, a lapsing schedule for the computation of depreciation for each depreciable road component must be prepared as basis for recording depreciation expenses and the required Local Road Network Ledger Card for each road network shall likewise be maintained; and

c.	the MGSO at the end or every accounting period to render a Report on Local Road Network using the pro-forma schedule in Annex A of same Circular and maintain local road inventory, Road Map and complete Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office, GSO and MPDC are in the process of gathering information to update their records to comply with the audit recommendations
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 12, page 41, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

expedite the implementation of all on – going programmed and funded projects under the 20% DF to optimize utilization thereof and to ensure timely delivery of intended benefits for the constituents of the municipality;

continuously monitor, coordinate, and evaluate PPAs to ensure the completion of most urgent, needed, appropriate and eligible development projects; and

continuously observe the policies and guidelines in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% DF as enjoined by DBM-DOF-DILG-JMC No. 1, S. 2020
observations.managementAction: Management has substantially implemented the PPAs.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 14, page 45, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

a.	ensure the allocation and utilization of LDRRMF pursuant to the policies and guidelines primarily embodied in NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1; and

b.	thru the LDRRMC, continuously monitor, coordinate and evaluate PPAs to ensure the completion of most urgent, needed, appropriate and eligible projects.
observations.managementAction: Management has complied with the recommendations

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

prepare the necessary adjusting entry to the affected accounts to ensure fair presentation of financial statements;

transfer unexpended balance of DRRMF other than those appropriated for capital outlays to the Trust Fund and recognized as Trust Liabilities – DRRMF; and

abide with the guidelines set forth under Items 5.1.10 and 5.1.11 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Management has partially adjusted the amount of CO transferred to STF but still, portion of the amount remained in the Trust Liability-DRRMF account
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 19, page 55, Part II of this AAR.

observations.managementAction: Discrepancies in the record still exists and management averred that they are waiting for the Organizational Structure of the LGU to be approved to hire additional personnel to do the tasks
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 20, page 57, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

prepare the necessary adjusting entry to the affected accounts to ensure fair presentation of financial statements; and

abide with the guidelines set forth under Item 5.1.11 and Annex C(A)(6) of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Management has complied with the audit recommendations
Management has recorded the inventories as recommended. However, Stock Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards were not maintained.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 22, page 61, Part II of this AAR.

observations.managementAction: Management informed the audit team that they have conducted validation and inventory of work/zoo animals but they still failed to reconcile the balances and inventory report.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 23, page 63, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous audit recommendation that:

the Municipal Accountant conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of the receivables and determine the concerned debtors to ascertain their collectability. If proven to be no longer collectible, request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts and attach required documents prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005; and

in the future, for transactions involving receivables, the Municipal Accountant should maintain subsidiary ledgers in accordance with Section 4 (e) of the NGAS to ensure complete information on the accounts and to facilitate the collection thereof.
observations.managementAction:
Management failed to prepare subsidiary ledgers. 

Nonetheless, management stated that they are preparing the documentary requirements to support their request for write-off.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 26, page 69, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous audit recommendation that the Municipal Accountant:

analyze the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts, trace back differences and the nature of the accounts;

prepare the necessary adjusting entries to bring the correct balances of the accounts; and

maintain subsidiary ledger as necessary and include disclosure in the Notes to FS for fair presentation of Financial Statements
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office is continuously reconciling the transactions which resulted to the discrepancy in the reciprocal accounts.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 27, page 71, Part II of this AAR.

observations.managementAction: Management informed the audit team that the concerned personnel attended the training on GAD and committed to use of monitoring tool such as HGDG for the Year 2024 GAD budget.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 28, page 73, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned Municipal Officials/SB members return the excessive and irregularly disbursed funds, otherwise, a Notice of Disallowance shall be issued. Further, management should observe the guidelines set by DBM LBC No. 103 in computing the amount of RATA to be paid to prevent irregular disbursements of government funds.
observations.managementAction: Management has complied with the audit recommendation on the payment of RATA.

observations.recommendation: We strongly recommended that the LGU stop the practice of paying RATA in advance to conform to the provisions of Section 88 of P.D. 1445 and DBM LBC No. 103.
observations.managementAction: Management has complied with the audit recommendation on the payment of RATA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Reconciliation Committee, composed with the accounting and property unit personnel, to continue the reconciliation of reports per inventory count, per property and per accounting records by taking into consideration the clear-cut guidelines provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, in order to analyze and review the existence, validity and accuracy of PPE items which comprised the bulk of unreconciled beginning balances. Effect necessary adjusting entries after reconciliation, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee is still in the process of reconciling the beginning balances.
observations.reason:
Volume and complexity of unidentified items to be reconciled;
Lack of personnel whose assignment is solely to implement COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 or the One-Time Cleansing of PPEs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to reassess the programmed projects whether to be charged to MOOE or Capital Outlay by taking into consideration the criteria laid out in IPSAS No. 17 and Section 4.2 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 on the determination whether to capitalize the cost or to expense outright. 
Moreover, directed the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to recognize the PPE accounts and the depreciation expense, if any, to fairly present the affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.reason: Adjustment was made by the Municipal Accountant’s Office was for one project only.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to require the Municipal Accountant to make adjusting entries to record the transferred projects pending compliance with the above-mentioned lacking documents and to ensure that all transferred assets are duly approved in accordance with Section 381 of Republic Act 7160, in order to present fairly the financial statements of the LGU. Henceforth, the Municipal Accounting Office to properly record capitalizable projects undertaken by LGU Quezon as assets and not as direct expense. For a more convenient and expeditious manner, the management may resort implementing the proposed projects thru financial assistance to be implemented by the component barangays and subject for subsequent liquidation.
observations.managementAction: Adjustment was made by the Municipal Accountant’s Office.
Effective monitoring and evaluation control on the status of implementation of infrastructure projects have been implemented to at least prevent negative slippage.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to ensure that disbursement vouchers for infrastructure-related disbursements in favor to contractors should be sufficiently and adequately attached with the required documents as prescribed under Section 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001. 
Specifically, proper coordination among offices involved in the processing of these claims should be made, especially the Municipal Engineering Office, BAC and the Municipal Accounting Office. The latter office should meticulously review and ensure that these pertinent documents are completely attached to DVs prior to disbursement. 

Moreover, submit immediately the lacking supporting documents for our further review and evaluation.
observations.managementAction: The lacking documents have been submitted to the audit team.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to be judicious in appropriating funds for priority projects to be undertaken by the municipality by ensuring that the intended projects are described with specificity and not in “generic” terms. In case that the projects are described in lumpsum and in “generic” terms, legislative approval should be sought prior to the incurrence of expense or entering into contract by the Local Chief Executive on behalf of the local government unit, in compliance with the framework laid out in the existing Supreme Court jurisprudence and applicable laws, rules and regulations. Attach the document evidencing approval of local sanggunian to the disbursement voucher in conformity with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended to the management to submit immediately to the audit team the lacking relevant authorization and identification documents for our further review and evaluation. Henceforth, the management to discourage the release of claims to persons other than the payees without requiring the said necessary documents of concerned individuals. 
More specifically, authorization documents must be meticulously reviewed by the concerned disbursing officers as to completeness of the required information such as the name of the authorized claimant, the amount to be claimed, the covering particulars, and the signature of the rightful payee, supported with identification documents of both parties. Also, make sure that the authorized person to claim should reflect his/her name and signature in the payroll, including the date of receipt.
observations.managementAction: The LGU already requires the Authorization and identification cards of the payee and the claimant.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to stop the practice of charging overtime services against MOOE appropriations. Instead, the Municipal Budget Officer to consider allocating/appropriating a considerable amount not exceeding 5% of Personnel Services budget, strictly adhering with CSC-DBM JC No. 02, s. 2015 dated November 25, 2015. 
	Subject to the discretion of the Local Chief Executive, the management may also resort to Compensatory Time-Off (CTO) as another form of remuneration for overtime services in accordance with CSC-DBM JC Nos. 2, s. 2004 and 2-A, s. 2005.
Moreover, the Municipal Accountant to ensure that payments for overtime services for regular and casual employees are properly recorded to the correct account title as prescribed by the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: Overtime pay of regular and casual employees were charged to Personnel Services already.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to the management to submit immediately to the audit team the identified lacking supporting documents, in order to substantiate the disbursements made. Moreover, the concerned personnel both from the Municipal Accounting Office and Human Resource Management Unit to revisit the complete documentary requirements prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and to make an initiative to revise the checklists of the required documents for pre-audit purposes as an aid in the processing of claims for each type of personnel claim.

We recommended to the management to stop the practice of utilizing the current year’s appropriations to pay prior year’s unrecorded obligations. Moreover, the Municipal Accounting Office to implement that all unpaid obligations shall be recorded as payables by drawing Journal Entry Voucher (JEV), along with the corresponding complete supporting documents. Other unpaid obligations but not recorded in the books of accounts as payables at year-end will not be considered next year for disbursement pursuant to the above-mentioned provisions of PD 1445 and RA 7160.

Further, submit immediately to the audit team the identified lacking supporting documents for our review and evaluation, in order to substantiate the disbursements made.

We recommended to the management to require the Municipal Accounting Office, in coordination with the BAC and Procurement Office, to submit the noted lacking supporting documents to the audit team for post-audit and further evaluation. Likewise, submit justification or resubmit documents that would at least explain/suffice the other deficiencies noted.
Moreover, implement measures to strictly monitor the deliveries of procured items vis-à-vis the agreed contract duration. The BAC and the Procurement Office to ensure that the awarded supplier faithfully complies with the terms and conditions of the contract and that it affix its signature on the pertinent Purchase Order, indicating the date of receipt/acceptance by the latter since the count of delay is dependent on it.
Further, enforced the collection of these liquidated damages from identified suppliers in default. Furthermore, in case similar situation may arise, the Municipal Accounting Office to be strict in imposing LD by deducting the computed LD amount against the supplier’s claim. Lastly, the same office to meticulously and sufficiently support disbursement vouchers with required pertinent documents prior to payment and submission to the audit team.
observations.managementAction: The lacking supporting documents were submitted to COA.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation: We recommended to direct the Municipal Accountant to reissue demand letters and exhaust all possible remedies to collect the receivables and to demand liquidation of cash advances and fund transfers. Conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of these accounts. Comply with all the conditions and requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 for the possible request for the write off of the specified dormant receivables and cash advances accounts to the Commission on Audit. Provide allowance for impairment thereafter and reconcile the accounts with unidentified/ unreconciled subsidiary ledgers from prior years in order to have a fair presentation of affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Demand Letters have been issued to accountable officers for the liquidation of the accounts. Request for write-off will be made after reconciliation of receivable accounts. Allowance for impairment of accounts to be provided. Ongoing reconciliation and analysis of the unidentified accounts.
observations.reason: Unwillingness of the accountable officers to settle the cash advances and resignation of some of them.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to require the Municipal Accountant to continue the reconciliation of land reported in lump amount and disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements necessary information on these real properties.

Require the Municipal Assessor to constantly follow up the status of the submitted documents (for titling of the real properties of the municipality at the Registry of Deeds and DENR provincial office at Malaybalay City to ensure titling of all the lands owned by the municipality in compliance to Section 47 (C) of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Local Government Code of 1991 and Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.   

Direct the General Services Officer to keep a complete record of all real properties owned by the LGU in compliance to Articles 122 and 440 of the IRR of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction:
The land account has been reconciled. Information about the land account has been disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements since 2018. 

The process of titling of the untitled land owned by the LGU Quezon through Land Administration Program (LAMP) is ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to require the Local Chief Executive to create an Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of all local roads and prepare Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System.

Direct the GSO to maintain a Local Road Inventory, Road Map, Local Road Network Property Card, and prepare Report on Local Road Network for all roads and its components annually. Then, instruct the Municipal Engineer to render complete description and cost segregation of all road components, serving as basis for the Municipal Accountant to prepare Journal Voucher to record the costing of local road network in the general ledger and in the Local Road Network Ledger Card.
observations.managementAction:
Inventory Committee reconstituted through Executive Order. 

Physical Inventory of all LRNs to be conducted.

Final reports for submission to COA.
observations.reason: Accounting all local road network for all roads and its components will require the reconstituted Inventory Committee ample time in order to provide accurate data to the same.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management through the MGSO prepare an updated physical inventory report of all its properties. We also recommended that the MGSO/Property Custodian maintain property records and furnish copy thereof to the Municipal Accountant. Both records must be reconciled regularly.
observations.managementAction: No management actions.
observations.reason:
The Management did not undertake physical count of PPE during CY 2021.

Reiterated under Finding No. 1

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management through the Municipal Accountant submit the necessary reports on timely basis and strictly comply with the provisions in COA Circular No 2009-006 and PD 1445 in order to avoid imposition of the sanctions provided therein.
observations.managementAction: The Management did not submit the monthly financial transaction documents within the prescribed period.
observations.reason:
The Management submits their monthly financial transaction documents 5-75 days delayed.

Reiterated under Finding No. 17

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management through the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers so as to provide historical data for the controlling accounts in the general ledger.
observations.managementAction: No Management action
observations.reason:
The Municipal Accountant did not maintain subsidiary ledgers to support the reported balances of each account.

Reiterated under Finding No. 5

observations.managementAction: Management stated that they are waiting for the Sangguniang Bayan Resolution for the opening of Special Trust Fund Account.
Management averred that they are waiting for the Organizational Structure of the LGU to be approved to hire additional personnel to do the tasks
No management action.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that the Local Chief Executive require the OIC - Municipal Accountant to investigate and make necessary adjustments on the variance of the two reciprocal accounts if needed, and see it to it that all future transactions in the said accounts are properly recorded.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant is still in the process of tracing records of the two reciprocal accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE

 (a) make sure the Annual GPB is at least 5% of the total annual budget appropriations; 

(b) ensure the implementation of GAD programs and activities in accordance with approved GAD plans and budget; and

 (c) instruct the GAD Focal Point Person to use the Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG) in the attribution of costs of regular program/project to GAD.
observations.managementAction: Inadequate budget allocation for GAD PPAs was still noted in CY 2021.

Moreover, directed the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to recognize the PPE accounts and the depreciation expense, if any, to fairly present the affected accounts in the financial statements.

Henceforth, the Municipal Accounting Office to properly record capitalizable projects undertaken by LGU Quezon as assets and not as direct expense. For a more convenient and expeditious manner, the management may resort implementing the proposed projects thru financial assistance to be implemented by the component barangays and subject for subsequent liquidation.

The latter office should meticulously review and ensure that these pertinent documents are completely attached to DVs prior to disbursement.

In case that the projects are described in lumpsum and in “generic” terms, legislative approval should be sought prior to the incurrence of expense or entering into contract by the Local Chief Executive on behalf of the local government unit, in compliance with the framework laid out in the existing Supreme Court jurisprudence and applicable laws, rules and regulations. Attach the document evidencing approval of local sanggunian to the disbursement voucher in conformity with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended to the management to submit immediately to the audit team the lacking relevant authorization and identification documents for our further review and evaluation. Henceforth, the management to discourage the release of claims to persons other than the payees without requiring the said necessary documents of concerned individuals.
More specifically, authorization documents must be meticulously reviewed by the concerned disbursing officers as to completeness of the required information such as the name of the authorized claimant, the amount to be claimed, the covering particulars, and the signature of the rightful payee, supported with identification documents of both parties. Also, make sure that the authorized person to claim should reflect his/her name and signature in the payroll, including the date of receipt.

We recommended to the management to stop the practice of charging overtime services against MOOE appropriations. Instead, the Municipal Budget Officer to consider allocating/appropriating a considerable amount not exceeding 5% of Personnel Services budget, strictly adhering with CSC-DBM JC No. 02, s. 2015 dated November 25, 2015.
Subject to the discretion of the Local Chief Executive, the management may also resort to Compensatory Time-Off (CTO) as another form of remuneration for overtime services in accordance with CSC-DBM JC Nos. 2, s. 2004 and 2-A, s. 2005.
Moreover, the Municipal Accountant to ensure that payments for overtime services for regular and casual employees are properly recorded to the correct account title as prescribed by the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs.

We recommend to the management to submit immediately to the audit team the identified lacking supporting documents, in order to substantiate the disbursements made. Moreover, the concerned personnel both from the Municipal Accounting Office and Human Resource Management Unit to revisit the complete documentary requirements prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and to make an initiative to revise the checklists of the required documents for pre-audit purposes as an aid in the processing of claims for each type of personnel claim.

We recommended to the management to stop the practice of utilizing the current year’s appropriations to pay prior year’s unrecorded obligations. Moreover, the Municipal Accounting Office to implement that all unpaid obligations shall be recorded as payables by drawing Journal Entry Voucher (JEV), along with the corresponding complete supporting documents. Other unpaid obligations but not recorded in the books of accounts as payables at year-end will not be considered next year for disbursement pursuant to the above-mentioned provisions of PD 1445 and RA 7160.
Further, submit immediately to the audit team the identified lacking supporting documents for our review and evaluation, in order to substantiate the disbursements made.

We recommended to the management to require the Municipal Accounting Office, in coordination with the BAC and Procurement Office, to submit the noted lacking supporting documents to the audit team for post-audit and further evaluation. Likewise, submit justification or resubmit documents that would at least explain/suffice the other deficiencies noted.
Moreover, implement measures to strictly monitor the deliveries of procured items vis-à-vis the agreed contract duration. The BAC and the Procurement Office to ensure that the awarded supplier faithfully complies with the terms and conditions of the contract and that it affix its signature on the pertinent Purchase Order, indicating the date of receipt/acceptance by the latter since the count of delay is dependent on it.
Further, enforced the collection of these liquidated damages from identified suppliers in default. Furthermore, in case similar situation may arise, the Municipal Accounting Office to be strict in imposing LD by deducting the computed LD amount against the supplier’s claim. Lastly, the same office to meticulously and sufficiently support disbursement vouchers with required pertinent documents prior to payment and submission to the audit team.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Designated General Services Officer to:
• Conduct a physical count of inventories;
• Prepare the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories; and
• Strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 124 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I on the prescribed timeline
in the submission of RPCI to allow the necessary verification of inventory account.

We recommended that the Provincial Governor:
 Create an Inventory Team to conduct complete physical inventory of all PPE
accounts of the Province taking into consideration the guidelines provided in
COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of the PPE and prepare
the RPCPPE in the prescribed format to facilitate the verification and
reconciliation of records between PGSO and accounting unit;
 Require the OIC-PGSO to update PPE Ledger Card (PPELC) and Property
