submit physical count/inventory of the breeding stocks and prepare and render the
report thereon complete with supporting documents as proof of the
status/condition of the animals.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. OIC-Municipal Assessor to accelerate and exert more effort on the ongoing
general revision of real properties in order to provide the MT the complete and
updated Assessment Roll; and

b. MT to submit to the Municipal Accountant the Certified List of Taxpayers
showing their names and amounts due and collectible for the year so that the
RPT/SET Receivables can be adjusted to reflect the correct balances.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to:

a. revert those account whose owners/creditors replied but was not able to
submit, upon request, the required legal documents pertaining to their claim
and secure from the SB a resolution with a condition that appropriation shall
be made in the event that they were able to provide those documents; and

b. continue to exert more effort to locate other owners/creditors and confirm
the account and request them to submit documents pertaining to their
claims with the Municipality. If creditors will not be able to support their
claims, revert the account to the Unappropriated Surplus of the GF, subject
to an authority from the SB through a Resolution with a condition that
appropriation shall be made in the event that a claim arises in the future
pursuant to the provisions of Section 98 of PD No. 1445.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to expedite and continue efforts in providing the details/breakdown of the
said accounts and remit the same to the aforementioned agencies so that member
beneficiaries may avail of the privileges and benefits due them.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the General
Services Officer-Designate to continue his effort in completing and submitting legal
documents required by the GSIS pertaining to the assessment of the insurable
properties and the provision of its insurance.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-
Municipal Assessor to:

a. accelerate the titling of the two remaining lots whose titling process were
already commenced in CY 2018; and

b. immediately start the process of the titling for the six lots where legal documents
were found, continue exerting more efforts in locating the documents of the five
lots and make all the necessary actions/representations with the DENR and RD
in the titling of the lots.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to
accelerate the modification of the construction layout/plan so that the said project
will be implemented for the benefit of the constituents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Health Officer
(MHO) to adhere with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 to ensure the safety
and quality of the procured drugs and medicines.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and
Municipal Accountant to release the RPT share of the component Barangays
promptly within five days after the end of each quarter in accordance with Section
36 of COA Circular No. 92-382 and Section 271 (d) of RA No. 7160.

We reiterated our prior years recommendations to management to:
Reconstitute the Inventory and Reconciliation Committees to conduct the mandatory annual physical count and the subsequent reconciliation of reports per inventory count, per GSO records and per accounting records, respectively, taking into consideration the clear-cut guidelines provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020. Afterwards, require the Municipal Accountant to effect necessary adjusting journal entries supported with working papers and other relevant documents, if warranted.
Require the Municipal Accountant to review and analyze the validity of the unidentified obligations and, if found non-existent and/or with no valid claims against the LGU, cause the reversion thereof to the unappropriated surplus as provided in Section 98 of PD 1445 subject for subsequent legislative authorization. If warranted, effect necessary adjusting journal entries, supported with working papers and other pertinent documents.

We recommended to the management to require the Municipal Accountant to effect adjusting journal entries to record the said remaining inventories at year-end. Record the receipt and issuance of all supplies using the perpetual inventory method, considering the proper accounts provided in COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015. Moreover, stop the improper practice of attaching Requisition Issue Slip (RIS) to disbursement vouchers as basis of expending inventories outrightly.
Further, required the General Services Office and Municipal Health Office to provide the Municipal Accounting Office a Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) supported with the prescribed format of RIS for already issued supplies and drugs and medicines during the period, either weekly or monthly whichever is more convenient, as basis for the latter to prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the already expended/issued supplies.

We recommended to the management to require the Municipal Engineer to submit pertinent documents to the Municipal Accountant as her basis to draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV), along with its working paper, to drop the demolished infrastructures from the books of accounts of the agency. Likewise, required the Municipal Accountant to draw JEV, along with its working papers and supporting documents, to adjust the recorded equipment which failed to meet the P15,000.00 capitalization threshold. Submit to this audit team copies thereof for our evaluation.

We recommended to the management to require the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer and BAC Chairman, to submit the noted lacking supporting documents to the audit team for post-audit, in order to conclude the appropriateness, legality and validity of the disbursed transactions.
Moreover, required the Municipal Accountant to, henceforth, meticulously and sufficiently support disbursement vouchers with required pertinent documents prior to payment and submission to audit team.

We recommended to the management of LGU Quezon to exercise prudence and good sense in the disbursement of government funds. Henceforth, stop the grant of mortuary financial assistance to Quezon (Bukidnon) Municipal Government Employees Union (QMGEU) which is not authorized by existing laws, rules and regulations.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that the Management establish effective measures to improve cash position in order to transfer unexpended balances of Local Disaster Risk Reduction Fund to a special trust fund so that the subject fund will be available during times of disaster/calamity.

We recommended that the Management continue its effort to further reduce cash deficit through introduction of cost-cutting measures and limiting the expenditures to the essential operating needs of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to 

(a) continuously exert efforts to submit on time the receipts and disbursements transactions of the Municipality as required by COA Circular No. 2009-006; and 

(b) cause the immediate submission of still unsubmitted accounts such as the official receipts, check and cash disbursement vouchers and payrolls to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the BAC Chairman/ Municipal Engineer and Acting HRMO to comply with the submission of contracts, purchase orders and all the documents forming part thereof within five (5) working days from its execution and/or issuance as provided by COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive require the OIC - Municipal Accountant to investigate and make adjustments on the variance of the two reciprocal accounts and see it to it that all future transactions in the said accounts are properly recorded.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive

(a) through the Municipal Development Council and Municipal Planning and Development Office

 (i) refrain from including expenditure items that are not allowed to be charged against the Development Fund (DF); and

 (ii) optimally utilize budget for Development Fund through realignment of unutilized balances to other eligible projects; and 

(b) require the Municipal Engineer to expedite the implementation of the unimplemented project for the benefit of its intended users.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Local School Board (LSB)

continue complying with the utilization and prioritization of SEF projects accordingly; and 

(b) instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory of all properties booked under SEF and prepare a Report thereon; and

 (c) require the Accounting and the Treasury office to properly maintain property ledger cards for all assets procured out of SEF.

We recommended that the LCE require the LDRRM Officer to

 (a) prepare the annual LDRRM Investment Plan and cause its submission to Office of the Civil Defense and DILG; 

(b) ensure implementation of planned programs/projects/activities (PPAs) and consider the reprogramming of unexpended/unutilized PPAs to strengthen the disaster and mitigation capacity of the LGU; and 

(c) cause the submission of the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to COA Auditor as required by COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We commended the management for its unceasing efforts to comply with R.A. No. 9003 or the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000.  We further recommended that the LCE direct MENRO to continue formulating programs, projects and activities that will increase solid waste diversion from disposal facilities to lessen its cost of waste disposal.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the 

(a) Bids and Awards Committee; Head, BAC Secretariat and Technical Working Group to work on the completion of the documentary deficiencies and submit the same to the Audit Team, henceforth ensure inclusion of the aforementioned lacking documents/noted deficiencies in future procurements through public bidding; and

 (b) Municipal Accountant to ensure that the required documents for each type of transaction are submitted/attached to the disbursement vouchers before the processing and payments.

We recommended that  the LCE

 (a) ensure the implementation of GAD programs and activities in accordance with approved GAD plans and budget; and 

(b) instruct the GAD Focal Point Person to use the Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG) in the attribution of costs of regular program/project to GAD.

We recommended that the Management continue complying with the BIR rules and regulations on the withholding and remittance of withholding taxes on value added taxes, percentage taxes and expanded taxes from government money payments to assure that the government was not deprived of taxes that can be used in the implementation of various programs and projects.  

However, the Accounting Unit should investigate the occurrence of negative balance in Due to BIR account and make the adjustment in the succeeding remittance to BIR if necessary.

We recommended that the management continue complying with the GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG regulations on the withholding and remittance of employees’ and employer contributions and loan amortizations of employees to the respective agencies and remit fully the remaining balance of due to GSIS to assure all its members of the social security benefits due them.

We recommended that the Management through the concerned Department Heads implement the following:

The Disbursing Officer fully liquidate his/her cash advances for operating expenses in order that the corresponding amount of expenses shall be recorded in the books of the Municipality.  Likewise, demand from the officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances to submit their liquidation documents or make refunds to fully settle their outstanding accounts, otherwise, the amounts shall be deducted from any amount due them or worse, they may be charged with malversation of public funds.
The Municipal Accountant monitor closely the liquidation of Cash Advances granted to Accountable Officers and   Cash Advances for operating expenses within the prescribed period to ensure prompt settlement of accounts. The no liquidation no cash advance policy must be strictly followed.
observations.managementAction:
Disbursing Officer did not fully liquidate his/her cash advances for operating expenses. 

Demand Letter to the officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances was sent to them individually.

The municipal accountant started to deduct from their payroll.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management through the Municipal Treasurer and Assistant Municipal Treasurer deposit regularly and intact the full amount of income collection in strict compliance with Section 69 of PD 1445 and Section 111 of GAAM. Likewise, stop the practice of leaving huge amount of cash in their possession to avoid possible loss through misuse or theft/robbery of government funds.
        We also recommended that the Petty Cash Fund Custodian fully liquidate the unexpended cash on hand as it has been idle already for almost two years. We also recommended that the Management strictly comply with the proper use of Petty Cash Fund through COA Circular 97-002.
observations.managementAction: Petty Cash Fund amounting to P23,697.71 was already returned or liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management through the MGSO prepare an updated physical inventory report of all its properties. We also recommended that the MGSO/Property Custodian maintain property records and furnish copy thereof to the Municipal Accountant.
observations.managementAction: MGSO prepared an incomplete physical inventory report of all its properties.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Management fast track   the utilization of said X-ray machine to fully attain its intended purpose. The Management should properly plan its undertakings and consider all necessary measures before the implementation/procurement thereof to ensure efficient and effective delivery of services to its constituents.
observations.managementAction: Management already communicated to ISELCO but up to this day the power cooperative had no action yet despite numerous follow up.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management submit immediately the required basic documents to support the payment of honoraria to the members of the Bids and Awards Committee and Technical Working Group to avoid the issuance of Notice of Suspension.
observations.managementAction: BAC already submit the required documents to support the payment of honoraria

observations.recommendation: The Management demand from the retired Municipal Treasurer to return immediately to the Collecting Officer of the LGU her unutilized petty cash fund amounting P23,697.91 to fully liquidate her cash advance that has been idle already for more than 1 year and 8 months. We also recommended that Management comply with the proper use of Petty Cash Fund as provided under COA Circular 97-002.
observations.managementAction: the retired Municipal Treasurer already returned the Collecting Officer of the LGU her unutilized petty cash fund amounting P23,697.91

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management through the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant refrain from processing and granting cash advances under the following circumstance:

Cash advances for elected officials other than official travel.
Cash advance without legally authorized purpose
Cash Advances for TEV for officers and employees with unliquidated cash advances.
No approved travel order and itinerary of travel.
observations.managementAction: the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant noted and implemented our recommendation

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management through the Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer discontinue the practice of charging prior year’s expenditure to current year appropriation and that proper matching of expenses against appropriation should always be observed pursuant to the above-cited regulations. Likewise, officials who continue to authorize the payment of such expenditures are jointly and severally liable for any disallowances.
observations.managementAction: There are still some who receives less than the NTHP

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management through:

The Municipal Engineer conduct regular monitoring of the implementation of projects to ensure that the allocated funds are utilized efficiently and only for the purpose intended;

The Accountant require the concerned implementing barangays to settle immediately their unliquidated fund transfers amounting to P200,000.00 each through the submission of the required reports and/or refund of the unused balance; and

       We also recommended that the Management strengthen the operation of preventive measures on disbursement of funds and monitoring controls in the liquidation of fund transfers to ensure timely submission of complete and proper documents.
observations.managementAction:
Municipal Engineer conducted regular monitoring of the implementation of projects and the Municipal 
Accountant required the concerned implementing barangays to settle immediately their unliquidated fund transfers amounting to P200,000.00 each through the submission of the required reports and/or refund of the unused balance

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the unexpended LDRRMF amounting to P317,521.77 as of December 31, 2019 be transferred to a special trust fund account pursuant to Section 21, paragraph 3 of Republic Act No. 10121 and Section 4.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We likewise recommended that the MDRRMO/LDRRM Focal Person and the Municipal Accountant submit the supporting documents enumerated above for our evaluation in the hiring of job order personnel charged to the DRRRM funds.
observations.managementAction: MDRRMO/LDRRM Focal Person and the Municipal Accountant submit the supporting documents enumerated above for our evaluation in the hiring of job order personnel charged to the DRRRM funds

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management through the Municipal Accountant submit the necessary reports on timely basis and strictly comply with the provisions in COA Circular No 2009-006 and PD 1445 in order to avoid imposition of the sanctions provided therein.
observations.managementAction: the Municipal Accountant submit the necessary reports earlier than their usual late submission.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The Management through the Municipal Accountant thoroughly review the disbursements pertaining to financial assistance/donations and ensure that these transactions are supported with complete documentary requirements. All grants must always be supported by request/solicitation letters and social case studies conducted by the MSWDO to determine the indigence of the beneficiaries, together with other documentary evidences necessary for each type of assistance to establish the necessity of its granting in compliance with Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.  Assistance must only be granted to qualified and needy constituents of the municipality; and

The Members of the Sanggunian Bayan desist from directly granting financial assistance/donations to its constituents regardless of the source of fund as it is not among their mandated powers, duties and functions under Section 447 of RA 7160.  They may however recommend through indorsements to the LCE the request received from constituents for appropriate action.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant thoroughly review the disbursements pertaining to financial assistance/donations and ensure that these transactions are supported with complete documentary requirements. 

Members of the Sanggunian Bayan desist from directly granting financial assistance/donations to its constituents

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management through the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers so as to provide historical data for the controlling accounts in the general ledger.
observations.managementAction: Municipality start to maintain subsidiary Ledger Cards for other accounts

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to undertake a complete periodic physical inventory of municipal properties and immediately reconcile the balances of any affected accounts to permit fair presentation of PPE in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management has conducted physical inventory and submitted the RPCPPE to the office of the auditor but failed to reconcile the balances with the accounting unit.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 11, page 46, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to ensure that the disbursements under the 20% Development Fund should be consistent with DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 17-1.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended management to:

direct its procuring department to prioritize acquisition of regular or ordinary office supplies through PS-DBM before resorting to the other alternative modes of procurement such as shopping; and 

in case of non-availability of stocks, attach to the Disbursement Vouchers the Agency Procurement Request duly acted by the PS-DBM and the Price Quotation and Stocks Availability Certification on the items which are available on stocks.
observations.managementAction: Management has partially complied with the audit recommendation on the procurement of common-use supplies from PS-DBM but it was not fully implemented due to limitations brought by the COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management, through the Bids and Awards Committee and the Municipal Accounting Office, ensure that all perfected contracts and their respective supporting documents including those required in auditorial/technical review be submitted to the Audit Team within five (5) days from the execution thereof to facilitate immediate review and evaluation as required by COA Circulars No. 96-010 and 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: Management has partially complied with the audit recommendation on the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 96-010 and 2009-001 but it was not fully complied due to limitations brought by the COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Local School Board, being the one responsible in the formulation, approval, and utilization of the SEF Budget as provided under RA No. 7160 or the Local Government Code to:

exert earnest efforts in ensuring that all budgeted projects, programs and activities are optimally and fully implemented;

revisit the revised guidelines on the use of SEF under DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 to ensure a more strategic and efficient formulation and utilization of resources for priority programs and projects; and

 consider realignment of funds to other priority programs, projects and activities, if warranted by circumstances.
observations.managementAction: Management has partially complied with the audit recommendation, but it was not fully complied due to limitations brought by the COVID-19 pandemic.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 19, page 65, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local School Board to revert the unexpended balances of SEF appropriations to the unappropriated surplus at the end of the fiscal year and re-appropriate the same in the SEF Budget of the succeeding year.
observations.managementAction: Management has reverted the unexpended balances of SEF appropriation and was re-appropriated during the year in compliance with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management: 

a.	require/demand the immediate settlement of all long overdue cash advances and if necessary withhold the salaries of the concerned officials and employees for the settlement thereof; and

b.	strictly comply with the provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445 and COA Cir. No. 97 – 002 dated February 10, 1997 in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Management has partially enforced the collection of outstanding cash advances through issuance of demand letters to accountable persons.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 17, page 60, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management exert more effort in enforcing the immediate settlement of the remaining disallowance so that said funds can be used for other development programs or projects for the benefits of its constituents and as compliance with Section 7.1.1 of the Revised Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Accounts as implemented under COA Circular No. 2009 – 006.
observations.managementAction: Management has partially enforced the collection of disallowances and has issued demand letters to accountable persons. Some DLs were not served due to unknown address of AOs who are no longer in service.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to monitor the progress of the implementation of the project which is still in progress and ensure its earliest possible accomplishment.
observations.managementAction: Management has completed all ADM-LGSF projects in compliance with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management expedite the implementation of all on – going and not yet started programmed and funded projects under the 20% MDF to optimize utilization thereof and timely deliver the desired benefits to the intended beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: Management has fully implemented the 20% projects in compliance with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

continue implementing the measures contained in the Municipality’s 10-Year SWM Plan;

fast tract the implementation of SLF; and

continue adopting such measures to comply with the mandate of the law.
observations.managementAction: Management has completed the municipal SLF and has also completed its 10-year SWM plan which was approved by the NSWMC in December 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accountable Officer observe proper safekeeping of government funds or property under her custody at all times to avoid incurrence of audit deficiencies in conformity with the aforementioned provisions of P.D. No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Cash Disbursing Officer has settled her cash shortage and presented the proof of payment to the auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accountable Officer monitor the proper use of the petty cash in compliance with Sections 4.3.2 and 1.1.2 of COA Circulars 97 – 002 and 2012 – 001, respectively
observations.managementAction: Management has complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management ensure the return of unutilized petty cash in the hands of any designated petty cash custodians to the municipal treasurer at year – end and for the latter to issue the corresponding official receipt as required under the regulation.
observations.managementAction: Petty cash balance was liquidated by the Municipal Treasurer within 20 days at the end of the year in compliance to GAM for LGUs and in compliance with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management continue the practice of depositing collections regularly in compliance to Section 69 of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 32 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I particularly on rules and regulations governing collections and deposits.
observations.managementAction: Management has complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accountable Officer continue the proper and regular monitoring of the accountable forms so that information as to the receipts, issuances and balances in her custody can be readily determined through preparation of the Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF).
observations.managementAction: Management has complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management effect the increase of the fidelity bond of the Liquidating Officer based on her total accountability in accordance with the Bureau of Treasury Circular No. 02 – 2009 in order to provide adequate protection on government funds.
observations.managementAction: The management instructed the Accountable Officer to increase her fidelity bond in compliance with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

strengthen its internal control by ensuring that all financial reports and forms are properly and completely accomplished to guaranty the propriety and regularity of its transactions; and

strictly adhere with the provisions of the Volume II of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: Management adhered with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GSO-Designate to prepare and maintain property cards and PAR and reconcile the same with the PPELCs of the Accounting Office. Furthermore, it was also recommended to update PAR within the prescribed period to monitor the custody and transfer of accountability of the properties for better property management.
observations.managementAction: Property cards and PAR were already prepared and maintained by the GSO-Designate.
observations.reason: Reconciliation with PPELC of the Accounting Office is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Assessor to accelerate and exert more effort on the on-going general revision of real properties in order to provide the Treasurer the complete and updated Assessment Roll; and b. Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Municipal Accountant the certified list of taxpayers showing their names and amounts due and collectible for the year so that the RPT/SET Receivables can be adjusted to reflect the correct balances.
observations.managementAction: Assessment Rolls for CYs 2018 and 2019 were completed. Designated one staff to prioritize the encoding of tax delinquencies.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to encode the list of taxpayers and late submission of Assessment Roll to the MTO caused the partial implementation of the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to expedite and continue efforts in providing the details/breakdown of the said accounts and remit the same to the aforementioned agencies so that member beneficiaries may avail of the privileges and benefits due them.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is amenable with the audit recommendations. Moreover, hard copies of old payrolls and records of remittances from the MTO were retrieved but the Municipal Accountant was unable to prioritize the establishment of the unreconciled balances due to the urgent programs and activities to address COVID-19 pandemic.
observations.reason: Analysis of payroll and remittances of the prior years is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the General Services Officer-Designate to continue his effort in completing and submitting legal documents required by the GSIS pertaining to the assessment of the insurable properties and the provision of its insurance.
observations.managementAction: Forms are already filled-out and the supporting documents are to be accomplished by the Municipality and to be submitted to the GSIS once completed.
observations.reason: Documentation for the application is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal Assessor to: a) accelerate the titling of the two remaining lots whose titling process were already commenced in CY 2018; and b) immediately start the process of the titling for the six lots where legal documents were found, continue exerting more efforts in locating the documents of the five lots and make all the necessary actions/representations with the DENR and RD in the titling of the lots.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has taken actions and already coordinated with the Barangays and other agencies concerned.
observations.reason: The Municipality experienced difficulty in the procedures in the titling of the lots.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to accelerate the modification of the construction layout/plan so that the said project will be implemented for the benefit of the constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was able to secure a Sangguniang Bayan (SB) Resolution No. 2020-18 that gives right of ownership and use of the said portion of land for public road and coordinated with the Department of Environmental and Natural Resources (DENR).
observations.reason: Coordination with the DENR revealed that the land is not a straight land instead it was a slanting land which requires modification of the earlier construction layout/plan. Also delayed due to COVID-19 pandemic and implementation of community quarantines.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly adhere with the regulations and promptly deposit all municipal collections to protect municipal funds against risk of loss and possible misuse.
observations.managementAction: The Acting Municipal Treasurer deposits all municipal collections promptly and intact in order to protect the funds and safeguard the interest of the government.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to update the entries in her cashbooks on a daily basis to ensure that the daily balances of her accountability are correctly stated.
observations.managementAction: Cashbooks are regularly updated by the Acting Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly adhere to the mandatory designation and bonding requirement of all accountable officers handling the collection of local taxes, fees and licenses of the Municipality pursuant to the provisions of PD No. 1445 and Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 to safeguard the funds and protect the interest of the government.
observations.managementAction: Only approved RLRC is used in collecting local taxes from the taxpayers; The Municipal Treasurer ensured that all accountable officers handling the collection of taxes, fees and licenses of the Municipality are now bonded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) coordinate with the Municipal Accountant in ensuring that all employees have a net take home pay of not less than P5,000.00 in adherence with General Appropriations Act of FY 2019.
observations.managementAction: The HRMO ensures that all employees have a net take home pay of not less than P5,000.00 in adherence with the General Appropriations Act of FY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to refrain from processing the claims through reimbursement and instead settle obligations by drawing checks payable to the supplier.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality settled internet subscription expenses by drawing checks payable to the supplier.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor: a) instruct the Municipal Agriculturist to resolve the issues pertaining to the implementation of the project under her Office; and b) require the Municipal Treasurer to remit the unexpended balances to its Source Agencies/National Treasury to enable the government to utilize the excess funds for other vital/essential projects/programs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Agriculturist made a follow-up with the contractors to proceed and finish the uncompleted projects. Furthermore, the Municipal Treasurer was required to remit the unexpended balances to its Source Agencies/National Treasury to enable the government to utilize the excess funds for other vital/essential projects/programs.
observations.reason: Despite the actions taken by the Municipality, the contractor was not able to finish the uncompleted projects. Remittance of the unexpended balance of the funds held in trust/comes from the national government is still pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the head of Municipal Planning and Development Office to ensure the efficient and effective utilization of the 20 percent Development Fund which is primarily intended to fund projects that shall contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic and environmental management outcomes.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Planning and Development Officer ensured the efficient and effective utilization of the 20 percent Development Fund which is primarily intended to fund projects that shall contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic and environmental management outcomes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. maximum utilization of the GAD funds through the implementation of GAD-related programs and projects in order to attain the objective for which funds were provided; and b. OIC–MPDC to expedite the establishment of CBMS and institutionalize a GAD Database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data providing the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation of the Municipality with the assistance of the Municipal GAD Focal Point System.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO commented that re-programming and re-alignment of funds were adopted to respond to COVID-19 pandemic issues and concerns and ultimately pursuing women empowerment and gender equality. Furthermore, the CBMS process was discontinued because of the COVID-19 Pandemic restrictions and compliance to health protocols. However, there is an appropriation in the current year budget to continue the establishment of CBMS once restrictions are lifted and the process is allowed.
observations.reason: Some of the PPAs planned were not implemented due to COVID-19 restrictions; CBMS process was discontinued due to the pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MENRO-Designate to work on securing all the requirements needed for the issuance of the ECC and establishment of the proposed Sanitary Landfill pursuant to RA No. 9003. We also recommended that the Municipality, through the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, follow-up the status of the review of the DENR and secure the approval of its 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan from the National Solid Waste Management Commission pursuant to the provisions under the above cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: The MENRO-Designate commented that the area of the open dumpsite is converted into a controlled dumpsite and Material Recovery Station. The establishment of Sanitary Landfill at Barangay Villa Mercedes is on-going. Furthermore, the revision of Solid Waste Management Plan is currently in-process.
observations.reason: The Municipality is still waiting for the assistance from the DENR and the review of the Solid Waste Management Plan is still in-process.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor: a. instruct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that only approved RLRC be used in collecting local taxes from the taxpayers; b. continue the granting of tax credits due the concerned taxpayers; and c. coordinate with the SB in ensuring that all necessary steps and requirements particularly the required review of the RLRC by the SP are undertaken prior to the implementation of any ordinance or revision of the Local Revenue Code of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor coordinated with the SB on the implementation of ordinances and the Local Revenue Code of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. execute a yearly contract of lease embodied in a public instrument for every market stall occupant in accordance with COA Circular No. 88-282-A to ensure that interests of both parties are protected; b. require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to maintain individual subsidiary ledgers of the market stall lessees for easy monitoring and enforcing of collections and intensify the collection efforts on the delinquent stall occupants and impose penalties on those who are not paying their market rental fees.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer ensured that a yearly contract of lease is executed for every market stall occupant. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer maintain individual subsidiary ledgers of the market stall lessees for easy monitoring and enforcing of collections.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare a separate set of financial statements for each economic enterprise so that adequate information as to assets, liabilities, equity and results of operations of the economic enterprise can be monitored and evaluated to determine its viability.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared separate financial statements for each economic enterprise.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. HRMO-Designate to exert more efforts and prioritize the updating of the salary adjustments or service records of the employees in the GSIS database; and b. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to prioritize the remittance of the amount of P2,208,408.38 to the GSIS upon reconciliation/updating of the employee records.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant, who is also the HRMO-Designate, commented that the updating of salary adjustments and service records are partially done. Remittances will be made immediately after the updates are reflected on the GSIS records system. In addition, the Acting Municipal Treasurer commented that the Municipality has already remitted a portion of the balance to the GSIS.
observations.reason: Updating of salary adjustments and service records are not yet completed; remittance of the full amount is still pending.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking disbursement vouchers, journal entry vouchers, payroll and collection reports in consistent with the existing rules and regulations in order to avoid issuance of Notice of Suspension and modification of auditor’s opinion on financial statements due to audit scope limitation, if warranted. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant to ensure that complete source documents listed in the reports/journals be completely forwarded to COA for post-audit and verification. 

Likewise, reminded the Municipal Treasurer to submit immediately the complete documents after payment so as not to incur delay in the monthly submission of accounts and reports.
observations.managementAction: The management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking disbursement vouchers, journal entry vouchers, payrolls and collection reports. Also, the Municipal Treasurer to be reminded to submit immediately the complete documents after payment so as not to incur delay in the monthly submission of accounts and reports.”

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the “unidentified beginning balances” subsidiary ledgers of the mentioned real accounts to establish fair presentation, reliability and accuracy of the CY 2019 Statement of Financial Position accounts. 

Directed the Inventory Committee and the concerned offices to continue and push through the reconciliation of reports per physical inventory count, per GSO and per accounting records, in order to analyze and review the existence, validity and accuracy of PPE items which comprised the bulk of unreconciled beginning balances. Effect necessary adjusting entries after reconciliation, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: The management to require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the ‘unidentified beginning balances’ subsidiary ledgers of the mentioned real accounts. Furthermore, the Inventory Committee and other concerned offices to be directed to continue and push through the reconciliation of reports per physical inventory count, per GSO and per accounting records.”
observations.reason: Inventory Committee failed to convene, conduct and reconcile the noted observations, thus, reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Assessor, the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the material differences among their respective records on a periodic basis to arrive at the correct real property tax and special education tax collectible balances, in order to enhance the reliability and accuracy of the financial statement accounts. Further, the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entry to fairly present the actual tax collectibles on the Statement of Financial Position based on the certified list of tax delinquencies prepared by the Municipal Treasurer.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Government of Quezon to instruct the Municipal Assessor, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the material differences among their respective records.”

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer to reconcile the differences between the accounting records and the Consolidated Quarterly Report on Government Project/Activities to ensure that projects already completed should no longer be recorded under the CIP accounts.  

Required the Municipal Engineer to submit immediately to the Municipal Accountant the relevant documents such as Certificate of Completion and Inspection Report, and other pertinent documents as bases of the latter to reclassify the CIP account to its specific PPE account.

Moreover, the Municipal Accountant to conduct detailed analysis and effect, if warranted, necessary adjusting entries relative to the unreclassified accounts and the affected accumulated depreciations.
observations.managementAction: The management to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer to reconcile the differences between the accounting records and the Consolidated Quarterly Report on Government Project/Activities. Also, the Municipal Engineer to require to submit immediately to the Municipal Accountant the pertinent documents as bases of the latter to reclassify the CIP account to its specific PPE account and the Municipal Accountant to conduct detailed analysis and effect, if warranted, necessary adjusting entries relative to the unreclassified accounts and the affected accumulated depreciations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to recognize the accrued interest expense incurred from the availed outstanding loans at year-end in observance to the accrual method of accounting interest expenses as prescribed by PPSAS and in order to improve the reliability and accuracy of the balances reflected in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to recognize the accrued interest expense incurred from the availed outstanding loans at year-end in observance to the accrual method of accounting interest expenses as prescribed by PPSAS and in order to improve the reliability and accuracy of the balances reflected in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to record deposits only when actually deposited to accurately report the actual balances of cash in bank and on hand. Moreover, both office should reconcile their records as frequently as possible so as to establish a sound and good internal control system on cash management.
observations.managementAction: The management to require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to record deposits only when actually deposited to accurately report the actual balances of cash in bank and on hand. Also, both offices to instructed to reconcile their records as frequently as possible so as to establish a sound and good internal control system on cash management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive to be prudent and judicious in exercising his fiscal responsibility, along with the Accountant and Treasurer, by strictly adhering to the proper disbursement process. Revisit and reevaluate the current disbursement procedures of the agency. Moreover, implement a good internal control system to prevent incurrence of such improper practice of paying disbursement vouchers with inadequate signatures of proper officials.
observations.managementAction: The management to revisit and reevaluate the current disbursement procedures in order to implement a good internal control to prevent incurrence of improper practice of paying disbursement voucher with inadequate signature of proper officials.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management stop immediately the disbursements of monthly grocery allowances to each employee and the existing loyalty retirement incentive scheme to retiring employees. Instead, adhere strictly to the provisions on the one-time grant of CNA benefits as set by a specific DBM Budget Circular.

Further, the officials of the Municipal Government of Quezon and the Employees Union to convene, revisit and revised the subsisting Collective Negotiation Agreement, with an intention to adopt a much better, valid negotiation agreements for the welfare and interests of its employees, in consonance with the Public Sector Labor-Management Council (PSLMC) Resolution No. 2, s. 2004 and some other related issuances.

Furthermore, the management to include on its priorities the grant of personnel compensation benefits expressly authorized by law, such as the grant of performance-based bonus (PBB) and monetization of earned leave credits.
observations.managementAction: The management to immediately stop the payment of these allowances/benefits and to revisit and revise the provisions of the existing CNA which are contrary to law, rules and regulation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive to be prudent in authorizing the grant of cash advances to Cash Disbursing Officer. Moreover, instructed the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to establish first the legal and specific purpose of the cash advance by detailing on its disbursement voucher and/or on its supporting document the identified payrolls and other claims to be paid out from the cash advance, in order to limit the disbursements thereof to its intended purpose/s.
observations.managementAction: The management to practice the establishment first of the legal and specific purpose of any cash advance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management stop immediately the excessive government share premium payments and strictly adhere to the mandatory government contributions at a monthly maximum amount of one hundred pesos (P100) per employee, as provided for in Section 7 of RA 9679. Henceforth, re-appropriate funds to other more beneficial priority projects and programs for the general welfare of its constituents.
observations.managementAction: The management to observe the mandatory government contribution as provided in Section 7 of RA 9679.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive to stop, henceforth, the granting of monthly communication-related expenses in the form of allowance to the identified officials and employees. Instead, consider the payment of monthly communication expenses through reimbursements by availing the mobile phone fixed plan offered by available telecommunications network/s. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant to strictly require appropriate documentations to payrolls such as statement of account and original official receipt evidencing payment of monthly plan, as required in COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The management to discontinue the granting of monthly communication-related expenses in the form of allowance. Instead to consider availing mobile plan offered by the telecommunication network and to consider other procedure for communication expenses provided that it is supported with proper or complete documentation.”

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management discontinue, henceforth, the grant of honoraria to the Head of BAC Secretariat performing a regular function on a full-time basis to avoid audit disallowance. Instead, if warranted, allow only the overtime pay for procurement-related services rendered in excess of official working hours. Adhere strictly to the explicit provisions of DBM Budget Circular (BC) No. 2004-5A dated October 7, 2005 on the grant of honoraria to government personnel involved in government procurement.
observations.managementAction: The management to discontinue the grant of honoraria to the Head of the BAC Secretariat.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management to direct the Inventory Committee and the concerned offices to exert extra efforts in reconciling the reports per physical inventory, per GSO and per accounting records. 

Expedite the disposition of all unserviceable and obsolete properties for the municipality to avoid further deterioration and to generate additional income from their disposal. Specifically, the Management should create Appraisal and Disposal Committees to undertake the appraisal and disposition of these properties in accordance to COA Circular No. 89-296 dated January 27, 1989 and PD 1445. Moreover, a request for audit action should be submitted to COA audit team before the conduct of proper disposal activity. The reports prepared should be furnished to the Municipal Accountant who shall prepare journal vouchers to drop the properties from the books of account.
	
The General Services Office to continue establishing good practices on supply and property management by extending more efforts to locate missing properties, by keeping updated records for property accountabilities and by demanding turn-over of either unserviceable properties from present accountable officers (AOs) or unused properties from resigned/retired/separated accountable officers.

Further, direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the “unidentified beginning balances” subsidiary ledgers of PPE accounts to establish reliability and accuracy of accounts reflected in financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of all property, plant and equipment per inventory from GSO and Accounting records.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of all property, plant and equipment per inventory from GSO and Accounting records, especially on “unidentified balances” were still not completed, hence, reiterated in this report. Moreover, disposition of unserviceable and obsolete properties were partially done.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to direct the Municipal Accountant to reissue demand letters and exhaust all possible remedies to collect the receivables and to demand liquidation of cash advances and fund transfers. Conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of these accounts. Comply with all the conditions and requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 for the possible request for the write off of the specified dormant receivables and cash advances accounts to the Commission on Audit. Provide allowance for impairment thereafter and reconcile the accounts with unidentified/ unreconciled subsidiary ledgers from prior years in order to have a fair presentation of affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The management to reissue demand letter to accountable Officers for the liquidation of the accounts. Ongoing reconciliation and analysis of the unidentified accounts. Request for write off will be made after reconciliation of receivable accounts.
observations.reason: Request for write-off was not requested for long outstanding and dormant accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to require the submission of the written conformity and acquiescence of the Solicitor General or the Government Corporate Counsel, as the case maybe, and the written concurrence of the Commission on Audit shall first be secured before the hiring or employment of a private lawyer or law firm.
observations.managementAction: Already submitted request to the Office of the Solicitor General but unfortunately was denied. The management to secure concurrence to COA.
observations.reason: Request for concurrence of COA will still be done.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to require the Municipal Accountant to continue the reconciliation of land reported in lump amount and disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements necessary information on these real properties.

Require the Municipal Assessor to constantly follow up the status of the submitted documents (for titling of the real properties of the municipality at the Registry of Deeds and DENR provincial office at Malaybalay City to ensure titling of all the lands owned by the municipality in compliance to Section 47 (C) of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Local Government Code of 1991 and Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.   

Direct the General Services Officer to keep a complete record of all real properties owned by the LGU in compliance to Articles 122 and 440 of the IRR of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: Process the entitlement of land owned by the municipality.
observations.reason: Still waiting for the issuance of title of deeds. The GSO has no record of all real properties owned by the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to require the Local Chief Executive to create an Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of all local roads and prepare Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System. 

Direct the GSO to maintain a Local Road Inventory, Road Map, Local Road Network Property Card, and prepare Report on Local Road Network for all roads and its components annually. Then, instruct the Municipal Engineer to render complete description and cost segregation of all road components, serving as basis for the Municipal Accountant to prepare Journal Voucher to record the costing of local road network in the general ledger and in the Local Road Network Ledger Card.
observations.managementAction: Creation of Inventory Committee thru Executive Order who will handle the physical inventory of local road networks
observations.reason: Neglected by management in CY 2020.  The Municipal Engineer failed to render cost segregation of all road components serving as basis for the Municipal Accountant to record the costing of local road networks. The GSO has not maintained the Local Road Inventory, Road Map, Local Road Network Property Card and the Report on Local Road Network for all roads and its components.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management prepare and updated physical inventory report of all its properties. We also recommended that the General Services Officer/Property Custodian maintain property records.
observations.reason: The physical inventory is still going on and is not completed before the FS date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prioritize the immediate operation of said X-ray machine. The Management should properly plan its undertakings and consider all necessary measures before the implementation/procurement thereof to ensure efficient and effective delivery of services to its constituents.
observations.managementAction: Management still waiting for the completion of the renovation of the building where it would be transferred.
observations.reason: The building where it would be mounted is still under renovation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that disbursements under the 20% Development Fund be consistent with DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 17-1.
observations.managementAction: Management failed to comply with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 2, page 27, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly adhere with the regulations and deposit all municipal collections promptly and intact in order to protect municipal funds against risk of loss and possible misuse.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor continues her efforts to remind the Municipal Treasurer, through office memorandum, to comply with audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Delay in the deposit of collections is still existing.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor fill-up or appoint the remaining two (2) support staff plantilla position in compliance with section 12 of R.A. No. 10121.
observations.reason: Difficulty of finding applicant fitted for the position.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor utilize the amount intended for the construction of the evacuation center taking into considerations the basic facilities and standards of evacuation center management set forth in DEPED-DSWD-DILG-DOH JMC No. 1, s. 2013.
observations.reason: No Engineering Design Plans and Program of Works as of this time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GFPS introduce improvement on the lactation station of the Municipality to comply with the minimum standards set forth by R.A. No. 7006, as amended by R.A. No. 10068.
observations.reason: Absence of Program of Work / Plan

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous year recommendations that the CAD, in coordination with the other concerned departments, particularly the CTO, GSD and CED continue to:
Exert effort in the retrieval of records/reports from respective files that would establish the details of the transactions that took place prior to CY 2003 in order to reclassify the unreconciled beginning balances’ to the appropriate SL accounts; and
Submit a timetable comprising the schedule of activities in retrieving documents, reconciling reports and adjusting the unreconciled beginning balances to the appropriate SL Accounts.

We recommended that:
The City Accountant -
Reconcile the records of bank accounts with the summary of existing accounts maintained by the CTO;
Make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect in the books the correct number of bank accounts and the balances of the Cash in Bank Account; and
Management require the continuous coordination between the CAD and CTO in reconciling the records of existing bank accounts to assure proper presentation of account balances in the financial records of the City.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that the City Accountant:
Make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect in the books the correct balances of affected asset accounts and submit to this office the JEVs for audit and verification; and
Closely monitor the recording of financial transactions and strengthen review of JEVs and GLs to ensure that all financial transactions are properly and accurately taken up in the books to prevent the occurrence of negative balances in the accounts.

We recommended that Management:
Maintain a separate depository account for each fund and properly classify the fund intended for the SATF in conformity with Sec 3 of COA Circular No. 92-382; and
Analyze the cause of the negative balance in the Quezon Memorial Circle - SATF and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect in the books the correct balance of the account.

We recommended that Management:
Submit the details of the Lump-sum Appropriation shown in the Appropriation Ordinance for CY 2019 such as but not limited to the names of creditors, types of expenditures and programs, projects or activities; and
Henceforth, strictly comply with Article 454 (q) of the IRR of the LGC and Section 4 (s) Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.

We recommended that Management increase efforts in strengthening the controls in the grant, liquidation and monitoring of cash advances by:
Requiring the concerned officers and employees to immediately liquidate their outstanding cash advances and for this purpose, submit a timetable when to liquidate the outstanding cash advances; and
Strictly monitoring the liquidation of cash advances by sending demand letters regularly, particularly to accountable officers and employees who are still in active service, and in case of failure to liquidate the same despite demand, withholding of the payment of any money due them may be made pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that Management:
Submit immediately the lacking documents/ justification enumerated in paragraph  no. 7.2;
Stop immediately the practice of processing liquidation of cash advances without complete supporting documents; and
Henceforth, comply strictly with Section 9 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that Management stop the practice of paying certain employees thru cash advance and subsequent distribution, and to instead effect payment of salaries, overtime pay or other incentives using the ATM facilities of the LBP as provided in the City’s Memoranda issued in 
CYs 2014 and 2016.

We recommended that Management:
Refrain from undertaking procurement unless it is in accordance with the approved APP, including authorized changes thereto; and
Strictly adhere with the provisions of the 2016 IRR of RA No. 9184 relative to budget linkage, procurement planning and activities.

We recommended that Management:
Submit immediately the lacking documents to the Office of the Auditor for verification and audit;
Stop immediately the practice of processing payments without complete supporting documents; and
Henceforth, comply strictly with Section 9 of COA Circular 
No. 2012-001.

We recommended the following:
The QCU Management expedite its process of application for institutional recognition by prioritizing the appointment of a full-time President of the University;
Institute changes/corrective measures for the noted deficiencies prior to requesting Government Authority for their Academic Programs with CHED; and
Henceforth, Management comply strictly with the provisions of CHED Memorandum No. 09, series of 2018, dated June 8, 2018 and the IRR of RA No. 10931.
We recommended that Management strictly adhere with the provisions of the Article 342 of the IRR of the LGC.

We recommended that Management require the various recipient NGOs for the immediate settlement/liquidation of the fund transferred to them pursuant to COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
Instruct the DF Focal Person to conduct regular coordination with the implementing offices to ensure that PPAs are undertaken within the target period; and
Henceforth, direct concerned officials of the City to observe the guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% DF under DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommended that Management:
Strictly monitor the implementation of all planned LDRRM PPAs to ensure the achievement of the City’s goal and objectives in mitigating the impact of disasters/calamities; and
Henceforth, strictly observe the guidelines regarding LDRRM PPAs under COA Circular No. 2012-002, COA Memorandum No. 2014-009 and other related issuances.
We recommended that Management coordinate with the Department of Energy and submit a revised Deployment Plan for approval, if feasible.

We recommended that:
The City Mayor instruct -
The CAD and the CTO to remit within the reglementary period  the full amount of withheld taxes from employees, contractors and suppliers;
The Human Resource Management and Development Office (HRMDO) to ensure that employees’ information with the BIR is accurate and up to date; and
Henceforth, Management strictly comply with Section 2.58 of BIR Revenue Regulations No. 2-98 and Section 11 of BIR Revenue Regulations No. 11-2018.

We recommended that Management:
Collect the taxes due from the suppliers/contractors and immediately remit the same to the BIR;
Refrain payments in the form of cash thru cash advances and comply with Section 2.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002; and
Strictly comply with the provisions of Section 4.114-2 of BIR Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 85-2017 dated October 11, 2017 and Section 2, paragraph (J) of Revenue Regulations No. 11-2018 dated January 31, 2018.

We recommended that:
The City Mayor instruct -
The CAD and the CTO to remit immediately all unremitted withheld premium contributions, consisting both government and employees shares;
The Human Resource Management and Development Office (HRMDO) to ensure that employees’ information with PhilHealth is accurate and up to date; and
Henceforth, Management strictly comply with Section 20 (b) Rule III Title III of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of the National Health Insurance Act.

We reiterated our recommendation that the City Accountant immediately enforce the settlement of all audit suspensions and disallowances in compliance with Sections 5.4 and 7.1.1, Chapter II of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009; and Management to be more vigilant in the implementation of existing accounting and auditing rules and regulations in processing of claims to avoid possible audit suspensions and disallowances.

We recommended that the Management through the concerned Department Heads implement the following:
The Disbursing Officer fully liquidate his/her cash advances for operating expenses in order that the corresponding amount of expenses shall be recorded in the books of the Municipality.  Likewise, demand from the officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances to submit their liquidation documents or make refunds to fully settle their outstanding accounts, otherwise, the amounts shall be deducted from any amount due them or worse, they may be charged with malversation of public funds.
The Municipal Accountant monitor closely the liquidation of Cash Advances granted to Accountable Officers and   Cash Advances for operating expenses within the prescribed period to ensure prompt settlement of accounts. The no liquidation no cash advance policy must be strictly followed.

We recommended that the Management through  the Municipal Treasurer and Assistant Municipal Treasurer  deposit regularly and intact the full amount of income collection in strict compliance with Section 69 of PD 1445 and Section 111 of GAAM. Likewise, stop the practice of leaving huge amount of cash in their possession to avoid possible loss through misuse or theft/robbery of government funds.
We also recommended that the Petty Cash Fund Custodian fully liquidate the unexpended cash on hand as it has been idle already for almost two years. We also recommended that the Management strictly comply with the proper use of Petty Cash Fund through COA Circular 97-002.

We recommended that the Management through the MGSO prepare an updated physical inventory report of all its properties. We also recommended that the MGSO/Property Custodian maintain property records and furnish copy thereof to the Municipal Accountant.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management fast track   the utilization of said X-ray machine to fully attain its intended purpose. The Management should properly plan its undertakings and consider all necessary measures before the implementation/procurement thereof to ensure efficient and effective delivery of services to its constituents.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management submit immediately the required basic documents to support the payment of honoraria to the members of the Bids and Awards Committee and Technical Working Group to avoid the issuance of Notice of Suspension.

Management Comment:
The former Municipal Treasurer committed to return the unutilized petty cash fund and to fully liquidate her cash advance before she retires on February 2020.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The former Municipal Treasurer already paid the balance of P23,697.71 last March 4, 2020.

We recommended that the Management through the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant refrain from processing and granting cash advances under the following circumstance:
Cash advances for elected officials other than official travel.
Cash advance without legally authorized purpose
Cash Advances for TEV for officers and employees with unliquidated cash advances.
No approved travel order and itinerary of travel.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management through the Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer discontinue the practice of charging prior year’s expenditure to current year appropriation and that proper matching of expenses against appropriation should always be observed pursuant to the above-cited regulations. Likewise, officials who continue to authorize the payment of such expenditures are jointly and severally liable for any disallowances.

We recommended that the Management through:
The Municipal Engineer conduct regular monitoring of the implementation of projects to ensure that the allocated funds are utilized efficiently and only for the purpose intended;
The Accountant require the concerned implementing barangays to settle immediately their unliquidated fund transfers amounting to P200,000.00 each through the submission of the required reports and/or refund of the unused balance; and
We also recommended that the Management strengthen the operation of preventive measures on disbursement of funds and monitoring controls in the liquidation of fund transfers to ensure timely submission of complete and proper documents.

We recommended that the unexpended LDRRMF amounting to P317,521.77 as of December 31, 2019 be transferred to a special trust fund account pursuant to Section 21, paragraph 3 of Republic Act No. 10121 and Section 4.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
We likewise recommended that the MDRRMO/LDRRM Focal Person and the Municipal Accountant submit the supporting documents enumerated above for our evaluation in the hiring of job order personnel charged to the DRRRM funds.

We recommended that the Management through the Municipal Accountant submit the necessary reports on timely basis and strictly comply with the provisions in COA Circular No 2009-006 and PD 1445 in order to avoid imposition of the sanctions provided therein.

We recommended that:
The Management through the Municipal Accountant thoroughly reviews the disbursements pertaining to financial assistance/donations and ensures that these transactions are supported with complete documentary requirements. All grants must always be supported by request/solicitation letters and social case studies conducted by the MSWDO to determine the indigence of the beneficiaries, together with other documentary evidences necessary for each type of assistance to establish the necessity of its granting in compliance with Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.  Assistance must only be granted to qualified and needy constituents of the municipality; and
The Members of the Sanggunian Bayan desist from directly granting financial assistance/donations to its constituents regardless of the source of fund as it is not among their mandated powers, duties and functions under Section 447 of RA 7160.  They may however recommend through indorsements to the LCE the request received from constituents for appropriate action.
We recommended that the Management through the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers so as to provide historical data for the controlling accounts in the general ledger.

We recommended that the Management comply strictly with COA Circular No. 2009-001 and COA Circular No. 94-006 such as:
1.  Furnish the Auditor copy of contract (including purchase orders and memorandum of agreement) and each of all the documents forming part thereof by reference of incorporation within five (5) working days from the execution of contract; and
2. Notify the Auditor within 24 hours from accepting deliveries of procured items.

We recommended that the Management strictly observe the General Provision of General Appropriations Act for FY 2019 which provides a limitation on the net take home pay of officials and employees of the government in the amount not lower than P5,000.00.

1.6	We recommended management to undertake a complete periodic physical inventory of municipal properties and immediately reconcile the balances of any affected accounts to permit fair presentation of PPE in the financial statements.
2.6	We recommended Management to ensure that the disbursements under the 20% Development Fund should be consistent with DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 17-1.

We recommended that management:
ensure that trust funds are used exclusively to the purpose/s for which they were granted following the provisions of Section 4(3) of P.D. No. 1445 and Sections 305(e) and 309(b) of R.A. No. 7160.
require the settlement of long outstanding receivables to augment the deficient cash under trust fund.

4.7	We recommended management to (a) direct its procuring department to prioritize acquisition of regular or ordinary office supplies through PS-DBM before resorting to the other alternatives mode of procurement such as shopping; and (b) in case of non-availability of stocks, attach to the Disbursement Vouchers the Agency Procurement Request duly acted by the PS-DBM and the Price Quotation and Stocks Availability Certification on the items which are available on stocks.
