5.7	We recommended that management, through the Bids and Awards Committee and the Municipal Accounting Office, ensure that all perfected contracts and their respective supporting documents including those required in auditorial/technical review be submitted to the Audit Team within five (5) days from the execution thereof to facilitate immediate review and evaluation as required by COA Circulars No. 96-010 and 2009-001.

6.9	We recommended the Local School Board, being the one responsible in the formulation, approval, and utilization of the SEF Budget as provided under RA No. 7160 or the Local Government Code to:
exert earnest efforts in ensuring that all budgeted projects, programs and activities are optimally and fully implemented;
revisit the revised guidelines on the use of SEF under DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 to ensure a more strategic and efficient formulation and utilization of resources for priority programs and projects; and
consider realignment of funds to other priority programs, projects and activities, if warranted by circumstances.

7.6	We recommended the Local School Board to revert the unexpended balances of SEF appropriations to the unappropriated surplus at the end of the fiscal year and re-appropriate the same in the SEF Budget of the succeeding year.

8.13	We recommended that management:
a.	require/demand the immediate settlement of all long overdue cash advances and if necessary withhold the salaries of the concerned officials and employees for the settlement thereof; and
b.	strictly comply with the provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445 and COA Cir. No. 97 – 002 dated February 10, 1997 in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

9.6	We recommended that management:
a.	Exert diligent effort on the liquidation of the receivables thru demand to effect immediate settlement of the account and send tracers if not acted upon; and
b.	Advise the Accountant to effect the adjustments on the related accounts and to strictly adhere to existing applicable accounting and auditing rules and regulations to ensure fair presentation of financial statements.

10.8	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
analyze the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts and trace back the differences by determining the nature of the accounts;
prepare the necessary adjusting entries to bring the correct balances of the accounts; and
maintain subsidiary ledger as necessary and include disclosure in the Notes to FS for fair presentation of Financial Statements

11.5	We recommended the Municipal Accountant to properly monitor and reconcile the balance of receivables on disallowances/charges recognized in the financial statements with that contained in COA reports and make the necessary adjustments.  Henceforth, proof of settlements should be simultaneously presented to the office of the auditor for recording and timely issuance of Notice of Settlement of Suspension/Disallowance/Charge (NSSDC).

12.5	We recommended that management exert more effort in enforcing the immediate settlement of the remaining disallowance so that said funds can be used for other development programs or projects for the benefits of its constituents and as compliance with Section 7.1.1 of the Revised Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Accounts as implemented under COA Circular No. 2009 – 006.

We recommended that management:
a. Create an Inventory Committee to undertake the physical count of inventory items as of June 30 and December 31 of each year; to prepare the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RCPI) by inventory account name and distribute the same as prescribed; to reconcile said report with the records of the Accounting Office in accordance with Section 124, Volume I of MNGAS; and to submit to the Office of the Auditor the duplicate copy of the RCPI not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the ﬁrst and second semester, respectively;
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to correct the errors in recording purchases and issuances of inventories and observe the Perpetual Inventory System as method of recording inventories as prescribed under Section 51, Volume I of the MNGAS for LGUS; and
c. Instruct the General Services Officer (GSO) and the Municipal Accountant to maintain updated Stock Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards and regularly reconcile records to detect discrepancies and effect immediate corrections; and for the GSO to promptly prepare and submit the monthly RSMIs to the Municipal Accountant for recording in compliance with Sections 119 and 120, Volume I, of the MNGAS for LGUs.
We recommended management to monitor the progress of the implementation of the project which is still in progress and ensure its earliest possible accomplishment.

15.6	We recommended that management expedite the implementation of all on – going and not yet started programmed and funded projects under the 20% MDF to optimize utilization thereof and timely deliver the desired benefits to the intended beneficiaries.

16.5	We recommended that management:
a.	continue implementing the measures contained in the Municipality’s 10-Year SWM Plan;
b.	fast tract the implementation of SLF; and
c.	continue adopting such measures to comply with the mandate of the law.

17.5	We recommended that the Accountable Officer observe proper safekeeping of government funds or property under her custody at all times to avoid incurrence of audit deficiencies in conformity with the aforementioned provisions of P.D. No. 1445.
18.3	We recommended that the Accountable Officer monitor the proper use of the petty cash in compliance with Sections 4.3.2 and 1.1.2 of COA Circulars 97 – 002 and 2012 – 001, respectively.

19.3	We recommended that management ensure the return of unutilized petty cash in the hands of any designated petty cash custodians to the municipal treasurer at year – end and for the latter to issue the corresponding official receipt as required under the regulation.

20.5	We recommended that management continue the practice of depositing collections regularly in compliance to Section 69 of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 32 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I particularly on rules and regulations governing collections and deposits.

21.4	We recommended that the Accountable Officer continue the proper and regular monitoring of the accountable forms so that information as to the receipts, issuances and balances in her custody can be readily determined through preparation of the Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF).

22.5	We recommended that management effect the increase of the fidelity bond of the Liquidating Officer based on her total accountability in accordance with the Bureau of Treasury Circular No. 02 – 2009 in order to provide adequate protection on government funds.

23.4	We recommended that Management:
strengthen its internal control by ensuring that all financial reports and forms are properly and completely accomplished to guaranty the propriety and regularity of its transactions; and
strictly adhere with the provisions of the Volume II of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for LGUs.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) Acting Municipal Treasurer to (i) ensure availability of all cashbooks of the Municipality at all times for review and verification of the Auditors; (ii) update cashbooks daily as required by NGAS Manual for LGUs; (iii) cause the use of correct format of Certification in cashbooks as required by Section 26, Chapter 1 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs Volume II; (b) Acting Municipal Treasurer and OIC - Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records at least quarterly to immediately detect any error/discrepancy for timely adjustment and accordingly, to establish an accurate and reliable records and reports on Cash in Treasury and Cash in Bank balances; and (c) OIC - Municipal Accountant to (i) investigate the existence and the nature of bank accounts with long outstanding subsidiary balances but with zero bank balances; (ii) prepare Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) for the remaining cash in bank accounts; (iii) provide sufficient details on the reconciling items in the BRS; (iv) make the necessary adjusting entry on the staled checks in coordination with the Acting Municipal Treasurer; and (v) trace the records of the four bank accounts with no reported outstanding balance in the books and make the necessary adjustments thereof.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) Inventory Committee to (i) conduct the physical inventory of all PPE of the Municipality and immediately submit related reports to the Office of the Auditor; (ii) reconcile the inventory report/results with the Accounting records to ensure fair presentation of the accounts; (b) Acting Municipal Treasurer to complete PPE records such as property cards, Property Acknowledgement Receipts and inventory tags for proper monitoring and control of the agency properties; and (c) OIC-Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation on slaughterhouse building.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that the Management establish effective measures to improve cash position in order to transfer unexpended balances of Local Disaster Risk Reduction Fund to a special trust fund so that the subject fund will be available during times of disaster/calamity.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to (a) reconcile their records of Real Property Tax (RPT) receivables in order to report reliably the said account in the financial statements; (b) Municipal Treasurer to (i) update and post notice of delinquency for property taxes at the main entrance of the municipal building and in publicly accessible and conspicuous place in each barangay where these properties are located; (ii) seek assistance from the concerned barangays for the issuance of Notice of Delinquency and conduct an information dissemination regarding the importance of paying taxes promptly and the consequences of non-payment of taxes; if warranted, enforced the provision of Section 254 (a) and (b) of the RA 7160 in the collection of delinquent taxes.

We recommended that the Management continue its effort to further reduce cash deficit through introduction of cost-cutting measures and limiting the expenditures to the essential operating needs of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to (a) continuously exert efforts to submit on time the receipts and disbursements transactions of the Municipality as required by COA Circular No. 2009-006; and (b) cause the immediate submission of still unsubmitted accounts such as the official receipts, check and cash disbursement vouchers and payrolls to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the BAC Chairman/ Municipal Engineer and Acting HRMO to comply with the submission of contracts, purchase orders and all the documents forming part thereof within five (5) working days from its execution and/or issuance as provided by COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive require the OIC - Municipal Accountant to investigate and make adjustments on the variance of the two reciprocal accounts and see it to it that all future transactions in the said accounts are properly recorded.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) through the Municipal Development Council and Municipal Planning and Development Office (i) refrain from including expenditure items that are not allowed to be charged against the Development Fund (DF); and (ii) optimally utilize budget for Development Fund through realignment of unutilized balances to other eligible projects; and (b) require the Municipal Engineer to expedite the implementation of the unimplemented project for the benefit of its intended users.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive and the Local School Board (LSB) agreed to (a) continue complying with the utilization and prioritization of SEF projects accordingly; and (b) instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory of all properties booked under SEF and prepare a Report thereon; and (c) require the Accounting and the Treasury office to properly maintain property ledger cards for all assets procured out of SEF.

We recommended that the LCE require the LDRRM Officer to (a) prepare the annual LDRRM Investment Plan and cause its submission to Office of the Civil Defense and DILG; (b) ensure implementation of planned programs/projects/activities (PPAs) and consider the reprogramming of unexpended/unutilized PPAs to strengthen the disaster and mitigation capacity of the LGU; and (c) cause the submission of the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to COA Auditor as required by COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We commended the management for its unceasing efforts to comply with R.A. No. 9003 or the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000.  We further recommended that the LCE direct MENRO to continue formulating programs, projects and activities that will increase solid waste diversion from disposal facilities to lessen its cost of waste disposal.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) Bids and Awards Committee; Head, BAC Secretariat and Technical Working Group to work on the completion of the documentary deficiencies and submit the same to the Audit Team, henceforth ensure inclusion of the aforementioned lacking documents/noted deficiencies in future procurements through public bidding; and (b) Municipal Accountant to ensure that the required documents for each type of transaction are submitted/attached to the disbursement vouchers before the processing and payments.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) ensure the implementation of GAD programs and activities in accordance with approved GAD plans and budget; and (b) instruct the GAD Focal Point Person to use the Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG) in the attribution of costs of regular program/project to GAD.

We recommended that the Management continue complying with the BIR rules and regulations on the withholding and remittance of withholding taxes on value added taxes, percentage taxes and expanded taxes from government money payments to assure that the government was not deprived of taxes that can be used in the implementation of various programs and projects.  However, the Accounting Unit should investigate the occurrence of negative balance in Due to BIR account and make the adjustment in the succeeding remittance to BIR if necessary.

We recommended that the management continue complying with the GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-Ibig regulations on the withholding and remittance of employees’ and employer contributions and loan amortizations of employees to the respective agencies and remit fully the remaining balance of due to GSIS to assure all its members of the social security benefits due them.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GSO-Designate to prepare and maintain property cards and PAR and reconcile the same with the PPELCs of the Accounting Office. Furthermore, it was also recommended to update PAR within the prescribed period to monitor the custody and transfer of accountability of the properties for better property management.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the:
(a) Municipal Assessor to accelerate and exert more effort on the on-going general revision of real properties in order to provide the Municipal Treasurer the complete and updated Assessment Roll; and
(b) Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Municipal Accountant the certified list of taxpayers showing their names and amounts due and collectible for the year so that the RPT/SET Receivables can be adjusted to reflect the correct balances as of December 31, 2019.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. revert those account whose owners/creditors replied but was not able to submit, upon request, the required legal documents pertaining to their claim and secure from the SB a resolution with a condition that appropriation shall be made in the event that they were able to provide those documents; and
b. continue to exert effort to locate other owners/creditor and confirm the account and request them to submit documents pertaining to their claims to the Municipality. If creditors will not be able to support their claims, revert the account to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund, subject to an authority from the SB through a Resolution with a condition that appropriation shall be made in the event that a claim arises in the future pursuant to the provisions of Section 98 of PD No. 1445.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to continue efforts in providing the details/breakdown of the accounts and remit the same to the aforementioned agencies so that member beneficiaries may avail of the privileges and benefits due them.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the GSO-Designate to continue effort in completing and submitting legal documents required by the GSIS pertaining to the assessment of the insurable properties and the provision of its insurance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor to:
a. accelerate the titling of the two remaining lots whose titling process were already commenced in CY 2018 and remain in process in CY 2019; and
b. immediately commence the titling of the six lots where legal documents were found, continue exerting more efforts in locating the documents of the five lots and make all the necessary actions/representations with the DENR and RD in the titling of the lots.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the negotiating committee to expedite the negotiation with the lot owners to resolve the right-of-way issue so that the said project will be implemented for the benefit of the constituents.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly adhere with the regulations and deposit all municipal collections promptly and intact in order to protect municipal funds against risk of loss and possible misuse.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to update the entries in her cashbooks on a daily basis to ensure that the daily balances of her accountability are correctly stated.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to adhere strictly with the mandatory designation and bonding requirement of all accountable officers handling the collection of local taxes, fees and licenses of the Municipality pursuant to the provisions of PD No. 1445 and Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 to safeguard the funds and protect the interest of the government.

We recommended that the management require the Human Resource Management Officer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant in ensuring that all employees have a net take home pay of not less than P5,000.00.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to refrain from processing the internet subscription claims through reimbursement and instead settle obligations by drawing checks payable to the supplier.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer maintain the prescribed cashbooks, keep them updated and reconcile the cashbook balances with the SLs of the Municipal Accountant for both the Cash in Local Treasury and Cash in Bank accounts every end of the month pursuant to Section 63.F. of the LTOM.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant furnish the Municipal Treasurer with copies of SLs, Bank Statements and Bank Reconciliation Statements for her reference in reconciling her cashbooks with accounting records, and issue a certification on the balances of all Accountable Officers who maintain cashbook for their reference in its reconciliation with the SLs.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor thru, the Municipal Accountant, regularly serve written notice to AOs concerned to demand settlement of their outstanding cash advances and institute the necessary legal action against those who failed to settle their accountabilities.

The Municipal Accountant exert efforts to locate documents and previous records to facilitate reconciliation and/or determination of the causes of the negative cash advance balances of various AOs and effect the necessary adjustment in the books of accounts, as  appropriate.

In all instances, the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Accountant must ensure that the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 are strictly observed and that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Mayor require the previous DOs to reconcile their accountabilities against the records of the Municipal Accountant and settle any remaining balance, otherwise file a case against the concerned DOs, if warranted;
b.   Municipal Accountant  send demand letters to the former DOs giving full details of their accountabilities appearing in the books of accounts that need to be settled immediately and require them to turn over their cashbooks as soon as possible; and
c.  Administrative Officer II of the Accounting Office prepare and submit to the Audit Team the reconcilation report of the concerned DOs’ accountabilities including the certified copy of their  cashbook for reference in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. require the Inventory Committee to complete the annual physical count of PPE, reconcile the results of the count with property and accounting records, and submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.  All discrepancies between physical and book inventories must be investigated, cleared and reconciled immediately.  If necessary, written explanation should be required from accountable and responsible person. He should likewise ensure that physical inventory is undertaken annually and reports are submitted accordingly; and
b.  require the Property Officer and the Municipal Accountant to reconstruct and thereafter maintain the property cards and PPE ledger cards, respectively, in the forms prescribed in the said Manual. Also, the Property Officer should ensure that all issued PPE for official use of officials and employees are covered by ARE which shall be renewed every January of the third year after issue.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant (i) review the PPE schedule for the unidentified PPE items and exert efforts to identify these properties; and (ii) coordinate with the Inventory Committee in the physical count of fully depreciated PPE items to assess their condition whether still serviceable or not and consider derecognition of its carrying amount from the books of accounts.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management, through the (a) Municipal Assessor facilitate the transfer of the title of the real properties under the name of the Municipal Government of Quezon to prevent future dispute of ownership; and (b) Municipal Accountant maintain Real Property Ledger Cards for all municipal lots to support the general ledger account and to provide ready information about municipal lots.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Municipal Engineer for the completion of required data and information regarding the Local Road Network to facilitate its recognition in the books of accounts including adequate disclosure thereof in the Notes to Financial Statements pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We reiterated the previous audit recommendation that the Municpal Mayor, through the Human Resource Officer, consider filling up or appointing  personnel for the remaining two (2) support staff plantilla position in compliance with Section 12 of R.A. 10121.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Muncipal Engineer, cause the preparation of the engineering design plan and necessary documents  to facilitate the construction of  evacuation center taking into considerations the basic facilites and standards of disaster-resilient evacuation center management set forth in DEPED-DSWD-DILG-DOH JMC No. 1, s. 2013 for the safety and protection of the evacuees in times of calamities.

We recommended that the Market Supervisor see that the contracts of lease are duly notarized to make them more binding and enforceable and thereafter enforce the terms and conditions of the contract.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Market Supervisor inform the lessees on this provision and include the same in the Contract of Lease. We also recommend that the Municipal Treasurer exert more efforts to collect the surcharges from the lessees, otherwise a Notice of Charge will be issued.

We recommended that the Market Supervisor and the Municipal Treasurer enforce the prescribed rates of additional 5% and 2.5% on the monthly rent of frontage stalls and stalls along the market roads for and exert more efforts to collect the additional rents from the concerned stallholders.

We recommended that the Market Supervisor strictly enforce the rules and regulations on the maintenance of cleanliness and sanitation of the market premises.

We also recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan through the Market Committee consider imposing higher fines and penalties to any violator of the Market Code, especially on the cleanliness and sanitation of the market premises.

We also reiterated our recommendation to the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate portion of the net income from market operations for the maintenance of buildings and structures in the market premises in accordance with the Department of Budget and Management (DBM) Local Budget Circular (LBC) No. 111 dated June 10, 2016 on the setting up and operation of the Local Economic Enterprises.

We recommended that the Market Supervisor cancel the lease contract and blacklist the stallholders who repeatedly violated the provisions of the Market Code and demand for the immediate settlement of all unpaid rents including the penalties and charges.

We recommended that the Market Committee limit the spaces occupied by the stallholders and award the excess stalls to other qualified stall applicants.

We recommended that the Market Committee investigate the extension of stall space and compel the stallholders to pay the applicable fees, fines and penalties in accordance with the Code. In case of non-adherence of the concerned stallholders to the Code, we recommend immediate cancellation of their privilege to lease the premises and automatically blacklist the said person.

We recommended that the Market Committee assess the cost to acquire the mentioned stalls and papags. Accordingly, the Municipal Budget Officer should include such cost in the supplement budget of the Municipality this year 2020, subject to availability of funds therefor.

We also recommended that the Market Supervisor apprise the Audit Team on their actions on the subject properties and submit an inventory of all properties of the public market.

We recommended that the Municipal Assessor assess all privately constructed stalls until the same has been acquired by the Municipality. The Municipal Treasurer should collect the applicable taxes due on those properties.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct both the Municipal Administrator and the Market Supervisor to implement the audit recommendations embodied in the Annual Audit Report of CY 2017 to improve the operation of public market, otherwise consider appointing a new Market Supervisor.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System-Technical Working Group (GFPS-TWG) improve in establishing clear baselines and realistic estimates in budget preparation for PPAs translated from the demands/commitments identified in the GAD agenda. The GFPS-TWG should also provide justification for all variances in the GAD AR to facilitate proper evaluation of PPAs.

We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking disbursement vouchers, journal entry vouchers, payroll and collection reports in consistent with the existing rules and regulations in order to avoid issuance of Notice of Suspension and modification of auditor’s opinion on financial statements due to audit scope limitation, if warranted. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant to ensure that complete source documents listed in the reports/journals be completely forwarded to COA for post-audit and verification.
Likewise, reminded the Municipal Treasurer to submit immediately the complete documents after payment so as not to incur delay in the monthly submission of accounts and reports.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the “unidentified beginning balances” subsidiary ledgers of the mentioned real accounts to establish fair presentation, reliability and accuracy of the CY 2019 Statement of Financial Position accounts.
Directed the Inventory Committee and the concerned offices to continue and push through the reconciliation of reports per physical inventory count, per GSO and per accounting records, in order to analyze and review the existence, validity and accuracy of PPE items which comprised the bulk of unreconciled beginning balances. Effect necessary adjusting entries after reconciliation, if warranted.

We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Assessor, the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the material differences among their respective records on a periodic basis to arrive at the correct real property tax and special education tax collectible balances, in order to enhance the reliability and accuracy of the financial statement accounts. Further, the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entry to fairly present the actual tax collectibles on the Statement of Financial Position based on the certified list of tax delinquencies prepared by the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer to reconcile the differences between the accounting records and the Consolidated Quarterly Report on Government Project/Activities to ensure that projects already completed should no longer be recorded under the CIP accounts.
Required the Municipal Engineer to submit immediately to the Municipal Accountant the relevant documents such as Certificate of Completion and Inspection Report, and other pertinent documents as bases of the latter to reclassify the CIP account to its specific PPE account.
Moreover, the Municipal Accountant to conduct detailed analysis and effect, if warranted, necessary adjusting entries relative to the unreclassified accounts and the affected accumulated depreciations.

We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to recognize the accrued interest expense incurred from the availed outstanding loans at year-end in observance to the accrual method of accounting interest expenses as prescribed by PPSAS and in order to improve the reliability and accuracy of the balances reflected in the financial statements.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to record deposits only when actually deposited to accurately report the actual balances of cash in bank and on hand. Moreover, both office should reconcile their records as frequently as possible so as to establish a sound and good internal control system on cash management.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to be prudent and judicious in exercising his fiscal responsibility, along with the Accountant and Treasurer, by strictly adhering to the proper disbursement process. Revisit and reevaluate the current disbursement procedures of the agency. Moreover, implement a good internal control system to prevent incurrence of such improper practice of paying disbursement vouchers with inadequate signatures of proper officials.

We recommended that management stop immediately the disbursements of monthly grocery allowances to each employee and the existing loyalty retirement incentive scheme to retiring employees. Instead, adhere strictly to the provisions on the one-time grant of CNA benefits as set by a specific DBM Budget Circular.
Further, the officials of the Municipal Government of Quezon and the Employees Union to convene, revisit and revised the subsisting Collective Negotiation Agreement, with an intention to adopt a much better, valid negotiation agreements for the welfare and interests of its employees, in consonance with the Public Sector Labor-Management Council (PSLMC) Resolution No. 2, s. 2004 and some other related issuances.
Furthermore, the management to include on its priorities the grant of personnel compensation benefits expressly authorized by law, such as the grant of performance-based bonus (PBB) and monetization of earned leave credits.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to be prudent in authorizing the grant of cash advances to Cash Disbursing Officer. Moreover, instructed the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to establish first the legal and specific purpose of the cash advance by detailing on its disbursement voucher and/or on its supporting document the identified payrolls and other claims to be paid out from the cash advance, in order to limit the disbursements thereof to its intended purpose/s.

We recommended that management stop immediately the excessive government share premium payments and strictly adhere to the mandatory government contributions at a monthly maximum amount of one hundred pesos (P100) per employee, as provided for in Section 7 of RA 9679. Henceforth, re-appropriate funds to other more beneficial priority projects and programs for the general welfare of its constituents.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to stop, henceforth, the granting of monthly communication-related expenses in the form of allowance to the identified officials and employees. Instead, consider the payment of monthly communication expenses through reimbursements by availing the mobile phone fixed plan offered by available telecommunications network/s. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant to strictly require appropriate documentations to payrolls such as statement of account and original official receipt evidencing payment of monthly plan, as required in COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that management discontinue, henceforth, the grant of honoraria to the Head of BAC Secretariat performing a regular function on a full-time basis to avoid audit disallowance. Instead, if warranted, allow only the overtime pay for procurement-related services rendered in excess of official working hours. Adhere strictly to the explicit provisions of DBM Budget Circular (BC) No. 2004-5A dated October 7, 2005 on the grant of honoraria to government personnel involved in government procurement.

Recommendations:
1.	The Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

2.	The General Services Officer maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map and shall keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components.

3.	The Municipal Accountant prepare the Journal Voucher to record the inventory of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card.  He shall keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network, and ensure that 100% of the value of road network of the Municipality shall be booked-up in 2019 consistent with COA Circular No. 2015-008. Full disclosure on road networks should be made in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the Municipality.

4.	The Municipal Engineer, General Service Officer and the Municipal Accountant coordinate to update reporting and accounting guidelines for the local road network.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality already recorded in its books the full values of its Road Network based on the inventory report submitted by the MGSO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Petty Cash Fund Custodian return immediately to the     Collecting Officer of the LGU her unutilized      petty cash fund amounting P23,697.91      to fully      liquidate her cash advance that has been idle       already for more than 8 months. We also recommended that Management comply with the proper use of Petty Cash Fund as provided under COA Circular 97-002.
observations.managementAction:
Management waits for the payment from the treasurer As of December 31, 2019.

On March 4, 2019 the former Municipal Treasurer already paid the balance and closed her petty cash fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

1.	Require the contractor to immediately construct the concrete panel box, otherwise, refund the payment made to him corresponding to the cost of uninstalled panel box.

2.	The uninstalled 25 KVA transformer will be used in the Community Hospital to support the newly purchased X-ray machine which were not put in operation due to lack of transformer.
observations.managementAction: The Management required the contractor to construct the concrete panel box which was successfully accomplished as validated by the Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management comply strictly with COA Circular No. 2009-001 and COA Circular No. 94-006 such as:
1.  Furnish the Auditor copy of contract (including purchase orders and memorandum of agreement) and each of all the documents forming part thereof by reference of incorporation within five (5) working days from the execution of contract; and

2. Notify the Auditor within 24 hours from accepting deliveries of procured items.
observations.managementAction: The Mgmt. only partially submitted the required documents as some were still found with incomplete supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:
Management strictly enforce the provisions of Section 33 of RA 7160 for the execution of a Memorandum of Agreement which includes among others, project name, intended beneficiaries, amount of fund based on the cost estimates, brief description of the project, location, and systems and procedures to implement the project.

Record the transfer of funds under the inter-agency receivables account Due from LGUs in order to establish accountability and responsibility and to ensure that the transfer is properly taken up in the books of both agencies.

Management establish specific guidelines and policies in the grant of all kinds of financial assistance.
observations.managementAction: The required MOA was prepared and the erroneous recording was rectified to the proper account consistent with NGAS.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management should strictly observe the General Provision of General Appropriations Act for FY 2018 which provides a limitation on the net take home pay of officials and employees of the government in the amount not below P5,000.00.
observations.managementAction: There are still some who receives less than the NTHP

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management ensure that the 20% DF of the LGU be allotted and used for priority development programs, projects and activities which shall contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic, development and environmental management as prescribe under DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.
observations.managementAction: Please state what measures or action has the Mgt. done partially

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management evaluate the status of the project implementation and identify the reason for the delay in the completion of the projects funded by the NGAs and to fast track the implementation of the programs/projects for the benefit of the constituents.  We also recommended that the financial assistance from the Province of Isabela be reclassified to its proper account Due to LGUs.
observations.managementAction: Management evaluated the status of the project implementation and identified the reason for the delay in the completion of the projects funded by the NGAs and  fast-tracked the implementation of the programs/projects for the benefit of the constituents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management undertake (1) a complete periodic physical inventory of municipal properties, (2) submit the same to the Auditor and (3) immediately reconcile the balances with accounting records to permit fair presentation of PPE in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management conducted physical inventory and submitted the RPCPPE to the office of the auditor but failed to reconcile the balances with the accounting unit.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 1, page 25, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management comply with the provisions of RA No. 9184 prohibiting splitting of contracts.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendation and the provisions of RA No. 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2007-001 and make representations to the NGOs/POs for the immediate liquidation of the Due from Non-Government Organizations amounting to P78,500.00.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to monitor the progress of the implementation of the remaining project still in progress and ensure its earliest possible accomplishment.
observations.managementAction: Management was able to implement the remaining projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to monitor the progress of the implementation of the projects and ensure its earliest possible accomplishment.
observations.managementAction: Management was able to complete all SALINTUBIG projects during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for management to revisit the program and explore the possibility of its full implementation by coordination with the source agency.
observations.managementAction: Management coordinated with the source agency and they are in the process of implementation of the proposed activities of the program.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended for management to:

1.	Revert the unexpended balance of the BuB to the source agency; or

2.	Explore the legal possibility for the full implementation of the project by making adequate representation with the source agency.
observations.managementAction: Management coordinated with the source agency and they are in the process of implementation of the remaining unspent balance of the program.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management (1) revisit and continue implementing the measures contained in the Municipality’s 10-Year SWM Plan, (2) fast tract the implementation of SLF and (3) continue adopting such measures to comply with the mandate of the law.
observations.managementAction: Management has started with the construction of their SLF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for management through the property disposal committee to (1) conduct inventory and appraise the value of the unserviceable properties; and (2) immediately dispose the same in accordance with existing regulation.
observations.managementAction: Management has prepared report on the inventory of unserviceable properties and in the process of preparing other required documents for COA inspection.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management (1) to adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-003 and be cautious in spending government funds and (2) refund the excess amount paid for the conduct of training.
observations.managementAction: Management explained the nature of the transaction and justified that clerical errors were made on the DV and attachments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for management to fast tract the implementation of all on – going and not yet started programmed and funded projects under the 20% Development Fund to optimize utilization thereof and timely brings the desired benefits to the intended beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: Management was able to implement all remaining prior year 20% projects
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 15, page 51, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for management to fast tract the implementation of all on – going and not yet started programmed and funded projects under the LDRRM Fund to optimize utilization thereof and strengthen its disaster risk reduction and management system.
observations.managementAction: Management has partially implemented the remaining PPAs under the LDRRMF of CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management continue to reinforce efforts on the collection of audit disallowances as required under Sections 5.4 and 7.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and necessary documentations on the actions of the Municipality must be complied, copy furnish the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: Management has partially enforced collections of audit disallowances.
Management has complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management continue the practice of depositing collections regularly to comply with the rules and regulations as mandated by Section 69 of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 32 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I on collections and deposits.
observations.managementAction: Management has complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accountable Officer continue the proper and regular monitoring of the accountable forms so that information as to the receipts, issuances and balances in her custody can be readily determined through preparation of the Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF).
observations.managementAction: Management has complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accountable Officer monitor the proper use of the petty cash so that such fund will not be abused. Also, to avoid use of personal money, the AO may request replenishment of the cash advance when its disbursements reach at least 75% or as the need requires.
observations.managementAction: Management observed the proper use of petty cash and has complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Accountable Officer:

a.	Restitute the cash shortage amounting to P499.45;
b.	Stop the unsound practices of handling cash; and
c.	Comply with the aforementioned provisions of P.D. No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The CDO has submitted liquidation report and justification on the cash shortage in her cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for management to submit to DOH the liquidation papers pertaining to the amount of P742,000.31 already adjusted in the books of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the liquidation reports to DOH.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management prepare and submit its CY 2017 Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation (AAPSI) within the period prescribed by the law using the prescribed form under COA Memorandum No. 2014 – 002 dated March 18, 2014. It is further recommended that a focal person be designated by the Local Chief Executive who will consolidate the report before submission to the audit team.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the submission of AAPSI.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the: 

(a) Municipal Treasurer to update the RPTAR and submit a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to the Accounting Unit  
to be used as basis in setting up RPT/SET receivable as required by Section 20 of Manual on NGAS for LGUs; and 



(b) Municipal Accountant to observe the aforementioned provisions as the basis in recording the RPT/SET receivables at the beginning of the year.
observations.managementAction: The LCE instructed the OIC-Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Accountant to comply with the audit recommend-dations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management establish effective measures to improve cash position in order to transfer unexpended balances of Local Disaster Risk Reduction Fund to a special trust fund to ensure its availability during times of disaster/ calamity.
observations.managementAction: The CY 2014 balance of LDRRMF was already transferred to special trust fund while the remaining years’ balance shall follow.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the OIC- Municipal Accountant to: 

(a) investigate the occurrence of negative/debit balances in the liability accounts and make the necessary adjustments in the books; and 

(b) refund the source agency or remit to Bureau of Treasury the unexpended balances of transferred funds/completed projects as prescribed by COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994
observations.managementAction: The LCE instructed the OIC-Municipal Accountant to comply with the audit recommend-dations.

observations.recommendation: See observation text for recommendation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has already created Inventory Committee and will comply with the recommend-dations of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to make a detailed review of the two reciprocal accounts and determine the cause of the unreconciled amounts. Thereafter, make the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to present the correct balances of the affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant is still in the process of tracing records of the two reciprocal accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipal officials continue to adminster the funds properly and continue to limit expenditures to the essential operating needs of the municipality in order to further reduce and eventually eliminate cash deficit.
observations.managementAction: The reflected deficit of the Municipality is continuously reducing and the Management ensured that the municipal officials will administer the funds properly.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations that the Local Chief Executive instruct the OIC- Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer to:
 
(a) make concerted effort to determine the causes of delay in the submission of accounts and plan and establish an effective measure on how to resolve it; and



(b) expedite the submission of still unsubmitted accounts such as official receipts, disbursement vouchers, payrolls, trial balance and supporting schedules to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: The Management already took the necessary action on this matter through issuance of Memorandum to the OIC-Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to provide justification on the delayed submission of SAAOB covering the period CY 2018, henceforth, ensure that the said report are submitted within 60 days after the 31st of December of each year as required by COA Accounting Circular Letter No. 2007-002.
observations.managementAction: The LCE thru HRMO issued Memorandum to Municipal Budget Officer to provide justification on the delayed submission of SAAOB.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the MPDC:

 (a) refrain from including expenditure items that are not allowed to be charged against the Development Fund (DF); and


(b) optimally utilize budget for Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: The LCE instructed the MPDO to comply with the recommend-dations of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and Local School Board (LSB):

(a) judiciously prepare SEF budget according to the needs of the stakeholders and consider the reprogramming/ realignment of unutilized funds so that more projects could be implemented by the Municipality for the benefits of the intended users; and

(b) comply with the reportorial requirements of DepEd, DBM, and DILG Joint Circular No.1, s. 2017, dated January 19, 2017
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was able to maximized its budget for SEF for CY 2019 with utilization rate of 99.50% while the reportorial requirements for SEF were already complied with.
The LCE and LDRRMO agreed to monitor the implementation of all PPAs in the LDRRMP in order to strengthen the disaster and mitigation measures of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: See observation text for recommendation.
observations.managementAction: The Management, MENRO and other concerned officials agreed to prioritize the establishment of MRF and implement the Ten-Year SWM plan of Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

(a) instruct the Municipal Treasurers Office to regularly conduct examination on the books of accounts of business establishments to determine the truthfulness of the declared gross sales/receipts used in the computation of business taxes paid and to collect the correct taxes as provided under Section 171 of R.A.7160;

(b) assign personnel at the MTO who are equip with the necessary knowledge and training in the examination of books of accounts to enable said Office in carrying on with their mandate efficiently; and



(c) instruct the BPLO to require taxpayers to submit Sworn Statement of Gross Receipts or Sales and Certified Copy of Income Tax Return to verify or assess the correct tax base to be used as provided by Section 2C.04 ( d) and (e) of the Revised Revenue Code of the Municipality of Quezon, Nueva Ecija.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer’s Office collected the tax deficiency of one business taxpayer and will implement Section 171 of RA 7160 and Section 2C.04 of the Revised Revenue Code of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: See observation text for recommendation.
observations.managementAction: The LCE instructed the GAD Focal Person to implement the recommend-dations of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee to submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team, and the GSO-Designate to prepare and maintain the property cards and reconcile the same with the PPELCs of the Accounting Office. Furthermore, observe the provisions of Section 124 of the MNGAS, Volume I on the prescribed timeline in the submission of RPCPPE to allow the Audit Team the necessary verification and reconciliation.
observations.managementAction: Physical count is to be conducted by the Municipality. Reconciliation of property cards with PPELC is still on-going. Ongoing.
observations.reason: There is no RPCPPE submitted to the Audit Team since reconciliation of property cards with PPELC is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to accelerate and exert more effort on the on-going general revision of real properties in order to provide the Municipal Treasurer the complete and updated Assessment Roll; and require the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Municipal Accountant the certified list of taxpayers showing their names and amounts due and collectible for the year so that the RPT/SET Receivables can be adjusted to reflect the correct balances as of December 31, 2019.
observations.managementAction: The General Revision was completed. Partial Assessment Rolls were forwarded to the Municipal Treasurer.
observations.reason: The Municipal Assessor was still in the process of completing the general revisions of the Assessment Roll.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: a. revert those account whose owners/creditors replied but was not able to submit, upon request, the required legal documents pertaining to their claim and secure from the SB a resolution with a condition that appropriation shall be made in the event that they were able to provide those documents; and b. continue to exert effort to locate other owners/creditor and confirm the account and request them to submit documents pertaining to their claims to the Municipality. If creditors will not be able to support their claims, revert the account to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund, subject to an authority from the SB through a Resolution with a condition that appropriation shall be made in the event that a claim arises in the future pursuant to the provisions of Section 98 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Some of the accounts were disbursed during the year. Confirmation with the creditors is still on-going.
observations.reason: Confirmation with creditors is still in process resulting to inability to revert the said amount to the Unappropriated Surplus.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to continue her efforts in providing the details/breakdown of the accounts and remit the same to the aforementioned agencies so that member beneficiaries may avail of the privileges and benefits due them.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant committed to continue efforts in reconciling the balances of the accounts and promised to remit the same to its respective agencies.
observations.reason: See Observation No. 4, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to immediately settle his outstanding cash advance. Otherwise, effect the withholding of her salary in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002. Henceforth, see to it that all cash advances are liquidated at the end of each year.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer was able to retrieve, compile supporting documents and submit the liquidation before the year ended.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the General Services Officer-Designate to expedite the compliance with the recommendation of the GSIS pertaining to the assessment of the insurable properties and the provision of its insurance.
observations.managementAction: The GSO-Designate is amenable with the audit recommendation. They are already preparing the required documents for the property assessment.
observations.reason: Toyota Fortuner and Municipal Ambulance totaling P2,366,000.00, were both covered by GSIS insurance valid until April 2020 but other insurable properties were still for completion and submission of the legal documents required for the application and evaluation of the said properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor to: a. Fast-track the titling of the three lots whose titling process were already commenced in CY 2018, and b. Make all the necessary actions/representations with the DENR and RD in the titling of the other 11 lots.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor is amenable with the audit recommendation and commented that the search for supporting documents relative to the titling of the lands is ongoing.
observations.reason: One of the three lots was titled in the name of the Municipality. Legal documents pertaining to six lots were presented to the Audit Team but for the other five lots, documents are still being located.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the negotiating committee to expedite the negotiation with the lot owners to resolve the right-of-way issue so that the said project will be implemented for the benefit of the constituents.
observations.managementAction: A committee was formed to closely monitor and negotiate the matter with the affected residents of the barangay. Legal documents were requested from them to support their claim of ownership. However, no proof of ownership was presented as of year-end.
observations.reason: See Observation No. 7, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Agriculturist to resolve the issues pertaining to the implementation of the project under her Office. Furthermore, require the Municipal Treasurer to remit the unexpended balances to its Source Agencies/National Treasury to enable the government to utilize the excess funds for other vital/essential projects/programs.
observations.managementAction: Some of the remitted balances were the following: Fishing Gears and Paraphernalia (BFAR) – 1,400.00; BHW Workshop (DOH) – 12,330.00; Core Local Road (LGSF) – 215.99. The PPAs included were implemented and the LDRRMF CY 2019 was optimally utilized.
observations.reason: Implementation of some projects are still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the head of Municipal Planning and Development Office to ensure the efficient and effective utilization of the 20 percent Development Fund which is primarily intended to fund projects that shall contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes.
observations.managementAction: Construction and Rehabilitation of Shoreline Erosion Control/Seawall Protection and Construction of Sanitary Landfill were implemented and completed during 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC–MDRRMO and the Chairman and Members of the Bids and Awards Committee to prioritize and optimally utilize the LDRRMF by setting definite procurement timelines of its PPAs to strengthen the capacity of the Municipality for disaster risk reduction and management.
observations.managementAction: The PPAs included in the LDRRMF IP were implemented and the LDRRMF CY 2019 was optimally utilized.
observations.reason: Implementation of some projects are ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC–MPDC to expedite the establishment of CBMS that will create a GAD Database that will assist the gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Accomplishment for Module 2 was 86.40% (3165/3663 households) as of December 2019. CBMS is ongoing, headed by the Municipal Mayor and MDRRMO-Designate as the Chairman and Vice Chairman of CBMS TWG respectively.
observations.reason: CBMS was not yet complete and the GAD database was still not established.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MENRO-Designate to work on securing all the requirements needed for the issuance of the ECC and establishment of the proposed Sanitary Landfill and the safe closure of the open dumpsite pursuant to RA No. 9003. We also recommended that the Municipality, through the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, secure the approval of its 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan from the National Solid Waste Management Commission.
observations.managementAction: Construction of Sanitary Landfill in Brgy. Villa Mercedes was completed. Safe closure of existing disposal facility (open dumpsite) is ongoing.
observations.reason: The Municipality was unable to secure the ECC and still in the process of conversion of its open dumpsite into a controlled dumpsite.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor: a. instruct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that only approved RLRC be used in collecting local taxes from the taxpayers; b. continue the granting of tax credits due the concerned taxpayers; and c. coordinate with the SB in ensuring that all necessary steps and requirements particularly the required review of the SP were undertaken prior to the implementation of any ordinance or revision of the Local Revenue Code of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: LRC TWG convened to discuss the proposed revision of LRC. The proposed LRC was already endorsed to the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) for review and approval.
observations.reason: The proposed LRC is still being reviewed by the SB.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. HRMO to prioritize the updating of the salary adjustments or service records of the employees in the GSIS records; and b. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to prioritize the remittance of the amount of P1,203,581.90 to the GSIS upon reconciliation/updating of the employee records.
observations.managementAction: Some of the new employees/officials were already enrolled as members. The HRMO continues to coordinate for the remittance of the said contributions.
observations.reason: See Observation No. 20, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer exhaust all possible means to locate and secure the documents to support the unrecorded disbursements and immediately transmit the documents to the Municipal Accountant to facilitate recording thereof in the book of accountants and subsequent submission to the Audit Team. 
We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant give the details of the said disbursement as reflected in the Bank Reconciliation Statements and forward a certified copy of the cleared checks to the Audit Team for our reference audit.
observations.managementAction: Obliged the DO to submit to MT the said disbursement vouchers.
observations.reason: DO was already retired last April 1, 2019.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer send written notice to the concerned payees at least one month before a check becomes stale. We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer verify in the Check Registers the recipients/signatory of those checks when released by their Office, otherwise, make the necessary adjustments if the same were unreleased checks. 
Moreover, we advised that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjustments in the books for the long outstanding/stale checks in accordance with Section 59 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Issued letter to claimant /payees.
observations.reason: No management comment was submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer obtain the necessary details of the transactions like Memorandum of Agreement, bank credit memoranda, and related communications from the source of the fund and forward the same to the Municipal Accountant to facilitate recording in the books.
observations.managementAction: Obtained the necessary details of transaction  MOA, Bank Credits Memo
observations.reason: Over looked by the assigned personnel

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer maintain the prescribed cashbook, keep it updated and reconcile her cashbook against the Subsidiary Ledgers of the Municipal Accountant for both the Cash in Local Treasury and Cash in Bank accounts every end of the month. 
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant furnish the Municipal Treasurer with the copies of Subsidiary Ledgers ], Bank Statements and Bank Reconciliation Statements for her reference in reconciling her cashbooks with accounting records, and issue a certification on the balances of all Accountable Officers who maintain a cashbook for their reference in its reconciliation against the SLs.
observations.managementAction:
Started updating and reconciling of all cashbooks with the   subsidiary ledgers of the Municipal Accountant.
The Municipal Accountant furnished the Municipal Treasurer with copies of SL’s and Bank Statements and Bank Recon Statements as reference for reconciliation.
observations.reason: Reconciliation conducted and review/verification of previews records of Treasury still on going to ascertain correctness of Cash Book Balances

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor thru the Municipal Accountant send demand letter to concerned Accountable Officers, who are still connected with the Municipality, to liquidate their cash advances and henceforth strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor appoint another DO to unload and help the current DO.
observations.managementAction: Demand letter was sent to concerned accountable officers.
observations.reason: Some of the accountable officers were no longer connected with the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the previous DO to reconcile her accountabilities against the records of the Municipal Accountant and settle any remaining balances, otherwise file a case against the DO, if warranted. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant send demand letter to the former DO.
We also recommended that the Administrative Officer II of the accounting office submit the reconciliation report and the DO transmit a certified copy of her cashbook to the Audit Team for reference in audit.
observations.managementAction: Issued memorandum letter to previous Disbursing Officer to reconcile the accountabilities against the records of the Municipal Accountant
observations.reason: The Disbursing Officer filed a leave of absence several months until she retired on April 1, 2019

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the:
a. GSO and the Municipal Engineer complete the conduct of physical inventory count of the remaining PPE of the Municipality and submit the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment in the prescribed form of acquisition costs and total;
b. GSO and the Municipal Accountant reconcile the Report on the Physical Count of PPE, Property Cards, Property Ledger Cards, ad accounting record, taking into consideration the noted deficiencies in audit, and furnish the Audit Team with the said report; and
c. GSO submit an Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) with transportation equipment’s checklist, pictures and in-house appraisal values.
observations.managementAction: The GSO submitted the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) with transportation equipment’s checklist, pictures and in-house appraisal values.
observations.reason:
Availability of the Responsible Person to take charge of the implementation.
Lack of manpower to focus on the reconciliation.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the management facilitate the transfer of the title of the real properties under the name of the Municipal Government of Quezon to prevent future dispute of ownership.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain the Real Property Ledger Cards for all the real properties of the Municipality with complete details to determine any difference between the records of the Municipal Assessor and financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
Allocated funds for the transfer of title.
Issued special patent for lots allocated to the LGU.
Directed the Municipal Accountant to maintain RPLC.
observations.reason: Processing of all kinds of patent still suspended in this municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant recognize in the books the Local Roads inventoried by the Municipal Engineer and adequately disclose to Road Network System in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Required the Municipal Engr. to submit inventory of local roads.
observations.reason: The Inventory of local roads submitted lacked some details.

observations.managementAction:
The environment and tourist fees were removed from the Annual Budget. 
Utilization report was submitted for monitoring purposes.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the HRMO:
a. Maintain a hand/written copy of PICLA for each permanent/regular employee ad update/record leave credits and its availment on a monthly basis. 
b. Exercise prudence in the posting and computation of entries in the PICLA; and 
c. Prepare summary of leave credit balances of all employees at the end of the year duly attested by each employee and post it in bulletin boards for their reference.
observations.managementAction:
Procured of 1 Digital Scanner
Leave forms already digitized.
Attached Digital leave form to individual PICLA excel from
Prepared leave credit balances at  yeared.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Market Committee facilitate the execution of a Contract of Lease between the Municipality and stallholders to be the basis in the imposition and collection of fees. 
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct both the Municipal Administrator and the Market Supervisor to implement the audit recommendations embodied in the CY 2017 Annual Audit Report within 60 calendar days from receipt hereof, otherwise consider appointing a new Market Supervisor.
observations.managementAction: Conducted Market Committee Meeting to discuss the implementation of having a Contract of Lease between the Local Government Unit of Quezon and the stallholders or vendors.
observations.reason: Management commented they will implement the audit recommendation this year 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GFPS submit the Accomplishment Report of CY 2018 together with the actions taken on the review note of the DILG on the AR of CY 2016
observations.managementAction: Submitted of GAD Accomplishment Report for CY 2018 to COA thru the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to require the Local Accountant to adjust immediately the reconciling items found in the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statement such as bank interest and tax, unrecorded disbursement and deposits for this ensures prompt detection of discrepancies, the parties at fault, information of their existence and timely identification and application of proper action/remedy to correct them. Also, confirm to the Local Treasurer the status of the checks if still outstanding over six months and prepare appropriate adjusting entries to correct the same. In addition, record deposits only when actually deposited and not as when collected to accurately account the actual cash in bank and on hand. Further, prepare adjusting entry to reclassify the Cash in Bank- Local Currency Time Deposit account to Cash in Bank- Local Currency Savings account.
Require the Local Treasurer to prepare checks only when funds are available so as not to incur bank overdraft and possible bank penalties for bouncing checks.
observations.managementAction: Adjustments through Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) were prepared for all reconciling items found in the BRS. Outstanding checks  over six months were confirmed to the local treasurer and adjustment were made. JEV was prepared for the reclassification of deposit from time to saving deposit maintained by the LGU at DPB Malaybalay City. Municipal treasurer was informed already to issue checks when there is available funds to avoid bouching check in future transaction.
observations.reason: There were still deposit in transit improperly recorded as of December 31, 2019. Hence, reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to require the Municipal Treasurer and Liquidating Officer to diligently monitor the remittance of cash collections received by the collecting officers. Collections should be cut-off at the end of the month to report collections made for the period. Also, require the accountable officers to submit monthly individual RAAFs as basis in the preparation of monthly Consolidated RAAF. Review the RAAF and CRAAF if the data reflected are correct and without errors before submission to COA. Moreover, the Municipal Treasurer should verify and sign the Report of Collections and Deposits prepared by the Liquidating Officer to establish a good internal control for cash management.
observations.managementAction: All Accountable Officers have remitted their collections daily, closing of collection monthly is observed and submissions of RAAF were also observed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management to direct the Inventory Committee and the concerned offices to exert extra efforts in reconciling the reports per physical inventory, per GSO and per accounting records. 

Expedite the disposition of all unserviceable and obsolete properties for the municipality to avoid further deterioration and to generate additional income from their disposal. Specifically, the Management should create Appraisal and Disposal Committees to undertake the appraisal and disposition of these properties in accordance to COA Circular No. 89-296 dated January 27, 1989 and PD 1445. Moreover, a request for audit action should be submitted to COA audit team before the conduct of proper disposal activity. The reports prepared should be furnished to the Municipal Accountant who shall prepare journal vouchers to drop the properties from the books of account.
	
The General Services Office to continue establishing good practices on supply and property management by extending more efforts to locate missing properties, by keeping updated records for property accountabilities and by demanding turn-over of either unserviceable properties from present accountable officers (AOs) or unused properties from resigned/retired/separated accountable officers.

Further, direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the “unidentified beginning balances” subsidiary ledgers of PPE accounts to establish reliability and accuracy of accounts reflected in financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of all property, plant and equipment per inventory from GSO and Accounting records.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of all property, plant and equipment per inventory from GSO and Accounting records were still not completed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to create detailed subsidiary ledger to support payable accounts to comply with procedural guidelines under Section 114 of PD 1445. Also, prepare aging schedule on a periodic basis to facilitate monitoring and evaluation of the accounts, indicating the status thereof. 

Likewise, the Municipal Accountant is further advised to exercise caution in certifying existence of obligation and make periodic review of the outstanding payables.
observations.managementAction: Created detailed Subsidiary Ledger to support payable accounts of the LGU. The office continually exercising caution in certifying existence of obligations and made review and aging of outstanding payables.
