observations.recommendation: We recommended for management to continuously comply with all regulations pertaining to GAD advocacies.
observations.managementAction: Management consistently complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management revisit the Municipality’s 10-Year SWM Plan and prioritize the activities as planned.  This shall be reviewed and updated every year by the municipal solid waste management board as provided under Section 16 of Republic Act No.  9003.
observations.managementAction: Management had started the bidding process for the construction of the SLF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management make representations with the Sangguniang Bayan to prioritize the passage into ordinance of the updated local revenue code of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: The SB is updating the revenue code and the same is on its third and final reading of the legislation process.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the management:

a.	Be more circumspect in the granting of clothing allowance by strictly adhering to regulations; and 

b.	For the payees to refund the amount erroneously paid.
observations.managementAction: The payees filed their appeal, hence the lifting of the disallowance per COA RO2 Decisions Nos. 2019-08 and 2019-10.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management require the submission of accomplishment reports and other necessary documents as requisites of payment to casual/contractual personnel as required by regulation.
observations.managementAction: The needed documentary requirements were provided.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

a.	The Municipal Accountant drop the invalid payables recorded in the books; and
b.	Ensure that only valid obligations are recognized in the books.
observations.managementAction: Management dropped the invalid payables in the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require all concerned officials to comply with the monthly submission of Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the format prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the monthly submission of the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended:

a.	For the Accounting Office and the Budget Office to reconcile the balances of the unexpended balances of LDRRMF – Special Trust Fund in their respective books; and
b.	That management revert the unexpended balance of CYs 2011 and 2012 appropriations back to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund for subsequent re-appropriation by the municipality for other social services.
observations.managementAction: Management averred that the balances of the LDRRMF in the Accounting and Budget Office were already reconciled.
observations.reason: The reconciliation working papers were not presented to the auditor for  validation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Accounting Department to withhold appropriate taxes due for every payment made by the municipality and remit the same to the BIR within the prescribed period and strictly comply with the above cited regulation.
observations.managementAction: Management withheld the required taxes as recommended.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the management: 
1.  Cause the refund of the excess payment of anniversary bonus erroneously paid; and
2.  Adhere to the conditions set forth under the regulation in the granting of anniversary bonus.
observations.managementAction: COA RO2 Decision No. 2018-38 upheld the disallowance, hence management started partial collection from the persons liable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management prepare and submit its CY 2016 Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation (AAPSI) within the period prescribed by the law using the prescribed form under COA Memorandum No. 2014 - 002 dated March 18, 2014. It is further recommended that a focal person be designated by the Local Chief Executive who will consolidate the report before submission to the audit team.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the AAPSI.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for management to fast tract the implementation of all on – going and not yet started programmed and funded projects under the 20% Development Fund to optimize utilization thereof and timely brings the desired benefits to the intended beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: Management fast tracked the implementation of projects not yet completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management observe the abovementioned provisions of Local Budget Circular No. 98 on Personal Services (PS) limitation on Local Government Budgets to avoid audit disallowances.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the PS Cap limitation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management reinforce efforts on the collection of audit disallowances as required under Sections 7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006.
observations.managementAction: Management enforced the collections of disallowances and partially collected 18.24% in the CY2017 balance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for management to fast tract the implementation of all on – going and not yet started programmed and funded projects under the LDRRM Fund to optimize utilization thereof and strengthen its disaster risk reduction and management system.
observations.managementAction: Management fast tracked the implementation of projects not yet completed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:

(a) create an Inventory Committee who will conduct physical count of properties of the Municipality; 


(b) submit Report on Physical Count of Plant, Property and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Office of the Auditor; and 

(c) instruct Municipal Treasurer to maintain property cards, Property Acknowledgement Receipts and inventory tags.
observations.managementAction: Observation No. 1, AAR CY 2017 | The management already instructed the concerned personnel to create the inventory committee who will be in charge in conducting physical count of properties of the Municipality.  The Municipal Treasurer will also maintain property cards, property acknowledgment receipt and inventory tags.
observations.reason:
RPCPPE was not submitted for CY 2018.


Property cards, PAR and inventory tags were not maintained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct municipal officials to administer the funds properly and to continue to limit expenditures to the essential operating needs of the municipality in order to reduce and eventually eliminate cash deficit.
observations.managementAction:
Observation No. 4, AAR 

CY 2017 | The Management 
will ensure that the Municipal officials will administer the funds properly and to continue to limit expenditures to the essential operating needs of the Municipality.
observations.reason: The Municipality still continue to incur overdraft/ deficit but the amount already decreased to ₱17.57 million as of December 31, 2018.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation and the  Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Accountant to:

(a) make concerted effort to determine the causes of delay in the submission of accounts and plan and establish an effective measure on how to resolve it; 

(b) submit explanation to the Office of the Auditor on why the submission was delayed and incomplete; and 

(c) expedite the immediate submission of still unsubmitted accounts such as the official receipts, disbursement vouchers and payrolls to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: Observation No. 5, AAR CY 2017 | The Management took the necessary action on the matter through issuance of Memorandum to the OIC-Municipal Accountant and ICO-Municipal Treasurer to ensure on timely submission of accounts.
observations.reason: The Municipality was still delayed in the submission of accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the MPDC to:

(a) refrain from including expenditure items that are not allowed to be charged against the Development Fund (DF); and

(b) comply with the reportorial requirements of DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1, dated February 22, 2017.
observations.managementAction: Observation No. 6, AAR CY 2017 | The LCE instructed the MPDC to refrain from including expenditures items that are not allowed to be charged against the Development Fund.
observations.reason: The budget for 20% Development Fund for CY 2018 still includes expenditures not allowed to be charged against the Development Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the LDRRMO to: 

carefully consider the 
LDRRMP in the appropriation of the LDRRMF; 

(b) strictly monitor the implementation of all program/projects/activities in the LDRRMP in order to strengthen the disaster and mitigation measures of the Municipality which will be beneficial in times of calamities; and

(c) comply with the reportorial requirements of JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.
observations.managementAction:
Observation No. 8, AAR CY 2017 | The Management took the necessary actions on this 
matter and instructed the OIC-LDRRMO to consider the LDRRMP in the appropriation of the LDRRMF.
observations.reason:
Several projects for LDRRMF also remained unimplemented for CY 2018.



No monthly and nor annual report on utilization were submitted to concerned agencies.

observations.managementAction: Observation No. 9, AAR CY 2017 | The Municipality reconstituted the MSWMB to include representatives from local environment NGO and recycling industry.  The Municipality also completed the safe closure and rehabilitation plan of dumpsite. The management continued the monitoring of the management of collection of solid waste and reiterated request to Environmental Management Bureau for the funding of construction of Municipal MRF.
observations.reason: Municipal MRF was not established and recycling and composting activities were not undertaken as to date.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to:

(a) update and post notice of delinquency for property taxes at the main entrance of the municipal building and in publicly accessible and conspicuous place in each barangay where these properties are located; 

seek assistance from the concerned barangays for the issuance of Notice of Delinquency and to conduct an information dissemination regarding the importance of paying taxes promptly and the consequences of non-payment of taxes; if warranted, enforced the 
provision of Sections 258 and 260 of the RA 7160 in the collection of delinquent taxes; and

to impose the 25% surcharge on rentals not paid on time, revocation of lease contract and the stall be declared vacant in accordance with Section 2B. 03. of Kautusang Bayan Blg. 04, series of 2002 and direct the Market and Water System Supervisors to strictly monitor the implementation of the policies to improve collections.
observations.managementAction: Observation No. 10, AAR CY 2017 | Some employees of the Municipal Treasurer conducted tax campaign to different barangays to collect delinquent taxes.
observations.reason: The Office of the Municipal Treasurer conducted tax campaign with the help of the officials of the barangay thereby decreasing the tax delinquency for RPT.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Council for Protection of Children (LCPC) agreed to:

(a) prepare an Annual Work and Financial Plan for LCPC to ensure prioritization of PPAs according to the needs of the children; and 

(b) present the PPAs implemented in the Accomplishment Report of LCPC.
observations.managementAction: Observation No. 12, AAR CY 2017 | To prepare separate accomplishment for LCPC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to exert efforts in providing the details/breakdown of said accounts and coordinate with the affected agencies to determine measures on how they will be able to remit the said balances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was able to adjust/remit to the Bureau of Internal Revenue P120,728.14 due to the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS).
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant is still on the process of providing the details/breakdown of the remaining balances from GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth mentioned in this observation. (Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 4, Page No. 26)

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to confirm from the account owners/creditors and to request them to submit documents pertaining to their claims to the Municipality. In the event that they fail to support their claims, revert the account to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund, subject to an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan through a Resolution with a condition that appropriation shall be made in the event that claim arises in the future pursuant to the provisions of Section 98 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was able to disburse/adjust some of the accounts payable in CY 2018.
observations.reason: Confirmation with the owners/creditors/employees is still on-going. (Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 3, Page No. 25)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to revert the unutilized balance of the DRRMF for the year 2012 amounting to P179,954.35 to the General Fund pursuant to the above-cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: The unutilized balance mentioned in this observation was already reverted to the General Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor oblige the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries for fair presentation of the accounts and avoid errors to maintain the accuracy and reliability of the accounts.
observations.managementAction: Adjustments were already made in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to demand all Municipal Collectors to submit the RCDs and remit their collections for all official receipts issued on a daily basis to her or to the Liquidating Officer.
observations.managementAction: All collectors of the Municipality was able to submit the RCDs and remit their collections for all official receipts issued on a daily basis to her or to the Liquidating Officer.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to update the entries in her cashbooks on a daily basis to ensure that the daily balances of her accountability are correctly stated. We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records on cash accounts at least every end of the quarter to ensure the reliability of cash accounts balances.
observations.managementAction: The cashbooks of the Municipal Treasurer are already updated. Also, the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant already reconciled their records on cash accounts at least every end of the quarter.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to remit the unexpended balances totaling P119,598.32 to its Source Agencies/National Treasury to enable the government to utilize the said funds for other vital projects/programs.
observations.managementAction: Of the aforementioned amount, P113,882.33 was already remitted/adjusted.
observations.reason: Additional unexpended amount that were not remitted totaling P85,048.61 were noted in CY 2018. (Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 10, Page No. 32)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MENRO-Designate to work for the conversion of the open dumpsite to controlled dumps and eventually to a Sanitary Landfill (SLF) and the permanent closure of the open dumpsite.
observations.managementAction: The MENRO-Designate already coordinated with the Environmental Management Bureau and was able to secure funds for the safe closure of the open dumpsite in CY 2018. They were also able to identify a new location for the sanitary landfill in Barangay Villa Mercedes and submitted its proposal for geological assessment.
observations.reason: In the Report on the Geological Assessment of the sanitary landfill, the Municipality still needs to comply with various conditions and/or mitigations for the issuance of ECC and establishment of the proposed SLF. (Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 14, Page No. 36)

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the LDRRMO-Designate to submit to the Audit Team the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: The monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF was submitted to the Audit Team in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor make representation with the lot owners to resolve the right-of-way issue so that the said project will be implemented for the benefit of the constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor was able to make initial representation with the lot owners. Negotiations are still being conducted.
observations.reason: The right-of-way issue remain unresolved despite representation made. Negotiations are still being conducted. (Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 8, Page No. 30)

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Assessor, Municipal Engineer and General Services Officer to conduct the physical inventory count of the PPE and furnish the Audit Team with the said report.
observations.managementAction: The Management continued soliciting the assistance of the Assessor’s Office and the Municipal Engineering Office to provide the condition, listing of Buildings, Land and Road Networks.
observations.reason: Incomplete data gathered due to lack of basis such as land titles and records of Municipal Assessor’s Office and the Municipal Engineering Office

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the GSO and Municipal Accountant reconcile the Report on the Physical Count of PPE, with Property Cards, Property Ledger Cards, and accounting records, taking into consideration the noted deficiencies in audit. We also recommended the GSO create a database of all properties owned by the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Continued Municipal Accounting Office tasked to reconcile the Inventory Records. The GSO to provide the needed assistance.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower specifically tasked to reconcile the records.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Management facilitate the transfer of title of the real properties under the name of the Municipal Government of Quezon to prevent future dispute of ownership. We also recommended the Municipal Accountant maintain the Real Property Ledger Cards for all the real properties of the Municipality with complete details to determine any difference between the records of the Municipal Assessor and financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Retrieved documents of the LGU acquired/owned real properties. Upon retrieval, facilitated the transfer under the name of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Suspension of issuance of patent in the entire Province of Palawan.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer immediately transmit the identified unrecorded disbursements to the Municipal Accountant to facilitate recording thereof in the book of accounts. We recommended the Municipal Accountant to identify the payees and make the necessary entries to record and adjust the Cash-in-bank account.
observations.managementAction: Not implemented. Documents cannot be located despite all efforts of the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to send a written notice to the concerned payees at least one month before a check becomes stale. Moreover, we required the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books for the long outstanding/stale checks in accordance with Section 59 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Reviewed the Bank Statements and Bank reconciliation.
observations.reason: Only two checks left for identification of stale checks.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation to the Management to strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002. We also reiterated our recommendation to the Municipal Accountant to: a. Discontinue the practice of partial liquidation of cash advances and direct the concerned accountable officers to settle their accounts immediately especially for the Special Disbursing Officers; and b. Prospectively, return to the DO/AO all LR/RDs that were not included in the cash advance list.
observations.managementAction: Served written notice to the concerned accountable officers as mandated by Section 7 of COA Circular No. 2012-004. Settlement of the accountable officer’s account is hereby enjoined within 15 days upon receipt hereof.
observations.reason: Not Late submission of liquidation reports by the accountable officers.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant serve a written notice to the concerned accountable officers on their outstanding cash advances as mandated by Section 7 of COA Circular No. 2012-004. We also recommended the Municipal Mayor to suspend the salaries of the concerned accountable officers upon failure to settle their obligations.
observations.managementAction: Served written notice to the accountable officers. Settlement of the accountable officer’s account is hereby enjoined within 15 days upon receipt hereof.
observations.reason: There was late submission of liquidation reports by the accountable officers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management secure a copy of the mentioned schedules. The Municipal Engineer should conduct an ocular inspection of the projects and submit a copy of the report to the Municipal Accountant for recording thereof in the books. We recommended the Municipal Mayor to submit the necessary documents of the Agreement to the Audit Team for our reference in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer conducted an ocular inspection of the roads and streets within the jurisdiction of the Municipality.
observations.reason: No document was provided by PGP despite written request of the Municipality. Reiterated in Observation No. 7.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to discontinue and reprogram the identified PPAs totaling ₱3,893,476.78 for current and continuing appropriations into development PPAs allowed to be funded out of the 20% DF in harmony with Section 3.0 of the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017. Further, ensured that PPAs to be funded out of the 20% DF should be carefully planned and must partake the nature of capital expenditures.
observations.managementAction: Reviewed the PPAs under the 20% DF. Discontinued and reprogrammed the identified PPAs totaling ₱3,893,476.78 for current and continuing appropriations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the collectors to remit their collections within the prescribed period. We also recommended the withholding of the salary of the Accountable Officers (AO) and/or administrative sanction in case of failure to settle his/her accountabilities. We also recommended that all collectors should be under the direct supervision of the Municipal Treasurer and to settle their accounts within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: Issued Memorandum Order to RCCs/AOs to remit their collections daily or weekly in case of field collection in compliance with COA Circular.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to: a. Use the PS appropriations for compensation and benefits of permanent and regular employees; b. Fund and charge the salaries and wages of COS/JO against the MOOE in the approved budget; and c. Fill up the vacant positions and the COS/JO personnel, who meet the appropriate eligibility and qualification requirements, shall be given priority in the hiring by the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Prepared supplemental budget for the purpose.
observations.reason: The Municipality complied with the recommendation in the use of savings.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that the use of savings for augmentation under Section 336 of R.A. No. 7160 were covered by an ordinance authorizing the same and that the use of savings under Section 321 of R.A. No. 7160 were covered by an Appropriation Ordinance providing for a supplemental budget. Henceforth, strict observance of the provisions of the LGC of 1991 and its IRR.
observations.managementAction: Complied with the recommendation in the use of savings. The Municipal Budget Officer ensured that the use of savings for augmentation were covered by an Appropriation Ordinance providing for a supplemental budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to compel the real property owners to pay the undercollection, otherwise she will be held personally liable. The Municipal Treasurer should direct all collectors to strictly comply with the provisions of the LGC on the granting discount to real property owners. We recommended the Sangguniang Bayan to revisit and revise the Revenue Code to comply with the provisions of the LGC.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer sent communication to the Provincial Treasurer informing him of the AOM. The Municipal Treasurer will collect the discount given in CY 2019.
observations.reason: Not all taxpayers paid their real property taxes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of 50% of the Municipality’s Road Network System to facilitate the recording thereof in the books and of accounts as at year-end.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer conducted an ocular inspection within the jurisdiction of the Municipality.
observations.reason: There was an inventory report conducted but the same was prepared only in May 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to mandate the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) to immediately submit a complete list of all on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented for year 2017 and the ensuing year, containing all necessary information relative thereto, otherwise, he should be subject to disciplinary action in accordance with Section 4.0 of the same Circular.
observations.managementAction: Reviewed the PPAs. A separate accounting and reporting of the PPAs under the 20% DF was maintained and PPAs were submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor and the Sangguniang Bayan to setup all existing and newly created LEEs in accordance with DBM LBC No. 111 dated June 10, 2016. We also recommended the Municipal Accountant to maintain separate books for those LEEs and submit the financial reports to the Audit Team for evaluation. We recommended the Management to submit the Feasibility Study and 5-year Business Plan of those LEEs for our reference in audit.
observations.managementAction: A separate accounting and reporting of the Local Economic Enterprise was maintained by the Municipal Accountant. The feasibility study and business plan to be submitted.
observations.reason: The Municipality will enact an ordinance for the creation of Municipal Development Office to manage the economic enterprise.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Assessor assess all privately constructed stalls until the same has been acquired by the Municipality. The Municipal Treasurer should collect the applicable taxes due on those properties.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor to assess the privately constructed stalls. The Municipal Treasurer to collect the taxes. The Tax Declaration on improvements are subject for approval of the Provincial Assessor.
observations.reason: The municipal assessor will submit the tax declarations for approval by the Provincial Assessor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant to recognize the receivable and deferred revenue in the books. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Market Supervisor should update the subsidiary ledgers of the stallholders for monitoring purposes.
observations.managementAction: To be implemented. The Municipal Accountant to maintain separate books for LEEs and update subsidiary ledgers.
observations.reason: To be implemented in CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the immediate submission of the following duly authenticated documents: a. Amended SPLA or duly approved amendments to the SPLA; b. Program of Works and Detailed Estimates for the eight (8) water sources used as basis for the cost allocation in Annex 2 of the SPLA dated January 15, 2015; c. Revised/New Program of Works and Detailed Estimates for the six (6) water sources; and d. Documents signifying approval of the Municipal Development Council and the Sangguniang Bayan for the changes/amendments in water sources.
observations.managementAction: The engineering and project documents were submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Subject to verification of the submitted documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, together with the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), re-evaluate the procurement method adopted and the PGP’s qualification as servicing agency; review the terms of the Agency-to-Agency Agreement for necessary adjustments; and furnish the Auditor copies of documents required in Section 5 of Annex A of GPPB Resolution No. 18-2007 for evaluation of compliance thereto.
observations.managementAction: The documents were submitted to the Audit Team for review.
observations.reason: Subject to verification of the submitted documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to: a. re-evaluate the economic, financial and technical viability of the water supply system projects; b. Review the implementation and procurement methods adopted for the project and the terms of contracts entered in relation thereto; and c. Inquire on the legal implication and advisability of availing the remedy of contract termination for convenience as provided under Section III.B of Annex A of GPPB Resolution No. 018-2004 should this proved to be in the best interest of the MGQ. Likewise, submit the authority from the Office of the President to warrant the undertaking of the project by administration.
observations.managementAction: The documents were submitted to the Audit Team. The Municipal Mayor to re-evaluate and submit required authority from Office of the President.
observations.reason: Subject to verification of the submitted documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to submit the required documents in compliance with Annex “A” of the R-IRR of R.A. No. 9184. Likewise, submit the certification from the consultants/government officials who prepared and approved the detailed engineering that: a. the detailed engineering surveys and designs have been conducted according to the prescribed agency standards and specifications in conformance with Annex “A” of the R-IRR of R.A. No. 9184, and; b. the detailed engineering outputs are adequate for the procurement at hand.
observations.managementAction: The documents were submitted to the Audit Team in compliance with Annex A requirements.
observations.reason: Subject to verification of the submitted documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipal government, through the Municipal Mayor, ensure the guidelines for the establishment and operation of public utilities and local economic enterprises, as contained in DBM LBC No. 111 dated June 10, 2016, be strictly observed. Copies of the feasibility study and business plan should also be submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The guidelines were reviewed and copies of feasibility study and business plan were submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Subject to verification of the submitted documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor, together with the BAC and the Municipal Engineer, to render an explanation on the noted discrepancy on the cost of materials and the absence of detailed engineering duly prepared and approved by municipal officials. The Program of Work/Detailed Estimates corresponding to the ABC should likewise be submitted.
observations.managementAction: The documents were submitted to the Audit Team for explanation.
observations.reason: Subject to verification of the submitted documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the municipal government, through the Municipal Mayor, to immediately secure the required water permits from the NWRB and submit authenticated copies thereof to the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: The documents were submitted to the Audit Team indicating application for permits and supporting documents.
observations.reason: Subject to verification of the submitted documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the inclusion of the provision on liquidated damages and modification of the provisions on posting of performance security and warranty in the Agency-to-Agency Agreement in accordance with pertinent provisions in the R-IRR of R.A. No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The documents were submitted to the Audit Team for review and inclusion of necessary provisions.
observations.reason: Subject to verification of the submitted documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to render an explanation on the discrepancies in the project costs and submit the Program of Work/Detailed Estimates and other detailed engineering documents used as bases for SPLA, Agency-to-Agency Agreement and Addendum to the Agency-to-Agency Agreement.
observations.managementAction: The documents were submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Subject to verification of the submitted documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the immediate submission of printout copies of posting in the PhilGEPS and certification from the Head of the BAC Secretariat on posting at conspicuous place in the municipal government’s premises of the subject Notices of Award, and Notice to Proceed.
observations.managementAction: The documents were submitted to the Audit Team including printouts and certifications.
observations.reason: Subject to verification of the submitted documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to make the necessary adjustments in the books and transfer the remaining balance of ₱602,593.38 to the Trust Fund Calamity bank account.
observations.managementAction: The documents were submitted and adjustments were processed in the books preparing for transfer.
observations.reason: Subject to verification of the submitted documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to appoint the remaining two (2) support staff plantilla position in compliance with Section 12 of R.A. No. 10121.
observations.managementAction: The documents were submitted. The Municipal Mayor promoted the LDRRMO I personnel to Local DRRM Officer III in March 2019 leaving vacancies which the Municipal Mayor committed to fill and include appropriation in year 2020.
observations.reason: The positions were filled partially; subject to verification of the submitted documents.

observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent to accountable officers every quarter. Some cash advances were already collected and/or deducted in payroll. Cash advances for travel were granted individually. All cash advances granted were supported by fidelity bond and authority to cash advance from the LCE. Payments were made through checks payable to suppliers with claims above P2,000.00.
observations.reason: There were still unliquidated cash advances as of December 31, 2018. Hence, reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to request the Honorable Mayor to create an inventory Committee who will be responsible in the conduct of the physical inventory of the property, plant and equipment, and thereafter require the conduct of physical count and submission of the report annually. 

Require the GSO to maintain property cards per category of property, plant and equipment.

 In addition, compute for the depreciation to all agency assets that are subjected to depreciation.
observations.managementAction: Created the Inventory and Appraisal Committee through an Executive Order. Inventory Committee conducted physical inventory of PPEs.
observations.reason: Failed to reconcile with the accounting records the physical inventory reports. Hence, reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to require the Municipal Accountant to continue the reconciliation of land reported in lump amount and disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements necessary information on these real properties.

Require the Municipal Assessor to constantly follow up the status of the submitted documents (for titling of the real properties of the municipality at the Registry of Deeds and DENR provincial office at Malaybalay City to ensure titling of all the lands owned by the municipality in compliance to Section 47 (C) of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Local Government Code of 1991 and Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.   

Direct the General Services Officer to keep a complete record of all real properties owned by the LGU in compliance to Articles 122 and 440 of the IRR of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: Process the entitlement of land owned by the municipality.
observations.reason: Still in the process of entitling the land owned by the municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to require the Local Chief Executive to create an Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of all local roads and prepare Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System. 

Direct the GSO to maintain a Local Road Inventory, Road Map, Local Road Network Property Card, and prepare Report on Local Road Network for all roads and its components annually. Then, instruct the Municipal Engineer to render complete description and cost segregation of all road components, serving as basis for the Municipal Accountant to prepare Journal Voucher to record the costing of local road network in the general ledger and in the Local Road Network Ledger Card.
observations.managementAction: Creation of Inventory Committee thru Executive Order who will handle the physical inventory of local road networks
observations.reason: Some local road networks were not yet segregated based on its components.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to require the Bids and Awards Committee to adhere to the procedures, and policy of determining eligibility of bidders in a competitive bidding, and to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report every semester and submit the same to the GPPB.
observations.managementAction: The BAC shall adhere to the procedures and policy of determining eligibility of bidders and submit PMR to GPPB semestrally.
observations.reason: The BAC failed to conduct public bidding on its procurement of fuel and gasoline.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to require the Bids and Awards Committee to demand suppliers to submit mandatory documentary requirements for alternative mode of procurement as provided by Appendix A of Annex “H” of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: The BAC and GSO should demand submission of documentary requirements as required.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to require the BAC Secretariat to furnish the COA audit team all perfected contracts, along with its related documents, in earliest time possible and examine carefully the completeness of the required documents before submission to avoid delay in the legal and auditorial review and the technical evaluation thereof, as explicitly provided in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

 Submit immediately the lacking documents to ensure that any claims against government funds are supported with proper documentation.
observations.managementAction: The BAC shall submit all perfected contracts to COA for technical/ auditorial review.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to require the representative of the Municipal Planning Development Office, to ensure that all necessary things to be done are well planned to avoid the non-implementation/delays of the planned/programmed activities chargeable to 20% development fund.  Monitor the utilization of the fund and implementation of the projects, including the portion allocated as counterpart to various projects funded by national agencies.
observations.managementAction: To plan well all the activities or programs for implementation under the 20% LDF including its counterpart to projects funded by the national government.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to withhold salaries and other emoluments of the person liable that are still in government service until the disallowance is fully settled.  Also, issue demand letter on the persons liable who are separated from government service.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters to be sent to accountable officers every quarter. Collect cash advances and/or deducted in payroll.
observations.reason: There were still audit disallowances not yet fully settled. Hence, reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management only grant allowances and benefits authorized under the salary standardization law. Other allowances and benefits not included in the aforementioned enumeration may only be granted with legal basis.
observations.managementAction: No management action.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

1.Comply strictly with the provisions of Section 10, Rule IV and Section 48.2, Rule XVI of RA No. 9184; and
2.Explain in writing why the Management did not conduct public bidding for construction materials, medicines and medical supplies, and jetmatic pumps and pipes.
observations.managementAction: No management action.
observations.reason: Management continues to procure common goods through shopping.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management properly observe the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 on the priority programs and projects covered by the 20% Development Fund and avoid the expenditure items not covered by the fund. We further recommended the replenishment of the misused 20% Development Fund from the General Fund appropriations of P 344,276.73.
observations.managementAction: No management action.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality and Local School Board:

1.	Strictly follow the qualifications set in Section 2 of R.A. No. 4090 on the scholarship grant to poor but deserving students; and
2.	Submit to COA the list of the students under the scholarship program and the documentation as to the qualification standard set by the Local School Board.
observations.managementAction: No management action.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the GSO to attach Inventory Tags/Stickers to newly acquired properties and equipment before these are distributed to intended end user. Aforementioned Tags/Stickers will aid to effective identification and control on the use of the government property and equipment.
observations.managementAction: No management action.
observations.reason: Instead of using inventory tags/stickers the GSO maintains acknowledgement receipt for Property, Plant and Equipment issued to officials and employees.

observations.managementAction: The instruction has been given to the concerned officials for the strict observance of the use of the 20% development fund to fully comply with the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular so as to avoid a repetition of the noted deficiencies/errors.
observations.reason: Ineligible expenditure items were still included in the budget of 20% Development Fund for CY2017.

observations.managementAction: The Mun. Mayor had specifically instructed the Mun. Accountant and the Treasurer in charge of Office to religiously follow the proper procedures and submit to the Auditors on time the vouchers as well as documents and papers relative to government obligations.
observations.reason: The total unsubmitted accounts as of January 31, 2018 amounted to ₱61,619,378.33

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant to serve a written notice to the concerned accountable officers on their outstanding cash advances as mandated by Section 7 of COA Circular No. 2012-004. Settlement of the accountable officer’s account is hereby enjoined within 15 days upon receipt hereof. We also recommended the Municipal Mayor to suspend the salaries of the concerned accountable officers upon failure to settle their obligations.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that:
The CAD, in coordination with the other concerned departments, particularly the City Treasurer’s Office (CTO), General Services Department (GSD) and the Engineering Department continue to exert effort in the retrieval of records/reports from their respective files that would establish the details of the transactions that took place before CY 2003 in order to reclassify the “unreconciled beginning balances” to the appropriate SL accounts;
The concerned Inventory Committee expedite the completion of the reconciliation of the results of the physical count with the records of the CAD and CGSD pursuant to COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992; and
Management require the appraisal of all property with no acquisition costs to provide reference for their estimated values, remaining useful lives and dates constructed/acquired.

For fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements, we recommended that:
The City Accountant-
Exert efforts to reconcile the reciprocal accounts Due to/from Other Funds for all funds and identify the causes of the discrepancies in the corresponding subsidiary ledgers;
Prepare/effect the necessary adjustments to reflect in the books the correct balances of Accounts Due to Other Funds and Due from Other Funds;
Closely monitor the recording of financial transactions, strengthen review of JEVs to ensure that all financial transactions are properly and accurately taken up in the books, and periodically reconcile the reciprocal accounts; and
Prepare correcting entries to record shares of the barangays from real property tax to the appropriate account Due to LGUs-Barangays under the General Fund.
The City Treasurer-
See to it that all funds disbursed correspond to the fund charged in the disbursement vouchers and proper deposits of collections are made to the proper fund; and
Require the settlement of intra-agency payables to the respective funds.

We recommended that the Management strengthen the management of AP by:
Considering the conduct of a thorough review of the accounts payable irrespective of age to check the existence of actual claimants;
Directing the City Accounting Personnel and the City Treasurer’s Office to cleanse the recorded AP and ensure that all recorded payables are duly supported with the required supporting documents; and
Reverting back to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund the payable accounts found to be no longer payable  or without valid claimants which are two year old or more and/or undocumented accounts payable irrespective of age, pursuant to  Section 98 of PD 1445.
We recommended that the City Accounting:
Perform account analysis and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the accurate balance of each claimant. Further, establish a separate subsidiary ledger account for North Triangle Hotel Ventures, Inc. to have proper records; and
Verify the posting in the subsidiary records of the account to check if there was error in charging of expenses and make necessary Journal Entry Voucher to correct the same.

We recommended that:
The Local Finance Committee and the Budget Department, as the lead Office:
Conduct a systematic and realistic forecasting of revenue taking into account prior years’ experience and statistics as aids in setting targets;
Review the present budgeting to set a clearer guide for the concerned revenue generating Departments towards attaining the targets set; and
Provide for the targets on accounts that did not have projections which should be based on statistics, prior year’s experience and future expectations.
The City Treasury and other Departments involved in generating revenues endeavor to meet its targets.  Periodic review should be made by the concerned officials in the City to ascertain whether collection targets are attained.  The reasons for the shortfall in collections should be analyzed and alternative actions should be looked into to avoid incurrence of misses in projections in the future;
The Local Finance Committee complement the effort of the City Treasury in monitoring revenue targets. Periodic review should be conducted to check the performance level and also to ascertain remedial measures necessary for revenue accounts that missed the projections and procedures that will improve more the collections of taxes that posts good results; and
The Budget Department correct the coding of projected revenues to align and conform with the Standard Chart of Accounts promulgated by the Commission on Audit pursuant to COA Circular No. COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated 01 December 2015.

We recommended that:
The City Treasurer:
Require the twenty five (25) business establishments to pay the correct amount of business taxes as well as the corresponding fines and penalties due to the City Government;
Institute appropriate charges against the owners/proprietors for the declaration of gross receipts or sales other than the actual amounts; and
Exercise the visitorial function as provided under Section 222 of the Revenue Code on these businesses to check on the accuracy of the assessment and collection of taxes, in view of the substantial differences in the declared gross receipts against the selling transactions disclosed by the audit of DOAS; and
The ITDO design a system that will capture the transactions of businesses engaged in the selling of real estate to provide a ready basis in the automated preparation of the billing or the Tax Order of Payment which can be downloaded in the City’s website.  This can reduce the process and business establishments can opt to pay in the banks to avoid the long lines during peak periods.

We recommended that:
Chief of the Real Estate Division of the CTO initiate appropriate steps towards determining the actual and correct addresses of the delinquent real property owners. The returned mails, particularly those sent on recurring basis, should already provide the input on the incorrectness of the addresses and therefore should be tackled by involving the City Assessor as the original source of information. Other reasons should be analyzed and accordingly dealt with so that probably, those that already ‘moved out’, ‘no such number in given address’, shall no longer be sent delinquency notices;
The City Treasury:
Cause the publication, in a newspaper of general circulation, the properties and the owners who were delinquent for over ten (10) years as an initial step towards cleansing the records. The publication may not necessarily be for the auction of delinquent properties but only to determine lawful and rightful claimants of the real estates; and
Exercise the collection remedies provided under Section 14 of the Revenue Code which can be enforced by administrative action through levy on real property or by judicial action; and
The ITDO enhance the automated system to have a facility that would automatically generate letter/notice to property owners when the tax becomes delinquent. This would preclude the individual preparation of letters or notices and computation that could be erroneous because of manual intervention.
The Chief of the Information and Technology Development Department in a reply dated 03 April 2018 informed that his Office is now coordinating with the City Treasury on the automatic generation of notices to avoid duplication of tasks among staff and further save cost of mailing to delinquent taxpayers.

We recommended that the CTO assess and collect taxes of:
50 percent of 1 percent of the gross receipts of Tollway Operators or Concessionaires; and
62.7 percent of 1 percent of the gross receipts of Owners, Operators, Concessionaires, Principal Contractors or Sub-contractors of all kinds of tollways and railways mass transit system pursuant to Quezon City Ordinance No. SP-2236, S-2013, in order to generate additional revenue for the benefit of its constituents.
In the Exit Conference, the City Mayor informed that the Ordinances were enacted in anticipation to the completion of the Metro Rail Transit (MRT) 7 which is still under construction but did not comment on the existing MRTs traversing the roadways of the City.

We recommended that the members of the Local School Board include allocation for the ECCD Program on the succeeding SEF Budget and to utilize the same according to the authorized purposes as required under Section 7 of RA No. 10410 dated 23 March 2013.

We recommended that:
The City Health Office furnish a copy to the CAD the reimbursement billing to Philhealth  as basis in recording the Due from Philhealth, Service and Business Income and Other Payable in the books of account in compliance to the above regulation; and
The CAD adjust the Due to Philhealth to its proper account. Further, transfer all the transactions of the above health facility from the Trust Fund book to Special Accounts in the General Fund book.

We recommended and Management agreed that:
The CAD, City Treasurer, HCDRD and other concerned offices/departments coordinate so that transactions on socialized housing may be properly accounted and recorded in the accounting records, specifically the receivables against the beneficiaries;
The CAD set-up and maintain subsidiary ledger for individual beneficiaries of socialized housing so that amount of receivables may be determined at any given period in support to the monitoring function on collection by the AMMS, HCDRD; and
The CAD make necessary correcting entries in the books of accounts, particularly Other Payables account, and record the collections to its proper account.

We recommended that the Task Force on Socialized Housing and Development, HCDRD and other concerned offices/ departments/committees coordinate and properly plan the implementation of the projects by adopting strategies that would minimize or avoid delays, expedite the procedures, streamline process or revisit the existing policies and guidelines so that by the end of CY 2019, the 52 percent or 3,744 housing units could be completed as planned.
The Management assured that they are closely coordinating with the national agencies involved in housing as weekly Local Inter-Agency Committee Meetings are being taken.
Further, the Management is confident that by the end of the term of the incumbent Mayor, substantial number of housing units and sustainable project would be finished and established that next administration may continue and expand.
We recommended and Management agreed that the City establish and maintain Socialized Housing database pursuant to RA No. 7279, in relation to RA No. 9710 and its guidelines.

We recommended that Management:
Convene the Technical Working Group as provided in Section 5, Article VII, of the Revised Quezon City Market Code, so that the related rules and regulations implementing the same may be drafted and finalized in CY 2018;
Provide all the necessary information and procedures in reporting for collections and status reports related to revenue generation in the implementing rules and regulations; and
Cause the MDAD to direct its attention to providing the steps to be undertaken such that vacant and closed stalls remain as such only for a short period.

We recommended that the City and School Officials concerned strictly comply with Sections 272 and 100 (c) of RA 7160 on the utilization of the Special Education Fund.
The City Budget Officer commented that school signages may be considered as “facilities” within the contemplation of DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular (JC) No. 1, s. 2017. In the said JC, “facilities” is described as “all physical properties of a school,” consisting of the grounds, buildings and various facilities within the school grounds and inside the school buildings, i.e., school sites, school buildings, school furniture and equipment. The school signages are among the physical properties of school. Although, signages are not among the physical properties specifically mention in describing “facilities” does not mean that they are excluded therefrom. The JC could not have intended to exclude the school fence, the flagpole and similar other ground installations from the coverage of the SEF. Like the school signages, all the foregoing are facilities within the school grounds and are considered physical properties of a school. Plainly, the JC envisaged all physical properties of a school among those where the SEF may be allocated.
The Auditor maintains that the fabrication of signages is not allowable under the SEF since the same does not partake the nature of an expense necessary in the maintenance and operations of public schools.

We recommended that the Assessor’s Office:
Update their records to come up with new assessments and issue notices to the concerned real property owners which classification should be in accordance with the actual or predominant use of real property.   Inspection should likewise be undertaken in other areas of the City which used to be purely residential areas but are now commercial hubs due to sprawling economic or business activities; and
Inspect RP/S with split assessments in order to come up with the correct assessment levels to be applied rather than classifying it as commercial or residential only.
We also required Management to submit the following:
Information on the RP/S located in the National Government Center presently occupied and used for residential and commercial purposes and courses of action that the Assessor’s Office plans to undertake for purposes of generating real property taxes from these sources; and
Recorded classification for the 158 RP/S to facilitate the completion of the audit and re-check the records if, indeed, the addresses of the six RP/S found in field validation do not actually exist in the Assessor’s records.
We recommended that Management resolve all problems encountered in the implementation of GAD programs and projects to ensure their accomplishments and the utilization of the budget.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and  the  Municipal
     Treasurer reconcile  regularly  the  ledger  and  cashbook/cash  record
     balances to ensure that the correct amounts of cash shall be  reflected
     in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management:

        a. Require all  officials  and  employees  with  unliquidated  Cash
           Advances to submit the liquidation documents to  the  Accountant
           for recording in the books and refund the  excess  balances,  if
           any, to fully settle their outstanding accounts and,

        b. Instruct the Accountant to monitor closely  the  liquidation  of
           Cash  Advances  granted  to  Accountable  Officers  within   the
           prescribed period to ensure prompt settlement of  accounts.  The
           no liquidation no cash advance policy must be strictly followed.

We  recommended  that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  create   an
      Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory, the result of which
       to be reconciled with the  accounting records.  We  also  recommended
      that the Property Custodian maintain property records.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee undertake a complete periodic physical inventory of municipal properties and immediately reconcile the balances of any affected accounts to permit fair presentation of PPE in the financial statements.
We recommended that management strictly adhere to the aforecited provisions.
We recommended that Management adhere with the prescribed rate of 1% of IRA be allocated for the strengthening and implementation of the programs of MCPC.
We recommended that management not to allow any of its officials and employees’ monthly net pay below P4,000.00.

We recommended that management stop granting transportation allowance to any officials who have been issued with government motor vehicle and strictly observe the rules and regulations set in DBM Local Budget Circular No. 103.
We recommended for management to continuously comply with all regulations pertaining to GAD advocacies.

We recommended that Management revisit the Municipality’s 10-Year SWM Plan and prioritize the activities as planned.  This shall be reviewed and updated every year by the municipal solid waste management board as provided under Section 16 of Republic Act No.  9003.
We recommended that the management make representations with the Sangguniang Bayan to prioritize the passage into ordinance of the updated local revenue code of the municipality.

We recommended that the management:
Be more circumspect in the granting of clothing allowance by strictly adhering to regulations; and
For the payees to refund the amount erroneously paid.
We recommended that the management require the submission of accomplishment reports and other necessary documents as requisites of payment to casual/contractual personnel as required by regulation.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Accountant drop the invalid payables recorded in the books; and
Ensure that only valid obligations are recognized in the books.

We recommended that the Management require all concerned officials to comply with the monthly submission of Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the format prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended:
a.	For the Accounting Office and the Budget Office to reconcile the balances of the unexpended balances of LDRRMF – Special Trust Fund in their respective books; and
b.	That management revert the unexpended balance of CYs 2011 and 2012 appropriations back to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund for subsequent re-appropriation by the municipality for other social services.

We recommended for the Accounting Department to withhold appropriate taxes due for every payment made by the municipality and remit the same to the BIR within the prescribed period and strictly comply with the above cited regulation.

We recommended that the management:
1.	 Cause the refund of the excess payment of anniversary bonus erroneously paid; and
2.	 Adhere to the conditions set forth under the regulation in the granting of anniversary bonus.

We recommended that management prepare and submit its CY 2016 Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation (AAPSI) within the period prescribed by the law using the prescribed form under COA Memorandum No. 2014 – 002 dated March 18, 2014. It is further recommended that a focal person be designated by the Local Chief Executive who will consolidate the report before submission to the audit team.

We recommended that the management through the Bids and Awards Committee and the Municipal Accounting Office ensure that the contract and all its supporting documents including those required in auditorial review be submitted to the Audit Team within five (5) days from its execution to facilitate its review and evaluation as required in COA Circulars No. 96-010 and 2009-001.

We recommended for management to fast tract the implementation of all on – going and not yet started programmed and funded projects under the 20% Development Fund to optimize utilization thereof and timely brings the desired benefits to the intended beneficiaries.
We recommended that Management observe the abovementioned provisions of Local Budget Circular No. 98 on Personal Services (PS) limitation on Local Government Budgets to avoid audit disallowances.
We recommended that programmed PPAs under the 20% Development Fund be consistent with DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 17-1.
We recommended that Management reinforce efforts on the collection of audit disallowances as required under Sections 7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006.

We recommended for management to fast tract the implementation of all on – going and not yet started programmed and funded projects under the LDRRM Fund to optimize utilization thereof and strengthen its disaster risk reduction and management system.

We recommended and Management agreed to (a) create an Inventory Committee who will conduct physical count of properties of the Municipality; (b) submit Report on Physical Count of Plant, Property and Equipment (RCPPE) to the Office of the Auditor; and (c) instruct Municipal Treasurer to maintain property cards, Property Acknowledgement Receipts and inventory tags.

We recommended and Management agreed to establish effective measures to improve cash position in order to transfer unexpended balances of Local Disaster Risk Reduction Fund to a special trust fund so that the subject fund will be available during times of disaster/calamity.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to (a) investigate the occurrence of negative/debit balances in the liability accounts and make the necessary adjustments in the books; and (b) refund to source agency or remit to Bureau of Treasury the unexpended balances of transferred funds/completed projects as prescribed by COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct municipal officials to administer the funds properly and to continue to limit expenditures to the essential operating needs of the municipality in order to reduce and eventually eliminate cash deficit.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation and the  Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Accountant to (a) make concerted effort to determine the causes of delay in the submission of accounts and plan and establish an effective measure on how to resolve it; (b) submit explanation to the Office of the Auditor on why the submission was delayed and incomplete; and (c) expedite the immediate submission of still unsubmitted accounts such as the official receipts, disbursement vouchers and payrolls to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the MPDC to (a) refrain from including expenditure items that are not allowed to be charged against the Development Fund (DF); and (b) comply with the reportorial requirements of DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1, dated February 22, 2017.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Local School Board (LSB) to comply with the reportorial requirements of DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017, dated January 19, 2017.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the LDRRMO to (a) carefully consider the LDRRMP in the appropriation of the LDRRMF; (b) strictly monitor the implementation of all program/projects/activities in the LDRRMP in order to strengthen the disaster and mitigation measures of the Municipality which will be beneficial in times of calamities; and (c) comply with the reportorial requirements of JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.

We recommended and the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (MSWMB) agreed to (a) include in the MSWMB representatives from local government NGO that promotes recycling and protection of air and water quality and from recycling industry; (b) expedite the processing of safe closure and rehabilitation of dumpsite; (c) enforced the practice of mandatory segregation of wastes at source and segregated collection; and (d) expedite the establishment of Municipal MRF and provision for recycling and composting equipment as stated in the Ten-Year Solid Waste Management of the Municipality.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to (a) update and post notice of delinquency for property taxes at the main entrance of the municipal building and in publicly accessible and conspicuous place in each barangay where these properties are located; (b) seek assistance from the concerned barangays for the issuance of Notice of Delinquency and to conduct an information dissemination regarding the importance of paying taxes promptly and the consequences of non-payment of taxes; if warranted, enforced the provision of Sections 258 and 260 of the RA 7160 in the collection of delinquent taxes; and (c) to impose the 25% surcharge on rentals not paid on time, revocation of lease contract and the stall be declared vacant in accordance with Section 2B. 03. of Kautusang Bayan Blg. 04, series of 2002 and direct the Market and Water System Supervisors to strictly monitor the implementation of the policies to improve collections.

We recommended and the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) agreed to (a) establish and maintain GAD database as required by Section 4.4 of PCW-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01; and (b) present appropriately the programs/projects/activities related to children protection and welfare in the Plan and Accomplishment Report of Local Council for Protection of Children.

We recommended and the Local Council for Protection of Children (LCPC) agreed to (a) prepare an Annual Work and Financial Plan for LCPC to ensure prioritization of PPAs according to the needs of the children; and (b) present the PPAs implemented in the Accomplishment Report of LCPC.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation to remit fully the balance of unremitted taxes to BIR as required by Revenue Regulation 2-1998 and Revenue Regulation 1-2013 on the withholding and remittance of withholding taxes on compensation and value added taxes, percentage taxes & expanded taxes from government money payments to assure that the government was not deprived of taxes that can be used in the implementation of various programs and projects.

We recommended that the management continue complying with the GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG regulations on the withholding and remittance of employees’ and employer contributions and loan amortizations of employees to the respective agencies and remit fully the remaining balance of due to GSIS to assure all its members of the social security benefits due them.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Assessor, Municipal
Engineer and General Services Officer to conduct the physical inventory count of the
PPE and furnish the Audit Team with the said report.

We reiterated our recommendation that the GSO and Municipal Accountant
reconcile the Report on the Physical Count of PPE, Property Cards, Property
Ledger Cards, and accounting records, taking into consideration the noted
deficiencies in audit, and furnish the Audit Team with the said report. We also
recommended the GSO create a database of all properties owned by the
Municipality.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management facilitate the
transfer of title of the real properties under the name of the Municipal
Government of Quezon to prevent future dispute of ownership.

We also recommended the Municipal Accountant to maintain the Real
Property Ledger Cards for all the real properties of the Municipality with
complete details to determine the differences in the records of the Municipal
Assessor and financial statements.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer
immediately transmit the identified unrecorded disbursements to the Municipal
Accountant to facilitate recording thereof in the book of accounts.
We recommended the Municipal Accountant to identify the payees and
make the necessary entries to record and adjust the Cash-in-bank account.
Henceforth, strict compliance by the Municipal Treasurer with regards to
the transmittal of the check disbursements and its supporting documents within
the prescribed period to the Municipal Accountant must be enjoined and strictly
monitored.

We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to send a written notice to the
concerned payees at least one month before a check becomes stale. Moreover, we
required the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the
books for the long outstanding/stale checks in accordance with Section 59 of the
NGAS Manual, Volume I.

We reiterated our recommendation to the Management to strictly comply
with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002.
We also reiterated our recommendation to the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Discontinue the practice of partial liquidation of cash advances and direct the
concern accountable officers to settle their accounts immediately especially for

b. Prospectively, return to the DO/AO all LR/RDs that were not included in the

We reiterated our recommendation to the Municipal Accountant to serve a
written notice to the concerned accountable officers on their outstanding cash
advances as mandated by Section 7 of COA Circular No. 2012-004. Settlement of
the accountable officer’s account is hereby enjoined within 15 days upon receipt
hereof.
We also recommended the Municipal Mayor to suspend the salaries of the
concerned accountable officers upon failure to settle their obligations.

We recommended the Management to secure a copy of the mentioned
schedules. The Municipal Engineer should conduct an ocular inspection of the
alliance projects and submit a copy of the report to the Municipal Accountant for
recording thereof in the books.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to submit the necessary documents
of the Agreement to the Audit Team for our reference in audit

We recommended the Management to discontinue and reprogram the
identified PPAs totaling ₱3,893,476.78 for current and continuing appropriations
into development PPAs allowed to be funded out of the 20% DF in harmony with
Section 3.0 of the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017. Further,
