and must partake the nature of capital expenditures.

Further, we recommended for the strict observance of the provisions of
DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 in the utilization of the
20% DF.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the collectors to remit their
collections within the prescribed period. We also recommended the withholding
of the salary of the Accountable Officers (AO) and/or administrative sanction in
case of failure to settle his/her accountabilities.

We also recommended that all collectors should be under the direct
supervision of the Municipal Treasurer. We also advised all the collectors to settle
their accounts within the prescribed period.

We recommended the Management to:
a. Use the PS appropriations for compensation and benefits of regular and
permanent employees;
b. Fund and charge the salaries and wages of COS/JO against the MOOE in the
approved budget; and
c. Fill up the vacant positions and the COS/JO personnel, who meet the
appropriate eligibility and qualification requirements, shall be given priority
in the hiring by the Municipality.

We recommended the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that the use of
savings for augmentation under Section 336 of R.A. No. 7160 were covered by an
ordinance authorizing the same and that the use of savings under Section 321 of
R.A. No. 7160 were covered by an Appropriation Ordinance providing for a
supplemental budget. Such supplemental budget will involve the reversion of the
savings and its corresponding re-appropriation to any item of expenditure under
any expense class and shall be subject to review by the Sangguniang Panlalawigan
pursuant to Sections 56 and 327 of R.A. No. 7160.

Henceforth, we recommended the Management to strictly observe the
provisions of the LGC of 1991 and its IRR.

We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to compel the real property
owners to pay the undercollection, otherwise she will be held personally liable.
The Municipal Treasurer should direct all collectors to strictly comply with the
provisions of the LGC on granting discount to real property owners.
We recommended the Sangguniang Bayan to revisit and revise the Revenue
Code to comply with the provisions of the LGC.

We recommended the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of
50% of the Municipality’s Road Network System to facilitate the recording
thereof in the books of accounts as at year-end.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to mandate the Municipal Engineer
and Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) to immediately
submit a complete list of all on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented
for year 2017 and the ensuing year, containing all necessary information relative
thereto, otherwise, he should be subject to disciplinary action in accordance with
Section 4.0 of the same Circular.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to mandate the Municipal Engineer
and Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) to immediately
submit a complete list of all on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented
for year 2017 and the ensuing year, containing all necessary information relative
thereto, otherwise, he should be subject to disciplinary action in accordance with
Section 4.0 of the same Circular.

We recommended to request the Honorable Mayor to create an inventory Committee who will be responsible in the conduct of the physical inventory of the property, plant and equipment, and thereafter require the conduct of physical count and submission of the report annually.
Require the GSO to maintain property cards per category of property, plant and equipment.
In addition, compute for the depreciation to all agency assets that are subjected to depreciation.

We recommended to require the Municipal Accountant to continue the reconciliation of land reported in lump amount and disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements necessary information on these real properties.
Require the Municipal Assessor to constantly follow up the status of the submitted documents (for titling of the real properties of the municipality at the Registry of Deeds and DENR provincial office at Malaybalay City to ensure titling of all the lands owned by the municipality in compliance to Section 47 (C) of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Local Government Code of 1991 and Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.
Direct the General Services Officer to keep a complete record of all real properties owned by the LGU in compliance to Articles 122 and 440 of the IRR of RA 7160.

We recommended to require the Local Chief Executive to create an Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of all local roads and prepare Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System.
Direct the GSO to maintain a Local Road Inventory, Road Map, Local Road Network Property Card, and prepare Report on Local Road Network for all roads and its components annually. Then, instruct the Municipal Engineer to render complete description and cost segregation of all road components, serving as basis for the Municipal Accountant to prepare Journal Voucher to record the costing of local road network in the general ledger and in the Local Road Network Ledger Card.

We recommended to require the Bids and Awards Committee to adhere to the procedures, and policy of determining eligibility of bidders in a competitive bidding, and to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report every semester and submit the same to the GPPB.

We recommended to require the Bids and Awards Committee to adhere to the procedures, and policy of determining eligibility of bidders in a competitive bidding, and to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report every semester and submit the same to the GPPB.

We recommended to require the Bids and Awards Committee to demand suppliers to submit mandatory documentary requirements for alternative mode of procurement as provided by Appendix A of Annex “H” of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended to require the BAC Secretariat to furnish the COA audit team all perfected contracts, along with its related documents, in earliest time possible and examine carefully the completeness of the required documents before submission to avoid delay in the legal and auditorial review and the technical evaluation thereof, as explicitly provided in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
Submit immediately the lacking documents to ensure that any claims against government funds are supported with proper documentation.

We recommended to require the representative of the Municipal Planning Development Office, to ensure that all necessary things to be done are well planned to avoid the non-implementation/delays of the planned/programmed activities chargeable to 20% development fund.  Monitor the utilization of the fund and implementation of the projects, including the portion allocated as counterpart to various projects funded by national agencies.

We recommended that the Local
Chief Executive  create an
Inventory committee to conduct
physical inventory and to
reconcile the RCPPE against the
accounting records. We also
recommend that the Property
Custodian maintain property
records.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management thru the Inventory Committee and General Services Officer to:
Prepare and maintain property and ledger cards; and
Conduct immediately physical count of assets and prepare RPCPPE to be reconciled with the Accounting records before submission to COA office.
observations.managementAction: Management conducted partial Inventory of PPE. Reconciliation with Accounting Unit is on-going.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management require the:

1.	Treasury Office- to determine the current tax receivable existing as at December 31, 2016, this amount should be the balance of the RPT and SET Receivable and Deferred Income;
2.	Accounting Office- to adjust the accounts based on the determined amount from Treasury Office and submit JEV to COA Office for evaluation; and
3.	Treasury and Accounting Offices- to properly record RPT and SET Receivable and Deferred Income as guided by Section 20, Volume I of MNGAS.
observations.managementAction: Management Adjusted the related Accounts.
Demand Letters were issued but no valuation was made as to the recoverable amount of the receivables.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

1.	Municipal Treasurer- Issue official receipt for the settlement made by the employees supported by the payroll with deduction and submit such to the COA office for issuance of NSSDC; and
2.	Municipal Accountant- Properly monitor and reconcile the balance of receivables on disallowances/charges recognized on the financial statements with COA reports and make the necessary adjusting entries in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation is on-going.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Accounting Office & Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office:

1.	Prepare monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the format prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-002; and
2.	Submit immediately to the Office of the Auditor the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF as of December 31, 2016.
observations.managementAction: Utilization Reports were submitted quarterly.
observations.reason: The utilization report was not submitted on a monthly basis.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management thru the Accounting Unit to:

1.	Exert effort to trace the records pertaining to Cash-Local Treasury and Petty Cash;
2.	Send demand letters to the previous Municipal Treasurer and Cash Liquidating Officer, as the case may be, for the accountability of Cash-Local Treasury and Petty Cash; and
3.	Adjust the balance reported in the financial statements, reclassify the Cash-Local Treasury and Petty Cash to Accounts Receivable and record Allowance for Uncollectible amounts for proper valuation.
observations.managementAction: Related accounts were adjusted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, together with the Municipal Treasurer, reconcile the discrepancy between the cash in bank balance per financial statement and per bank reconciliation as at December 31, 2016 totaling P 52,243.87 and effect the necessary adjustments. The result of the reconciliation should be duly documented and a copy of which furnished to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation was made.
observations.reason: The result of reconciliation was not furnished to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality stop the practice of allowing reimbursement to Officials and Employees when direct payment is possible to prevent losses and provide reasonable assurance that the transaction is regular.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer maximize the use of petty cash, use petty cash vouchers for small operating expenses and replenish the fund thru imprest system.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management ensure the implementation of all programmed projects during each budget year to optimize the utilization of the 20% development fund as mandated under DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-1.
observations.managementAction: One (1) project is still unimplemented as of date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management revisit the Municipality’s 10-Year SWM Plan. This shall be reviewed and updated every year by municipal solid waste management board as provided under Section 16 of Republic Act No.  9003.
observations.managementAction: Management updated its 10 year SWM plan.
observations.reason: Some of the planned activities were not accomplished as of date.

observations.managementAction: The concerned department heads are already conducting the inventory of properties of the different offices of the LGU. The ICO Mun. Treasurer & OIC Mun. Accountant have already conducted inventory in the different offices to update the property ledger cards & schedules for the PPE. Instructions were already made to concerned departments and steps were already undertaken to compute the correct depreciation of PPE accounts in order to have an accuracy in financial position.
observations.reason: Management failed to conduct physical inventory of property during CY2017. Schedules were already prepared by the Accounting Unit while no property cards were prepared and maintained.

observations.managementAction: The Mun. Treasurer – ICO already updated their RPT Account Register and prepared the certified list of Taxpayers and Taxes, in fact, these are the basis of the OIC-Mun. Accountant for the current year.

The LCE already directed the MENRO to closely supervise the implementation of all existing laws, rules and ordinances of ecological solid waste management and other related issuances geared towards environmental protection.

We recommended that the MSWMB: Involve closely the barangay officials in the strict implementation of the existing environmental laws and intensify the information campaign on the “no segregation, no collection policy and the no littering policy” among the residents; and Exercise strong political will to implement the environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment as mandated under RA No. 9003.
observations.reason: Information, education and communication (IEC) activities were undertaken by the Municipality however some deficiencies were still noted in relation to the implementatio n of Solid Waste Management.

observations.managementAction: The municipality complied to the recommendation of Auditor that the 10% retention money be deducted for every progress payment on infrastructure projects and the LCE strictly monitored this matter.

The municipality already coordinated with and issued directives to the MSWDO and MPDO for the establishment of the GAD Focal Point System. In effect, the LGU already held the GAD Focal Point Training System last May 3-5, 2017. The members thereof immediately established and maintained the GAD Database and consequently formulated a GAD Plan and Budget for approval of the Sangguniang Bayan.
observations.reason: GAD Database was not yet established.

observations.managementAction:
Please be informed that as per records of our office, the said account only bears an ending balance of P5,129,791.57 as of June 30,2017. As we cannot pay this remaining amount through installment payments.

Regular payments for the remaining balance will be remitted to BIR agent assigned in our Municipality which started already on Last May.
observations.reason: The balance of Due to BIR reduced to P3,394,326.66 as of December 31, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the GSO and Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Report on the Physical Count of PPE, Property Cards, Property Ledger Cards, and accounting records, taking into consideration the noted deficiencies in audit, and furnish the Audit Team with the said report. We also recommended the GSO create a database of all properties owned by the Municipality, change the property numbers and accordingly modify the property tags.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor created the Committee
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation Nos. 1 and 2.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Assessor, Municipal Engineer and General Services Officer to conduct the physical inventory count of all immovable PPE and furnish the Audit Team with the said report.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and GSO committed to finish the reconciliation of the PPE acquired in CY 2017 by September 2018. Afterwards, they will reconcile the acquisitions for CY 2016 and prior years’ accounts
observations.reason: Reiterated in Implemented Observation No. 1.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to perform quarterly reconciliation of banks and cash books of accounts and identify discrepancies so necessary adjustments can be effected; disbursing officers to reconcile their records with subsidiary ledgers.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer completed the reconciliation but the results were not yet adjusted in the books and cashbooks.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant to submit the Disbursement Vouchers and Report of Disbursements together with the supporting documents to the Audit Team for post audit, otherwise the transaction will be suspended in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Vice Mayor, then former Municipal Treasurer, will coordinate with the Municipal Accountant on the Disbursement Vouchers of unrecorded disbursements and submit the same once found.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to send a written notice to the concerned payees at least one month before a check becomes stale, and Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments for long outstanding/stale checks.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer will coordinate with the concerned payees and reissue the checks.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002; Municipal Accountant to include in checklist, process only justified travels, discontinue partial liquidation, require DO/AO to return LR/RDs not included in cash advance list; Municipal Treasurer to require DOs to settle accountabilities, segregate DO cash advances by type, ensure liquidation documents conform to cash advance list and forward softcopies of RD and RCI to Municipal Accountant.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant enjoined to reconcile and settle; Municipal Treasurer to require settlements and proper segregation; Municipal Accountant to implement checklist and disallow partial liquidations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to direct the Disbursing Officer to discontinue the practice of transferring her accountability to another AO. We recommended the Municipal Accountant to reject all liquidation of the DO covering transfer of accountability and require for the refund thereof.

We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to stop the practice of withdrawing cash for payment of various expenditures; refund remaining balance of cash withdrawals made in 2017; and that disbursements and obligations should be through payment of checks or where impractical, by cash advance of the DO/SDO.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer complied and refunded/adjusted as necessary.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to immediately transmit the identified unrecorded disbursements to the Municipal Accountant to facilitate recording thereof in the book of accounts. We recommended the Municipal Accountant to identify the payees and make the necessary entries to record and adjust the Cash-in-bank account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Vice Mayor, then former Municipal Treasurer, will coordinate with the Municipal Accountant on the Disbursement Vouchers of unrecorded disbursements and submit the same once found.
observations.reason: The Notices of Suspension were issued to those unrecorded disbursements and the prescribed period of ninety (90) days within which to submit those documents were already lapsed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management facilitate the transfer of title of the real properties under the name of the Municipal Government of Quezon to prevent future dispute of ownership. We also recommended the Municipal Accountant to maintain the Real Property Ledger Cards for all the real properties of the Municipality with complete details.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor and Municipal Accountant will compare their records to determine the discrepancy on the number of parcel of lands acquired by the Municipality. The Municipal Assessor will secure all the necessary documents to facilitate its transfer under the name of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the land held for distribution. The Management should submit to the Audit Team all the necessary documents for the implementation and distribution of the lots to the respective recipients.
We recommended the Municipal Accountant to ensure recognition of the monthly depreciation of all the PPE of the Municipality for fair presentation in the financial statement.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary entry to remove the inventoriable items from the PPE. Further, we recommended the Municipal GSO to issue an Inventory Custodian Slip to the Accountable Officer, who is in actual custody of these items. The Municipal GSO should make a separate database/lists of these items for monitoring purposes.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to insure all government properties with the Property Insurance Fund of the GSIS. We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to personally pay for the penalties incurred for late remittance of the employees’ premium contributions to the GSIS.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer complied and remitted contributions; properties insured as required.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to maximize utilization of the 20% DF by speeding up implementation of prioritized projects and ensure coordination among planning and implementing officials; instruct MPDO to look into unimplemented developmental projects, identify causes and develop solutions; instruct Municipal Engineer to be meticulous in evaluating implementation and conduct regular inspections; impose Liquidated Damages for delays; reappropriate unexpended balances to identified development PPAs.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to create an Inventory Committee and enjoin the concerned department heads’ compliance with requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-008 and submit corresponding reports to the Audit Team for evaluation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor created an Inventory Committee and enjoined compliance; however the physical transfer and recording are pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to require the Municipal Engineer to produce the needed Detailed Engineering Design; coordinate with PGP to settle legitimate owner of land for resettlement site; comply with Annex A of RA 9184 on Detailed Engineering before procurement; require the contractor to submit the Irrevocable Standby Letter of Credit/Security Bond/Bank Guarantee in compliance with Annex E, Item 4.2 of IRR of RA 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer undertook actions to secure design and documentation; coordination with Provincial Government initiated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to strictly adhere to the guidelines in the rendition of overtime services in accordance with CSC-DBM JC No. 1 series of 2016.

We recommended the management to: a) stop the practice of engaging services of private legal counsel and to strictly comply with COA Circular No. 98-002 dated June 09, 1998; b) strictly comply with the provisions of RA 9184 and its revised IRR on the procurement of consultancy services which must be done through competitive bidding or alternative methods of procurement.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality discontinued the practice and complied with procurement rules.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to introduce improvements on the facilities in the sanitary landfill taking into consideration the noted discrepancies between projects and the SWMP and the actual facilities constructed. We also recommended assignment of permanent equipment in the facility to undertake daily application of soil cover.
observations.managementAction: The Management introduced improvements and assigned equipment; partial works remain ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the MENRO to introduce improvements to the sanitary landfill facilities and operations based on the noted deficiencies in audit.
observations.managementAction: Improvements were initiated and some facilities upgraded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the LDRRMO, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to reconcile their versions of utilization and submit a copy of the final report to the Audit Team for validation. We also recommended the LDRRMO to submit the necessary reports, resolutions, and lists of projects implemented and to be implemented in order for the Audit Team to determine the unexpended portion of the fund.

We recommended the LDRRMO and Municipal Budget Officer to charge the mentioned expenses to the General Fund and return the amount obligated to the LDRRMF otherwise it will be disallowed in audit. The amount returned should be reprogrammed to disaster prevention, mitigation and preparedness related activities.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer and MDRRMO complied and returned charged amounts; MDRRMO to comply with RA 10121 in the application of LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management implement strict observance of the reglementary period in the submission of financial reports and accounts to the Auditor to give him ample time for review.

We recommended to stop the practice of charging to LDRRMF expenses which are not related to and do not support disaster risk management activities and to adhere strictly the guidelines set under Section 2 of RA 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the rules and regulations set in the DBM-DILG-DND-GCG-COA Joint Circular No. 2015-01 relative to the granting of cash advance and its subsequent liquidation be strictly observed and implemented. We also recommended to management to furnish immediately the lacking documents to the ICFAU to expedite the audit of your Intelligence and Confidential Fund (ICF).

We recommended that the Municipality should comply with the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 and ensure proper granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances. The Municipal Accountant should ensure proper monitoring of liquidation of cash advances and implementation of existing rules and regulations on cash advances, including the withholding of salaries of accountable officers who fail to effect prompt liquidation in accordance with existing rules and regulations. All cash advances shall be fully liquidated at the end of each year. Except for petty cash fund, the AO shall refund any unexpended balance to the Cashier/Collecting Officer who will issue the necessary official receipt. It is also strictly recommended that each accountable officer with a total cash accountability of P2,000.00 or more shall be bonded. The amount of bond shall depend on the total accountability of the officer as fixed by the Head of the Agency. An official or employee who has both money and property accountability, shall be bonded only once to cover both accountabilities, but the amount of the bond shall be in accordance with the Schedule.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent to accountable officers every quarter. Some cash advances were already collected and/or deducted in payroll.
observations.reason: There were still unliquidated cash advances as of December 31, 2017. Hence, reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to withhold salaries and other emoluments of the person liable that are still in government service until the disallowance is fully settled.  Also, issue demand letter on the persons liable who are separated from government service.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accounting Office issued demand letters. Payroll deductions were also instituted.
observations.reason: Payroll deductions were made from the benefits/salary received by some concerned officials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board (LSB) be guided by the provisions of RA 7160 and the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circulars in the preparation of the SEF Annual Budget and identify the priority expenditures pursuant thereto. Any issues or conflicts arising from its implementation and cases not covered by the provisions shall be submitted to the DepEd for resolution and in consultation with the municipal government. We also recommended that the LSB stop the charging of water and electric consumption, security, janitorial and admin services of public elementary and high schools to the SEF for they have their own budget for said expenditures and instead utilize the funds for the needs of other schools. In addition, expenses incurred by the municipality for scholarship grants should not be charged against the Special Education Fund, but against the General Funds of the municipality of Quezon. It is strictly recommended also that the SEF be utilized by the Local School Boards for the operation and maintenance of elementary and secondary public schools only which shall mean government financed and managed schools which include pre-school, elementary, secondary schools, national and general comprehensive high schools and their annexes and those public elementary and secondary schools established by law and should not include private educational institutions.

We recommended that the procuring entity submit a copy of the contracts and purchase orders within five days from issuance as required under COA Circular 2009-001, together with the bidding documents, BAC resolution, minutes of the proceedings, abstracts, and such other documents peculiar to the contract and/or to the mode of procurement which are deemed necessary in the conduct of auditorial review and in the technical evaluation thereof.
observations.reason: There were still perfected contracts unsubmitted and/or partially submitted to COA for review. Hence, this audit recommendation is reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require all the Accountable Officers to apply for fidelity bond and the amount of bond shall be in accordance with their maximum cash accountability.

We recommended that the prescribed minimum employees’ monthly net take home pay of P3,500.00 under the GAA be observed. We also recommended that management stop immediately the practice of including the payments of loan amortizations to private lending institutions as deductions from the payroll and performing the collection and monitoring activities of the private lending institutions unless it is classified as a thrift bank or rural bank accredited by BSP. Also, require the personnel involved to transact directly with the private institutions.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Engineer and other officers concerned for proper prioritization of fully funded projects to ensure their timely completion, thus precluding unwarranted delay of the implementation of programs, projects and activities.

We recommended management to direct the officials and employees concerned to submit the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel consumption to the auditor for review and evaluation, Also, require the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare/monitor Registry of Appropriation, Allotments and Obligations – Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (RAAOMO) to avoid over charging.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  create an Inventory committee to  conduct physical inventory and to reconcile the RCPPE against the accounting records.
observations.managementAction: No physical inventory was conducted
observations.reason: and reiterated in this report as Finding No. 1

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the discrepancies noted in the Due to LGU amounting to P10,796,060.45 with the Provincial Government of Isabela records.
observations.managementAction: No reconciliation was made between the Province and LGU
observations.reason: and reiterated in this report as Finding No. 2

observations.recommendation: We reiterate our recommendation for the  immediate remittance of the share from RPT collections to the provincial government and to the barangays concern.
observations.managementAction: No action
observations.reason: and reiterated in this report as Finding No. 3

observations.recommendation: We recommended to  the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit monthly Bank Reconciliation Statement pursuant to Section 74 of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445, and to make the necessary adjustment in the books of accounts, if any.
observations.reason: and reiterated in this report as Finding No. 3

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the concerned responsible official to submit the required financial reports in compliance to Section 39 and 122 of PD 1445 and Section 70 of the Manual on New Government Accounting System (NGAS).
observations.managementAction: No action
observations.reason: and reiterated in this report as Finding No. 5

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to submit a copy of the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment as of December 31, 2015 to the Office of the Auditor and we also recommend to reconcile the counted municipal properties with the subsidiary ledgers in order that the financial statements reflect the true value of existing municipal properties at hand as of year-end.
observations.managementAction: No management action.
observations.reason: The Municipality did not conduct inventory of its physical assets as of to this date due to lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the GSO to attach Inventory Tags/Stickers to newly acquired properties and equipment before these are distributed to intended end user. Aforementioned Tags/Stickers will aid to effective identification and control on the use of the government property and equipment.
observations.managementAction: No management action.
observations.reason: Instead of using inventory tags/stickers the GSO maintains acknowledgement receipt for Property, Plant and Equipment issued to officials and employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management properly observe the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 on the priority programs and projects covered by the 20% Development Fund and avoid the expenditure items not covered by the fund. We further recommended the replenishment of the misused 20% Development Fund from the General Fund appropriations.
observations.managementAction: No management action.
observations.reason: The management continues to disburse DF for payrolls of JO’s.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to comply with the provisions of Section 10, Rule IV and Section 48.2, Rule XVI of the IRRA-A, and explain in writing why the public bidding was not conducted for commonly used office supplies.
observations.managementAction: No management action.
observations.reason: Management continues to procure common goods through shopping.

observations.managementAction: No management action.
observations.reason: Management did not reconcile RPT and SET receivable during CY 2016.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation and management agreed that:
the Local Chief Executive will require the annual physical inventory of property and submit the related report to the Office of the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year;
the Office of the Municipal Treasurer and Accounting Unit will prepare and maintain property ledger cards and schedules for the PPE; and
the Accounting Unit will update the lapsing schedule for the PPE items to facilitate the computation of the periodic depreciation of PPE accounts with no depreciation.
observations.reason: No reason was given by management

observations.recommendation: We reiterate our previous year’s recommendation and management agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to update the Real Property Tax Account Register in order to prepare the Certified List of Taxpayers and Taxes Due needed in setting up of RPT/SET Receivable and Deferred RPT/SET Income at the beginning of each year.

We recommended and management agreed to utilize the 20% Development Fund in accordance with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 to contribute in the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management, for the benefits of the intended beneficiaries, and to avoid the sanctions provided under RA No. 7160 and other applicable laws.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation and management agreed that management shall plan and establish an effective measure on how to comply with the deadline in the submission of accounts.  The Accounting Unit and the Office of the Municipal Treasurer should make a concerted effort to expedite the submission of the still unsubmitted accounts amounting to P60,207,618.53 to the Office of the Auditor.  
This Audit Observation Memorandum shall also served as the demand required under appropriate law, rules and regulations to submit the remaining unsubmitted accounts for CY 2015 transactions.

We recommended and management agreed that the MSWMB shall :

expedite the execution of disposal agreement with a private company;
 
involve closely the Barangay officials in the strict implementation on the existing environmental laws and intensify the information campaign on the “no segregation, no collection policy and the “no-littering policy” among the residents; and 

exercise strong political will to implement the environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment as mandated under Republic Act No. 9003

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records on cash accounts at least at the end of every quarter to strengthen the reliability of cash accounts balances.
observations.reason: This was not given priority by the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant. Reiterated in Observation No. 9, page 36.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Treasurer to update entries on her cashbooks to determine her accountability.
observations.reason: This was not prioritized by the Municipal Treasurer. Reiterated in Observation No. 8, page 34.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to make necessary follow-ups/actions to hasten the titling of these parcels of land in the name of the Municipality to protect said property from  possible third party claims in the future.
observations.reason: Absence of necessary documents/requirements for the title transfer. Necessity of the Municipal Assessor to confer with the Land Management Officer of DENR regarding the matter. Please refer to Observation No. 11, page 39.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor  require:

an explanation from the BAC on the failure to verify the authenticity of the said tax clearances submitted by the bidders and if indeed found spurious may result in the disallowance of the said payments; and

the Bids and Awards Committee to verify all documents submitted by bidders and ensure that they are eligible, prior to award of the projects/ contracts.
observations.managementAction: Upon receipt of the Audit Observation Memorandum, the BAC is already verifying all documents submitted by the bidders prior to award of the projects/contracts.
observations.reason: No written explanation was received by the Audit Team. The said transactions were disallowed in audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the LDRRMO to submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF (duly certified by the Municipal Accountant) to the Auditor to facilitate the timely audit on whether the utilization of the fund is in accordance with COA Memorandum No. 2014-009.
observations.reason: The MDRRMO-Designate was not able to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant who will certify the Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (DRRMF), hence was not able to submit the report. Reiterated in Observation No. 16, page 45.

observations.recommendation:
8. We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that:


    a.  The  CAD,  in  coordination  with  the   other   concerned
       departments,  particularly  the  City  Treasurer’s   Office
       (CTO),  General   Services   Department   (GSD)   and   the
       Engineering Department continue  to  exert  effort  in  the
       retrieval of records/reports from  their  respective  files
       that would establish the details of the  transactions  that
       took place before  CY  2003  in  order  to  reclassify  the
       “unreconciled beginning balances”  to  the  appropriate  SL
       accounts;

    b. The concerned Inventory Committee expedite  the  completion
       of the reconciliation of the results of the physical  count
       with the records of  the  CAD  and  CGSD  pursuant  to  COA
       Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992; and


    c. Management require the appraisal of all  property  with  no
       acquisition costs to provide reference for their  estimated
       values,    remaining     useful     lives     and     dates
       constructed/acquired.

2.8     We  recommended   that   Management  look  into  the  real
        cause/s of  the  misplacement/late  submission  of  the  DVs,
        Payrolls and other documents to support the bank transactions
        and determine the persons who may be responsible for the lost
        documents, giving emphasis on the consequences of failure  of
        compliance as stated under Section 39 (3) of P.D. 1445.

    2.9     We also reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that
        the  CTO  exert  extra  effort  in   locating   the   missing
        DVs/Payrolls and other  supporting  documents  pertaining  to
        prior years reconciling items and cause the reconstruction of
        documents, if necessary, by obtaining secondary  evidence  to
        account  for  actual  disbursements  and  submit   the   same
        immediately to CAD. The CAD, upon receipt of  the  documents,
        prepare immediately the necessary entries for the reconciling
        items and coordinate with the CTO for the immediate  clearing
        of reconciling items that need to be consulted with the  bank
        concerned and  for  appropriate  action/adjustment  in  their
        records.


    2.12    We  recommended  that the CAD  and  CTO  exert efforts  to
        further analyze  and  determine  the  reasons  for  the  non-
        movement of the unconfirmed deposit  accounts  and  make  the
        necessary adjustments to correct the book balances.


    2.16 We recommended that the CTO and CAD coordinate closely to
        effect the necessary adjusting entries to  take  up  all  the
        operations of the City’s economic enterprises from the TF  to
        the GF books. Moreso, the CTO should ensure that the  correct
        fund name is indicated in the documents that are submitted to
        the CAD for accurate recording of transactions in the  proper
        books of accounts.

7. We recommended that:


    a. Management require the  accountable  officers  to  strictly
       comply with the general guidelines in  the  liquidation  of
       cash advances under COA Circular No. 97-002 dated  February
       10, 1997 and Joint Circular No. 2015-01  dated  January  8,
       2015;


    b. The CAD and the CTO strictly  monitor  the  liquidation  of
       cash  advances  by  sending   demand   letters   regularly,
       particularly to accountable officers and employees who  are
       still  in  active  service,  and  in  case  of  failure  to
       liquidate the  same  despite  demand,  withholding  of  the
       payment of any money due them may be made pursuant  to  the
       afore-cited COA Circular; and


    c. Management expedite the processing of liquidation  for  the
       purpose of its timely recognition in the books of  accounts
       and strictly  impose  the  settlement  of  accountabilities
       especially to those planning to retire, resign or  transfer
       to other  agencies  prior  to  the  issuance  of  clearance
       certificate to them.

12. We recommended the following:


    The Management –


    a. Create an Inventory and  Reconciliation  Committee  on  PPE
       accounts, tasked to undertake  and  complete  the  physical
       inventory of  property  and  the  reconciliation  with  the
       property  and  accounting  records.  The  committee  to  be
       created  shall  preferably  be  a   composite   team   with
       representative from CGSD, CAD and concerned department, who
       will be required to render a report on the  completion  and
       reconciliation of the PPE accounts  within  the  prescribed
       timetable;


    b.  Assign  an  appraisal  committee  or  consider  hiring  an
       independent appraiser to establish the reasonable costs  or
       appraised values of the  unrecorded  PPE  particularly  the
       real property in order to record them in the books;



    The Property Officer, CGSD, CAD –


    c. Determine and effect the proper documentation and immediate
       disposition of all the unserviceable property;


    d. Set-up an effective system to keep track  and  capture  the
       related  transactions  on  PPE;   reconcile   PPE   records
       regularly and adjust accordingly; and to promptly reconcile
       the results of the physical count with the accountabilities
       recorded in the books of accounts and the property cards;


    e. Maintain updated and properly accomplished  property  cards
       for property accountabilities;


    The CAD –


    f. Include pertinent details in the property description (e.g.
       serial, plate, motor  and  chassis  numbers,  etc)  in  the
       recording of the PPE in the e-NGAS; and


    g. Exert effort in locating  or  producing  the  documents  to
       support the legitimacy  of  the  undocumented  PPE  account
       balances  and  henceforth,  strengthen  the  control   over
       documentation of all financial transactions.

5.8     We recommended that  the  Real  Estate  Division  of the
        CTO in coordination with the Committee of Public Auction of
        Delinquent Properties and the CAD to:


    a. Review the status of the registry/record of property
       subjected to auction sale to check which units had already
       reached the prescribed period but have not been redeemed by
       the owners;


    b. Effect the immediate refund to the bidders, the payment
       made by the latter as the result of winning in the auction
       sale in the event that the identity of the delinquent real
       property owner is already established and redemption of the
       property is made by the owner within the prescribed period;


    c. Cause the issuance of the final deed of conveyance to the
       purchaser/winning bidder in the failure of the owner of the
       delinquent real property to make redemption within the
       prescribed period; and


    d. Analyze the above four SL accounts to ascertain which among
       the entries/balances are due for adjustments/recognition in
       favor of the City, after redemption of property owner or
       conveyance of property to purchaser/winning bidder is
       established/made, as the case maybe.

6.5     We  recommended  that   Management   cause   a   thorough
        verification of the  real  properties  to  be  subjected  for
        auction   by   the   concerned   committee   and   officials.
        Representation be  made  with  the  Register  of  Deeds  with
        respect to  responses  on  the  levies  issued  by  the  City
        Treasurer. Also, we recommended the cleansing of  records  in
        the CAO to determine the correctness of information necessary
        for decision making.

We recommended that the Local  Chief  Executive  create  an  Inventory Committee to conduct physical  inventory  and  to  reconcile  the  inventory records against  the  accounting  records.  We  also  recommended  that  the Property Custodian maintain property records.
We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief  Executive require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile reciprocal accounts with the Books of PGI.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief  Executive require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and  submit  monthly  Bank Reconciliation Statements together with a  copy  of  bank  statements, Debit/Credit Memo and paid checks and to make the necessary adjustment in the books of  accounts,  if  any  Also,  we  recommend  to  hire  a competent staff to do the regular BRS.

We reiterated our recommendation that the concerned  responsible official submit  the  required  financial  reports  in  compliance  to Section 39 and 122 of PD 1445. Also, we recommended that the plan  for the development of the account      system  will  be  included  in investment priority programs of the municipality for funding.

We recommended the Management thru the Inventory Committee and General Services Officer to:
Prepare and maintain property and ledger cards; and
Conduct immediately physical count of assets and prepare RPCPPE to be reconciled with the Accounting records before submission to COA office.

We recommended that the Management require the:
1.	Treasury Office- to determine the current tax receivable existing as at December 31, 2016, this amount should be the balance of the RPT and SET Receivable and Deferred Income;
2.	Accounting Office- to adjust the accounts based on the determined amount from Treasury Office and submit JEV to COA Office for evaluation; and
3.	Treasury and Accounting Offices- to properly record RPT and SET Receivable and Deferred Income as guided by Section 20, Volume I of MNGAS.

We recommended that the:
1.	Municipal Treasurer- Issue official receipt for the settlement made by the employees supported by the payroll with deduction and submit such to the COA office for issuance of NSSDC; and
2.	Municipal Accountant- Properly monitor and reconcile the balance of receivables on disallowances/charges recognized on the financial statements with COA reports and make the necessary adjusting entries in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Accounting Office & Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office:
1.	Prepare monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the format prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-002; and
2.	Submit immediately to the Office of the Auditor the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF as of December 31, 2016.

We recommended that management only grant allowances and benefits authorized under the salary standardization law. Other allowances and benefits not included in the aforementioned enumeration may only be granted with legal basis.

We recommended that the Management:
1.	Comply strictly with the provisions of Section 10, Rule IV and Section 48.2, Rule XVI of RA No. 9184; and
2.	Explain in writing why the Management did not conduct public bidding for construction materials, medicines and medical supplies, and jetmatic pumps and pipes.

We recommended that Management properly observe the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 on the priority programs and projects covered by the 20% Development Fund and avoid the expenditure items not covered by the fund. We further recommended the replenishment of the misused 20% Development Fund from the General Fund appropriations of P 344,276.73.

We recommended the Management thru the Accounting Unit to:
1.	Exert effort to trace the records pertaining to Cash-Local Treasury and Petty Cash;
2.	Send demand letters to the previous Municipal Treasurer and Cash Liquidating Officer, as the case may be, for the accountability of Cash-Local Treasury and Petty Cash; and
3.	Adjust the balance reported in the financial statements, reclassify the Cash-Local Treasury and Petty Cash to Accounts Receivable and record Allowance for Uncollectible amounts for proper valuation.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, together with the Municipal Treasurer, reconcile the discrepancy between the cash in bank balance per financial statement and per bank reconciliation as at December 31, 2016 totaling P 52,243.87 and effect the necessary adjustments. The result of the reconciliation should be duly documented and a copy of which furnished to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that the Municipality stop the practice of allowing reimbursement to Officials and Employees when direct payment is possible to prevent losses and provide reasonable assurance that the transaction is regular.
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer maximize the use of petty cash, use petty cash vouchers for small operating expenses and replenish the fund thru imprest system.

We recommended that the Municipality and Local School Board:
Strictly follow the qualifications set in Section 2 of R.A. No. 4090 on the scholarship grant to poor but deserving students; and
Submit to COA the list of the students under the scholarship program and the documentation as to the qualification standard set by the Local School Board.

We recommended that management ensure the implementation of all programmed projects during each budget year to optimize the utilization of the 20% development fund as mandated under DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-1.

We recommended that Management revisit the Municipality’s 10-Year SWM Plan. This shall be reviewed and updated every year by municipal solid waste management board as provided under Section 16 of Republic Act No.  9003.

1.4 We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation to the management that:
the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to conduct an annual physical inventory of property and submit the related report to the Office of the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year;
the Office of the Municipal Treasurer and Accounting Unit prepare and maintain property ledger cards and schedules for the PPE; and
the Accounting Unit update the lapsing schedule for the PPE items to facilitate the computation of the periodic depreciation of PPE accounts with no depreciation.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that management direct the Municipal Treasurer to update the Real Property Tax Account Register in order to prepare the Certified List of Taxpayers and Taxes Due as basis of the Municipal Accountant in setting up RPT/SET Receivable and Deferred RPT/SET Income at the beginning of each year.

3.5 We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that management utilize the 20% Development Fund in accordance with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 to contribute in the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management, for the benefits of the intended beneficiaries, and to avoid the sanctions provided under RA 7160 and other applicable laws.

4.11	We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation and management agreed that management shall plan and establish an effective measure on how to comply with the deadline in the submission of accounts.  The Accounting Unit and the Office of the Municipal Treasurer should make a concerted effort to expedite the submission of the still unsubmitted accounts amounting to P48,441,256.01 to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that the MSWMB:
involve closely the Barangay officials in the strict implementation on the existing environmental laws and intensify the information campaign on the “no segregation, no collection policy and the “no-littering policy” among the residents; and
(b) exercise strong political will to implement the environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment as mandated under Republic Act  No. 9003.

6.4 We recommended that the 10% retention money be deducted for every progress payment on infrastructure projects until fifty percent (50%) completion, provided that the work is satisfactory done and on schedule, to cover uncorrected discovered defects and third party liabilities.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Management:
create the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to ensure and sustain the LGUs critical consciousness in supporting gender and development, women’s empowerment and responding to gender issues as prescribed in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01;
establish and maintain a GAD Database to ensure and sustain the LGUs critical consciousness in supporting gender and development, women’s empowerment and responding to gender issues as prescribed in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01; and
require the submission of the reviewed and approved  Annual GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) by the DILG Regional Office and the corresponding accomplishment report to COA Audit Team pursuant to COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the conduct of physical count of property and submit the RPCPPE by the Inventory Committee created for the purpose and the GSO-Designate to prepare and maintain property cards for reconciliation of the PPE balance with the PPELC of the Accounting Office. Observe the provisions of Section 124 of the MNGAS, Volume I on the prescribed timeline on the submission of RPCPPE to allow necessary verification and reconciliation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MT to submit to the MA the certified list of taxpayers showing the amount due and collectible for the year so that the PRT/SET Receivables can be adjusted to reflect the correct balances as of December 31, 2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MA and the MT to reconcile the accounts and remit whatever balance due the agencies concerned to avoid incurrence of penalties and surcharges and enable the member beneficiaries of the Municipality to avail of the benefits from the said offices and the government the immediate use of the funds for various programs and projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MA to confirm with the account owners/creditors/employees and to request them to submit documents pertaining to their claims to the Municipality. In the event that they fail to support their claims, revert the account to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund, subject to an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan through a Resolution with a condition that appropriation shall be made in the event that claim arises in the future pursuant to the provisions of Section 98 of P.D. No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MT to notify the payees or holder of checks issued one (1) month prior to the date when issued checks will become stale so that concerned payees will be aware of the depository bank’s action on these issued checks six months after the date of its issuance.
We also recommended to require the MA to adjust the books of the affected accounts whenever stale checks are discovered, particularly, during the preparation of BRS.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MA to reclassify the Bail Bonds Payable of P4,550.02 to Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the MT to demand all Municipal Collectors to submit the RCDs and remit their collections for all ORs issued on a daily basis and require her to verify the RCDs and duplicates of the issued ORs by the collectors to ensure correctness/completeness of data. Moreover, indicate the amount received in the summary of collections and remittances in all copies of RCD on a daily basis.
We also recommended the Municipal Mayor to consider appointing a liquidating officer to whom the Collectors will submit the RCDs and remit the collections daily in the absence of the MT.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MT to update the entries in her cashbooks on a daily basis to ensure that the daily balances of her accountability are correctly stated.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the MT and MA to reconcile their records on cash accounts at least every end of the quarter to ensure the reliability of cash accounts balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MT to reinstitute the amount of P569,401.75 and to produce the missing booklets of AF No. 51 and BIR Form No. 0016 (CTC), without prejudice on us filing appropriate charges, if warranted.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to exert efforts in securing all the requirements/documents needed for the titling of the said lots in the name of the Municipality to avoid third party claims in the future and possible land disputes.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MT and MA to remit the RPT share due to the component Barangays within the prescribed period in accordance with Section 271(d) of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the MT see to it that all the insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with insurance with the GSIS in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 and RA No. 656.

We recommended that the Municipality, through the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (MSWMB), prepare a 10-year SWMP and have it approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission (NSWMC) and work for the conversion of open dump to controlled dump and its closure pursuant to R.A. No. 9003 to achieve the goals, objectives and intent of the said Act.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MBO to appropriate one percent (1%) of IRA for LCPC programs, projects and activities for the protection of children as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMO to submit to the Audit Team the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and the LDC in compliance with item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended the GSO and Municipal Accountant to reconcile the
Report on the Physical Count of PPE, Property Cards, Property Ledger Cards,
and accounting records, taking into consideration the noted deficiencies in audit,
and furnish the Audit Team with the said report.

We also recommended the GSO create a database of all properties owned
by the Municipality, change the property numbers and accordingly modify the
property tags.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Assessor,
Municipal Engineer and General Services Officer to conduct the physical
inventory count of all immovable PPE and furnish the Audit Team with the said
report.

We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the cashbook of
all bank accounts against the Subsidiary Ledgers of the Municipal Accountant.
Both the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant must identify as soon
as possible the discrepancies between the cashbook and the general ledger so
that necessary adjustments can be effected.

We also recommended the Municipal Treasurer to require all Accountable
Officers to reconcile their respective records with the subsidiary ledgers of the
Accounting Office.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to submit the Disbursement
Vouchers and Report of Disbursements together with the supporting documents
to the Audit Team for post audit, otherwise the transaction will be suspended in
audit.

We recommended the Management to strictly comply with the provisions
of COA Circular No. 97-002 and adopt the policy of “no liquidation, no cash
advance”.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Include in the checklist of documents the noted deficiencies and implement
the same in the processing of cash advances and liquidations;
b. Process only the travels of JO and COS with justifiable reasons and endorsement
from their Department Head in compliance with DILG MC No. 2004-130;
c. Discontinue the practice of partial liquidation of cash advances and direct the
concern accountable officers to settle their accounts immediately
especially for the Special Disbursing Officers; and
e. Prospectively, return to the DO/AO all LR/RDs that are not included in the cash
advance list.

We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Direct the previous and current Disbursing Officer/s to settle their accountabilities
within the prescribed period provided by COA Circular No. 97-002;
b. Discontinue the practice of transferring the accountability of the DO/SDO to
another AO;
c. Segregate the cash advances of the DO per type of expenses – Personal
Services, MOOE, Special Activities, payroll of job order or contract of service
personnel and other petty operating expenses;
d. Liquidation documents should conform to the cash advance list; and
f. Forward the softcopies of the RD and RCI to the Municipal Accountant for
their reference.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to serve a written notice to the
concerned accountable officers on their outstanding cash advances as mandated
by Section 7 of COA Circular No. 2012-004. Settlement of the accountable
officer’s account is hereby enjoined within 15 days upon receipt hereof.

We also recommended the Municipal Mayor to suspend the salaries of the
concerned accountable officers upon failure to settle their obligations. The
settlement of their outstanding cash advances through salary deduction should
not be allowed.

We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to direct the Disbursing
Officer to discontinue the practice of transferring her accountability to another
AO.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to reject all liquidation of the
DO covering transfer of accountability and require for the refund thereof.

We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Stop the practice of withdrawing cash for payment of various expenditures;
b. Refund the remaining balance of cash withdrawals made in 2016;
c. All disbursements and obligations should be through payment of checks.
However, in certain instances where it is difficult, impractical or impossible
to settle obligations through checks, payments can be made the through the
cash advance of the Disbursing/Special Disbursing Officer.

We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to immediately transmit the
identified unrecorded disbursements to the Municipal Accountant to facilitate
recording thereof in the book of accounts.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to identify the payees and
make the necessary entries to record and adjust the Cash-in-bank account.
Henceforth, strict compliance by the Municipal Treasurer with regards to
the transmittal of the check disbursements and its supporting documents within
the prescribed period to the Municipal Accountant must be enjoined and strictly
monitored.

We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to send a written notice to the
concerned payees at least one month before a check becomes stale. Moreover, we
require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the
books for the long outstanding/stale checks in accordance with Section 59,
Volume I of the NGAS Manual.

We recommended the Management to facilitate the transfer of title of the
real properties under the name of the Municipal Government of Quezon to
prevent future dispute of ownership. We also recommended the Municipal
Accountant to maintain the Real Property Ledger Cards for all the real
properties of the Municipality with complete details.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the land held for
distribution. The Management should submit to the Audit Team all the
necessary documents for the implementation and distribution of the lots to the
respective recipients.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to ensure recognition of the
monthly depreciation of all the PPE of the Municipality for fair presentation in
the financial statement.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary entry
to remove the inventoriable items from the PPE. Further, we recommended the
Municipal GSO to issue an Inventory Custodian Slip to the Accountable Officer,
who is in actual custody of these items. The Municipal GSO should make a
separate database/lists of these items for monitoring purposes.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to insure all government
properties with the Property Insurance Fund.

We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to personally pay for the
penalties incurred for late remittance of the employees’ premium contributions
to the GSIS.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended to the Municipal
Mayor to:
a. Maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund by speeding up the
implementation of the prioritized programs, projects and activities as per
Annual Investment Program and that closer coordination among the officials
involved in planning, execution and monitoring be undertaken to achieve the
desired level of outcome.
b. Instruct the Municipal Planning Development Officer to immediately look
into the unimplemented developmental projects as embodied in the Annual
Investment Program. Identify causes of non-implementation and develop
feasible and specific solutions to effectively implement developmental
projects.
c. Instruct the Municipal Engineer to be very prudent, meticulous,
conscientious and precise in evaluating the timely and perfect
implementation of each and every project. Likewise, he should also conduct
regular inspection of the projects so that accomplishment of the contractor is
monitored and evaluated based on the required accomplishment against the
number of days stipulated in the contract.
d. Should there have been delays incurred in the project implementation,
Liquidated Damages must be imposed from the contractors concerned. Such
amount shall be computed and deducted from any money due or which may
become due the contractor under each contract and/or collect such liquidated
damages from the retention money or other securities posted by the
contractor, whichever is convenient to the Municipality, in accordance with
Item 8.3, Annex “E” of the Revised I.R.R. of R.A. 9184.
e. Reappropriate the unexpended balance of the completed projects of the 20%
DF to finance development PPAs identified to be funded under the 20% DF
for the ensuing year.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to create an Inventory Committee
and enjoin the concerned department heads’ compliance with the requirements
of COA Circular No. 2015-008 and submit the corresponding reports to the
Audit Team for evaluation and verification.

We recommended the Management to:
a. Require the Municipal Engineer to produce the needed Detailed Engineering
Design specifically the designs for road sections including line canal
otherwise he must explain why he should not be charged of gross neglect of
duty under CSC rules and regulations;
b. Coordinate with the Provincial Government of Palawan on how to settle the
legitimate owner of the land for the resettlement site;
c. Comply with all the provision of Section 17.6 and Annex “A” of RA 9184 on
the Detailed Engineering for the Procurement of Infrastructure Projects
before conducting the bidding and award of infrastructure contract to avoid
delay in construction, waste of time & effort and additional loss to
government funds; and
d. Require the contractor to submit the required Irrevocable Standby Letter of
Credit/Security Bond/Bank Guarantee in compliance with the provisions of
Annex E, Item. 4.2 of IRR of RA 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated
June 14, 2012.

We recommended the Management to strictly adhere to the guidelines in
the rendition of overtime services in accordance with CSC-DBM JC No. 1 series
of 2015.

We recommended the management to:
a. Stop the practice of engaging the services of private legal counsel and to
strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 98-002 dated June
09, 1998.

b. Strictly comply with the provisions of RA 9184 and its revised IRR on the
procurement of consultancy services. The procurement must be done
through competitive bidding or alternative methods of procurement.
We recommended that management implement strict observance of the reglementary period in the submission of financial reports and accounts to the Auditor to give him ample time for review.

We recommended to stop the practice of charging to LDRRMF expenses which   are not related to and do not support disaster risk management activities and to adhere strictly the guidelines set under Section 2 of RA 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended  that the rules and regulations set in the  DBM-DILG-DND-GCG-COA Joint Circular No. 2015-01 relative to the granting of cash advance and its subsequent liquidation be strictly observed and implemented.
We also recommended to management to furnish immediately the lacking documents to the ICFAU to expedite the audit of your Intelligence and Confidential Fund (ICF).

We recommended that the Municipality should comply with the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 and ensure proper granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances. The Municipal Accountant should ensure proper monitoring of liquidation of cash advances and implementation of existing rules and regulations on cash advances, including the withholding of salaries of accountable officers who fail to effect prompt liquidation in accordance with existing rules and regulations. All cash advances shall be fully liquidated at the end of each year. Except for petty cash fund, the AO shall refund any unexpended balance to the Cashier/Collecting Officer who will issue the necessary official receipt.
It is also strictly recommended that each accountable officer with a total cash accountability of P2,000.00 or more shall be bonded. The amount of bond shall depend on the total accountability of the officer as fixed by the Head of the Agency. An official or employee who has both money and property accountability, shall be bonded only once to cover both accountabilities, but the amount of the bond shall be in accordance with the Schedule.

We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to withhold salaries and other emoluments of the person liable that are still in government service until the disallowance is fully settled.  Also, issue demand letter on the persons liable who are separated from government service.

We recommended that the Local School Board (LSB) be guided by the provisions of RA 7160 and the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circulars in the preparation of the SEF Annual Budget and identify the priority expenditures pursuant thereto. Any issues or conflicts arising from its implementation and cases not covered by the provisions shall be submitted to the DepEd for resolution and in consultation with the municipal government.
We also recommended that the LSB stop the charging of water and electric consumption, security, janitorial and admin services of public elementary and high schools to the SEF for they have their own budget for said expenditures and instead utilize the funds for the needs of other schools. In addition, expenses incurred by the municipality for scholarship grants should not be charged against the Special Education Fund, but against the General Funds of the municipality of Quezon.
It is strictly recommended also that the SEF be utilized by the Local School Boards for the operation and maintenance of elementary and secondary public schools only which shall mean government financed and managed schools which include pre-school, elementary, secondary schools, national and general comprehensive high schools and their annexes and those public elementary and secondary schools established by law and should not include private educational institutions.

We recommended that the procuring entity submit a copy of the contracts and purchase orders within five days from issuance as required under COA Circular 2009-001, together with the bidding documents, BAC resolution, minutes of the proceedings, abstracts, and such other documents peculiar to the contract and/or to the mode of procurement which are deemed necessary in the conduct of auditorial review and in the technical evaluation thereof.
We recommended that management require all the Accountable Officers to apply for fidelity bond and the amount of bond shall be in accordance with their maximum cash accountability.

We recommended that the prescribed minimum employees’ monthly net take home pay of P3,500.00 under the GAA be observed. We also recommended that management stop immediately the practice of including the payments of loan amortizations to private lending institutions as deductions from the payroll and performing the collection and monitoring activities of the private lending institutions unless it is classified as a thrift bank or rural bank accredited by BSP. Also, require the personnel involved to transact directly with the private institutions.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Engineer and other officers concerned for proper prioritization of fully funded projects to ensure their timely completion, thus precluding unwarranted delay of the implementation of programs, projects and activities.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant, Treasurer and Budget Officer to account for the unremitted due to GSIS amounting to P2,260,833.90 from CY 2007 and previous years. Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant verify/reconcile the long overdue past due to GSIS  with the GSIS copy  furnish  the Auditor with  its supporting schedules.
observations.managementAction: A reconciliation was made

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to send demand letters to previous officers and employees to settle their cash advances immediately and require the incumbent officers and employees to liquidate their cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Demand letter tracers sent.
observations.reason: Demand letters were sent to accountable officers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the immediate restitution of said shortage. This should be resolved immediately to avoid initiation of administrative or criminal actions against the accountable officers.
observations.managementAction: Management imposed restitution from accountable officers.
observations.reason: The accountable officers partially restituted the shortages.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Accountable Officers to reconcile their cash book records with the Accounting ledgers at least quarterly. Discrepancies identified should be adjusted accordingly to come up with updated and more accurate and reliable reports.
observations.managementAction: Management imposes quarterly reconciliation by the accountable officers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit complete documents to support disbursements of supplemental feeding activities to avoid the suspension of these expenditures in audit.
observations.managementAction: The management ensures complete documentation through MSWD.
