Management ensures compliance with NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-01.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to require all concerned officials to comply with the monthly submission of Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the format prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.reason: Management thru LDRRMO submitted the required report in the first quarter but failed to submit subsequently.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to purchase motor oils from gasoline service stations and not from an exclusive dealer to avoid extravagant and excessive purchases.
observations.reason: Management did not purchase excessive and extravagant additive oils during the last semester of the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the accounting office to accordingly adjust the books to reflect the correct balance of cash in bank.
observations.managementAction: Management adjust the stale checks for CY 2015.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to withhold corresponding taxes from the bonuses and other benefits granted to employees in excess of the statutory ceiling of P 82,000.00 and remit the amount to the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) in accordance with the existing laws to avoid commencement of appropriate legal action from the BIR against the erring withholding agents and employees.
observations.managementAction: Management collected tax for the said bonus.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipality to adopt and promulgate the provisions of RA 9003 on the establishment of Materials Recovery Facility to ensure the promotion of health, hygiene and sanitation of the residents of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Management updated its 10 year SWM plan.
observations.reason: Management lacks budget to implement fully R.A. 9003.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipality initiate activities to pursue the formulation and operationalization of its own Gender and Development (GAD) Code.
observations.managementAction: Resolution

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed that members of Municipal School Board:
prepare a balanced budget with conscious and concerted efforts of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization;
request for inputs from the Deped official/School Division Superintendent in the prioritization of SEF projects and expenditures in accordance with the DepEd criteria and with due regard to the provisions of laws, rules and regulations prescribing the limitations on the use of the fund; and
maximize the utilization of SEF to attain its objectives and take appropriate actions such as realignment or reprograming, if necessary.
We recommended and management agreed to follow strictly the imprest fund system of petty cash fund as provided in Section 48 of the Volume I of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for LGUs.

We recommended and management agreed to establish and maintain a GAD Database and formulate and pass GAD Code or ordinances to ensure and sustain the LGUs critical consciousness in supporting gender and development, women’s empowerment and responding to gender issues as prescribed in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

We recommended and management agreed to create the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to ensure and sustain the LGUs critical consciousness in supporting gender and development, women’s empowerment and responding to gender issues as prescribed in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and management agreed to remit fully the balance of unremitted taxes to BIR as required by Revenue Regulations No. 02-98.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
instruct the GSO-Designate to exert efforts in locating the Property Acknowledgement Receipts to determine the persons responsible for the unreconciled properties.


require the Municipal Accountant and GSO-Designate to complete/update the ledger cards and property cards and reconcile the same to establish the correct PPE balances. Investigate and adjust accordingly any discrepancy that may be noted.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office is already maintaining the ledger cards.
observations.reason:
Difficulty in locating the Property Acknowledgement Receipts (PARs) as there is no central file previously maintained and the PARs were not renewed to establish the current persons responsible for the properties.

The             completing/
updating of the property cards was not prioritized due to the busy schedule of the GSO–Designate who is also acting as the MDRRMO–Designate, hence reconciliation was not facilitated. 
For this reason, the physical inventory was also not conducted for CY 2016. Please refer to Observation No. 1, page 25.

observations.recommendation:
We restated our recommendation to the Municipal Mayor to:

instruct the Municipal Treasurer to require all Collectors to remit their collections for all official receipts issued, otherwise, relieve them from their post. Also, require her to verify the RCD prepared by the collectors and the duplicates of the issued official receipts to ensure correctness/ completeness of data and to indicate the amount received in the summary of collection and remittance in all copies of RCD.

require the Collector with expired fidelity bond to update his application for bond with the Bureau of Treasury; and

stop the practice of assigning JO employee as collector since they are not considered government employee.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Collectors already prepared the RCDs daily.







The application for bond with the Bureau of Treasury was already made.






The Job Order is now a casual employee.
observations.reason: Collections were still not remitted by the Collectors daily as the Municipal Treasurer was not able to verify the RCDs during the time she is absent, on official travel or attending seminars. Please refer to Observation No. 7, page 33.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor implement strictly the regulations on the proper granting and liquidation of all cash advances, in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002.

We also recommended to require the:

Municipal    Officials/
Employees concerned to liquidate immediately the total amount of ₱79,655.00. Failure of the AO to liquidate his cash advance within the prescribed period shall constitute a valid cause for the withholding of his salary and the instruction of other sanctions as provided for under Paragraphs 9.2 and 9.3 hereof.

Municipal Accountant to stop granting additional cash advance unless the previous one is liquidated, and to ensure that claims for cash advances and the liquidation reports are completely accomplished and supported with proper documentation;

We further recommended to require the:

Municipal Treasurer to implement the withholding of salaries or any amount due the Municipal Officials/Employees who failed to liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction:
The regulations on the granting and liquidation of cash advances were already observed.




The Cash Advances were already liquidated.





The Municipal Accountant strictly implements the rules on cash advances and is not granting additional cash advances unless the previous one is liquidated.




Municipal Officials/ Employees are now liquidating the cash advances within the prescribed period, thus withholding of salaries is no longer necessary.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the recommendation of the previous Audit Team that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to restitute the remaining amount of ₱133,334.66 and to produce the 350 pcs. missing Accountable Form No. 51 and CTC without prejudice to filing of appropriate charges, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: The amount of P1,233,334.66 was already restituted on various dates. The missing accountable forms were already submitted by the Municipal Treasurer except for one booklet of AF No. 51 and one booklet of BIR Form No. 0016 (CTC).
observations.reason: During the cash count/ examination conducted by the Audit Team on June 14, 2017, the cash and cash items presented by the MT was short by a total amount of P569,401,75. One booklet of AF No. 51 and one booklet of BIR Form No. 0016 (CTC) remain unaccounted. Please refer to Observation No. 10, page 37.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to send collection notices/ demand letters and exert all efforts to collect the said receivables from the concerned debtors to ensure the continuity of the livelihood programs/ projects and give other prospective beneficiaries equal opportunity in availing livelihood assistance. If all means failed, resort to legal action to avoid loss of government funds.
observations.managementAction: Efforts were made to collect from the concerned debtors resulting to a minimal collection of P1,500.00 for CY 2016.
observations.reason: Financial constraints of the debtors affected their ability to settle their obligations to the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor submit the:

list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year, as of January 1, 2015; and

monthly monitoring report in compliance with the above cited regulations.
observations.managementAction:
The list of PPAs was already submitted to the Audit Team.




The report was already submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GAD’s Focal person to submit to the Audit Team the Accomplishment Report for GAD Fund in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014, to enable the Audit Team to conduct timely audit of funds, programs and activities on gender and development.
observations.managementAction: The GAD accomplishment report was submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
the Office of the Municipal Accountant should
continue its efforts to locate
pertinent source documents to
reduce the unidentified balances in
the property ledger cards.
observations.managementAction:
Partially
Reiterated in Observation No.
1.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to require the ICO-Municipal Accountant to provide documents such as respective CIP Inventory/Inspection Report to prove the completion of the CIP assets that were transferred to Infrastructure accounts on the Converted Financial Position. Otherwise, revert the transferred asset amounting to ₱40,960,916.36 to its respective CIP asset account.
observations.managementAction: The transferred assets were already reverted to its Asset Account.

We submit to what have observed in the audit memorandum. We have sent demand letter to concerned accountable officers and employees to settle their accounts. In addition, the Management advised that several employees and officers now have signed an authority to deduct from monthly payroll to settle their accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Treasurer to instruct the collectors on the strict adherence to Section 29 Volume I of the Manual on New Government Accounting System (NGAS) and Section 26 of COA Circular No. 92-382. Furthermore, implement measures to ensure prompt reporting and remittances of collections.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer adhered to rules and regulations on recording and reporting of collections. Strictly implemented daily report of collections and depositing all collections weekly.
The BAC strongly observed the recommendations of the COA, the agency maintains separate subsidiary ledgers and journals of special accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC, or other officer assigned as the case maybe, ensure the timely submission of copies of government contracts and each of all the documents forming part thereof within five (5) working days from execution pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 to ensure timely review and evaluation of the same so that the management could be immediately notified of any defects/deficiencies noted thereon.
observations.managementAction: The BAC replied the AOM that the timeframe of submission of the documents pertaining to procurement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC should adhere with the provisions of Section 12.2 of the IRR of RA 9184 and must prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report in its prescribed format for the CY 2015 for the approval and submission by the Municipal Mayor to the GPPB and shall be done on each ensuing semesters thereafter.
observations.managementAction: Procurement Monitoring Report for 2015 was prepared and submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Head of the Engineering Office to comply with the provisions of COA Circular 2013-004 so that the evaluation/validation/inspection thereof could be conducted.
observations.managementAction: The list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities to be implemented as of December 2016 was already submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to require that all POs issued are acknowledged with the date of receipt by the supplier as well as the date of Inspection and Acceptance Reports (IARs), and ensure that all other transaction documents are properly dated so that application of liquidated damages can be enforced.
observations.managementAction: The documents pertaining to the transactions were submitted and the transactions are now properly dated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to impose the appropriate amount of liquidated damages for late deliveries under their respective contracts in accordance with Section 3.1, Annex D of the RIRR of RA 9184, and to exercise the rights allowed under Sections 3.1 & 3.2 of the same RIRR for late deliveries.
observations.managementAction: We deducted the amount of variation order & liquidated damages from the final payment to the contractor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly abide by the standards set forth under R.A. No. 9184 and its Revised IRR and other relevant laws on government procurement. The LCE, as head of the procuring entity, should ensure that the members of the BAC and TWG shall give utmost priority to the BAC assignments at hand and complete due diligence on evaluation of bid and post-qualification procedure, and ensure that the technical and financial qualifications of a bidder meet all the requirements and conditions specified in the Bidding Documents. Also ensure contractors meet required qualifications including licensed personnel and extend performance security to cover defects liability period as necessary.
observations.managementAction: The Contractor claimed the final payment but with the certificate of objection issued by the ICO-Municipal Accountant dated October 30, 2015. The BAC submitted a rejoinder for this transaction.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to deduct the amount due the contractor to cover the cost of the variation order amounting to ₱1,081,017.79. The management should strictly abide by the standards set forth under Annex “G” of R.A. No. 9184 and its Revised IRR for the design and build of infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction: We deducted the amount of variation order & liquidated damages from the final payment to the contractor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Engineer and LGU officials concerned to explain why the construction of the multi-purpose centers have not yet been constructed despite the delivery of the construction materials. Moreover, ensure that payments are supported with complete documents and submit the lacking supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: Tourism and Women’s center multi-purpose building Phase I were already implemented as of December 31, 2015. The lacking documents were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to improve programs and project implementation by strengthening planning, budgeting, execution and monitoring: 1) improve planning and budgeting capabilities including preparing APPs properly indicating mode of procurement and linking to AIP; 2) enlist experts for feasibility studies; 3) widen collaboration; 4) include guidelines and policies in planning and budgeting; 5) provide timeline and fast track implementation; 6) evaluate prior funded projects; 7) enhance accountability and monitoring by MPDC; 8) conduct feasibility and detailed engineering prior to implementation; 9) follow DPWH DO No. 22 for Design Plans and cost estimates.
observations.managementAction: The Agency hired additional technical staff in the Engineering office. Some of the unfinished projects were already implemented and some are in the process for bidding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management observe the posting of public notice of all the projects in conspicuous places in the venue of the projects indicating sufficient and relevant information about the projects to promote transparency and accountability and encourage public participation and to secure the right of the people to know information in compliance with COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2010-101 dated September 23, 2010.
observations.managementAction: The management implemented the recommendation to adhere to the COA rules & regulation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to exercise prudence and diligence in the disbursements of the Special Education Fund and to strictly adhere to Section 272 of Republic Act No. 7160 and DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01 s. 1998 dated April 14, 1998. Traveling and other expenses that are not priority expenditures should not be charged to the SEF.
observations.managementAction: The Management implemented the recommendation and stopped charging non-priority expenditures to the SEF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 76-41 dated July 30, 1976 and RA No. 9184 in all government procurement especially on splitting of government contracts to avoid suspension and/or disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Management strictly complied and stopped the practice of splitting of contracts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to instruct the BAC through the ICO-Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking supporting documents to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The lacking supporting documents have been submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive must observe and follow the limitations imposed under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2010-139 dated December 2, 2010 and Memorandum Circular No. 2011-59 dated April 25, 2011. Traveling expenses which are excessive and extravagant shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Management only allowed trainings & seminars within the region, however conventions, conferences of Associations, groups exclusively held outside the province are also allowed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Government:
1. Stop the practice on the use of prepaid load cards and avail only the most economical corporate plan currently available in the market being offered by mobile phone companies that will suit the needs of the LGU.
2. Require the Accounting Office as an internal control unit to verify and determine whether calls and messages charged to the LGU are all official in nature, and ensure billing statements are completely submitted including breakdown of additional charges incurred before processing the vouchers.
3. Provide justification/supporting notes to the allowable amounts indicated in the Memorandum Order such as nature of work of the officials and employees, frequency of official calls, name and office of person being contacted on official business.
4. Grant only to officials and employees who are department heads and consider the necessity, amount and rank in the grant of cell phone loads. Be guided by provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-003 with Annex D as reference. Payment found to be excessive shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Management stopped the practice of using prepaid load cards. The Department heads are allowed to have postpaid plans of ₱1,000 each and the officials of ₱1,500 each plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Government: a. To strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. Moreover, cause the immediate submission of the documentary deficiencies. b. To strictly observe the provisions of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA No. 10121 in planning and implementation of the projects, programs and activities of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office. c. To submit to the auditors updated list of serviceable vehicles of the municipality with their corresponding plate numbers for verification of fuel consumption and validation purposes. d. To exercise due diligence in the preparation of required documents to support government transaction to ensure accuracy, completeness and reliability of the same.
observations.managementAction: The Management implemented the recommendations accordingly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management observe the proper review procedure of GAD Plan & Budget in accordance with PWC-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01, dated July 18, 2013 and ensure GAD programs implemented are based on duly approved GAD Plan and Budget by the DILG Regional Office.
observations.managementAction: The Management complied and submitted the GAD Plan & Budget for review.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management ensure that at least 5% of total appropriation in the Annual LEP is set aside for GAD Fund to support gender-responsive programs, projects and activities. We further recommended providing supplemental GPB for additional GAD appropriation to ensure that the fund is solely used for GAD related PPAs.
observations.managementAction: The Management provided supplemental GPB for the additional GAD appropriation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the institutionalization of Gender and Development (GAD) in the Municipality and the integration thereof to its overall annual plans and programs and strict adherence to the provisions of PWC-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01, dated July 18, 2013. Furthermore, formulate GAD Plan that would reflect the actual gender issues and concerns prevailing in the locality.
observations.managementAction: Fully implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend to stop the practice of charging to LDRRMF expenses which are not related to and do not support disaster risk management activities and to adhere strictly the guidelines set under Section 2 of RA 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002.
We recommend that management should follow Section 6.1.8.4 of COA-DBM_JC2015-01 dated January 8, 2015.

We recommend for the MPMC member to strictly follow the guidelines provided for the utilization of funds appropriated for various projects and to receive honoraria only when there is a specific grant thereof by proper authorities.
We recommend to strictly adhere to the provision of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2004-78 and RPMES Manual of Operation.
Instruct the Municipal Accountant and General Services in charge to follow  strictly the prescribed accounting procedures for inventories.

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to return the unutilized PDAF balance to the Bureau of Treasury for reversion to the unappropriated surplus in the general fund of the national government in accordance with the Supreme Court decision dated November 19, 2013.

Submit the LDRRMP for review and evaluation. Direct the officials concerned to follow strictly of COA Circular No.2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
Ensure the proper charges of the 5% LDRRMF.

Instruct the Officials concerned to adhere  to the provisions of Section 119 of PD. 1445 and Section 350 of RA. 7160 in the use of appropriations to avoid disallowance in the audit of the subsequent transactions.

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to withhold salaries and other emoluments of the person liable that are still in  government service until the disallowance is fully settled.  Also, issue demand letter on the persons liable who are separated from government service.

Require officers and employees concerned to observe the prescribed liquidation period of cash advances.
Withhold payment of salaries and other emoluments due to the officials and employees with outstanding cash advances until after final liquidation or settlement of their cash advances shall have been made.

We recommend that the Local School Board (LSB) be guided by the provisions  of RA 7160 and the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circulars in the preparation of the  SEF Annual Budget and identify the priority expenditures pursuant thereto. Any issues or conflicts arising from its implementation and cases not covered by the provisions shall be submitted to the DepEd for resolution and in consultation with the municipal government.

Require the Municipal Accountant to adhere the provision of Section 272 of RA 7160 and Item 4 of Joint Circular No. 01, s. 1998 dated April 14, 1998 of the DECS, DBM and DILG in the utilization of  Special Education Fund.

Direct the officials and employees concerned to submit the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel consumption to the auditor for review and evaluation, Also, require the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare/monitor Registry of Appropriation, Allotments and Obligations – Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (RAAOMO) to avoid over charging.

Accredit the NGOs/POs and execute MOA or loan agreements covering the loan releases following the guideline prescribed under COA Circular No, 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007.  Further, direct the accountant to maintain individual subsidiary ledger to the loan account.
Direct the Livelihood Officer to intensify the collection on long overdue accounts of the livelihood project/assistance.
